En: I can't understand how I didn't notice a giant ice bear fridge myself...
Zh: “我不明白，为什么我自己没有注意到一台巨大的冰熊冰箱呢……”

En: One more thing. How do I get inside the building?
Zh: “还有一件事。我怎么进到那里面去？”

En: One more thing. How do I get inside the building? I already talked to your mom, but she's not letting anyone in.
Zh: “还有一件事。我怎么进到那里面去？我已经跟你妈妈聊过了，不过她不让任何人进去。”

En: The girl shrugs cheerfully: "Well, it is pretty dark in there..."
Zh: 女孩愉快地耸耸肩。“这个嘛，那里特别黑……”

En: Um... that's a problem. The only way in is through the bookstore, but my mum is pretty strict about not letting anyone in.
Zh: “呃……这是个问题。唯一的入口就是书店，不过我妈妈挺严格的，她不让任何人进去。”

En: But, I don't know, you're a *police man*... She says it with admiration, eyeing you up and down. "Maybe you can convince her somehow?"
Zh: “但是，我不知道，你是一个*警察*……”她上下打量着你，口气很是钦佩。“也许你可以想办法说服她？”

En: Um... she can be pretty strict, you're right about that. I don't really know what to say. You could try sneaking in? She smiles shyly, like she's not sure it's the right answer.
Zh: “呃……你说的没错，她有时候是很严格。我真的不知道该说什么好。你可以试着溜进去？”她害羞地笑了，似乎不确定这是不是正确答案。

En: I'm sorry, sir, I really don't know.
Zh: “我很抱歉，先生，我真的不知道。”

En: Uh... She doesn't know what to say.
Zh: “呃……”她不知道该说什么了。

En: Say, child... *you* wouldn't happen to know a good fridge, would you?
Zh: “话说，小朋友……*你*不会碰巧知道哪里有一台好冰箱吧？”

En: A fridge?
Zh: “一台冰箱？”

En: Yes. Like a big, big fridge a cop could put a dead body into.
Zh: “是的。比如一台特别特别大的冰箱，可以让警察在里面放一具尸体。”

En: Hah! She smiles. "You mean like the ice bear fridge? Man, that's scary."
Zh: “哈！”她笑了。“你是说冰熊冰箱吗？老天啊，那家伙特别吓人。”

En: Ice bear fridge?
Zh: “冰熊冰箱？”

En: Ice bear fridge?
Zh: “冰熊冰箱？”

En: Yes. Like a bear, but white. There's a fridge below the building, in the basement. She points underground. "With red glowing eyes. I went back there once. Behind the bookstore..."
Zh: “是的。像一头熊，不过是白色的。这栋建筑下面有一台冰箱，在地下室里。”她指了指地下。“它的红色眼睛会发光。我曾经去过一次。就在书店背后……”

En: She lowers her voice. "Mum doesn't want me to go there anymore. Not that I want to. It was pretty scary.
Zh: 她压低了声音。“妈妈不想再让我过去了。也不是说我想去。那里真的挺吓人的。”

En: And there's a big fridge there?
Zh: “所以那里有一台冰箱？”

En: Yes. She smiles.
Zh: “是的。”她笑了。

En: You don't look much like a policeman. She examines you, as if to find something policeman-like.
Zh: “你看起来不像个警察。”她打量着你，似乎想找出一些像警察的东西。

En: Huh, well what does a cop look like then?
Zh: “呃，那警察看起来什么样？”

En: What could you do to help her anyway?
Zh: 再说了，你能帮她什么呢？

En: Did someone say *sneak around in the cellar*? We should do that.
Zh: 好像有人说*偷溜进地下室*？我们应该这么做。

En: Yeah, poor people are boring.
Zh: “是啊，穷人都挺无聊的。”

En: I guess, yes. People in books are always very interesting. Especially the romance-people.
Zh: “我猜是吧。书里的人总是很有意思。特别是浪漫的那些。”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: Sometimes they break up for a little while, but then they always get back together.
Zh: “有时候他们会暂时分手，不过之后总是会和好的。”

En: Why can't you help me? I'm beyond help, right?
Zh: “你为什么不帮我呢？我已经没救了，对吗？”

En: Wonderful.
Zh: “太棒了。”

En: What *was* that? An idea for an unfinished novel stuck somewhere in your fore-brain?
Zh: *那是*什么？一个未完成的小说点子卡在你前脑的某个地方了？

En: You clearly have issues you need to address.
Zh: 你显然有需要解决的问题。

En: Later when you get the chance, you should address these issues -- by getting *drunk*. That'll show them!
Zh: 之后有机会的时候，你应该解决这些问题——只要*喝醉*就可以了。这样就能让他们好看！

En: They're all dorks.
Zh: 他们全都是笨蛋。

En: There's more that can be achieved here. Ask her to do the same.
Zh: 能取得的成就还远远不止于此。她也应该做同样的事。

En: You have absolutely no idea. Familiar how? You must have forgotten something you heard again.
Zh: 你完全不知道。怎么个熟悉法？你肯定又忘记之前听到的什么东西了。

En: The swivelling eyes of a loony drug addict. That is what she meant. You were probably gurning too.
Zh: 一个疯狂的瘾君子，他的眼睛会快速转动。她说的是这个意思。你也可能是在做鬼脸。

En: I'm glad I could help you, sir. She smiles a wide, helpful smile.
Zh: “我很高兴能帮到你，先生。”她露出了一个大大的、热情的微笑。

En: Out of the rain and into the gutter.
Zh: 认清现实，陷入毁灭。

En: You just can't win.
Zh: 你赢不了的。

En: Wait, where did you get it?
Zh: “等等，你是从哪里找到的？”

En: Just what Dick Mullen would ask! She lowers her voice. "I got it from behind the curtains. I'm not really supposed to go there..."
Zh: “迪克·马伦也会这么问的！”她压低了声音。“我是从窗帘背后弄到的。我真的不应该到那边去……”

En: Yes! I used to stand out there all the time, before mother told me to focus on my homework.
Zh: “是的！我以前总是站在外面，后来妈妈告诉我说要专心做家庭作业。”

En: I doubt that.
Zh: “我表示怀疑。”

En: No, you don't have party eyes anymore.
Zh: “不，你没有派对眼了。”

En: Good thing she's didn't say PARTY EYES loud, her mother's nearby...
Zh: 还好她说派对眼的时候声音不大，她妈妈就在附近……

En: I am the law.
Zh: “我就是法律。”

En: Hi.
Hello, sir! Step right in, the store is open! A young girl with chubby red cheeks waves at you, smiling. Her nose is also red from the cold.
Zh: “你好，先生！进去看看吧，商店开门了！”一个脸颊胖乎乎、红彤彤的小姑娘朝你微笑着招手。鼻子也因为寒冷冻得通红。

En: What is your name?
Zh: “你叫什么名字？”

En: It's a bookstore, sir! We sell books, postcards, and some board games. She points at the window. "It's called *Crime, Romance, and Biographies of Famous People*."
Zh: “这是家书店，先生！我们卖书、明信片还有些桌游。”她指向窗户。“名字叫*犯罪、浪漫和名人传记*。”

En: My name is Annette, sir. My mum, her name is Plaisance, she owns the store. She's inside, minding the register... or organizing the stock. The girl gazes at the window, then suddenly jolts, her eyes wide, as if recalling something:
Zh: “我的名字叫安妮特，先生。我妈妈叫普莱桑斯，她是这家店的老板。她在里面，照料柜台……或者是整理库存。”

En: What is this crime business?!
Zh: “犯罪是怎么回事？！”

En: Crime fiction is about murders or burglaries or things like that. And the work of a policeman or a private detective who's trying to solve a crime and catch the criminals.
Zh: “犯罪小说讲的是凶杀案，或者入室盗窃之类的内容。还有警察或者私家侦探破案和抓捕犯人的工作。”

En: Why would anyone want to *read* about crime?
Zh: “为什么会有人想要*阅读*犯罪故事呢？”

En: It's exciting to people, I guess. They get to imagine dangerous things. And it's kinda like a puzzle, where you can guess who the criminal is or how the good guys are going to catch him.
Zh: “我猜对于大家来说挺刺激的吧。他们可以想象很危险的事情。而且有点像谜题，你可以猜谁是犯人，或者好人是怎么抓住他们的。”

En: I'm a policeman myself, by the way. *The Law*, as I said.
Zh: “顺便说一句，我本人就是警察。就像我说过的，我是*法律*。”

En: I'm a policeman myself, by the way.
Zh: “顺便说一句，我本人就是警察。”

En: If I looked like Mr. Mullen, all the criminals would go off running the very moment they saw me. This way I can go undercover, blend in, collect evidence.
Zh: “如果我看起来像马伦先生，所有罪犯一看见我就会撒腿就跑。所以我要隐藏身份，混入其中，收集证据。”

En: Okay, then maybe I'm not a policeman? Or should stop being one.
Zh: “好吧，那也许我不是个警察？或者不再当警察了。”

En: Didn't mean to offend, sir, sorry, sir. It's just that you don't look like Dick Mullen. She points to a book cover, on which you see a strapping Vespertine officer. He stands grimly over the body of a dead woman.
Zh: “我不是有意冒犯你的，先生。抱歉，先生。只是你看起来不像迪克·马伦。”她指着一本书的封面，你在上面看见一个魁梧的维斯珀裔警官。他正冷酷地站在一具女尸前面。

En: I used to be exactly like that Mullen guy. Then I decided to *live a little*.
Zh: “我以前看起来跟那个叫马伦的家伙一模一样。然后我决定要*及时行乐*。”

En: If you grow up to be clever, you'll discover there's no such thing as a 'grown-up.'
Zh: “如果你以后长成个聪明人，就会发现根本没有所谓的‘大人’这回事。”

En: She doesn't know what to make of that. But she'll remember it.
Zh: 她不知道这是怎么回事。不过她会记住的。

En: Okay... sir.
Zh: “好吧……先生。”

En: And everything's better now, sir?
Zh: “现在一切好些了吗，先生？”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: No.
Zh: “没有。”

En: I don't know.
Zh: “我不知道。”

En: Maybe you can show me some *real* police work, sir? Like in the books! The cover image of Dick Mullen seems to stare at you with harsh disapproval.
Zh: “也许你可以让我看看*真正*的警察工作是怎么样的，先生？”迪克·马伦的封面形象死死地盯着你，似乎极其不以为然。“就像书里写的一样！”

En: She looks at Dick Mullen, frowning.
Zh: 她看着迪克·马伦，皱起眉头。

En: He isn't even drawn right. This is not how human shoulders work, the perspective is all wrong.
Zh: “他的样子画的都不对。人类的肩膀不可能这样的，透视完全错了。”

En: Anyhow, it's much easier to work undercover if one doesn't look like him.
Zh: “无论如何，如果长得不像他的话，做卧底就容易多了。”

En: She examines the picture, trying to find whatever is wrong with it. She then shrugs and puts the book aside, unconvinced.
Zh: 她仔细查看图片，试着找出到底哪里不对。接着，她耸耸肩，把书放在了一边，心有疑虑。

En: You fail to deduce anything substantial.
Zh: 你没能推断出任何实质性的东西。

En: Come on, anyone would notice that! She rolls her eyes.
Zh: “拜托，是个人都能注意到的！”她翻了翻白眼。

En: What is romance?
Zh: “浪漫是什么？”

En: It's the type of book where there's a rich lady and she has to choose between the good man and the bad man. She smiles at the thought, perhaps imagining herself in that situation.
Zh: “这类书讲的都是有钱的女性需要在好男人和坏男人之间做出选择的故事。”一想到这里，她笑了起来，也许是在想象处于这种情况下的自己。

En: What about a book where the *man and lady business* doesn't work out at all?
Zh: “那有没有讲*男女关系*完全没结果的书呢？”

En: I don't think it's a romance story if the main characters break up, though... She pauses, trying to figure out the appropriate answer.
Zh: “不过，如果书中的主角分手的话，我觉得就不叫浪漫小说了……”她暂停了一下，试着找出合适的答案。

En: What about a poor man getting a rich lady?
Zh: “那有没有讲穷小子跟富家女在一起的故事？”

En: It happens. But usually the guy gets rich in the process, or should actually be rich himself, but has lost his family property unjustly, like during the Revolution or something.
Zh: “也有。不过一般穷小子会在中途变富有，或者其实本来就是有钱人，但却不公平地失去了家庭财产，比如大革命或者什么的。”

En: I see.
Zh: “我明白了。”

En: Maybe I will.
Zh: “也许我会吧。”

En: Shouldn't you be at school or something?
Zh: “难道你不该去上学的吗？”

En: I do my studies at home at the moment. I have to help Mum keep this place running.
Zh: “现在我在家学习。我要帮妈妈维持这家店的运转。”

En: (Shake off the cold.) "And how's this business doing?"
Zh: （摆脱掉寒冷。）“生意怎么样？”

En: How's the business going?
Zh: “生意怎么样？”

En: Mum says it's peachy. She was a little afraid at first, there's talk about this house being... She looks over her shoulder. "Cursed."
Zh: “妈妈说很好。一开始她也有些害怕，有人说这个房子……”她转过头望了望。“被诅咒了。”

En: Cursed? In what way?
Zh: “被诅咒？怎么个诅咒法？”

En: I don't think curses are real.
Zh: “我觉得诅咒不是真的。”

En: Cursed in a way that makes them say that no business has ever really thrived here, sir. That they all go... She's looking for the right word...
Zh: “他们说这个诅咒让这里的商店永远也不会真正的兴旺发达，先生。还说它们全都会……”她在寻找正确的词汇……

En: Bankrupt.
Zh: “破产。”

En: Ass up.
Zh: “完蛋。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Exactly. But we've been doing fine so far.
Zh: “没错。不过目前我们的生意还不错。”

En: I wouldn't really say it like that... but I guess so. She blushes.
Zh: 她脸红了。“我不会真的这么说……不过我猜是吧。”

En: Well... they run out of money and have to stop doing business.
Zh: “呃……钱会全部用完，然后就不能做生意了。”

En: They shouldn't be, but they seem real. Anyhow, they say that these grounds are doomed for businesses.
Zh: “是不应该，但是好像是真的。总之，他们说在这里做生意会被诅咒的。”

En: Okay, I'm going to *deduce* something now.
Zh: “好吧，现在我要做一些*推论*。”

En: Anxious body language.
Zh: 紧张的身体语言。

En: Should be a book of you!
Zh: 应该有本关于你的书！

En: Saw she's cold.
Zh: 注意到她很冷。

En: Afraid of the curse.
Zh: 害怕诅咒。

En: Thinking of killing famous people.
Zh: 想杀名人。

En: You're uptight because of your mother and the pressure she's putting on you.
Zh: “你紧张是因为你妈妈，还有她给你带来的压力。”

En: Dismay flickers in her eyes. "Maybe so, sir." She sighs. "Okay, I know, it's a bad habit and I shouldn't."
Zh: 她的眼中闪烁着惶恐的目光。“也许是吧，先生。”她叹了口气。“好吧，我知道，这个是坏习惯，我不该这么做。”

En: Rats have been nibbling on your fingers. Child labour in dismal working conditions...
Zh: “老鼠一直在啃你的手指甲。恶劣工作环境的童工……”

En: What rats? I just read books. Haven't seen a rat in this building in forever.
Zh: “什么老鼠？我只是看书而已。从来没在这个地方见过老鼠。”

En: Chewed-on-nails... means you're recycling your body material.
Zh: “啃指甲……这就意味着你在回收自己的身体材料。”

En: That is so gross.
Zh: “那也太恶心了。”

En: One could say that entire economies are doomed, as they tend to crash every once in a while.
Zh: “你可以说整个经济都被诅咒了，所以每隔一段时间就会发生崩溃。”

En: Yes... sir. You realise she doesn't have the faintest idea what you're talking about.
Zh: “是的……先生。”你意识到她完全不明白你在说什么。

En: Why are they doing that?
Zh: “你为什么要这么做？”

En: That is a book. She points at the stands. "They have stories inside them -- it's like someone told you a story in a really long letter."
Zh: “那是一本书。”她指向货摊。“书里面有故事——就好像某人用一封特别长的信给你讲了个故事。”

En: Interesting. Thanks.
Zh: “有意思。谢谢。”

En: And you're standing out here in the cold because...?
Zh: “这么冷的天，你站在外面是因为……”

En: I'm signalling that the store is open. She nods eagerly. "Otherwise people might not know... they'd miss out on the *Crime, Romance, and Biographies of Famous People*."
Zh: “我是一个信号，代表书店是开着的。”她急切地点点头。“不然大家可能不知道……他们会错过*犯罪、浪漫和名人传记*的。”

En: Hmm... I actually had other questions.
Zh: “嗯……其实吧，我还有别的问题。”

En: More about books, maybe? Annette nods eagerly.
Zh: “也许，是关于书的问题？”安妮特热切地点点头。

En: Who are these... famous people?
Zh: “这些……名人是谁？”

En: Oh, kings and queens and generals of old, or artists and writers, or musicians, those kinds of people. There's usually something extraordinary about them. She scratches her cold-reddened cheek, then continues:
Zh: “哦，国王啊，皇后啊，老将军，或者是艺术家和作家，还有音乐家，就是那类人。他们通常都有些与众不同的地方。”

En: Seems like most people who read those books fail to get more famous from reading them.
Zh: 看来绝大多数看这些书的人都没有因此而变成名人。

En: Reading those doesn't make the readers more famous, does it?
Zh: “看那些书也没能让你变得更有名，不是吗？”

En: These famous people sound like a bunch of dorks.
Zh: “这些名人听起来像是一群笨蛋。”

En: Annette's expression remains ever so helpful, but she doesn't say anything.
Zh: 安妮特还是摆出一副乐于助人的神情，不过她什么也没说。

En: But it does make the famous people more famous! She smiles gleefully.
Zh: “但是它能让名人变得更有名！”她愉快地笑了。

En: Right, I had a different question in mind.
Zh: “没错，我在想一个不同的问题。”

En: Never mind... I literally had nothing else to say.
Zh: “没关系……我其实也没什么要说的了。”

En: Why would they do that, sir?
Zh: “他们为什么要这么做呢，先生？”

En: Because I'll be a superstar cop, in the papers and everything! That'll show 'em.
Zh: “因为我会变成一个超级明星警探，出现在报纸和所有地方！这样就能让他们看见了。”

En: Hmm... why indeed? I'm just an old, tired cop. What use am I?
Zh: “嗯……为什么呢？我只是个又老又疲惫的警察。我能有什么用？”

En: You're not that old... maybe you'll do something really important. Something that *wows the world*!
Zh: “你没那么老……也许你还能做些真正重要的事。某些可以*震惊全世界*的事！”

En: You think so?
Zh: “你这么觉得吗？”

En: Yes! There's hope... a lot of famous people got famous late, or even after they died.
Zh: “是的！还有希望的……很多名人都是晚年成名，甚至是死后才出名的。”

En: Thanks, girl. You are very kind.
Zh: “谢谢你，小姑娘。你人很好。”

En: Yes, sir! She smiles and takes a sip from her vacuum bottle.
Zh: “是的，先生！”她笑了笑，从保温瓶里喝了一口。

En: That sounds like a nice story to tell yourself at night. Maybe fame isn't that important after all.
Zh: “听起来像是很适合晚上给自己讲的故事。也许名声其实并没有那么重要。”

En: Some can't get noticed, others can't get properly forgotten. That's life for you.
Zh: “有些人不会注意到，其他人没有被恰当地忘掉。这就是生活。”

En: You're cold. Can I help in some way?
Zh: “你很冷。我能帮到你吗？”

En: Kind of you to offer, sir... She doesn't know what else to say.
Zh: “感谢你的好意，先生……”她不知道还能说些什么。

En: You can stop calling me 'sir.' I am but a working man.
Zh: “你不用叫我‘先生’的。我也只是个工人而已。”

En: It's an intense dedication to the Way of Business. There are no riches without personal sacrifice.
Zh: “这是对经营之道的一种热情奉献。没有个人的牺牲，就不会有财富。”

En: Thank you, sir! I work hard. She beams a smile.
Zh: “谢谢你，先生！我工作很努力的。”她面带微笑。

En: When she grows up, and if she grows up to be clever, she'll discover there's no such thing as a 'grown-up.'
Zh: 当她长大的时候，如果长成个聪明姑娘，她会发现根本没有所谓的‘成人’这一说。

En: Yeah, you're certainly the type who might find fame *posthumously*. Who knows, you might even get famous for the *manner* of your death!
Zh: 是啊，你肯定是那种*死后*成名的人。谁知道呢，你甚至可能会因为死去的*方式*出名呢！

En: Okay, bye. See you around, Annette. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧，再见。回见了，安妮特。”[离开。]

En: Okay, got it. See you around. [Leave.]
Zh: “好的，明白了。回见。”[离开。]

En: I gotta go. [Leave]
Zh: “我得走了。”[离开]

En: Hello again, sir! You see Annette sipping on her hot juice. She looks at you with shy amusement.
Zh: “又见面了，先生！”你看见安妮特正在喝热果汁。她看着你，脸上带着害羞又打趣的表情。

En: Hello again, sir! The girl is sipping on her hot juice. She looks at you with shy amusement.
Zh: “又见面了，先生！”女孩正在喝热果汁。她看着你，脸上带着害羞又打趣的表情。

En: No, sir, I can't. It would be too tiring to refrain from it. It's already tiring enough to remember to say it all the time. It's nice of you to say I could stop, though.
Zh: “不，先生，我不能。克制实在是太累人。要一直记住这样说已经够辛苦的了。不过，我还是很感谢你说我可以停下来。”

En: I get you. I, for example, can't stop making this face. (Point at your face.)
Zh: “我懂你。我，举个例子，就是控制不住地要做出这幅表情。”（指向你的脸。）

En: Okay, yeah, when the next person comes, you'd have to 'sir' or 'ma'am' them again.
Zh: “好吧，是啊，等下一个人来的时候，你又要开始叫他们‘先生’或者‘女士’了。”

En: 'Sir' it is, then. Soldier on.
Zh: “那就叫‘先生’吧。继续坚持。”

En: That's a friendly enough face, most of the time.
Zh: “这幅表情挺友好的，大部分时间来说。”

En: Yes... sir. She smiles apologetically.
Zh: “是的……先生。”她略带歉意的笑了。

En: By your command!
Zh: “遵命！”

En: My pleasure! Anything else you'd like to know?
Zh: “我很荣幸！还有什么你想知道的吗？”

En: Why is that?
Zh: “为什么会那样？”

En: Anyway... She looks around again, her nose red from the cold.
Zh: “不管怎么说……”她又看了看周围，鼻子被冻地通红。

En: I know, sir. She stomps her feet to feel warmer.
Zh: “我知道，先生。”她跺跺脚，为了让自己暖和一些。

En: What's that mean?
Zh: “那是什么意思？”

En: Uh, you know -- cut loose, raise hell, blow off steam.
Zh: “呃，你知道的——挣脱束缚，大吵大闹，释放压力。”

En: That's great, sir! She smiles enthusiastically.
Zh: “那很棒啊，先生！”她的笑容非常热情。

En: Look at me! A hideous beast, a failure of a cop. A disgrace.
Zh: “看看我！一头丑恶的野兽，一个失败的警察。一个耻辱。”

En: A man can *never* be truly happy with who he is.
Zh: “一个*永远*也无法为自己真正感到高兴的人。”

En: You'll understand when you're older.
Zh: “等你再大一点就能理解了。”

En: Oh. Okay. She blinks at you, not knowing how to reply. "I shouldn't have said that thing about Dick Mullen..."
Zh: “哦。好吧。”她朝你眨眨眼，不知道该怎么回应。“我不应该提起迪克·马伦的事……”

En: You know, nobody actually looks like the guy in the picture. That's just a stupid fantasy of a man.
Zh: “你知道吗，其实没人会长成画上这个人的模样。这只是对于男人的愚蠢幻想罢了。”

En: Why is that? She looks puzzled.
Zh: “为什么会这样呢？”她看起来很困惑。

En: I'm a detective. I deduce that you... are a girl.
Zh: “我是一个警探。我推断你是……一个女孩。”

En: She waits intently.
Zh: 她一心一意地等待着。

En: The girl keeps her hands folded, hidden. Why is that?
Zh: 女孩的手一直是紧握的，隐藏着。为什么？

En: A postcard, she observes you for a moment, "is a small cardboard picture. You can write a few words on the other side and send it to your friend or your *beloved*."
Zh: “一张明信片，”她观察了你一阵子。“是一张小的纸片画。你可以在另一面上写字，然后把它送给你的朋友或者是*爱人*。”

En: Board games are like little games on a table, made to pass the time... There are several different ones. She pauses to ponder. "But sailors here mostly buy cards."
Zh: “桌游就像是在桌子上玩的游戏，用来打发时间的……有很多不同的桌游呢。”她停下来深思了一会儿。“不过这里的水手大部分都会买卡片。”

En: She is unfazed by your questions. She would consider it impolite to point out any perceived weirdness.
Zh: 她并没有因为你的问题感到苦恼。她觉得指出任何察觉到的古怪都是不太礼貌的。

En: Or there could be a story about a poor lady getting a rich man. It's about man and lady business, sir.
Zh: “或者是穷姑娘爱上有钱人的故事。总之就是男女之间的事，先生。”

En: What about when both of the men are bad?
Zh: “如果两个男人都很坏呢？”

En: I think that's why people read them. To find the secrets of their fame.
Zh: 她挠了挠冻得通红的小脸，继续说道：“我觉得大家就是为这个看的吧。为了找出他们成名的秘密。”

En: You know, the economy, it's like... the network of everyone buying and selling and producing and consuming and stuff.
Zh: “你知道的，经济，就像……每一个人买卖、生产和消费东西的网络。”

En: A sudden gush of wind turns the pages of the books on the counters. She covers her face, smiling, but she's cold.
Zh: 一阵突如其来的风吹起了货架上的书页。她遮住脸笑了，不过她很冷。

En: I should have a word with the store-owner, maybe?
Zh: “也许，我应该跟店主聊几句？”

En: Oh, no-no, sir! I'm happy to help Mum by luring in customers! She stands upright and smiles like a little soldier. "Besides, I have some hot juice in my vacuum bottle to keep warm."
Zh: “哦，不用，先生！我很愿意帮助妈妈吸引顾客！”她站直身体，像个小士兵一样笑了。“而且，我的保温杯里有热果汁，可以帮我保暖。”

En: Who is that? (Point to the woman browsing books.)
Zh: “那是谁？”（指着浏览书本的女人。）

En: Oh, that's auntie Billie. She's nice, if a bit distressed.
Zh: “哦，那是比莉阿姨。她人很好，只是有点忧虑。”

En: She's your aunt?
Zh: “她是你亲戚？”

En: No-no, sir. She's a working woman who comes to look at the books a lot.
Zh: “不是，先生。她是个女工人，经常来这里看书。”

En: Why is she distressed?
Zh: “她为什么忧虑？”

En: I think she has a bad husband. Not very nice or helpful.
Zh: “我想是因为她的丈夫很坏。人不好，要不就是帮不上什么忙。”

En: Such a good trooper you are, already learning the value of hard work. (Pat her head.)
Zh: “你真是个好士兵，已经懂得努力工作的价值了。”（拍拍她的头。）

En: Thank you, sir! I'm happy to help Mum out with the store. She smiles and stands upright like a little soldier.
Zh: “谢谢你，先生！能帮妈妈看店我很开心。”她笑了，像个小士兵一样站的笔直。

En: What is a book?
Zh: “书是什么？”

En: What's a postcard?
Zh: “明信片是什么？”

En: What's a board... game?
Zh: “桌游……是什么？”

En: Sir, please, sir, don't say things like that. You look... nice enough.
Zh: “先生，拜托了，先生，请不要这么说。你看起来……已经很好了。”

En: Nice enough for a small child, sure... maybe.
Zh: “对一个小孩子来说，肯定很好了吧……也许。”

En: Don't be sad, sir. She seems genuinely concerned. "I'm sorry I said that thing about Dick Mullen."
Zh: “别伤心，先生。”她似乎真的很担心。“很抱歉我说了迪克·马伦的那些话。”

En: No rats in this dilapidated building? Strange.
Zh: “这栋危房里居然没有老鼠？怪了。”

En: Life is gross. So very gross.
Zh: “生活很恶心。非常恶心。”

En: Maybe they're just afraid... of books! She laughs, with a slight nervous undertone.
Zh: “也许它们只是害怕……这些书！”她笑了，略微有点紧张。

En: Eww. She shudders.
Zh: “呃。”她耸耸肩。

En: What about when everyone is poor?
Zh: “那所有人都很穷的时候呢？”

En: That's really not a proper romance story. That's more like everyday life!
Zh: “那就算不上是合格的浪漫小说了。更像是日常生活！”

En: Mhm. I'm sure she can help out! She smiles wide. "Anything else you're curious about?"
Zh: “嗯。我肯定她能帮忙的！”她笑的很开心。“你还有什么好奇的吗？”

En: Those are unhappy books, for most of the pages. People sad about what they have lost. But then it all turns out just fine in the end!
Zh: “那些书的绝大多数篇幅都是不开心的。大家为他们失去的东西感到悲伤。不过最后一切都会好起来的！”

En: These are not very common. You can't have a choice between bad and bad, nobody wants to read a story like that.
Zh: “这种情况不是很常见。你不能在两个坏人之间做出选择吧，没人想看那种故事的。”

En: What if it's written really well?
Zh: “如果真的写的很好呢？”

En: Well... maybe then it's fine. Maybe if the lady then decides not to pick either, because she doesn't need a bad man. Yes, that would be interesting!
Zh: “呃……那也许可以吧。也许那个女士会决定谁都不选，因为她不需要坏男人。是的，那样一定会很有意思的！”

En: Sometimes you have to write about real life things.
Zh: “有时候，你必须写点现实生活中的事。”

En: Not in romance books, sir. These are about nice and pretty people, and everyone is happy in the end.
Zh: “浪漫小说不会写的，先生。这里面写的都是些漂亮又善良的人，而且最后大家都很开心。”

En: That's enough romance for me. I had other questions.
Zh: “浪漫小说的问题就到这里吧。我还有别的问题。”

En: Maybe some about other books? She rubs her chilled nose with her fist.
Zh: “也许看看其他书？”她用拳头揉了揉冻僵的鼻子。

En: Are you interested in a new and exciting book?
Zh: “你对令人兴奋的新书感兴趣吗？”

En: Are you interested in a new and exciting book? She stomps her feet to feel warmer.
Zh: “你对令人兴奋的新书感兴趣吗？”她跺跺脚，为了让自己暖和一些。

En: Fame sounds delicious. Maybe someone will write a book about me one day.
Zh: “名声听起来挺不错的。也许某天，有人会写一本关于我的书。”

En: Fame is... for vain people. I have better things to do.
Zh: “名声……就留给那些自负的人吧。我还有更好的事要做。”

En: Okay, sir! She stamps her feet, waiting for what comes next.
Zh: “好吧，先生！”她跺跺脚，等待接下来的事。

En: That's so cool! Maybe they'll make you a book cover picture and everything! Standing over a dead body, holding a gun...
Zh: “那真是太酷了！也许他们会用你的相片做封面什么的！站在一具尸体旁边，拿着一把枪……”

En: But what if I get famous only through dying? I couldn't enjoy the fame at all.
Zh: “不过，如果我死了才出名呢？那样就享受不到名声带来的好处了。”

En: I don't think you should live life with that fear. She stamps on her feet. "Just try to be the best you can!"
Zh: “我觉得你不应该带着那种恐惧生活。”她跺跺脚。“只要努力做到最好就行！”

En: Maybe not! No one *needs* a book written about them anyway. She grins. "It's always better to read books!"
Zh: “也许没有吧！反正没人*需要*一本关于他们自己的书。”她咧开嘴笑了。“看书总是比较好的！”

En: I think you just have to live a happy life! She rubs her nose. "No need to worry about fame or think bad thoughts!"
Zh: “我觉得你应该过上幸福的生活！”她揉了揉鼻子。“不用担心名声或者不好的想法！”

En: You have to spend money to make money, see.
Zh: “你必须花钱才能赚到钱，明白了。”

En: Mum says it's necessary to do both, because it builds character. Mum says a proper worker is dutiful -- that's how you get ahead in life, you succeed.
Zh: “妈妈说两者都有必要，因为这样能塑造性格。妈妈说一个合格的工人是尽职尽责的——这样才能出人头地，才能成功。”

En: Isn't going to school more important than... this?
Zh: “去上学难道不比这个……更重要吗？”

En: What do you know about the other failed businesses?
Zh: “你对其他那些失败的企业了解多少？”

En: Nothing really, sir. Mum doesn't allow me to sneak around in the back rooms or the cellar. She looks a bit disappointed. "I don't really know what's there."
Zh: “不太了解，先生。妈妈不让我溜进后面的房间或者地下室。”她看起来有些失望。“我真的不知道那里有什么。”

En: The economy is all about trust, okay.
Zh: “经济完全是关乎信任的，好吧。”

En: If you say so, sir! She smiles, perhaps a bit too eagerly.
Zh: “如果你这么说的话，先生！”她笑了，也许有些过于激动了。

En: That makes sense, sir! She nods, perhaps a bit too eagerly.
Zh: “你说的很有道理，先生！”她点点头，也许有些过于激动了。

En: Because that keeps the network... working... right.
Zh: “因为这样能让网络……正常……运作。”

En: Okay, I understand, sir! She looks puzzled.
Zh: “好吧，我明白了，先生！”她看起来很困惑。

En: She doesn't really understand, but says so to make you feel better about yourself. Your explanation was garbage anyway.
Zh: 她并没有真的明白，这么说只是为了让你感觉好一些。反正你的解释都是垃圾。

En: The economy is about the rich exploiting the poor as much as they can.
Zh: “经济就是富人尽可能地剥削穷人。”

En: Why would they do that?
Zh: “他们为什么要这么做呢？”

En: Greed, my child. It's always greed.
Zh: “贪婪，孩子。一直都是因为贪婪。”

En: That's not nice at all. She frowns.
Zh: “那样一点也不好。”她皱起眉头。

En: Yeah, you heard me. Only losers waste their time with that garbage.
Zh: “是啊，你听到我的话了。只有失败者才会把时间浪费在那种垃圾上。”

En: Isn't that kind of a childish thing for a grown-up to say?
Zh: “一个成年人说这种话不会有些孩子气吗？”

En: What makes you think I'm grown up?
Zh: “你为什么会觉得我是个成年人呢？”

En: You look all wrinkly and hairy, like an old person!
Zh: “你看起来好多皱纹，毛也很多，像个老年人！”

En: Hrm! I'm not that old, I'm doing just fine, thank you very much.
Zh: “咳！我没那么老！我挺好的，谢谢你。”

En: You also *feel* old: tired, hardly able to catch your breath, your joints aching constantly.
Zh: 你也*感觉*很老：疲惫不堪，呼吸困难，时不时还会关节痛。

En: If you say so, sir! She beams you a piercing smile.
Zh: “如果你这么说的话，先生！”她的笑容很打动人心。

En: There is stress and unease behind these words. She's reciting etiquette.
Zh: 这些话语背后隐藏着压力和不安。她是在背诵规矩。

En: Hello.
Zh: “你好。”

En: Feel free to step in and browse our wares!
Zh: 小女孩凝视着窗户，突然间吃了一惊，瞪大了眼睛，好像回想起了什么：“请随便进去看看我们的商品吧！”

En: I look *Harry*? That can't be me, not *Harry* -- what are you talking about?
Zh: “我看起来像*哈里*？那不可能是我，不是*哈里*——你在说什么呢？”

En: Hairy, sir. I said hairy. Covered with hair... She sways on her feet, puzzled.
Zh: “不是哈里，先生。我说的是毛很多。头发多……”她摇摇晃晃地站着，有些困惑。

En: Maybe...?
She gives you a quizzical look, not understanding the reasoning behind your odd outbursts.
Zh: 她露出一个探询的表情，并不理解你古怪行动背后的含义。

En: Yeah? You think she has one about an *excruciatingly painful* breakup?
Zh: “是吗？你觉得她有*痛苦至极*的分手故事吗？”

En: This sounds rather serious. I should probably... look into this. (Narrow your eyes and look through the creaking boards on the window.)
Zh: “听起来挺严重的。也许我应该……调查一下。”（眯起眼睛，看着窗户上嘎吱作响的木板。）

En: This sounds rather serious. I should probably... look into this.
Zh: “听起来挺严重的。也许我应该……调查一下。”

En: School is stupid. You're lucky not to be there.
Zh: “学校太愚蠢了。没去上学是你的运气。”

En: That's not a healthy look. Muscles get bigger, heart grows weaker.
Zh: 这可不是什么健康的模样。肌肉越来越健壮，心脏越来越弱。

En: He isn't even drawn right.
Zh: 他的样子画的根本就不对。

En: Check out the pose -- rigid as a stick. He couldn't even wipe his own ass. You don't want to be like that.
Zh: 看看这个姿势——僵硬地像跟棍子。他甚至连自己的屁股都擦不了。你可不想变成这样。

En: Dark, arrogant, violent. Likely overcompensating for something.
Zh: 黑暗，傲慢，暴力。很有可能是对某种东西的过度补偿。

En: What kind of a store is this anyway?
Zh: “话说回来，这是一家什么样的商店？”

En: What is this feeling?
Zh: 这是什么感觉？

En: Discard thought.
Zh: 抛弃这个想法。

En: Remorse. The parquet of assembly halls, the rows of stall bars are growing cold. The corridors stand empty. A girl's voice somehow finds its way through this strange feeling...
Zh: 悔恨。会馆的镶木地板上，一排排墙梯变得越来越冰冷。走廊空空荡荡。不知为什么，一个女孩的声音从这种奇怪的感觉中穿透而来……

En: You haven't heard a word of what she's been saying these past few moments. You only hear...
Zh: 在过去的几分钟里，你没有听见女孩的任何话语。你只听到了……

En: She sways slowly side-to-side, her wide eyes fixed at you standing there, silently, like a weirdo.
Zh: 她慢慢地左右摇晃，圆溜溜的眼睛注视着站在原地的你，一言不发，就像一个怪物。

En: It does not *manifest* itself in any way. It does not exist. I liked it better when we were talking about different types of books.
Zh: “它没有以任何方式*表现*。它根本不存在。我更喜欢像刚才那样——讨论不同种类的书。”

En: It does not *manifest* itself in any way. It does not exist. I liked it better when we were asking about Dick Mullen.
Zh: “它没有以任何方式*表现*。它根本不存在。我更喜欢像刚才那样——询问迪克·马伦的事。”

En: It does not *manifest* itself in any way. It does not exist. I liked it better when we were asking about romance novels.
Zh: “它没有以任何方式*表现*。它根本不存在。我更喜欢像刚才那样——询问浪漫小说的事。”

En: It does not *manifest* itself in any way. It does not exist. I liked it better when we were asking about famous people.
Zh: “它没有以任何方式*表现*。它根本不存在。我更喜欢像刚才那样——询问名人传记的事。”

En: It does not *manifest* itself in any way. It does not exist. I liked it better when we were talking about whether it's appropriate to stand out in the freezing weather.
Zh: “它没有以任何方式*表现*。它根本不存在。我更喜欢像刚才那样——讨论是不是应该大冷天的站在外面。”

En: Her eye lands on the sign on the window that says *Crime, Romance, and Biographies of Famous People*. She brightens up and points to it: "But I do know books! We can talk about books."
Zh: 她的目光落在了窗户的标志上，上面写着*犯罪，浪漫和名人传记*。她面露喜色，指着它说道：“但是我知道那些书！我们可以讨论书的事。”

En: It does not *manifest* itself in any way. It does not exist.
Zh: “它没有以任何方式*表现*。它根本不存在。”

En: But Kim... the plasmic manifestations...
Zh: “但是金……诅咒的表现……”

En: Fine. I'll show you!
Zh: “好吧。我会展示给你看！”

En: Uh... The girl looks back and forth between you two. "Anything else you wanted to talk about, sir?"
Zh: “呃……”女孩在你们俩之间来回地看。“还有什么别的要谈的吗，先生？”

En: No such thing. The lieutenant stands at your side, stern and serious.
Zh: “没有这种东西。”警督站在你身边，坚定又严肃。

En: She studies the picture on the cover. "That sounds smart, I guess."
Zh: 她研究着封面上的图画。“我猜，听起来好像挺聪明的。”

En: Probably got a violent streak because he's emotionally stunted.
Zh: “也许他的情感上有些发育不良，所以才会有暴力倾向。”

En: She examines the picture closely. The Vespertine detective exhibits few signs of health or unhealth.
Zh: 她仔细研究着封面上的图画。维斯珀裔的警探没有表现出健康或者不健康的迹象。

En: She's trying to examine the shadow-obscured hips of Dick Mullen. "They don't look rigid to me..."
Zh: 她试着检查迪克·马伦被阴影遮住的臀部。“在我看来不是很僵硬啊……”

En: She examines the cover, trying to gauge the inner workings of the detective.
Zh: 她检查着封面，试着估量警探的内心活动。

En: Being smart is the only way to *cop*, little girl. I can do this way better than him.
Zh: “聪明是当*警察*的唯一方法，小姑娘。我能比他做得更好。”

En: If you say so, sir. She smiles mischievously. "He's just a fictional character, he's no match for you."
Zh: “如果你这么说的话，先生。”她调皮地笑了。“他只是一个虚构的角色，根本比不上你。”

En: That's just no way to *cop*. I can do it way better than Dick Mullen."
Zh: “这根本不是当*警察*的办法。我要比迪克·马伦厉害多了。”

En: But that's like... the same thing. She squints her eyes, trying to solve this puzzle.
Zh: “不过那好像是……同样的事情。”她眯起眼睛，试图破解这个难题。

En: Obviously, sir. She smiles mischievously. "He's just a fictional character, he's no match for you."
Zh: “很明显，先生。”她调皮地笑了。“他只是一个虚构的角色，根本比不上你。”

En: Poor vitality is no way to *cop*, little girl. I could take him down easy.
Zh: “没有活力的话，当*警察*可是不行的，小姑娘。我可以轻轻松松地拿下他。”

En: Mobility is the proper way to *cop*, girl. Dick Mullen can't compete with my hip-grind.
Zh: “机动性才是当*警察*的正确方法，小姑娘。迪克·马伦根本无法跟我的翘臀相提并论。”

En: Emotional ineptitude is no way to *cop*. He can't compare to my sensitive soul.
Zh: “感情愚钝可不是当*警察*的料。他无法跟我敏感的灵魂相提并论。”

En: The girl is at a loss for words.
Zh: 小女孩不知道该说什么好。

En: She shifts and sways on her feet, looking confused and uncomfortable.
Zh: 她摇摇晃晃，频繁更换双脚，看起来困惑又不安。

En: Ummm... no? I don't know... She looks at you with puzzlement.
Zh: “呃……没有？我不知道……”她疑惑地看着你。

En: Doesn't ring a bell? Alright, I'll ask your mom.
Zh: “没想起来吗？好吧，我去问你妈妈。”

En: Yes! She nods, relieved. "She knows books, definitely."
Zh: “好呀！”她宽慰地点点头。“她确实很懂书。”

En: I don't need *help*, I need a real book that tells it how it *is*, not this drivel.
Zh: “我不需要*帮助*，我需要一本讲述它到底是*怎么回事*的书，而不是这些乱七八糟的话。”

En: Mhm! She nods enthusiastically, obviously relieved.
Zh: “嗯！”她热切地点点头，显然是松了一口气。

En: Sir, sorry, sir. I'm still little? She stumbles on her words. "I don't... I can't..."
Zh: “抱歉，抱歉，先生。我还太小了？”她结巴了起来。“我不知道……我不能……”

En: Damn right I am! Proud as the Lion of Serber.
Zh: 这就是我！像塞尔维亚的雄狮一样骄傲。

En: Excuse me, I'm not sure I heard that properly...
Zh: 抱歉，我不确定自己是不是听错了……

En: It's time to leave it be. You're about to make a child cry. Are you proud of yourself?
Zh: 是时候放手了。你都快把小孩弄哭了。这样会让你感到很骄傲吗？

En: I... uh... don't worry, little girl. Got a bit carried away there. Let's back up out of this.
Zh: “我……呃……别担心，小姑娘。我有点过于激动了。我们不说这个了。”

En: I said I'm proud like a goddamn lion!
Zh: “我说我骄傲的像一头该死的雄狮！”

En: Her eyes grow wide as she tries to comprehend the outburst. "I'm sorry, sir?"
Zh: 她的眼睛瞪的大大的，试图理解你突然爆发的情绪。“我很抱歉，先生？”

En: You heard me! I'm full of pride and I ready to talk about books. Let's do this.
Zh: “你听见我说的话了！我充满自豪，已经准备好谈论这些书了。我们开始吧。”

En: Yes... yes, sir! She blinks her eyes rapidly, in confusion and relief.
Zh: “是的……好的，先生！”她飞快地眨巴着眼睛，即困惑又放松。

En: Not a single shred of pride remains in me.
Zh: 我的心里没有留下一丝骄傲的情绪。

En: This is your *tiny* chance for redemption. Apologize.
Zh: 这是你挽救的一个*小*机会。快道歉。

En: Little girl, I'm sorry I got agitated. I just can't handle things sometimes. Let's get back to books.
Zh: “小姑娘，很抱歉，我太激动了。我有时候处理不好这种事情。我们还是说回书的事吧。”

En: Yes... yes, sir! She blinks her eyes rapidly, in confusion and relief.
Zh: “是的……好的，先生！”她飞快地眨巴着眼睛，即困惑又放松。

En: On second thought, this seems like an inappropriate topic to discuss with children.
Zh: “仔细一想，这似乎并不是适合跟小孩子讨论的话题。”

En: Right. Activate Denial Mode.
Zh: 没错。激活否认模式。

En: You're not really an automaton, you do know that?
Zh: 你不是真正的机器人，你应该知道的吧？

En: Little girl, pardon the deviation. I wish to discuss books and other such matters.
Zh: “小姑娘，请原谅我跑题了。我想跟你聊聊书，还有其他类似的话题。”

En: Okay! She looks at you with her wide eyes.
Zh: “好吧！”她睁大眼睛看着你。

En: I wonder how I'll be when I get old... She mumbles. "Maybe I'll also get all wrinkly..." She giggles to herself.
Zh: “不知道我老了之后会怎么样……”她嘟囔到。“也许我也会变得皱巴巴的……”她暗自笑了起来。

En: Sir?! She looks at you, eyes wide in shock.
Zh: “先生？！”她吃惊地睁大眼睛看着你。

En: They *deserve* punishment for all the things they had to do get so famous.
Zh: “他们为了出名做了所有那些事，也就*应该*得到惩罚。”

En: Haha, never mind, girl. I'm quite the joker. I would never do something like that.
Zh: “哈哈，没关系，小姑娘。我只是喜欢开玩笑而已。我永远不会做出那种事的。”

En: Okay... She coughs. "Maybe buy some books then?"
Zh: “好吧……”她咳嗽了几声。“那要不买几本书吧？”

En: That's not a funny joke. At all.
Zh: “这个笑话不好笑，一点也不好笑。”

En: Hey... Why do you keep your hands folded?
Zh: “喂……你为什么总是把手缩起来呢？”

En: What do you mean, sir? She looks wary.
Zh: “你是什么意思，先生？”她看起来很谨慎。

En: She knows where this is going.
Zh: 她知道接下来会怎么样。

En: You can show them to me.
Zh: “你可以让我看看。”

En: She looks around anxiously. Her hands remain folded in front of her. She doesn't want to show them.
Zh: 她紧张地看了看周围。双手还是紧紧握在身前。她不想展示出来。

En: It's okay.
Zh: “没事的。”

En: You heard me. Hands up.
Zh: “你听见我的话了。把手举起来。”

En: She brings out her reddened hands -- her nails are frayed, nearly chewed down to the flesh.
Zh: 她伸出红彤彤的小手——她的指甲磨破了，几乎啃到了肉里。

En: You bite your nails.
Zh: “你在咬指甲。”

En: And you knew this from me keeping my hands folded? She shoots you a suspicious glance.
Zh: “你是因为我一直握着手知道的吗？”她疑惑地看了你一眼。

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: There were a few other hints...
Zh: “还有些其他提示……”

En: Well... that proves nothing! Anyone could do an easy deduction like that! Her eyes flash with defiance. She's not impressed.
Zh: “呃……那也证明不了什么！任何人都能做出这样简单的推论！”她的眼里闪烁着反抗的光芒，并没有被你折服。

En: She actually is impressed, this is more like a defensive reflex.
Zh: 其实她被折服了，她的反应更像是一种防御反射。

En: You think so? Fine, do better -- deduce something about me.
Zh: “你这么觉得吗？好吧，那就做得更好一些——做些跟我有关的推断吧。”

En: That was a master class move. Whatever, kid, I don't need to prove myself to you.
Zh: “这一步走得很高超。不管怎么说，孩子，我不需要向你证明我自己。”

En: If you say so, sir. She grins victoriously.
Zh: “如果你这么说的话，先生。”她露出了胜利的笑容。

En: You're quite sober. She snaps back quickly.
Zh: “你的酒醒了。”她快速回复到。

En: Wait... How do you know I'm usually not?
Zh: “等等……你怎么知道我平时不这样呢？”

En: Because you usually aren't.
Zh: “因为你平时不这样。”

En: There's something you're missing...
Zh: 你好像错过了什么……

En: Hang on. So you... know me? We've met before?
Zh: “等一下。所以说……你知道我吗？我们之前见过？”

En: You've been running around all week without your shirt on, sir. Telling people about being a star or something. I don't really understand who those stars are...
Zh: “你光着上半身到处跑了整整一个星期，先生。跟大家说你是个明星什么之类的。我真的不明白那些明星是谁……”

En: You've been running around all week without your shirt on, sir. Telling people the end of the world is coming. I don't really understand what you mean by it...
Zh: “你光着上半身到处跑了整整一个星期，先生。跟大家说世界末日就要到了。我真的不明白那是什么意思……”

En: You've been running around all week without your shirt on, sir. Apologizing to everyone. I don't really understand what you've done wrong...
Zh: “你光着上半身到处跑了整整一个星期，先生。跟每一个人道歉。我不明白你到底做了什么错事……”

En: You've been running around all week, sir. Sometimes without your shirt on -- asking people questions. I haven't really understood what about...
Zh: “你整个星期都在外面跑来跑去的。有时候还没穿衬衫——不停地问别人问题。我不太明白是怎么一回事……”

En: You've been running around for several days, talking about small churches and how everyone betrayed you, sir.
Zh: “这几天你一直东奔西跑，说些什么小教堂，还有大家都背叛了你的话，先生。”

En: What do you mean better? I look like shit.
Zh: “你说好多了是什么意思？我看起来就像坨屎一样。”

En: Yeah, but you don't have party eyes anymore.
Zh: “是啊，但是你已经没有那种派对眼了。”

En: *Party eyes*?
Want more? Bet I can figure out *why* you bite your nails. I got a few reasons in mind.
Zh: “还不够吗？我敢打赌，我能推断出你*为什么*要咬指甲。我已经想出好几个理由了。”

En: It was okay, sir. She's still got a rebellious streak.
Zh: “还可以吧，先生。”她还是有些抵触的倾向。

En: She nods -- half provocative, half enthusiastic.
Zh: 她点点头——一半是出于挑衅，一半是出于热忱。

En: It's super simple for a detective such as myself.
Zh: “对于我这种警探来说，这实在是太简单了。”

En: I hope this entertained you.
Zh: “希望这样能让你开心。”

En: Either way, another *ace* deduction by the #1 detective in town.
Zh: “不管怎么说，城里的头号警探又做出了一次*精彩绝伦*的推断。”

En: And I'm having a grand time!
Zh: “而且我现在很开心！”

En: I sure hope you are, sir! She rubs her red nose.
Zh: “希望如此，先生！”她揉了揉自己的红鼻子。

En: On second thought, maybe I shouldn't remember. Maybe there's a reason I forgot.
Zh: “仔细一想，也许我不应该想起来。说不定我忘记是有原因的。”

En: Yeah, about that... maybe you could point me toward some booze?
Zh: “是啊，关于那个……也许你可以告诉我去哪里买酒？”

En: Uhm... Frittte, maybe? She shrugs, a compassionate look in her eyes. "Alcohol is not good for you, sir."
Zh: “呃……弗利多，也许吧？”她耸耸肩，眼里闪烁着同情的目光。“酒精对你不好，先生。”

En: Yes, sir. The girl looks at you quizzically. "Maybe you are interested in books?"
Zh: “是的，先生。”小姑娘疑惑地看着你。“也许你会对书感兴趣呢？”

En: Did I ever talk to you?
Zh: “我有跟你说过话吗？”

En: Of course. You stopped by a few times. She looks at you intently. "You certainly look better than the last time I saw you!"
Zh: “当然。你来过好几次呢。”她专注地看着你。“你比我上次看见你的时候绝对好多了！”

En: You know... like a cat in the dark! All big-and-wide-eyed. She giggles at the thought. "It certainly looks odd on a man."
Zh: “你知道的……就像是一只黑夜里的猫！眼睛又大又圆。”一想到这里，她咯咯地笑了起来。“在一个男人脸上看见确实挺古怪的。”

En: The girl doesn't know what she's talking about! Forget about this.
Zh: 这个小姑娘根本不知道自己在说什么！忘了吧。

En: Fuck yeah! You should get some party eyes right now! Snap those sequins on you, boy.
Zh: 哦耶！你现在就应该找回那种派对眼。把那些亮片贴在眼睛上，小子。

En: Does that mean... I've been partaking in *narcotics*?
Zh: 那是不是说……我磕了*麻醉剂*？

En: So... why didn't you tell me you knew me to begin with?
Zh: “所以……一开始你为什么不说认识我呢？”

En: Thanks -- I've learned something about myself today. (Conclude.)
Zh: “谢谢——今天我对自己又有了一些了解。”（总结。）

En: Don't leave us hanging like that, man. Open yourself to the experiences of the world, expand your consciousness. With amphetamines.
Zh: 别这么吊着我们，老兄。敞开胸怀，体验这个世界，扩展你的感悟和知觉——用安非他命。

En: Oh baby, that's not what you have to worry about. Worry about the important thing!
Zh: 宝贝，这种事用不着你担心。你只要担心重要的事情就好！

En: I didn't know I had to do that. She looks puzzled.
Zh: “我不知道我必须这么做。”她看起来很困惑。

En: Yes! I stand in this spot all the time. She sways back and forth on her feet.
Zh: “是的！我一直都站在这里。”她站在原地，来回摇晃。

En: I don't really like this detective-deduction game anymore... (Conclude.)
Zh: “我真的不喜欢这种侦探推理游戏了……”（总结。）

En: She gives you a rather concerned look. "I guess we can change the subject, sir. I didn't mean to bring you down."
Zh: 她露出一个非常关切的表情。“我想我们可以换个话题，先生。我不是有意要让你感到失落的。”

En: I'm a detective. I deduce that... You are very small.
Zh: “我是一名警探。我推断……你很小。”

En: Come on, don't be silly. That's not a proper deduction. She rolls her eyes.
Zh: “拜托，别傻了。这可不算是恰当的推断。”她翻了翻白眼。

En: She gazes at the image of Dick Mullen, frowning. "Nooo, he's wicked smart in the stories."
Zh: 她盯着迪克·马伦的形象，皱起了眉头。“不，他在这些故事里很聪明。”

En: She looks at the cover illustration and frowns. "That's a bit childish..."
Zh: 她看着封面的插图，皱紧了眉头。“那好像有些小孩子气……”

En: Isn't that very dangerous? She examines the picture of Dick Mullen.
Zh: “那不是很危险吗？”她看着迪克·马伦的插图。

En: She examines the illustration of Dick Mullen, attempting to find his soul. "I'm not sure I understand..."
Zh: 她看着迪克·马伦的插图，试着探寻他的灵魂。“我不太明白……”

En: Sure you can, sir. She smiles mischievously. "He's just a fictional character, he'll be no match for you."
Zh: “你当然可以，先生。”她调皮地笑了。“他只是一个虚构的角色，根本比不上你。”

En: Sure you could, sir. She smiles mischievously. "He's just a fictional character, he's no match for your strength."
Zh: “你当然可以，先生。”她调皮地笑了。“他只是一个虚构的角色，根本比不上你。”

En: Sure he can't, sir. She smiles mischievously. "He's just a fictional character, he's no match for you."
Zh: “他当然不行，先生。”她调皮地笑了。“他只是一个虚构的角色，根本比不上你。”

En: Of course not, sir. She smiles mischievously. "He's just a fictional character, he'll be no match for you."
Zh: “当然不会，先生。”她调皮地笑了。“他只是一个虚构的角色，根本比不上你。”

En: In the eyes of a kid, maybe. The difference between me and Mullen is vast. He's nothing!
Zh: “在一个小孩子的眼里，也许是吧。我和马伦之间可是天差地别。他什么都算不上！”

En: Sure, when the author does all the thinking for him. Real policemen have to think and act on their own.
Zh: “当然了，他的想法都是作者给的。真正的警察都是自己思考，自己行动。”

En: Policemen live and breathe danger, little girl. Mullen obviously lacks the *chameleonic* skill.
Zh: “小姑娘，警察活在危险里，呼吸地都是危险的空气。很明显，马伦缺少*变色龙般*的技巧。”

En: A policeman's gotta have *the right stuff*. An ingrained sense of The Law. No one would follow a weakling like Mullen.
Zh: “警察必须有*正确的东西*。一种根深蒂固的法律意识。没人会跟着马伦这样的弱者。”

En: Unlike you, sir. She smiles mischievously. "He's just a fictional character, he's no match for you."
Zh: “不像你，先生。”她调皮地笑了。“他只是一个虚构的角色，根本比不上你。”

En: If you say so, sir. She smiles mischievously. "He's just a fictional character, he's no match for your... *soul*."
Zh: “如果你这么说的话，先生。”她调皮地笑了。“他只是一个虚构的角色，根本比不上你的……*灵魂*。”

En: Like you, sir, of course. She smiles mischievously. "He's just a fictional character, he's no match for you."
Zh: “像你一样，先生，当然了。”她调皮地笑了。“他只是一个虚构的角色，根本比不上你。”

En: If you say so, sir. She smiles mischievously. "He's just a fictional character, he's no match for you."
Zh: “如果你这么说的话，先生。”她调皮地笑了。“他只是一个虚构的角色，根本比不上你。”

En: Her eyes shine with sympathy. "Cheer up, sir. No need to compare yourself to a fictional character."
Zh: 她的眼睛里闪烁着同情的光芒。“振作一点，先生。不需要把你自己跟一个虚构角色对比的。”

En: She looks at you with sympathy. "Cheer up, sir. It's much easier being a fictional character, he doesn't have to deal with... real things."
Zh: 她看着你，眼底满是同情。“振作一点，先生。做个虚构角色要容易得多，他都不用处理……真实的事情。”

En: Maybe you can show me some *real* deductive police work, sir? Like in the books! Her smile is warm and consoling, unlike the imperial image of Dick Mullen.
Zh: “也许你可以给我展示一些警察*真正的*推理技巧，先生？”她的笑容温暖又安慰，不像迪克·马伦那副高高在上的模样。“就像书里写的那样！”

En: I don't really want to DEAL with this. It's too much.
Zh: “我真不想这么做。太过了。”

En: She looks at you with sympathy. "We can talk about something else if you want. Something nicer."
Zh: 她看着你，眼底满是同情。“如果你想的话，我们可以聊点别的。聊点更好的事。”

En: There's the mind... and there's *the mind*, if you catch my drift.
Zh: “需要头脑……还有*思想*，如果你能明白我的意思。”

En: Uh, not really, sir.
Zh: “呃，不太明白，先生。”

En: See, that's what reading those fakey-fake Mullen books gets you. Now you're dealing with a real cop.
Zh: “看见没，这就是看那些胡编乱造的马伦的书给你带来的影响。现在你面对的可是一个真正的警察。”

En: Yes, sir. She smiles mischievously. "He's just a fictional character, I understand. He's no match for you."
Zh: “是的，先生！”她调皮地笑了。“他只是一个虚构的角色，我明白的。他根本比不上你。”

En:
Actually, I... I... I'm sorry... I can't deduce anything.	“事实上，我……我……我很抱歉……我什么也推断不出来。”
0x0100002F0000B9B8	Dialogue Text	PLAZA / ANNETTE	Annette	A small disappointment rolls over her, but she promptly gets over it and says: It's okay, sir."
Zh: 她感觉到一点点失望，不过很快就走了出来，说道：“没关系的，先生。”

En: You disappointed a small child. Well done, champ.
Zh: 你让一个小孩子失望了。干得漂亮，冠军。

En: So I can absorb their *essence*. All their fame will flow into me.
Zh: “这样我就可以吸收他们的*精华*。他们的名气都会流到我身上。”

En: I don't think that's how fame works, sir...
Zh: “我觉得名气好像不是这么回事啊，先生……”

En: Because it's the nature of the powerful to exploit the weak.
Zh: “因为剥削弱者是强者的本性。”

En: There must be an economic reason for this.
Zh: 这里面肯定有经济方面的原因。

En: No, it's too much. I don't want to say that to a little girl.
Zh: “不，这样太过了。我不想跟一个小女孩说这种话。”

En: Psst! You forgot the subterfuge. You're saying this out loud.
Zh: 嘘！您忘记托词了。您大声说出来了。

En: She raises her eyebrows. "Say what, sir?"
Zh: 她扬起眉头。“说什么，先生？”

En: The lieutenant stands by -- looking at the two of you with little interest.
Zh: 警督站在一边——毫无兴趣地看着你们俩。

En: A façade of true professionalism. He is far more intrigued by the situation than his poise reveals.
Zh: 这就是真正专业主义的体现。他对眼下这种状况的兴趣远远超出了他表露出来的镇定。

En: Thanks. I'm trying.
Zh: “谢谢，我在努力。”

En: Yeah, I can see. You don't have party eyes anymore.
Zh: “是啊，我能看出来。你已经没有派对眼了。”

En: The lieutenant slowly -- ever so slowly -- realizes something. "Party eyes. Yes, of course. That makes sense."
Zh: 警督慢慢地——极其缓慢地——意识到了什么。“派对眼。是的，当然了。那就说得通了。”

En: I'm not surprised children have seen you running around with *party eyes* on, he thinks. Not at all.
Zh: 小孩看见你顶着一双*派对眼*到处跑，我一点也不觉得惊讶，他想到。一点也不。

En: What are you missing here? Why does this feel familiar?
Zh: 你错过了什么？为什么感觉这么熟悉？

En: Knows the Law.
Zh: 了解法律。

En: Helped her in.
Zh: 帮助她。

En: Because you know each other. She's been talking to you so openly because you've talked before.
Zh: 因为你们彼此认识。她能这么坦然地跟你说话，是因为你们之前聊过。

En: I am also sad and my head hurts.
Zh: “我很伤心，而且我的头很痛。”

En: I'm sorry, sir. I hope things get better soon. She looks you in the eye, a gleam of sympathy on her face.
Zh: “我很抱歉，先生。”她看着你的眼睛，脸上闪过一丝同情。“希望情况很快就会变好。”

En: The lieutenant does not flinch at the comment. He does not flinch even a single bit. He is intensely not flinching. It takes effort.
Zh: 警督没有因为这个评论而畏缩，完全没有一丝畏缩。毫不畏缩，这是需要付出努力的。

En: There she stands, swaying on her feet, assaulted by the early spring breeze. She smiles at you. The whole situation suddenly feels familiar, somehow.
Zh: 她就在站在那里，晃来晃去，被早春的微风侵袭。她朝你微笑。不知为什么，这个情况突然让你感觉有些熟悉。

En: Nothing, forget it. Dick Mullen sucks, is what I meant.
Zh: “没什么，忘了吧。迪克·马伦烂透了，我就是这个意思。”

En: You don't have to be, sir. She smiles mischievously. "He's just a fictional character, he's no match for you."
Zh: “你不用这样的，先生。”她调皮地笑了。“他只是一个虚构的角色，我明白的。他根本比不上你。”

En: Never you mind. Either way, I'm not impressed with this Mullen guy.
Zh: “和你没关系。不管怎么说，我对这个叫马伦的家伙完全无感。”

En: Of course, sir. She smiles mischievously. "He's just a fictional character, he's no match for you."
Zh: “当然了，先生。”她调皮地笑了。“他只是一个虚构的角色，我明白的。他根本比不上你。”

En: Actually, I'd rather not deduce anything. Every time I deduce people get hurt.
Zh: “其实，我宁愿不做任何推断。每次我这么做的时候，就会有人受伤。”

En: I can't get into the Doomed Commercial Area. Your mother won't let me through the back door.
Zh: “我没办法进入诅咒商业去。你妈妈不让我从后门进去。”

En: Hey, guess what. I found a way into the Doomed Commercial Area.
Zh: “哈，你猜怎么着。我想办法进入诅咒商业区了。”

En: You don't need to be worried. I'm here to help.
Zh: “你不用担心。我是来帮忙的。”

En: She sighs -- not relieved, but resigned. "No," she says. "You're right..."
Zh: 她叹了口气——不是放松，而是听天由命。“不。”她说道。“你说得对……”

En: Not really. [Leave.]
Zh: “不见得。”[离开。]

En: Okay, I guess I will.
Zh: “好吧，我想我会的。”

En: Mhm! She stamps her feet, waiting for you to say something.
Zh: “嗯！”她跺跺脚，等待着你开口。

En: I could help -- by brutally dismantling the free market.
Zh: “我可以帮忙——只要残暴地废除自由市场就好。”

En: Thank you, sir! I'm glad to help Mum out with the store. She doesn't seem to understand what you said.
Zh: “谢谢你，先生！我很高兴能帮妈妈看店。”她似乎并不理解你的话。

En: Whoah! WHOAH-WHOAH-WHOAH! Hold your horses little girl!
Zh: 哇！喂喂喂！别着急，小姑娘！

En: Don't be ridiculous, I know all these things.
Zh: 别开玩笑了，这些事我都知道！

En: What is *school* anyway?
Zh: “话说，*学校*是什么？”

En: School? She rubs her red, chilled nose. "Well, mine is a big yellow building on Boogie Street. And the people there run it. They say it's a *charity*."
Zh: “学校？”她揉了揉被冻得通红的鼻子。“呃，我的学校在布吉街，是一栋黄色的大房子。那边的人负责管理。他们说它是一个*慈善团体*。”

En: The gale wind blows in from the coast, a wind of needles. It wraps itself around a building of brick and mortar. Somewhere, a clamor rings through the honeycomb of classrooms.
Zh: 海岸吹来一阵狂风，像针一般刺痛的狂风。它将一栋钻块和灰浆组成的建筑团团围住。某个地方，喧闹的声响穿过蜂巢一般的教室。

En: Behind her the window has been boarded up. You sense the boards creaking, twisting for a second... and some kind of *doubt* in her tense shoulders.
Zh: 她身后的窗户被木板封上了。你感到木板在嘎吱作响，还扭曲了一两秒……她紧绷的肩膀预示着某种*怀疑*的情绪。

En: Good to hear that it's going well. I'm sure there's nothing to worry about.
Zh: “很高兴能听到你说一切进展顺利。我肯定完全没什么好担心的。”

En: We can go into the bookstore and ask about the case, but I don't see much more to look *into* here. The lieutenant makes a note in his notebook.
Zh: “我们进去书店问问怎么回事，不过我觉得这里没什么可以*调查*的。”警督在笔记本里记了几笔。

En: Yes! She chirps. "Please do look at our wares inside -- the postcards and board games you asked about are also there, sir."\n
Zh: “是的！”她愉快地说道。“请去里面看看我们的商品吧——你问过的明信片和桌游都在里面，先生。”

En: Yes! She chirps. "Please do also look at our wares inside -- the postcards and board games are there, sir."
Zh: “是的！”她愉快地说道。“请去里面看看我们的商品吧——明信片和桌游都在里面，先生。”

En: How does this curse... manifest itself?
Zh: “那这个诅咒……有什么表现吗？”

En: Uh... I'm not sure, sir. I don't really know anything about curses... She looks around, confused.
Zh: “呃……我不确定，先生。我真的不知道诅咒的事……”她看了看周围，有些困惑。

En: Hmm-hmm. (Stroke your chin.) "Enough about the curse... for now."
Zh: “嗯嗯。”（摸摸下巴。）“诅咒的事……暂时先说道这里吧。”

En: Annette looks at your shaved, prickly chin. "Maybe I can tell you about some of our books instead?"
Zh: 安妮特看着你刮过胡子之后刺楞楞的下巴。“也许我可以跟你说说我们的书？”

En: Annette looks at your shaved, prickly chin. It distinctly contrasts with the oily mutton chops that surround it. "Maybe I can tell you about some of our books instead?"
Zh: 安妮特看着你刮过胡子之后刺楞楞的下巴。它与周围油腻腻的络腮胡形成了鲜明的对比。“也许我可以跟你说说我们的书？”

En: They're not doomed. But your mother should learn from their mistakes.
Zh: “他们没有被诅咒。不过你妈妈应该从他们的错误当中学习。”

En: It's just regular trading and selling of things, child. It doesn't work out for most people.
Zh: “这只是普通的物品买卖，孩子。对于大多数来说都是行不通的。”

En: Of course, sir. Uhm... She doesn't know what else to say.
Zh: “当然了，先生。呃……”她不知道还能说些什么。

En: Okay, I get it. Crime-murder gets the people going.
Zh: “好吧，我明白了。犯罪凶杀能让大家兴奋起来。”

En: Mhm-mhm, she replies. "And it's kinda like a puzzle, too. You can guess who the criminal is or how the good guys are going to catch him."
Zh: “嗯嗯，”她回答道，“而且还有点像是谜题。你可以猜谁是犯人，或者好人是怎么抓住他们的。”

En: Wait -- not crime *fiction*. I need to know what *crime* is.
Zh: “等等——不是犯罪*小说*。我需要知道什么是*犯罪*。”

En: It's that bad. (Point to your head.)
Zh: “就有那么糟糕。”（指指你的脑袋。）

En: Crime is a deviation from the law punishable by either a civil or criminal sentencing. It's what *The Law* prevents.
Zh: “犯罪是一种违背法律规定的行为，需要接受民事或者刑事判决的惩罚。它是被*法律*禁止的。”

En: Crime is what we were solving before this conversation began.
Zh: “在这次对话开始之前，我们想要解决的问题就是犯罪。”

En: Crime is what criminals do. And policemen... solve?
Zh: “犯罪就是罪犯做的事。还有警察……解决的事？”

En: It's not your body that's important in police work, anyway. It's your... (Point to your head.)
Zh: “不管怎么说，在破案的时候，你的身体状况并不重要。而是你的……”（指着你的脑袋。）

En: Muscle training is flower arranging for men. I bet he thinks he's real manly like that. He isn't. It's just unhealthy.
Zh: “肌肉训练对于男人来说就是插花。我敢说他觉得这样一定很有男子气概。但是并没有。这一点都不健康。”

En: Bet he can't even bend over to pick the gun his rigid fingers dropped. Real detectives are sinewy and snake-hipped. Like me.
Zh: “我敢打赌，他那僵硬的手指把枪弄掉之后，他甚至都没办法弯下腰把它捡起来。真正的警探都是很结实的，而且臀部动作很灵活。像我一样。”

En: I don't know. Let's talk about something else. This making me feel sad.
Zh: “我不知道。我们说点别的吧。这让我感觉很难过。”

En: Don't feel sad, sir! Mullen isn't even real. You're real!
Zh: “别难过，先生！马伦甚至都不是真人。你是真人啊！”

En: Head. Yes!
Zh: “头。没错！”

En: No, I meant your *mind*.
Zh: “不对，我的意思是*头脑*。”

En: No -- It's your soul, your blue soul.
Zh: 不——是你的灵魂，忧郁的灵魂。

En: Soul. That Mullen guy looks like a *Hampelmann* -- who could respect that face? It's not even drawn correctly! He lacks *soul*.
Zh: “灵魂。那个叫马伦的家伙看起来像个*提线玩偶*——谁会尊重那张脸啊？根本画都没画对！他缺少*灵魂*。”

En: Not head, child, *headssss*!
Zh: 不是头，孩子，是*头脑*！

En: Flexibility. There are millions of different people out there and you have to get into their heads -- sometimes you gotta be the killer to catch the killer.
Zh: “灵活性。世界上有成千上万个不同的人，你必须进入他们的大脑——有时候你必须变成凶手，才能抓住凶手。”

En: No -- your resilience.
Zh: 不——是你的恢复力。

En: No -- your *mind*.
Zh: 不——是你的*头脑*。

En: Grit. A total disregard for personal safety. You gotta take the pain. This Mullen guy looks like he'd run to his mom. Ain't got no balls.
Zh: “坚韧不拔。完全不顾个人安危。你必须学会承受痛苦。这个叫马伦的家伙看起来就是会哭着跑回去找妈妈的人。根本没有胆量。”

En: A dick with no balls! Bam! Mullen's no real deal, he's got nothing on me.
Zh: “没种的男人！马伦没什么了不起的，什么也比不上我。”

En: A quicksilver mind. This Mullen guy looks like he needs four hours just to come up with a single idea.
Zh: “当机立断的思考能力。这个叫马伦的家伙看起来需要4小时才能想出一个主意来。”

En: No-no, think about it! One where they plunge into a torrid spiral of pain and recrimination. Only it's *really long* and drawn out. Scarred for life. Phantom limb!
Zh: “不不，你想想！他们陷入痛苦和互相指责的恶性循环。这个过程只会变得*特别冗长*，让人筋疲力尽，终其一生都伤痕累累，就像幻肢症一样！”

En: I'm sorry, sir, I don't know how to help you...
Zh: “很抱歉，先生，我不知道该怎么帮你……”

En: I haven't read many of... those. Maybe you should ask Mum?
Zh: “那方面的书……我看得不多。也许你应该问问我妈妈？”

En: Yeah. Yeah! And it drags on for *years* and *years* with no resolution in sight...
Zh: “是啊。是啊！而且*一年*又*一年*的拖下去，完全看不到可以解决的迹象……”

En: ... each of you filling with resentment and bitterness for having *ruined* each other's lives...
Zh: “……然后双方都会因为*毁掉*对方的生活而充满怨恨和苦涩……”

En: You can take this! Blossom like a pain-flower.
Zh: 你能承受住！让痛苦之花绽放吧。

En: Stop. There's no need for this. It's in your power to change the subject.
Zh: 停下来。没必要这样。你能够改变话题的。

En: ... each of you tearing at the other's innards, leaving a gaping emptiness, a vacuum heart that still hurts ceaselessly!
Zh: “……每一个人都在撕扯对方的内心，留下一个空虚的缺口，一颗还会不停刺痛的真空心脏！”

En: Can I become famous by reading one of those books?
Zh: “看这些书能让我变成名人吗？”

En: I don't know about that... But I imagine life is great when you're famous.
Zh: “那我就不知道了……不过我能想象，出名之后生活一定会变得更好吧。”

En: Yeah, I'm old. That's just what happens to people.
Zh: “是啊，我老了。大家身上都会发生这种事。”

En: Wow, look at the guy. I'll never be as good as Mullen.
Zh: “哇哦，看看这家伙。我永远也不会变得像马伦一样好。”

En: Don't say that. He's not even real. You're real.
Zh: “别这么说。他甚至都不是个真人。你是真实的。”

En: That's right. A real *failure*.
Zh: “没错。一个真正的失败。”

En: Overshadowed by a fake man? That's harsh.
Zh: “在一个虚构的男人面前黯然失色？这也太残酷了吧。”

En: These people deserve to *die*.
Zh: 这些人该*死*。

En: Maybe I should start *killing* these famous people. Hunt them down.
Zh: “也许我可以从*杀害*这些名人开始。追捕他们。”

En: Mhm. Mum can get really agitated about that. She sways on her feet. "I guess you can try making her less afraid somehow... then she might trust you."
Zh: “嗯。妈妈真的会变得特别激动。”她摇摇晃晃地站着。“我觉得你可以想办法消除一些她的恐惧……然后她可能就会相信你了。”

En: Oh, that's great! Her smile is warm. "I hope exciting adventures lie ahead! And maybe you can help mother become... less afraid."
Zh: “哦，那很棒啊！”她的笑容很温暖。“我希望前面有刺激的冒险等着你！而且，也许你可以让我妈妈变得……没那么害怕。”

En: I'm sorry, sir, I can't talk right now. I'm very busy with my homework... I have so much homework now.
Zh: “我很抱歉，先生，现在我没办法跟你聊天。我正忙着我的家庭作业呢……我现在有好多家庭作业要做。”

En: Hi, ace detective. She smiles warmly. "Are you here for more books?"
Zh: “你好啊，大警探。”她的笑容很热情。“你又是来买书的吗？”

En: Hello, sir. How is the investigation going? Found any *cuuurses* yet?
Zh: “你好，先生。调查进行地怎么样了？有没有找到什么*诅咒*呢？”

En: Is it okay if I ask you some questions?
Zh: “我可以问你点问题吗？”

En: Okay, sir. I'll try to answer any questions you have. I hope they're about books.
Zh: “好吧，先生。我会努力回答你的问题。希望是关于书的问题。”

En: Hello again, sir! Are you interested in a new and exciting book this time? She sways slowly on her feet.
Zh: “又见面了，先生！这次你对令人兴奋的新书感兴趣吗？”她慢慢地来回摇晃。

En: Yes, people get that you are *the law*. You really don't have to keep saying it.
Zh: “是的，大家都明白你是*法律*。你真不用一直挂在嘴边。”

En: Everyone understands you're *the law*. That's why you have that insignia on your arm. So you don't have to keep saying it.
Zh: “大家都明白你是*法律*。所以你的胳膊上才有那个徽章。你真不用一直挂在嘴边的。”

En: Pfft... books? Postcards? Easy! Even a kid would know all of this.
Zh: 啐……书？明信片？简单！这些就连小孩都知道。

En: Little girl, I know what a book is. Books are like very long letters, with stories inside them.
Zh: “小姑娘，我知道书是什么。书就像很长的信，里面还有故事。”

En: Nah, it feels inappropriate to lecture this girl.
Zh: 不，给这个小姑娘讲课感觉不太合适。

En: Yes! She nods, smiling. "That's exactly it."
Zh: “是的！”她笑着点点头。“就是这样。”

En: You're fooling nobody.
Zh: 你骗不到任何人的。

En: Sir? Are you okay? Her voice suddenly reaches your mind. "You've been standing here silently for a while now..."
Zh: “先生？你还好吗？”她的声音突然传进你的脑海。“你已经默默地站了好一会儿了……”

En: Don't you sass me.
Zh: 别跟我顶嘴。

En: I said I know all of these things and I do, god dammit.
Zh: 我说这些东西我都知道，而且我的确知道，该死。

En: Sir? Are you okay? Her voice suddenly reaches your mind. "You've been standing here silently for a while now..."
Zh: “先生？你还好吗？”她的声音突然传进你的脑海。“你已经默默地站了好一会儿了……”

En: Don't tell me what to do.
Zh: 别告诉我该怎么做。

En: Hey now, I already made up my mind.
Zh: 好了，我已经下定决心了。

En: Don't be a fool -- as an expert, it's your duty to tell what you know to everyone.
Zh: 别傻了——作为一个专家，你的职责就是把自己知道的告诉所有人。

En: I also know what a postcard is... it's a small cardboard picture that you can send to a friend or a loved one.
Zh: “我还知道明信片是什么……那是一张小的纸片画。你可以把它送给你的朋友或是爱人。”

En: Mhm mhm. You've got it, sir. What about board games?
Zh: “嗯。你说得对，先生。那桌游呢？”

En: Who cares about board games? No one.
Zh: “谁在乎桌游呢？没人。”

En: The police don't have time for games.
Zh: “警察没时间玩游戏。”

En: Okay, sir. This was all very... *enlightening*. Can I help you with anything else?
Zh: “好吧，先生。这一切都非常有……*启发性*。还有什么我能帮你的吗？”

En: Alright, I think that was enough. I have proven myself.
Zh: “好吧，我想也差不多了。我已经证明了自己。”

En: If you say so, sir. Can I help you with anything else?
Zh: “如果你这么说的话，先生。还有什么我能帮你的吗？”

En: I like playing games... with peoples' minds.
Zh: “我喜欢玩游戏……跟别人斗智斗勇。”

En: Every snowflake is like a little gift from the sky. She shivers as one falls on her nose.
Zh: “每片雪花都像是天空赐予的一份小礼物。”一片雪花飘落在她的鼻子上，她打了个冷战。

En: It's going fine, I think.
Zh: “我想，一切都会好的。”

En: That's very good to hear. She flicks you a thumbs up. "You'll get to the bottom of this in no time! Just like a detective in the stories."
Zh: “听你这么说我很高兴。”她朝你竖起了大拇指。“你很快就会彻底查明真相的！就像故事里的那些警探一样。”

En: It's going bad, *really* bad.
Zh: “情况会变得很糟糕，*真正的*糟糕。”

En: Oh, that's very sad to hear. She frowns. "But it'll pick up soon, don't worry. There's always a... bad spot in the stories, right before the detective figures things out."
Zh: “哦，听你这么说我很难过。”她皱起眉头。“不过很快就会好起来的，别担心。故事里……总会遇到一些不好的事情，就在警探查清真相之前。”

En: Yeah, I found a way into the Doomed Commercial Area.
Zh: “是啊，我找到进入诅咒商业区的办法了。”

En: Oh, that's great! Her smile is warm. "I hope exciting adventures lie ahead! And maybe you can help mother become... less afraid."
Zh: “哦，那很棒啊！”她的笑容很温暖。“我希望前面有刺激的冒险等着你！而且，也许你可以让我妈妈变得……没那么害怕。”

En: The lights are so pretty when it gets dark. She scans the surrounding buildings, a happy look on her face.
Zh: “天黑的时候，灯光就会变得特别漂亮。”她扫视着周围的建筑，脸上露出了愉快的表情。

En: Little girl, help. My Dick Mullen book ripped before I could get to the end. Do you know what happens?
Zh: “小姑娘，帮帮忙。这本迪克·马伦的书被撕破了，我还没看到结局呢。你知道是怎么一回事吗？”

En: Which book was it, sir?
Zh: “哪本书呢，先生？”

En: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity.
Zh: “迪克·马伦与错误的身份。”

En: Oh... She frowns. "I'm very sorry, sir. I haven't read that one yet. I wish I could help you."
Zh: “哦……”她皱起眉头。“非常抱歉，先生。那本我还没读过呢。我也很想帮你。”

En: Then why does all of this feel like some sad, strange dream?
Zh: 那为什么这一切感觉就像是个悲伤又奇怪的梦境？

En: What are you doing now?
Zh: “你现在在做什么？”

En: Math... She looks into her notebook with trepidation. "It's really difficult. Like -- really. They say you need it to get rich. Better than standing outside in the cold, I guess."
Zh: “数学……”她忧虑地看着自己的笔记本。“真的很难。怎么说呢——是真的难。他们说变富有就需要它。我想，总比站在外面挨冻要好吧。”

En: Oh, oh...! Suddenly, she smiles and perks up. "I found something while you were away."
Zh: 突然间，她绽开了笑容，似乎想起了什么。“哦，哦……！你不在的时候我发现了这个。”

En: It's a... fedora?
Zh: “这是……软呢帽吗？”

En: A detective hat?
Zh: “一顶侦探帽？”

En: Maybe? It's the hat Dick Mullen wears all the time... She grins with joy. "You'll look way more serious with that."
Zh: “也许吧？迪克·马伦总是带着这种帽子……”她高兴地笑了。“有了它的话，你会看起来更严肃一些。”

En: Yes! Just like the one Dick Mullen wears all the time... She grins. "You'll look way more serious with that."
Zh: “是的！就像迪克·马伦一直戴的那顶一样。”她咧开嘴笑了。“有了它的话，你会看起来更严肃一些。”

En: What is it?
Zh: “这是什么？”

En: I thought this would fit you... Like thanks for helping out! Not me -- the city I mean. Like a detective does. She gives you a hat -- almost exactly like the one Dick Mullen wears on the covers.
Zh: “我想它可能会适合你……”她递给你一顶帽子——几乎跟封面上迪克·马伦的帽子一模一样。“感谢你的帮助！不是对我——而是对这座城市。就像警探一样。”

En: Okay, I get it. I can look just like Dick Mullen -- except I'm an actual police officer.
Zh: “好吧，我明白了。我可以看起来跟迪克·马伦很像——只不过我是个真正的警官。”

En: Yes! She grins. "I bet it looks good on you. Really serious."
Zh: “是的！”她咧开嘴笑了。“我打赌你戴上一定很好看。肯定显得很严肃。”

En: Right. I have to get back to my homework now, before Mum notices. Man, this is *hard*... She looks back at the infernal scribblings under her nose.
Zh: 突然间，她回头看了看鼻子下面潦草到可怕的字迹。“好吧。现在我要继续做作业了，免得被妈妈看见。天啦，这玩意真够*难*的……”

En: Your mother told me about the curse, but she wouldn't let me investigate.
Zh: “你妈妈跟我说了诅咒的事，不过她不肯让我调查。”

En: I'm currently investigating the curse on your mother's behalf.
Zh: “我目前正代表你妈妈调查诅咒的事。”

En: Yes, she can be quite strict about it. Annette nods. "My mum doesn't let anyone sneak around in the back rooms or the cellar."
Zh: “是的，她会因为这件事变得特别严厉。”安妮特点点头。“妈妈不让我溜进后面的房间或者地下室。”

En: I've been in this house and you're right, there's an awful lot of failed businesses there.
Zh: “我去里面看过了，你说得对，里面确实有很多破产的企业。”

En: Annette's eyes go wide: "She let you in there? Usually she doesn't let anyone sneak around in the back rooms or the cellar."
Zh: 安妮特瞪大了眼睛：“她让你进去了？一般她不会让任何人溜进后面的房间或者地下室的。”

En: I hope you can help her. Help her become less afraid or something... She looks at her freezing hands, before hiding them in her coat sleeves.
Zh: “希望你能帮到她。让她没那么害怕，或者……”她看着冻僵的双手，然后把它们藏进了大衣袖子里。

En: You've been there? Her eyes go wide. "My mum never lets me sneak around in the back room or the cellar!"
Zh: “你去过那里？”她瞪大了眼睛。“妈妈从来不让我溜进后面的房间或者地下室！”

En: This must have been really scary... She looks at her freezing hands, before hiding them in her coat sleeves.
Zh: “肯定非常吓人吧……”她看着冻僵的双手，然后把它们藏进了大衣袖子里。

En: This coin-operated viewer is facing South. It has given up all its secrets -- there seems to be a sunken seafort on the islet in the bay...
Zh: 这个投币式望远镜朝向南方。它放弃了自己的所有秘密——海湾小岛上似乎有一座沉没的海堡……

En: This coin-operated viewer is facing South. The instruction manual says to insert twenty five *centims* and pull the handle while looking inside, then use the focus knob to zoom in if necessary.
Zh: 这个投币式望远镜朝向南方。操作手册上说只要投入25*分*钱，然后拉动手柄朝里面观看，如果有必要的话，还可以使用聚焦旋钮来放大画面。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Your money disappears into the coin-slot. A clunk, the ring of metal...
Zh: 你的钱消失在投币口。咔哒，是金属的声音……

En: Why place a tourist attraction in the middle of such poverty?
Zh: 为什么要在贫瘠的最中央建一个旅游景点呢？

En: Pull the handle and look inside.
Zh: 拉动手柄，朝里面观看。

En: The curtains on the display open. You lean in to catch the view -- it's blurry, different blues and greens. In the middle of the shimmer stands a drab grey shape, like a ghost.
Zh: 显示器上的幕布打开了。你倾下身，查看里面的风景——视线很模糊，不同的蓝色和绿色交织在一起。在微光中央，耸立着一个灰褐色的东西，像个幽灵。

En: Turn the knob to focus your vision.
Zh: 转动旋钮，聚焦视野。

En: The lenses shift, the ghost sharpens into an islet in the bay. In the ruins a man-made structure is visible: a half-sunken sea-fort, its concrete almost reconquered by nature.
Zh: 随着镜头的变换，幽灵逐渐幻化成海湾中的一座小岛。在一片废墟之间，可以看见一栋人造建筑：一座半沉没的海堡，它的混凝土结构几乎已经被大自然重新征服了。

En: Hang in there, little one.
Zh: “坚持住，小东西。”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: The little brave birch-tree seems to wave back in the wind.
Zh: 勇敢的小白桦树似乎在风中朝你挥手。

En: It looks as if it was abandoned quite some time ago, nothing but a rotten tooth remains of the anti-aircraft tower. A lonely birch tree grows out of it.
Zh: 看起来像是很久之前就废弃了，只剩下这座防空塔，宛如一颗腐烂的牙齿。一棵白桦树孤零零地生长在中央。

En: Insert a coin.
Zh: 投入一枚硬币。

En: What's a tourist attraction doing... here?
Zh: “一个旅游景点……在这里做什么？”

En: There was a revitalization project in '49. A design studio tried restoring Martinaise to its pre-war glory. The lieutenant looks around and concludes: "It didn't stick."
Zh: “49年的时候有过一个复兴工程。一间设计工作室想要让马丁内斯恢复战前的荣光。”警督看了看周围，总结到：“没有坚持下去。”

En: They got as far as the street lamps and that statue on the intersection before something went sour. He lowers his voice. "I suspect it was Evrart Claire's doing. He muscled them out -- it's how it usually goes around here."
Zh: “他们换了新街灯，还有十字路口的那座雕像，然后就出问题了。”他压低了声音。“我怀疑是艾弗拉特·克莱尔干的。他把他们赶出去了。这里一般都是这种情况。”

En: They got as far as the street lamps and the statue on that intersection. Then something went sour. He lowers his voice. "I suspect that *something* was Evrart Claire -- the Union leader. He muscled them out. It's how it usually goes around here."
Zh: “他们换了新街灯，还有十字路口的那座雕像。然后就出问题了。”他压低了声音。“我怀疑那个*问题*是艾弗拉特·克莱尔——工会领袖。他把他们赶出去了。这里一般都是这种情况。”

En: What happened?
Zh: “发生什么事了？”

En: Its leaves ripped from it by the winter wind...
Zh: 它的树叶被冬日的寒风扯了下来……

En: There are ruins of some kind of building there...
Zh: “那里好像有什么建筑的废墟……”

En: Could the shot have come from there?
Zh: “子弹有没有可能是从那里射出来的？”

En: Could the shot have come from there? Then entered the window upstairs the Whirling-in-Rags?
Zh: 子弹有没有可能是从那里射出来的？然后钻进了褴褛飞旋楼上的窗户？

En: Really? I don't have the eyesight to make it out... The lieutenant takes off his glasses and cleans them.
Zh: “真的吗？我视力不好，看不清……”警督摘下眼镜擦了擦。

En: It's not possible to know from this distance. The details are blurry, there's water vapour in the way and waves cut into the islet...
Zh: 从这个距离看是不可能知道的。细节很模糊，还有水蒸气的妨碍和不断拍打在小岛上的海浪……

En: Can you tell? From this distance? I don't think it's possible.
Zh: “你能看出来吗？从这个距离吗？我觉得不太可能。”

En: It's not. The details are blurry, there's water vapour in the way and waves cut into the islet...
Zh: 不能。细节很模糊，还有水蒸气的妨碍和不断拍打在小岛上的海浪……

En: Cool. I dig organized crime. It's the best.
Zh: “厉害。我喜欢有组织犯罪。它是最棒的。”

En: Can't we... do something about it?
Zh: “我们就不能……做点什么吗？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: We should have *done* something about the Union ten years ago. That ship has sailed, officer.
Zh: “我们十年前就应该对工会*做点*什么。现在机会已经溜走了，警官。”

En: The lieutenant adjusts his collar. It's frisky outside.
Zh: 警督整了整衣领。外面很欢闹。

En: That's odd. I could swear you were a *police officer*...
Zh: “那就怪了。我可以发誓你是一名*警官*……”

En: Vandalism. (Shake your head.)
Zh: “故意破坏公共财物。”（摇摇头。）

En: Probably some kids... The lieutenant inspects the rigged slot.
Zh: “也许是小孩子做的吧……”警督检查着被堵死的投币口。

En: That's CUNO on the lens.
Zh: 镜头上写的是坤诺。

En: The church looks old and weather-worn. There are no lights in the windows.
Zh: 教堂看起来饱经风雨，残破不堪。窗户里没有透露出光亮。

En: Around the large wooden building you see chunks of sea ice, gathered on the beach. And a small tent set up on the ice.
Zh: 在巨大的木质建筑周围，你看见大块的海冰聚集在海滩上。冰面上搭了一座小帐篷。

En: The mute machine is unable to confirm or deny your theory.
Zh: 沉默的机器无法确认或是否定你的理论。

En: But yes -- it was vandalism.
Zh: 不过没错——这就是在故意破坏公共财物。

En: This coin-operated viewer is facing southwest. Its coin-slot is full of fossilized bubblegum, rendering the machine permanently inaccessible.
Zh: 这个投币式望远镜朝向南方。它的投币口里塞满了硬邦邦的口香糖，让这台机器永远也无法使用了。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Look inside.
Zh: 看看里面。

En: A thick layer of graffito covers the lenses -- you spell out the word "ONUC" written on the other side -- with N and C scribbled backwards.
Zh: 一层厚厚的涂鸦遮住了镜头——你在另一边分辨出“ONUC”的字样——而且N和C是倒着写的。

En: Poor little viewer... (Pat it.)
Zh: “可怜的小望远镜……”（拍拍它。）

En: The metal feels cold and wet under your palm. It looks unhygienic.
Zh: 掌心下的金属感觉冰冷而潮湿。它看起来很不卫生。

En: Under the graffito a sea of blues and greys appears -- behind the water lies a coast studded with concrete and reeds. On it -- a church on stilts, lanky weather-worn wooden planks, an x-shaped cross topping its tower.
Zh: 涂鸦下面，出现一片蓝灰色的海洋——海洋的背后，是一条布满混凝土和芦苇丛的海岸。在那上面——一座高高耸立的教堂，修长的木板饱受风雨侵蚀，尖塔顶端是一个X形的十字。

En: You know this to be the Star of Perikarnassis, or the Chi-Rho. The central symbol of the Perikarnassian church -- a star, a great moral height to be strived towards.
Zh: 你知道这就是佩里卡纳西斯之星，又叫做凯乐符号。佩里卡纳西斯教堂的中心象征——它是一颗星，一种值得我们奋斗的道德高度。

En: A simple but clever solution to ruining a coin-operated viewer. It took ingenuity.
Zh: 这是破坏投币式望远镜的一种简单又聪明的办法。这是需要费脑筋的。

En: Shift your focus to the background.
Zh: 将注意力转移到背景上。

En: Look in the suspect transport enclosure.
Zh: 看看嫌疑人押送厢内部。

En: What do you mean you 'confiscated' them?
Zh: “你说‘没收’是什么意思？”

En: A couple weeks ago I stopped a young man for going slower than the speed of traffic. Turns out he was some Coalition official's son -- and high.
Zh: “几周前，我拦下了一个快車道上低速驾驶的年轻人。结果发现他是一位联盟官员的儿子——而且还是个高官。”

En: (Look at Kim.) "Isn't that *corruption*?"
Zh: （看着金。）“那不是*腐败*吗？”

En: I don't know, is it? The lieutenant looks back, stone-faced. "I was going to take them into evidence, but they weren't necessary for conviction. He never asked for them back."
Zh: “我不知道，是吗？”警督看着你，面无表情。“我打算把它们当做证物，不过定罪倒是用不着。他也从来没开口要回去。”

En: (Look at the spinners.) "I get it, I would have confiscated them too -- they're mesmerizing."
Zh: （看着轮圈。）“我懂的，我也会没收它们的——它们太吸引人了。”

En: That they are. The lieutenant looks at the spinners and sighs. "I was going to take them into evidence, but they weren't necessary for conviction. He never asked for them back."
Zh: “的确如此。”警督看着轮圈，叹了口气。“我打算把它们当做证物，不过定罪倒是用不着。他也没开口要回去。”

En: A vanity he wouldn't mind.
Zh: 一种他不介意的虚荣和浮华。

En: Sorry, I can't take them. It wouldn't be right. (Don't take the spinners.)
Zh: “对不起，我不能接受。这样是不对的。”（不拿轮圈。）

En: All right, let's *not* take them now, then come back once we realize we have to -- have this conversation *again* and then take them.
Zh: “好吧，那就先*别拿*，等我们意识到必须：*再*进行一次这样的对话，然后再回来拿也不迟。”

En: The prybar feels nice and cold in your hand. Heavier than you'd think.
Zh: 你手中的撬棍感觉顺手又冰冷。比你想象的要重一些。

En: Cold and heavy -- like truth. You feel like you're reunited with *truth* once more.
Zh: 冰冷而沉重——宛如真相。你觉得自己再一次与*真相*重逢了。

En: The handles are long and sleek. *Snap-snap* go the cutters in your hand.
Zh: 把手修长而光滑。手里的刀具发出*咔擦咔擦*的声响。

En: You can do good work with these: cut chains, locks, and ropes -- especially belts...
Zh: 有了它，你可以做很多事：剪链条，锁，还有绳子——特别是皮带……

En: Useful for opening all sorts of doors and lids...
Zh: 很适合开启各种各样的大门和盖子……

En: It's robust, weatherproof and well made. Police issue -- blue.
Zh: 它很牢固，防风雨，制作精良。警用物品——蓝色的。

En: Let's you see things in the dark you would otherwise miss...
Zh: 能让你看清容易在黑暗中错过的东西……

En: Take what you need, officer. It's going to be a long case. I'm not *protective* of my tools. Like some men are...
Zh: “有什么需要就随便拿，警官。这个案子会耗费很长的时间。我不会*过度保护*我的工具。不像有些人那样……”

En: He's clearly *a little* protective of his tools, but what can you do? Work is work.
Zh: 他显然对自己的工具还是有*一点点*保护心理的，不过你能怎么办呢？工作就是工作。

En: Kim, how do I turn on the headlights?
Zh: “金，我该怎么打开前灯呢？”

En: How do I turn on the headlights?
Zh: 我该怎么打开前灯呢？

En: Translation: We're not going anywhere right now.
Zh: 翻译过来就是：我们现在哪里都不去。

En: Tap on the fuel pre-heater gauge.
Zh: 拍拍燃油预热仪表。

En: Uhm... When Lieutenant Kitsuragi is here? It's his MC.
Zh: 呃……等曷城警督在这里的时候再说？这是他的汽車。

En: You need his key to turn on the engine if you want the foldable lights to pop up.
Zh: 如果想要打开折叠前灯的话，需要用他的钥匙启动发动机。

En: Press ENGINE START.
Zh: 按下发动机按钮。

En: There you go. I'll turn them off from the remote once you're done -- we just need to stand in front of the machine now.
Zh: “好了。等你办完事，我会用遥控器把它们关掉——现在只用站到汽車前面就可以了。”

En: Inside you see a set of steering levers, a radio-microphone, a pull-out toolbox, and the soft glow of the fuel pre-heater gauge.
Zh: 你在里面看见一组操纵杆，一台挂钩式话筒，座椅下的抽出式工具箱，还有燃油预热仪表的柔光。

En: So you took his spinners?
Zh: “所以你拿走了他的轮圈？”

En: Mhm, he nods. "I took them *and* arrested him for driving under the influence."
Zh: “嗯，”他点点头。“我拿走了，*而且*还因为酒后驾車把他逮捕了。”

En: Sounds likely.
Zh: 听起来很有可能是这样。

En: In the cabin you see a set of steering levers, a radio on a hook, a pull-out toolbox and the soft glow of the fuel pre-heater gauge.
Zh: 在車厢里，你看见一组操纵杆，一台挂钩式话筒，座椅下的抽出式工具箱，还有燃油预热仪表的柔光。

En: Damn it -- the lieutenant had the number and he's not here.
Zh: 见鬼——号码在警督这里，但他人不在。

En: This is Precinct 57. Can I connect you to someone? The voice on the shortwave repeats.
Zh: “这里是57分局。需要我帮你联系谁吗？”短波电台里的声音重复着。

En: Please connect me to Sylvie again.
Zh: “请再帮我接塞尔维。”

En: Yes, hello. You recognize her voice.
Zh: “是的，你好。”你认出了她的声音。

En: Sylvie Malaìika on the line for you, officer.
Zh: “塞尔维·玛莱卡已经接通了，警官。”

En: Just a second, officer...
Zh: “请稍等，警官……”

En: Officer, she finally returns. "I have Sylvie Malaìika on the line for you."
Zh: “警官，”她总算是回来了。“塞尔维·玛莱卡已经接通了。”

En: Give it a minute, she might be busy at the moment... takes a bit to get to the phone.
Zh: “稍等一下，她可能正在忙……需要一点时间才能接电话。”

En: Just wait. Relax.
Zh: 耐心等待。放松。

En: What is *wrong* with you? Why did you ask to be connected to her? Who *does* that?! Act professional now.
Zh: 你有什么*毛病*吗？为什么要跟她联系？谁会*做出*这种事？！现在请表现地专业一点。

En: Alternative translation: Don't even *think* you can drive my MC.
Zh: 另一种翻译是：想开我的汽車，你*想*都别想。

En: A motorized vehicle, officer. I'm sure you are familiar with the concept. We've had these for nearly a century.
Zh: “一辆汽車，警官。我肯定你对这个概念很熟悉。我们拥有这玩意已经快一个世纪了。”

En: Honestly, that sounds like an *amazing* physics demonstration.
Zh: 老实说，那个听起来像是一个*惊人的*物理演示。

En: Um.... did you want to put these spinners on your machine? (Point at Kineema.)
Zh: “嗯……你想把轮圈装在你的車上吗？”（指向锐影。）

En: No, no. That would be silly. I just... He hesitates. "I don't know why I kept them. It doesn't matter. I couldn't put them on this MC anyway. A cop with spinners -- that would be outrageous."
Zh: “不，不想。那样太傻了。我只是……”他有些犹豫。“我不知道为什么要留着它们。不过也没关系。反正我也不能把它们装在这台汽車上。装着轮圈的警車——那样太离谱了。”

En: Yes. Too wacky.
Zh: “是的。太古怪了。”

En: Yes. Outrageously cool.
Zh: “是的。酷到没朋友。”

En: I'm sorry you have to sell them because of me.
Zh: “很抱歉，你要为了我卖掉它们。”

En: He flashes a smile, barely visible in the dark.
Zh: 他的脸上闪过一丝笑容，在黑暗中很难察觉。

En: It's only right that we sell them.
Zh: “把它们卖掉才是正解。”

En: Exactly.
Zh: “没错。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: As I said, they're useless anyway. I should have remembered I have these earlier...
Zh: “就像我说的，反正它们也没用了。我应该早点想起还有这玩意的……”

En: Don't make your unpaid hostel bill a *moral boon*, officer. It won't stick.
Zh: “警官，不要把你没有支付的旅社账单当成是一种*道德福利*。这样站不住脚的。”

En: Shot down. I've nothing to add.
Zh: 被打败了。我没什么好补充的。

En: But thank you.
Zh: 但还是谢谢你。

En: The spinners shine so bright they reflect on the lieutenant's glasses. He doesn't say anything either.
Zh: 轮圈发出耀眼的光芒，映照在警督的眼镜上。他也一言不发。

En: Pick up the radio again.
Zh: 再次拿起无线电。

En: Just nod.
Zh: 只是点点头。

En: *You* told him to do that stupid thing...
Zh: 是*你*让他干傻事的……

En: He sounds worried, yet ready to assist. This is how people get when the police call.
Zh: 他听起来有些忧虑，但还是准备好协助。警察打来电话的时候，大家就应该这么做。

En: A dark feeling. This is going wrong. Let go of the clutch.
Zh: 一种黑暗的感觉。这样是不对的。松开离合器。

En: So strange. The machine is not on the bottom of the sea at all. It's right in front of you, well kept. Why did it say that?
Zh: 太奇怪了。車根本不在海底。它就在你面前，安然无恙。它为什么要那么说？

En: What?
Zh: 什么？

En: Operating the radio is easy. Just be confident -- you've probably done it a thousand times.
Zh: 操作电台很容易。只要充满信心——你可能已经做过几千次了。

En: Wait, what? 'Wild one'? That doesn't sound right...
Zh: 等等，什么？‘野姑娘’？听起来不太对啊……

En: C'mon, wake up! The way she said she's not Sylvie -- practically begging you to make a move. Just go for it.
Zh: 拜托，清醒一点！她说自己不是塞尔维的方式——实际上是在请求你采取行动。放手去做吧。

En: Hold on! As a feminist I find these implications offensive.
Zh: 等一下！作为一个女权主义者，我觉得这种暗示很无礼。

En: Oh, did you hear that? Pure lust. She's a wild one! Throw her a curve-ball, see if she's down to party.
Zh: 哦，你听见了吗？纯粹的欲望。她是个野姑娘！给她扔个弧线球，看看她会不会参加派对。

En: Oh, did you hear that? How she said 'officer'?! She's a wild one, wants it bad, bratan. Get that party going!
Zh: 哦，你听见了吗？她说出‘警官’的口气？她是个野姑娘，她很渴望，兄弟。派对开起来吧！

En: Offensive-smoffensive, just ask her, alright!
Zh: 冒犯，那就直接问她好了！

En: As your parasympathetic nervous system I call bullshit. Now ask her!
Zh: 作为你的副交感神经系统，我说这都是废话。现在快问她！

En: Yeah, bratan, otherwise you're always gonna be wondering what could've been. Just ask her!
Zh: 是啊，兄弟，不然你会一直想知道会发生什么。快问她吧！

En: Sir, how can I help you with your *investigation*? The voice on the other end is becoming impatient.
Zh: “先生，你的*调查*需要我的帮助吗？”另一头的声音变得有些不耐烦。

En: Sir, you're already connected to the 57th Precinct. Her voice is sharp and noticeably less friendly.
Zh: “先生，你已经连到57分局了。”她的声音很尖锐，明显感觉到没那么友善了。

En: What an unbelievable prude... Oh well, at least now you know.
Zh: 真是令人难以置信的假正经……哦，好吧，至少现在你知道了。

En: Odd... she didn't sound menopausal. Must be frigid or something. Oh well.
Zh: 奇怪……听起来她没到更年期啊。肯定是太冷了或者什么的。好吧。

En: Who doesn't, she states matter of factly. "Now, how can I help you in your *investigation*?"
Zh: “谁不是呢，”她实事求是的说道。“现在，你的*调查*需要什么帮助吗？”

En: He turns the pre-heater on, waits, takes out his keys and says: "All right. Ready. I turn, you press START -- it's next to the pre-heater."
Zh: 他打开预热器，等待着，接着掏出钥匙，说道：“好吧。准备好。我转动钥匙，你按下启动——按钮就在预热器旁边。”

En: Isn't that dangerous?
Zh: “那样不危险吗？”

En: Okay. (Turn off the radio.)
Zh: “好吧。”（关闭无线电。）

En: No more dangerous than stepping between three armed mercenaries and eight Union men, I hope. He glances over his shoulder.
Zh: “不会比站在3个武装雇佣兵和8个工会成员之间危险，希望如此。”他转头望了望。

En: I don't like it either, but that's the way it is. The streets seem safe enough to me. If anything, taking out the mercs made things calmer. For now... He flicks off the radio. Silence.
Zh: “我也不喜欢，但就是这么回事。在我看来，这些街道已经足够安全了。如果说还有什么的话，没有雇佣兵会让事态更平静一些。暂时而已……”他关掉无线电。一片寂静。

En: They're shutting you up. Silencing you. Don't fucking drop your guard.
Zh: 他们想让你闭嘴。让你安静。别他妈放松警惕。

En: Everything sounds okay. No drumbeat of total war yet. If anything, everything sounds *too* okay...
Zh: 听起来一切都还好。全面战争的鼓点声还没有响起。如果说还有什么的话，听起来一切都有点*好过头*了……

En: Silence.
Zh: 寂静。

En: Something is wrong. Only static hisses through the speaker.
Zh: 有点不对劲。扬声器里只传来嘶嘶的声响。

En: Precinct 57, we've been attacked. I repeat -- Lieutenant Kim Kitsuragi and...
Zh: “57分局，我们被攻击了。重复一遍——曷城警督和……”

En: Hello?
No reply. Only the mindless drone of static, crawling through the air.
Zh: 没有回复。只有机械般的静电声在空中蔓延。

En: It's been this way for a while now. The lieutenant shrugs. "My guess is, the Union is listening in on our conversations -- and jamming outward communications to protect themselves from Krenel."
Zh: “这样已经有一段时间了。”警督耸耸肩。“据我猜测，工会在监听我们的通话——而且还干扰外部通信，来保护自己不被科奈尔攻击。”

En: It only happens when someone mentions the attack -- the rest is unaffected. Our best bet is to carry on like nothing happened -- that is, if we don't want us cut off the grid completely.
Zh: “只有在有人提到攻击的时候会这样——别的都不受影响。我们最好的办法就是当作什么也没发生——也就是说，如果我们不想被完全切断网络的话。”

En: Turn off the radio.
Zh: 关掉无线电。

En: If only Lieutenant Kitsuragi were here to make sense of it...
Zh: 要是曷城警督在的话，就能明白是怎么一回事了……

En: You can try calling again -- just don't mention the tribunal. And remember, they're listening in.
Zh: “你可以试着再呼叫一遍——只是不要提到宣判。还有要记住，他们在监听。”

En: Items that can be pawned at Bird's Nest Roy's appear in your INVENTORY, under the ITEMS tab. You can pawn these items when talking to Roy.
Zh: 能在罗伊的鸟巢里典当的物品会出现在道具栏里，道具的标签下。跟罗伊说话的时候，你可以典当这些物品。

En: Yes, the Bird's Nest Roy, near the canal -- we've been there. If I'm not mistaken it should be open late.
Zh: “是的，罗伊的鸟巢，就在运河边——我们去过那里。如果我没弄错的话，那里应该开到很晚。”

En: Alice this is Firewalker. Re-connect me to the 41st.
Zh: “爱丽丝，我是火行者。请再帮我接41分局。”

En: Alice, please connect me to the 41st again.
Zh: “爱丽丝，请再帮我接一下41分局。”

En: This is Officer Alice DeMettrie, Precinct 57. How may I assist you? a voice replies in the radio.
Zh: “我是57分局的爱丽丝·德梅特里警官。请问你需要什么帮助？”无线电里传来一个声音。

En: Vapour emanates from the large engine on the back of the vehicle. It hasn't had time to cool off yet.
Zh: 蒸汽从汽車背后的大型发动机中散发出来。它还没来得及冷却呢。

En: Before you stands a motor carriage. The bodywork is covered in blue and white livery bearing the number 57.
Zh: 你面前是一台汽車。車身是蓝白相间的，上面印着数字57。

En: This must be the infernal machine that tore you from oblivion.
Zh: 这一定就是那台把你从湮灭中撕裂的地狱机器。

En: In the cabin you are welcomed by a set of steering levers, a radio-microphone on a hook, a pull-out toolbox under the seat, and the soft glow of the fuel pre-heater gauge.
Zh: 在車厢里，迎接你的是一组操纵杆，一台挂钩式话筒，座椅下的抽出式工具箱，还有燃油预热仪表的柔光。

En: This must be the infernal machine that tore you from oblivion -- the Coupris Kineema motor carriage.
Zh: 这一定就是那台把你从湮灭中撕裂的地狱机器——库普瑞斯锐影汽車。

En: Pull out the pull-out toolbox.
Zh: 拉开抽出式工具箱。
