En: Run your fingers over one of the steering levers.
Zh: 用手指抚过其中一个操纵杆。

En: Tap on the fuel pre-heater gauge.
Zh: 轻轻敲打燃油预热仪表。

En: Close the door. [Leave.]
Zh: 关上门。[离开。]

En: The white suede feels luxurious under the touch and the metal clutch handle so very familiar in your palm...
Zh: 白色小山羊皮那奢侈的触感，还有掌心的金属离合器手把，都让你感到异常熟悉……

En: Your fingers waste no time closing around the handle. Clutch disengaged. Release the handle -- clutch drops -- right foot yearns for the familiar touch of the accelerator pedal. You have synced with the machine's mechanical circulation.
Zh: 没有浪费片刻的时间，你的手指抓紧了把手。松开离合器。松开把手——离合器下陷——右脚向往着油门踏板那熟悉的触感。你已经跟这台机器的机械循环同步了。

En: Do I know how to operate this machine?
Zh: 我知道怎么操作这台机器吗？

En: Release the clutch handle.
Zh: 松开离合器手把。

En: How are you, my friend?
Zh: 你还好吗，我的朋友？

En: You feel an uninterrupted connection to the mechanics.
Zh: 你感觉自己和机械之间存在着不间断的联系。

En: Where have you been?
Zh: 你去哪了？

En: Wait, does it mean I know how to pilot it?
Zh: 等等，这是不是意味着我知道怎么驾驶它？

En: Release the clutch and squeeze it again.
Zh: 松开离合器，然后再紧踩一下。

En: After a while you realize silence is your only answer. Do what you will with it.
Zh: 过了一会儿，你意识到沉默就是你唯一的答案。你随便怎么处理吧。

En: The smell of freshly treated leather, the lack of dirt and dust on the dashboard, and a neat little brush in the cup-holder all seem to be whispering:
Zh: 新保养过的皮革味道，仪表盘上没有灰尘，杯座上放着一只干净的小刷子，似乎在轻声说道：

En: At the bottom of the sea...
Zh: 深深的海底……

En: Because it's lonely. Just like you. Seaweed and strange fish around it, limpets clinging to the carcass... The sounds of the streets above...
Zh: 因为这样很孤独。就像你一样。周围都是海草和奇形怪状的海鱼，帽贝纠缠在我的残骸上……上方传来街道的声响……

En: A metallic drawer slides out from under the seat and clicks into place. The toolbox is empty.
Zh: 一个金属抽屉从座椅底下滑了出来，咔哒一声停住了。工具箱是空的。

En: A metallic drawer slides out from under the seat and clicks into place. The tools inside are neatly organized.
Zh: 一个金属抽屉从座椅底下滑了出来，咔哒一声停住了。里面是整齐排放的工具。

En: Push in the pull-out toolbox.
Zh: 推入抽出式工具箱。

En: The pull-out toolbox slides back into its nest. Pre-heater gauge casts a warm glow on the steering levers and the radio on its hook.
Zh: 抽出式工具箱滑回它的巢穴。预热计量器在操纵杆和挂钩上的无线电上投下温暖的光晕。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: A scent of leather-work and heavy fuel oils washes over you.
Zh: 一股皮革和重燃油的味道朝你迎面扑来。

En: Open the door.
Zh: 打开门。

En: Of course it's only in your head -- of course it is -- but it almost feels as if the clutch handle is gently squeezing back.
Zh: 当然，这只是你脑海里的一种感觉——理应如此——不过你几乎感觉到离合器手把也在回握着你。

En: I'm good, cherished and cared for, in the hands of a tending owner.
Zh: 多亏了主人的悉心关照，我状态良好，能感觉到重视和关爱。

En: This is clearly feverish nonsense -- let's rationalize it away. Grab the radio, or tools, or do something normal.
Zh: 这明显就是极度兴奋下的胡言乱语——我们把它合理化一些。拿起无线电，或者工具，或者做点普通的事情。

En: The handle is pulled back. Somewhere deep inside the drivetrain the disk is mated to the flywheel again.
Zh: 手把被拉了回去。传动系统深处的某个地方，离合片再次跟飞轮匹配成双。

En: Take the rubber-handled chaincutters.
Zh: 拿走橡胶手柄的切链钳。

En: I'm ready to try dialing Speedreaks FM again.
Zh: “我准备好再次接通狂飙怪人.FM了。”

En: Alice, I need you to connect me to Speedfreaks FM.
Zh: “爱丽丝，请你帮我接通狂飙怪人.FM。”

En: Come again, officer?
Zh: “请再说一遍，警官？”

En: Speedfreaks FM. A radio station. It's for the case.
Zh: “狂飙怪人.FM。一个无线电台。这和案子有关。”

En: I said 'Speedfreaks FM'. Kim's a big fan.
Zh: “我说了‘狂飙怪人.FM’。金是这个电台的忠实粉丝。”

En: Really? he says, low and tired.
Zh: “真的？”他疲惫地低声说道。

En: The lieutenant sighs under his breath.
Zh: 警督克制地叹息一声。

En: Okay, officer. One moment.
Zh: “好吧，警官。请稍等。”

En: Right... One moment, officer.
Zh: “好的……稍等，警官。”

En: Excited, Kim?
Zh: “你激动吗，金？”

En: No.
Sorry, officer. Your radio personalities are unavailable, probably until tomorrow. Is there anything else I can assist you with?
Zh: “抱歉，警官。你的电台个人频道暂时不可用，或许明天再来试试。还有什么我能帮你的？”

En: I have *DJ Mesh* on the line. I'll connect you now.
Zh: “*DJ麦斯*目前在线。我会为你连线他。”

En: Yo! The word shoots out of the man's mouth like a bullet.
Zh: “哟！”这个词像子弹一样从这个男人的口中喷出。

En: The starting gun fires. And we're off!
Zh: 发令枪响了，我们出发！

En: Yo yo!
Zh: “哟哟！”

En: Yo yo yo! You got DJ Mesh here, my man. I got a few minutes before I gotta go back on air. What do you need?
Zh: “哟哟哟！你和DJ麦斯连线了，老兄。再过几分钟我就得回去打碟了。你有什么事吗？”

En: Hello, is this DJ Mesh?
Zh: “你好，是DJ麦斯吗？”

En: It is. I got a few minutes before I gotta go back on air. What can I do for you, cop-man?
Zh: “我是。再过几分钟我就得回去打碟了。你有事找我吗，条子老哥？”

En: The Lamb is calm before its slaughter. It does not know what will befall it.
Zh: “待宰的肥羊是平静的。它不知道前方等待它的命运如何。”

En: Uh... What? He laughs, confused. "That's the wildest shit I heard today. You are a cop, right?"
Zh: “呃……什么？”他迷惑地笑了起来。“这是我今天听到的最荒诞的事儿了。你是个条子，对吧？”

En: I'm so sorry to bother you. I just have a question.
Zh: “非常抱歉打扰你。我有一个小问题。”

En: Ain't a bother, brother. Anyway, I only got a few minutes before I gotta go back on air. What do you need?
Zh: “别担心，老兄。不过再过几分钟我就得回去打碟了。你有什么事吗？”

En: Yes. And I have a question.
Zh: “是的。我有个问题。”

En: You aren't the only one. Anyway, I only got a few minutes before I gotta go back on air. What can DJ Mesh tell the RCM?
Zh: “来问吧。不过再过几分钟我就得回去打碟了。DJ麦斯能为RCM做点什么？”

En: I'm just calling to confirm -- did you debut a new track from DJ Flacio a few days ago?
Zh: “我只想确认一下——前几天你是不是替DJ弗莱科发布了一首新曲？”

En: 'An Asshole Is A Mouth For Shit (And I'm Puking).'
Zh: “狗混蛋满嘴放屁（我要吐了）。”

En: That's the name of the song. Vulgarity is la belle du jour.
Zh: “就该叫这个名字。粗口才是精髓。”

En: Great title, man! Number one stuff.
Zh: “好名字，老兄！一流水平。”

En: I'm guessing that's the song.
Zh: “我想这就是那首歌。”

En: ... What?
An Asshole Is A Mouth For Shit (And I'm Puking)?
Zh: “狗混蛋满嘴放屁（我要吐了）。”

En: Okay... But what is the song called?
Zh: “好吧……你说这首歌叫什么来着？”

En: Are you insulting an officer of the Law, punk?
Zh: “你在侮辱执行法律的警官吗，朋克青年？”

En: What? No, I'm not telling you off, man. That's the name of the song! 'An Asshole Is A Mouth For Shit (And I'm Puking).'
Zh: “什么？不，我没在骂你，老兄。这首歌就叫这个名字。‘狗混蛋满嘴放屁（我要吐了）。’”

En: Yeah, that is the song. 'An Asshole Is A Mouth For Shit (And I'm Puking).'
Zh: “对，就是这首歌。‘狗混蛋满嘴放屁（我要吐了）。’”

En: An Asshole Is A Mouth For Shit (And I'm Puking)!
Zh: “狗混蛋满嘴放屁（我要吐了）！”

En: 'An Asshole Is A Mouth For Shit (And I'm Puking),' he says more slowly.
Zh: “狗混蛋满嘴放屁（我要吐了），”他放慢语速又说了一遍。

En: It ain't just a song! It's the shit-hot new single from DJ Flacio that's shredding up the sonic waves.
Zh: “这不仅仅是一首歌！这是DJ弗莱科捣碎声波的热腾腾的新单。”

En: Thank you very much! Yeah, it's the shit-hot new single from DJ Flacio. Really shredding up the sonic waves.
Zh: “谢谢你！没错，这就是DJ弗莱科热腾腾的新单。直击声波。”

En: Do people like it?
Zh: “大家喜欢它吗？”

En: They're lovin' it, man! The requests keep coming in for more.
Zh: “大家爱死它了，老兄！订单像雪片一样飞来。”

En: I really think it's the future of music, I do. Fuckin', like, stinkcore, or something. Shit's so nasty, those stinkwaves come up like soundwaves, you know what I mean?
Zh: “我真的相信这就是音乐的未来。操，臭核音乐之类的。臭气冲天，臭波像声波一样袅袅升起。你懂我的意思吧？”

En: Is that legal to play?
Zh: “唱这个合法吗？”

En: It's illegal not to play, man. When you got that kind of music in you, the universe demands you let it out, know what I mean?
Zh: “不唱才是违法，老兄。如果你也有了这样的灵感，整个宇宙都会逼你释放出来，明白我的意思吧？”

En: Plus, Flacio and I got it in our contracts that we could play our own tracks. So really, it would be illegal not to.
Zh: “再说，弗莱科和我在合同里写了，我们能放自己写的曲子。所以实在没有理由不唱。”

En: Okay. Let's move on. We talked to some kids who said they heard it in their heads. Do you know how that could be possible?
Zh: “好吧。我们继续。之前我们和几个孩子聊了聊，他们说在脑子里听到这首歌。你知道这有可能是什么因素造成的吗？”

En: He sighs. "Come on, man, I just told you we're cleared to play shit like that. Listen, it's not our problem if kids decide to tune into R-R-R-Revachol's hottest station."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“拜托，老兄，我刚刚还在和你说我们能放这类曲子。听着，如果孩子们决定听瑞-瑞-瑞-瑞瓦肖最火爆的电台，那可不是我们的问题。”

En: He sighs. "Is that what this is about? Listen, it's not our problem if kids decide to tune into R-R-R-Revachol's hottest station."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“你就想问我这个？听着，如果孩子们决定听瑞-瑞-瑞-瑞瓦肖最火爆的电台，那可不是我们的问题。”

En: It's not that they played it on a radio. They heard it in their heads. Do you know how that's possible?
Zh: “他们不是在电台上听的。他们在脑子里听到。你知道这是怎么回事吗？”

En: I don't understand. Are you asking if we can, like, play our station into people's brains?
Zh: “我没听懂。你是想问我们能不能，呃，在人们的脑子里播放我们的电台？”

En: This is preposterous. Neither sound nor scent work anything as he's describing. What does he even *mean*?
Zh: 这太荒唐了。不管是声音还是气味都不是这样传播的。他到底在*说*什么？

En: Sure, man. I'm young, cool, and with it.
Zh: “当然，老兄。我又年轻又酷，你说的我一清二楚。”

En: I don't have any idea what you mean.
Zh: “我完全不明白你是什么意思。”

En: Very cool, man, I'm dead serious when I say, Flacio is going to make it big in this world.
Zh: “非常酷，老兄，我可是非常认真地相信弗莱科会让这个题材发扬光大的！”

En: Doesn't matter. Listen, I'm dead serious when I say, Flacio is going to make it big in this world.
Zh: “这不要紧。听着，我可是非常认真地相信弗莱科会让这个题材发扬光大的！”

En: Sure! Forced radio. Can't tune out. You'd make a killing on ads!
Zh: “那当然！不能换台的强制收听电台。你们能靠广告费大赚特赚！”

En: Yeah. Like a new way to equalize people, getting them all on the same station. A great tool for Communism.
Zh: “不错，一种让所有人平等的新方式，让他们全都听同一个频道。它会成为建设康米主义的好帮手。”

En: Exactly like that. Because I think you're an enemy of traditional values forcing your filth on the innocent.
Zh: “就是那样。你是传统价值观的敌人，把你离经叛道的思想强加给无辜的人。”

En: Yeah, like that. Maybe it was part of your civic duty, you were just testing it out for the government.
Zh: “差不多这样。这也许是你应尽的公民义务之一，你只是在给政府测试这项功能。”

En: Speedfreaks FM is elective listening, man. We don't want to force the people to adopt our thinking. That's fascist bullshit.
Zh: “狂飙怪人.FM不是强制收听的频道，老兄。我们没想强迫人们接受我们的想法。那是法西斯式的狗屎观念。”

En: No, I was just asking -- and dreaming up a bold new money making scheme.
Zh: “不，我只是在询问——想象一种狂野的新型盈利方案。”

En: Right, well -- no, we don't force radio into peoples heads. Don't know how you'd even do that, man.
Zh: “好吧，但是——不，我们没有把广播强行塞进人们的脑袋里。我甚至根本不知道那要怎么做，老兄。”

En: Was there anything else you need to know?
Zh: “你还有什么想知道的？”

En: No, that wasn't my fascist line, that was my ultraliberal one. I can do the fascist one if you want.
Zh: “不，这不是法西斯台词，这是我的极端自由主义台词。如果你想听的话，我也可以来一句法西斯台词。”

En: You're a fucking wild man. Sure, let's hear it.
Zh: “你他妈的也太怪了。行吧，那你说来听听。”

En: Okay, the fascist thing was me calling you an enemy of the state for force-pumping filth into children, thus corrupting traditional values.
Zh: “好的，法西斯台词是这样的，我会骂你是国家的敌人，因为你把离经叛道的思想强加给儿童，因此腐蚀传统价值观。”

En: The radio host doesn't try to contain his laughter. "Oh, man, I love it! You're great. No, we didn't do anything like that. Wouldn't know how to -- we just played it on air like normal people."
Zh: 电台主持人肆无忌惮地狂笑起来。“哦，老哥，我爱死这句话了！你可真棒。不，我们不做这种事情。也不知道要怎么做——我们只是很正常地广播这些曲子而已。”

En: Right on, brother. We gotta make sure everybody tunes into this sonic revolution!
Zh: “好极了，兄弟。我们要让所有人都听听这场声音的革命！”

En: But we didn't, like, force it into people's brains. Got no idea how to do that, short of them just tuning in on their favourite radio receiving device.
Zh: “但我们没有，呃，把它强塞进人们的脑子里。我也不知道那要怎么做。难道不是人们自己用收音机调到他们最喜欢的电台吗。”

En: Okay. I'd recommend looking into it, though. For the revolution.
Zh: “好吧。但我推荐你试试看。为了革命。”

En: I understand. Thank you for clearing that up.
Zh: “我明白了。谢谢你的澄清。”

En: Revolution-brain-broadcast. Got it. Yeah, we'll look into it.
Zh: “革命——大脑——广播。懂了，我们会试试看的。”

En: My pleasure, comrade cop. Is there anything else you needed?
Zh: “我的荣幸，条子同志。你还需要什么吗？”

En: Was there anything else you wanted to ask?
Zh: “你还有什么想问的？”

En: We ain't testing anything out for the Man, unless it's how many bricks you need to break all of his windows.
Zh: “我们不会给他测试任何东西，除非是多少块砖头能砸烂他家所有的玻璃。”

En: That wasn't a threat, by the way. Just a figure of speech, I'm a spoken word guy. Now, is there anything else you needed?
Zh: “顺带一提，这不是威胁，只是这么一个说法，我是个用词丰富的人。那么，你还需要什么吗？”

En: Shit keeps getting weirder with you, man. Fucking unbelievable.
Zh: “你越来越诡异了，老哥。简直不可理喻。”

En: No, we didn't force radio into people's heads. The fuck is it with you fascists and conspiracy theories?
Zh: “不，我们没有把电台强塞进人们的脑子里。你们这些法西斯和阴谋论爱好者脑子有问题吗？”

En: Tell him the truth, bröther. Even if he isn't *hard* enough to hear it.
Zh: 告诉他真相，孩子。即使他没有*坚强*到能够听完这件事。

En: It's not a conspiracy theory, it's logic. The lesser races, still fresh and DRIPPING from the proto-slime, can't beat us outright -- so they resort to impossibly devious schemes.
Zh: “这不是阴谋论，这是基本逻辑。那些低等的种族，新鲜热乎着从原初的黏液里滑落出来，他们不能立刻正面击败我们——所以就采取了无比邪恶的计划。”

En: The lieutenant stares at you, his jaw clenched. A vein strains on his temple.
Zh: 警督瞪着你，咬紧牙关。他的太阳穴上突起一根青筋。

En: You know, it'd be one thing if you were just crazy. Like, I could forgive you then. I know lots of crazy people.
Zh: “呃，可能你确实在疯言疯语，这样的话我可以原谅你。我认识很多疯子。”

En: But you're just fucking hateful. And I got no time for that shit. Anything else you need, or can I hang up?
Zh: “但可真他妈讨厌。我没时间听你放屁。你还需要什么吗，还是说我能挂电话了？”

En: I'm just thinking of the children.
Zh: “我只是为了孩子们着想。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Right. Is there anything else you need, or can I hang up the fucking phone now?
Zh: “好吧。你还需要什么吗，还是说我他妈的终于能挂电话了？”

En: Just one thing -- give a big shoutout to my man, Lieutenant Kim Kitsuragi. He's a huge fan of the station.
Zh: “还有一件事——给我的好兄弟，金·曷城警督一点掌声。他很喜欢你们的电台。”

En: Just one more thing -- give a shoutout Lieutenant Kim Kitsuragi. He *coincidentally* has you saved to his radio.
Zh: “还有一件事——给金·曷城警督一点掌声。他*阴差阳错*地收藏了你的电台。”

En: No, that was it. Thank you for your time.
Zh: “我问完了。谢谢。”

En: Huh? The sound bubbles out of him, even to his own surprise.
Zh: “哈？”他发出一声疑问，好像把他自己也吓到了。

En: Okay, cop-man! Can do, will do!
Zh: “好吧，条子老兄！我可以，我会做！”

En: DJ Mesh out.
Zh: “DJ麦斯下线。”

En: Not going to happen, you fascist prick.
Zh: “想都别想，你这个法西斯混蛋。”

En: Not going to happen, you fascist prick. Tell your friend to not listen to us any more.
Zh: “想都别想，你这个法西斯混蛋。让你的朋友永远别再听我们的音乐。”

En: The lieutenant's breath catches in his chest.
Zh: 警督的呼吸停滞了一拍。

En: No parting words; only the dull cry of the dial tone.
Zh: 没有告别，只有单调沉闷的拨号音。

En: Nah, man, thank you. This has been an absolute pleasure.
Zh: “没事，老兄，谢谢你。和你聊天很愉快。”

En: Later, cop-man.
Zh: “回见，条子老兄。”

En: You crossed a line. This will not be forgotten.
Zh: 你越过了底线。这是不可原谅的错误。

En: The party has terminated the call. Is there anything else I can do for you, officer?
Zh: “对方终止了连线。我还能为你做什么，警官？”

En: You should check the radio again!
Zh: 你应该再试试无线电！

En: You should check the radio!
Zh: 你应该试试无线电！

En: Look at that! His eyes dilate. That's joy, my man. You wouldn't be able to tell, but his glasses *just* magnify it.
Zh: 瞧瞧！他的瞳孔扩大了。他一定是太高兴了，我的老兄。要不是他的眼镜这么*恰好*地强调了这一点，你还看不出来呢。

En: Tune Kim's radio into Speedfreaks FM.
Zh: 把金的无线电调到狂飙怪人.FM。

En: Press the button labelled "Saved" and turn on Speedfreaks FM.
Zh: 按下标注“收藏”的按钮并打开狂飙怪人.FM。

En: It requires no adjustment. The radio is already set to Speedfreaks FM.
Zh: 不用换频道。无线电已经处在狂飙怪人.FM。

En: After a moment of fiddling with the radio, you push the glowing SAVED button. And on comes a familiar voice: "...SPOKE TO THE W-W-WILDEST COP IN REVACHOL."
Zh: 摆弄了一会儿无线电之后，你按下发光的“收藏”按钮。\n无线电传来一道熟悉的声音：“……和瑞瓦肖最狂-狂-狂-狂野的条子讲了话。”

En: ...SPOKE TO THE W-W-WILDEST COP IN REVACHOL.
Zh: “……和瑞瓦肖最狂-狂-狂-狂野的条子讲了话。”

En: After a moment of fiddling with the radio, you push the glowing SAVED button. And on comes a familiar voice: "FASCIST PIGS, I SWEAR. YOU SHOULD ALL..." The assault gives way to burning quiet.
Zh: 摆弄了一会儿无线电之后，你按下发光的“收藏”按钮。\n无线电传来一道熟悉的声音：“法西斯肥猪，我发誓。你们都该……”辱骂逐渐被灼人的寂静取代。

En: FASCIST PIGS, I SWEAR. YOU SHOULD ALL... The assault gives way to burning quiet.
Zh: “法西斯肥猪，我发誓。你们都该……”辱骂逐渐被灼人的寂静取代。

En: After a moment of fiddling with the radio, you push the glowing SAVED button. And on comes a familiar voice: "...SPOKE TO THE W-W-WILDEST COP IN REVACHOL. HE ASKED US TO GIVE HIS BUDDY COP, LONG TIME LISTENER KIM KITSURAGI, A BIG SHOUTOUT. KIM, WE LOVE YOU."
Zh: 摆弄了一会儿无线电之后，你按下发光的“收藏”按钮。\n无线电传来一道熟悉的声音：“……和瑞瓦肖最狂-狂-狂-狂野的条子讲了话。他让我们给他的条子伙伴，我们的忠实听众金·曷城一个大大的欢迎。金，我们爱你。”

En: ...SPOKE TO THE W-W-WILDEST COP IN REVACHOL. HE ASKED US TO GIVE HIS BUDDY COP, LONG TIME LISTENER KIM KITSURAGI, A BIG SHOUTOUT. KIM, WE LOVE YOU.
Zh: “……和瑞瓦肖最狂-狂-狂-狂野的条子讲了话。他让我们给他的条子伙伴，我们的忠实听众金·曷城一个大大的欢迎。金，我们爱你。”

En: After a moment of fiddling with the radio, you push the glowing SAVED button. And on comes a familiar voice: "FASCIST PIGS, I SWEAR. YOU AND KIM KITSURAGI SHOULD ALL..." The assault gives way to burning quiet.
Zh: 摆弄了一会儿无线电之后，你按下发光的“收藏”按钮。\n无线电传来一道熟悉的声音：“法西斯肥猪，我发誓。你和金·曷城都该……”辱骂逐渐被灼人的寂静取代。

En: FASCIST PIGS, I SWEAR. YOU AND KIM KITSURAGI SHOULD ALL... The assault gives way to burning quiet.
Zh: “法西斯肥猪，我发誓。你和金·曷城都该……”辱骂逐渐被灼人的寂静取代。

En: Another equally furious voice seizes on the silence. "HERE'S SOME MUSIC."
Zh: 另一道同样暴怒的声音打破了沉默。“现在来放点音乐。”

En: The lieutenant snaps off the radio, turning it back to PRIMELINE. He stares at you silently.
Zh: 警督啪地关掉无线电，调回总机频道。他无声地凝视着你。

En: An audio-assault descends full force on you. Sonic daggers fly forth from the Kineema's speakers. The soundwaves would slice you if they could.
Zh: 声音的攻势向你全力冲来。声波如同匕首般从锐影的扬声器里射出，像要将你撕碎。

En: Turn the radio back to PRIMELINE.
Zh: 把电台调回总机频道。

En: KEEP IT FREAKY, another voice cuts in, "LIKE DJ MESH, DJ FLACIO, AND YOUR W-W-W-WILD FRIEND. THIS ONE'S FOR YOU!"
Zh: “燥起来，”另一道声音插进来：“就像DJ麦斯和DJ弗莱科，还有你的狂-狂-狂-狂野朋友。这首曲子献给你！”

En: We aren't discussing this. After a few bars, the lieutenant turns the radio back to PRIMELINE.
Zh: 听了一会儿之后，警督把电台调回总机频道。“我不会跟你讨论这个的。”

En: If, when a motor-carriage's engine was ignited, it could drive by its own accord off to a high mountain and there write a song -- perhaps it'd be something like what you're hearing now.
Zh: 当一辆車的引擎被点燃，如果它能够自己飞跃群山，谱写乐曲——或许这就会是你现在所听到的音乐。

En: What a reserve of willpower. It's taking all he's got to not smile.
Zh: 真是意志坚定。他一定竭尽全力忍住笑意。

En: Elsewhere in Revachol, the lieutenant has also just turned on the radio. In his privacy, he doesn't even try to stop himself from smiling.
Zh: 在瑞瓦肖的别处，警督或许会打开无线电。独自一人的时候，他不再克制自己的微笑。

En: The lieutenant snaps off the radio, turning it back to PRIMELINE. He stares at you silently.
Zh: 警督啪地关掉无线电，调回总机频道。他无声地凝视着你。

En: Another equally furious voice seizes on the silence. "HERE'S SOME MUSIC."
Zh: 另一道同样暴怒的声音打破了沉默。“现在来放点音乐。”

En: DIDN'T EVEN GET HIS NAME. BUT WHOEVER THAT MYSTERY COP WAS, WE SALUTE HIM.
Zh: “我们甚至不知道他的名字。但是不管那位神秘的条子是谁，我们向他致敬。”

En: An audio-assault descends full force on you. Sonic daggers fly forth from the Kineema's speakers. The soundwaves would slice you if they could.
Zh: 声音的攻势向你全力冲来。声波如同匕首般从锐影的扬声器里射出，像要将你撕碎。

En: I HOPE HE'S R-R-R-ROCKING WITH US A LONG TIME, another voice cuts in. "THE WORLD NEEDS MORE FREAKS. THIS ONE'S FOR YOU, MYSTERY COP!"
Zh: “我希望他和我们一起永远摇-摇-摇-摇滚下去。”另一道声音插进来：“世界需要更多的怪人。这首歌献给你，神秘的条子！”

En: That quickfire patter -- is this the voice the twins heard?
Zh: 那连续的哒哒哒——是那对双胞胎听到的声音吗？

En: That's DJ Mesh! That was Speedfreaks FM!
Zh: “这里是DJ麦斯！我们是狂飙怪人.FM！”

En: Isn't that the station we have to call? Speedfreaks FM? Why's it saved as 'favourite'?
Zh: “这就是我们要联系的电台对吧？狂飙怪人.FM？为什么它被设置为‘最爱’？”

En: The lieutenant is resolutely silent. Staring at the console.
Zh: 警督沉默不语，盯着控制面板。

En: A look of betrayal. The secret has been revealed!
Zh: 他被出卖了。他的秘密被揭开了！

En: Aw, man! You should have told me you liked them! I could have asked for a shoutout.
Zh: “喔，老兄！你至少该告诉我你喜欢他们！我本可以让他们欢迎你一番。”

En: It's okay to like what you like, Kim.
Zh: “你想喜欢什么就喜欢什么，金。”

En: You lying to me right now, Kim?
Zh: “你在对我撒谎吗，金？”

En: I don't listen to them, the lieutenant says sharply.
Zh: “我不听他们的音乐。”警督尖锐地说道。

En: I don't like it. He shrugs very slightly. "I don't know how that channel got marked."
Zh: “我不喜欢他们。”他轻微地耸了耸肩：“我也不知道为什么那个频道被标注了。”

En: Excuse me?
Zh: “什么？”

En: He's too stiff. Like a grunt during a military inspection, hoping their commanding officer overlooks them.
Zh: 他太僵硬了。像个新兵蛋子在接受军事检阅，期望他们的长官别来找他麻烦。

En: Why the fuck can't he just admit it? Don't let this go. Hound him until he admits it.
Zh: 他妈的为什么他就不肯承认？别放过他。逼他承认。

En: Just leave it alone. He clearly doesn't want to share this with you.
Zh: 别烦他了。他显然不想和你分享这个爱好。

En: I said, are you fucking lying to me?
Zh: “我说，你他妈的在对我撒谎吗？”

En: Nothing. Never mind.
Zh: “没什么，别在意。”

En: The lieutenant raises an eyebrow. The ocean wind blows, bringing in the smell of salt.
Zh: 警督挑起一边的眉毛。海风呼啸，吹来海盐的腥气。

En: Mhm. The lieutenant frowns at you.
Zh: “嗯……”他对你皱眉。

En: That eyebrow! He's using his Authority against you... you... can't resist...
Zh: 眉毛！他又在对你使用他的争强好胜了……你……你无法抵抗……

En: This has gone far enough. You're going to show dignity toward your fellow officer and *apologize*.
Zh: 够了。你得给自己的伙伴一点体面，向他*道歉*。

En: I... gah... (Grab your head.) "I'm... sorry, Kim."
Zh: “我……呃……”（挠挠头）“我很抱歉……金。”

En: He remains silent.
Zh: 他一言不发。

En: C'mon, man! You can tell me!
Zh: “别这样，老兄！你可以告诉我的！”

En: Wait! Can you send me any Speedfreaks merch?
Zh: “等等！你能送我一点狂飙怪人的周边吗？”

En: I can understand why you'd ask, our shit's hot. Think we gave the last of it away at our last live show, but -- what's your address anyway?
Zh: “我理解你的愿望，我们的音乐酷毙了。我记得我们在上次演唱会上把最后一点周边送完了，不过——你的地址是什么来着？”

En: Uh, I'm gonna need better than 'streetless shack' if you can get that for me.
Zh: “呃，如果你能送我周边的话，我的地址恐怕得比‘不知名棚屋’好点儿。”

En: Merchandise. Ask for Speedfreaks FM merchandise.
Zh: 周边。问他要狂飙怪人.FM的周边。

En: Send it to the Whirling-in-Rags, in Martinaise.
Zh: “请寄到马丁内斯的褴褛飞旋。”

En: I'm staying in a shack by the coast. No one knows the street name, they just call it *Illisible*.
Zh: “我现在住在海岸边的一座棚屋里。没人知道那条街叫什么名字，他们都管它叫‘不清楚’。”

En: I'm staying in a shack by the coast, in a small fishing village near Martinaise.
Zh: “我现在住在马丁内斯附近的渔村，在海岸边的一座棚屋里。”

En: I don't really know my address...
Zh: “我其实不知道我的地址……”

En: Thank you!
Zh: “谢谢！”

En: Wait! Don't you need to know my name?
Zh: “等等！你难道不需要我的名字吗？”

En: I don't want to know, man. I like leaving it to the imagination. Now, anything else?
Zh: “我不想知道，老兄。我喜欢留出一点想象空间。还有别的事吗？”

En: Hey, don't thank me yet! Again, I don't think we got anything left, but, well... Anything else?
Zh: “嘿，先别急着谢我！我觉得我们应该没有存货了，但是，好吧……还有别的事吗？”

En: That's, uh... Well, have you got *any* address I can send shit to, man?
Zh: “这，呃……好吧，你就真的找不到*任何*一个地址让我送点东西吗，老兄？”

En: That I can do. Whirling-in-Rags, Martinaise. Got it.
Zh: “这个没问题。马丁内斯的褴褛飞旋，我记住了。”

En: Even if a package arrives, how will it get to you if it hasn't got your name on it?
Zh: 即使真的寄来了包裹，如果上面没有你的名字，你要怎么收货？

En: The lights unfold with a little click, casting electrical light onto the ground before the vehicle.
Zh: 伴随着一阵清脆的咔哒声，車灯打开了，灯光映照在汽車前面的地面上。

En: Yes, hello! A female voice greets you through static -- it sounds like she's million miles away from here.
Zh: “喂，你好！”一个女性的声音通过电流向你传来问候——听起来她距离这里有万里之遥。

En: Of course. What is her number, officer?
Zh: “当然。她的电话号码是多少，警官？”

En: Kim, didn't Garte give you Sylvie Malaìika's number?
Zh: “金，加尔特有给你塞尔维·玛莱卡的号码吗？”

En: Kim, didn't Garte give you Sylvie's number?
Zh: “金，加尔特有没有把塞尔维的号码告诉你？”

En: Come in dispatch, come in dispatch!
Zh: “紧急情况，紧急情况！”

En: Come in, Delta-Ten, this is Firewalker, copy.
Zh: “呼叫，德尔塔10号，我是火行者，收到请回答。”

En: Actually, let's not do that right now. (Back.)
Zh: “其实吧，还是先不要了。”（返回。）

En: Officer? You hear phones ringing and people talking in the background.
Zh: “警官？”你听见背景里传来电话铃声和人们的交谈声。

En: I'm done with the radio for now. (End call.)
Zh: “我没什么要问的了。”（结束呼叫。）

En: This is Officer Alice DeMettrie, Precinct 57. How may I assist you? You could swear she was friendlier with the lieutenant.
Zh: “我是57分局的爱丽丝·德梅特里警官。请问你需要什么帮助？”你可以发誓，她对警督的态度更友好一些。

En: Fifty-seventh, over and out. Her voice disappears into the void.
Zh: “57分局通话完毕。”她的声音消失在虚无中。

En: Lieutenant Kitsuragi slowly begins to tap a little rhythm with his right foot. Quite a lot of time has passed.
Zh: 曷城警督用右脚慢慢地、有节奏地拍打着。很长一段时间过去了。

En: Wait patiently.
Zh: 耐心等待。

En: Start slapping a marching rhythm on your thighs.
Zh: 开始在大腿上拍打出行军的节奏。

En: Yes, hold on... the lieutenant takes a look at his notes. "Her number is 005 1944 298."
Zh: “是的，等一下……”警督看了一眼笔记。“她的号码是0051944298。”

En: Received. Hold on, officer.
Zh: “收到。请稍等，警官。”

En: I need you to connect me to a civilian -- a Sylvie. She may have reported a murder.
Zh: “我需要你帮我联系一位市民——她叫塞尔维。她可能报告过一起凶杀案。”

En: The frequency tableau lights up and a green button labelled PRIMELINE glows like a feline eye... and then you hear something.
Zh: 频率面板亮了起来，一个标着“总机”的绿色按钮像猫眼一样发着光……然后你听到了一些动静。

En: Pick up the radio.
Zh: 拿起无线电。

En: This is Precinct 57! Hello, lieutenant, how may I assist you?
Zh: “这里是57分局！喂，警督，有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Hello, Alice, please assist our colleague from the 41st Precinct here. I'm putting him on.
Zh: “你好，爱丽丝，请协助一下我们41分局的同事，我把话筒交给他了。”

En: Hi, Alice, this is the officer from the 41st Precinct speaking. Nice to meet you.
Zh: “你好，爱丽丝，我是41分局的警官。很高兴见到你。”

En: Under the green PRIMELINE a yellow SAVED button catches your eye... You wonder what the lieutenant's default radio station is?
Zh: 在绿色总机按钮下方，有一个黄色的收藏按钮吸引了你的注意……不知道金的默认电台是什么？

En: Change of plans, Alice -- connect me to *you*.
Zh: “改变计划，艾丽丝——帮我联系*你*。”

En: What do you mean, officer? I'm not Sylvie.
Zh: “你是什么意思，警官？我不是塞尔维。”

En: I don't know, Alice... just connect me.
Zh: “我不知道，艾丽丝……只要联系我就行。”

En: Sir, you're already connected to the 57th Precinct. Her voice is sharp.
Zh: “先生，你已经连到57分局了。”她的声音很尖锐。

En: Ignore Alice and press the button labelled "Saved".
Zh: 无视爱丽丝，按下“收藏”标签的按钮。

En: ...AS ALWAYS IT'S DJS MESH AND FLACIO AND YOU'RE LISTENING TO S-S-S-SSS-SSSPEE-EED FREAKS FM, BRINGING YOU THE HOTTEST, THE NASTIEST, THE MOST VULGAR...
Zh: “……一如既往，这里是DJ麦斯和弗莱科，你正在收听的是狂飙怪人.FM，为你带来最热门，最肮脏，最通俗的……”

En: Right away the lieutenant reaches into the cabin and turns off the radio. He's not looking at you as he says:
Zh: 警督立即探进車厢，关掉了电台。他没有看着你，说道：

En: Someone must have been messing with the radio, or maybe it picked up a random frequency... You wanted the primeline, right?
Zh: “肯定是有人瞎摆弄过无线电，要不就是它随便选了一个频道……你想连总机，对吧？”

En: Sorry about it, I wanted to contact the dispatch. (Pick up the radio and turn it back to PRIMELINE.)
Zh: “抱歉，我想连总台。”（拿起无线电，调回总台。）

En: *Speedfreaks FM*, huh? (Look him in the eye.)
Zh: “*狂飙怪人.FM*，是吗？”（看着他的眼睛。）

En: Oh, is that what it was called? He's trying hard to act surprised.
Zh: “哦，原来叫这个名字吗？”他努力想要表现地吃惊一些。

En:
...AND DE FACTO THE FASTEST MUSIC IN ALL OF R-R-REVACHOL R-ROCK CITY. ALL YOU HOOLIGANS, DOWNTOWN DRIFTERS, SIDEWAYS SALLIES AND POWERSLIDING PIERRES...	“……还有整个摇滚城市瑞瓦肖城最快节奏的音乐。你们这群小流氓，闹市流浪汉，娘娘腔，还有漂移狂魔……”
0x0100004C0000212E	Dialogue Text	PLAZA / COUPRIS KINEEMA	Volition	This radio station is even worse than Cuno -- it feels like someone defecating right into your ear.	这个电台甚至比坤诺还要搞糟——感觉就像有人在你的耳朵里大便。
0x0100004C0000213B	Dialogue Text	PLAZA / COUPRIS KINEEMA	Volition	This must be the most annoying thing you've ever heard -- it feels like someone defecating right into your ear.	这绝对是你听过的最烦人的东西——感觉就像有人在你的耳朵里大便。
0x0100004C00002141	Dialogue Text	PLAZA / COUPRIS KINEEMA	DJ Mesh	... GET INTO GEAR, DROP THE MOTHERFUCKING CLUTCH AND LET THE P-P-PPP-PPPPOWER TAKE YOU ON A JOURNEY OF A LIFETIME!"
Zh: “……动起来吧，放开该死的离合器，让马力带领你踏上终生难忘的旅程！”

En: ... AND NOW FOR SOME MUSIC! another voice screams into the microphone. Rhythmic rock music fills the cabin, as the back-to-back DJs mix it with sounds from car crashes and illegal speeding competitions.
Zh: “……现在，音乐燥起来！”麦克风里又传出一阵尖叫声。动感的摇滚乐充满了整个車厢，紧接着，DJ又混入了撞車和非法飙車比赛的声音。

En: This is art... *underground* art... a *scene*.
Zh: 这是艺术……*地下*艺术……一副*景象*。

En: This is Precinct 57, the operator greets you through the static. "How may I assist you?"
Zh: “这里是57分局，”电流里传来话务员的问候声。“有什么需要帮助的吗？”

En: Pick up the radio again.
Zh: 再次拿起无线电。

En: Right, you don't want to get into this, no problem.
Zh: “好吧，你不想聊这个，没问题的。”

En: Nothing to *get into* really, but sure, let's focus on the important things. He pats the motor carriage.
Zh: “真没什么好*聊*的，不过当然了，我们还是集中在重要的事情上吧。”他拍了拍汽車。

En: Go for it -- turn the radio back on while it's still on the Speedfreaks FM!
Zh: 来吧——乘着无线电还调在狂飙怪人.FM的时候，再把它打开！

En: ...SSSSOUPED UP MOTOR CARRIAGE FOR ONE BAD BAD MOMMA'S BOY, F-F-F-FFFOR THE H-H-HEAVY OF F-F-F-FFFOOT AND FREAKY IN...
Zh: “……给你的汽車增强马力吧，妈妈的坏小子们，把車飙起来，狂欢……”

En: God-damn-it! The lieutenant moves quick as a viper as he switches off the radio and sets it on primeline. Then he turns to you:
Zh: “该死！”警督像毒蛇一般飞快地关掉无线电，转到了总机。然后他转向你：

En: Look, it helps me to stay alert on long nights, okay? It's a method. I'm not some kind of *speed-freak* or... He shakes his head, furiously staring at his feet.
Zh: “听着，它能让我在漫长的夜晚中保持警惕，好吗？这是一种方法。我不是什么*狂飙怪人*，也不是什么……”他摇摇头，愤怒地盯着自己的双脚。

En: What about *heavy of foot*?
Zh: “那*飙車*是什么意思？”

En: Sure, you don't have to explain yourself. I'll just forget about it.
Zh: “当然了，你不用解释的。我会忘掉的。”

En: Thank you. His eyes glide over the plaza, making sure that no one heard the sudden blast from the radio, before he turns back to the cabin.
Zh: “谢谢。”他的眼睛掠过广场，确保没人听见无线电里震耳欲聋的声音，接着转身回到了車厢。

En: You would be too if you had this motor carriage, he snaps half-seriously.
Zh: “如果你有这台車的话，你也会这么做的，”他半真半假的厉声说道。

En: Take the hand-cranked flashlight.
Zh: 拿走手摇手电筒。

En: I have something here we could sell. Look in the back, in the suspect transport enclosure.
Zh: “我这里有些可以拿去卖的东西。看看背后，在嫌疑人押送厢里。”

En: Transport enclosure? Regular people just call it *the cage*.
Zh: 押送厢？普通人一般都叫它*笼子*。

En: The cage at the back of the motor carriage looks rather uncomfortable. Four shiny hubcaps are stacked against the seat. Their silver edges sparkle in the dark.
Zh: 汽車背后的笼子看起来很不舒服。四个闪亮的轮圈堆放在座椅上。它们银色的边缘在黑暗中闪闪发光。

En: I confiscated these four a little while back. We can take them to the pawnshop down by the Martinaise Canal.
Zh: “不久前我没收了这四个玩意。可以拿去马丁内斯运河旁边的当铺里问问。”

En: Yes, there's one 100 metres south of here, I think it's called Roy's Nest or something. If I'm not mistaken it should be open late.
Zh: “是的，离这里往南大概100米的地方，我想名字叫罗伊的安乐窝或者什么的。如果我没记错的话，它应该开得挺晚的。”

En: Those are some fancy words. Most people would just call it *the cage*.
Zh: 这不过是些花里胡哨的词汇。大部分人都会叫它*笼子*。

En: I have something in the back we could sell so you can pay for your room. Open the suspect transport enclosure.
Zh: “車后面有些东西，可以拿出卖了给你付房费。去把嫌疑人押送厢打开。”

En: You mean the cage, right?
Zh: “你是说笼子，对吗？”

En: Yes. He frowns. "I've never liked that crude name. It doesn't do much to improve the RCM's reputation."
Zh: “是的。”他皱起眉头。“我从来都不喜欢这个野蛮的名字。这对提升RCM的名望没多大帮助。”

En: Open the door.
Zh: 打开门。

En: What *are* those things?
Zh: “那些东西*是*什么？”

En: They're spinner hubcaps -- frivolous things you put on your wheels. When the wheels come to a stop, he makes a spinning motion with his hands, "the caps keep on spinning. There's no real use for them, it's just for vanity."
Zh: “这是轮圈——装在轮子上的无聊玩意。每当車轮停下来的时候，”他用手做了一个旋转的动作，“罩壳会继续旋转。本身真没什么实际用途，只是为了满足虚荣心。”

En: Thanks. I appreciate your help. (Take the spinners.)
Zh: “谢谢。我很感激你的帮助。”（拿走轮圈。）

En: The lieutenant nods as you take the spinners.
Zh: 警督看着你拿走轮圈，点了点头。

En: You said something about a pawnshop?
Zh: “你好像提到了一家当铺？”

En: Now, was there anything else you wanted to ask?
Zh: “现在，你还有什么想问的吗？”

En: Could you connect me to the 41st Precinct? I have something I need to report.
Zh: “你能帮我接41分局吗？我有些事需要报告。”

En: Have you heard back from the ICP about the serial number?
Zh: “ICP有没有回复你序列号的事？”

En: Could you run a serial number from a pair of armoured boots for me?
Zh: “你能帮我查一双盔甲靴的序列号吗？”

En: Did you find out more about the owner of the armoured boots?
Zh: “你有没有查到盔甲靴主人的更多信息？”

En: In the cabin you see a set of steering levers, a radio on a hook, a pull-out toolbox and the soft glow of the fuel pre-heater gauge.
Zh: 在車厢里，你看见一组操纵杆，一台挂钩式话筒，座椅下的抽出式工具箱，还有燃油预热仪表的柔光。

En: Yes, hello? Who is this? There's a pause. "My records show this channel is used by Lieutenant Kim Kitsuragi."
Zh: “喂？请问你是谁？”短暂的停顿。“我的记录显示这个频道是曷城警督使用的。”

En: Who is this? There's a pause. "My records show this channel is used by Lieutenant Kim Kitsuragi."
Zh: “你是谁？”短暂的停顿。“我的记录显示这个频道是金·曷城警督使用的。”

En: Yes, I know Kim! He's my partner.
Zh: “是的，我知道金！他是我的搭档。”

En: This is... Harry. I guess. Lieutenant Kitsuragi is my partner on a case.
Zh: “我是……哈里。我猜是吧。我跟曷城警督正在合作调查一起案件。”

En: Oh. Do you have permission to call from the lieutenant's vehicle? Her voice doesn't sound very friendly. "Where is he?"
Zh: “哦。你拿到使用警督汽車内部无线电的许可了吗？”她的声音听起来不是很友好。“他在哪？”

En: Lieutenant Harrier Du Bois, reporting in. I'm on a case with Lieutenant Kitsuragi.
Zh: “哈里尔·杜博阿警督报告。我跟曷城警督正在调查一起案件。”

En: Uh, the name's Mr. Costeau. Raphaël to friends. Kim's my friend.
Zh: “呃，我叫库斯托先生。朋友们都叫我拉斐尔。金就是我朋友。”

En: I prefer to be called Tequila Sunset. Kim's my partner. We're solving a case.
Zh: “我更喜欢别人叫我龙舌兰日落。金是我的搭档，我们正在调查一起案件。”

En: From the 41st Precinct. Where is Lieutenant Kitsuragi?
Zh: “是41分局的吧。曷城警督在哪？”

En: Mr. Harry, do you have permission to call from the lieutenant's vehicle? Where is he?
Zh: “哈里先生，你拿到使用警督汽車内部无线电的许可了吗？他在哪？”

En: Right... Mr. Sunset. Her voice does not sound friendly. "Do you have permission to call from the lieutenant's vehicle? Where is he?"
Zh: “好吧……日落先生。”她的声音不太友好。“你拿到使用警督汽車内部无线电的许可了吗？他在哪？”

En: Mr. Costeau... if that is your real name, unauthorized use of a police officer's radio is a *major* crime. I will have to alert my colleagues.
Zh: “库斯托先生……如果这是你的真名的话，越权使用警察无线电是一种*严重*的犯罪行为。我必须警告我的同事。”

En: He's resting. It's been rough around here. Listen, I need assistance.
Zh: “他在休息。这里的情况比较艰难。听着，我需要你的协助。”

En: He's investigating a lead on his own. Listen, I need help.
Zh: “他正在单独调查一条线索。听着，我需要帮助。”

En: Affirmative, Lieutenant Du Bois. There's a pause, tense with anticipation.
Zh: “可以的，杜博阿警督。”她停顿了一下，因为期待而略显紧张。

En: No, wait! We're solving a murder case in Martinaise.
Zh: “别，等一下！我们正在马丁内斯调查一起凶杀案。”

En: Hang up quickly.
Zh: 快速挂断。

En: Her voice does not sound friendly. "Where is the lieutenant?"
Zh: 她的声音听起来并不友好。“警督在哪？”

En: I don't know, it's not like we're joined by the hip. Anyway, I need assistance.
Zh: “我不知道，又不是说我们俩是连体婴。总之，我需要帮助。”

En: I'll contact him later for a full report. Her voice hasn't become any friendlier. "For now, I'm Officer Alice de Mettrie, how can I help you?"
Zh: “我稍后会联系他提供一份完整的报告。”她的声音并没有变得友好一些。“我是爱丽丝·德梅特里警官，有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Why aren't you using your own radio to contact your precinct?
Zh: “你为什么不用自己的无线电联系你们分局呢？”

En: That's one of the things I may need help with. (Cough.)
Zh: “这可能是我需要帮助的事情之一。”（咳嗽。）

En: Reasons... uh, for one, I don't know where my radio is.
Zh: “理由……呃，举个例子，我不知道我的无线电在哪。”

En: It's a long story. Bad shit has gone down.
Zh: “说来话长。不好的事情已经过去了。”

En: A-ha. Sounds like trouble. She seems to sigh. "Alright. Next time, have Lieutenant Kitsuragi with you."
Zh: “啊哈。听起来挺麻烦的。”她好像在叹气。“好吧。下次记得带上曷城警督。”

En: That is a pretty big problem, yes. She seems to sigh. "Alright. Next time, have Lieutenant Kitsuragi with you."
Zh: “那的确是个大问题，没错。”她好像在叹气。“好吧。下次记得带上曷城警督。”

En: There's a pause. "Alright. Next time, have Lieutenant Kitsuragi with you."
Zh: 短暂的停顿之后。“好吧。下次记得带上曷城警督。”

En: I need to report a dead body on the Martinaise boardwalk.
Zh: “我需要汇报，马丁内斯的木板道上发现了一具尸体。”

En: Connect me to Jamrock Public Library.
Zh: “请帮我接加姆洛克公共图书馆。”

En: Hold on, officer.
Zh: “请稍等，警官。”

En: I've got Central Jamrock Public Library on the line and I've already introduced you to their librarian. Connecting the call in 2... 1...
Zh: “我接通了加姆洛克公共图书馆，已经向他们的管理员介绍过你了。正在接通，2,1……”

En: Yes, this is Central Jamrock Public Library here. A male librarian answers the call. "How can I help you, officer?"
Zh: “你好，这里是加姆洛克公共图书馆。”一个男管理员接了电话。“有什么可以帮你的吗，警官？”

En: I'm afraid they're closed. It says here that the library is open from 10 AM to 6 PM.
Zh: “恐怕那里已经关门了。上面说图书馆的营业时间是早上10点至下午6点。”

En: Alice, I need medical attention. Please connect me to Nix Gottlieb at Station 41.
Zh: “爱丽丝，我需要医疗看护。请帮我接41分局的尼克斯·戈特利布。”

En: Alice, please connect me to Station 41's lazareth.
Zh: “爱丽丝，请帮我接41分局检疫所。”

En: I was told I need to connect to my station... the 41st lazareth.
Zh: “别人告诉我，我需要跟我们分局取得联系……41分局检疫所。”

En: Take the red-tipped prybar.
Zh: 拿走红头撬棍。

En: Kim, this is the Coupris Kineema motor carriage, right?
Zh: “金，这是库普瑞斯锐影汽車，对吗？”

En: Kim, what are we looking at? What is this machine?
Zh: “金，这是什么机器？”

En: This is the Coupris Kineema, my motor carriage. You can use the toolbox and the radio if you'd like. He nods at the cabin.
Zh: “这是库普瑞斯锐影，我的汽車。如果你需要的话，可以用里面的工具箱和无线电。”他朝着車厢点了点头。

En: But this is different -- this is a sports model.
Zh: 但是这不一样——这是跑車款的。

En: This must be what woke me up when you arrived in Martinaise. The infernal noise.
Zh: “你到达马丁内斯的时候，我一定就是被它给吵醒的。来自地狱的噪音。”

En: Yes, it's my patrol vehicle. You can use the toolbox and the radio if you'd like. He nods at the cabin.
Zh: “是的，这是我的巡逻車。如果你需要的话，可以用里面的工具箱和无线电。”他朝着車厢点了点头。

En: Do all policemen in the RCM have such *cool* motor carriages?
Zh: “RCM的所有警官都有这么*酷炫*的汽車吗？”

En: Yes, sorry about that... the Coupris Kineema does have a rather... distinctive engine sound.
Zh: “是的，我很抱歉……库普瑞斯锐影的发动机声音……的确很有特色。”

En: He says it with very badly concealed pride.
Zh: 他说话的时候，带着一种难以掩饰的骄傲。

En: (Squint your eyes) "Okay, but what *is* a motor carriage?"
Zh: （眯起眼睛）“好吧，但是汽車又*是什么*？”

En: The Coupris MotorCorp does provide most of our patrol vehicles, yes.
Zh: “我们的绝大多数巡逻車都是库普瑞斯汽車公司提供的，没错。”

En: I might be wrong, but this one is a *sports model*, right?
Zh: “如果我说错了请别介意，但它好像是*跑車款*的，对吗？”

En: You're right. A vainglorious smile spreads over his face as he pats his machine. "I didn't take you for a motorcar enthusiast. Do you also like TipTop, detective?"
Zh: “你说得对。”他轻轻拍了拍他的爱車，脸上露出了自负的笑容。“我没想到你也是个汽車爱好者。你也喜欢大师赛吗，警探？”

En: Can we turn it on and drive somewhere?
Zh: “我们可以发动它，开到什么地方去吗？”

En: Look around the cabin again.
Zh: 再看一遍車厢。

En: No, I'm afraid not. He straightens his spine. "We have a murder case on our hands, remember?"
Zh: “不，恐怕不行。”他挺直了后背。“我们手上还有一起凶杀案呢，还记得吗？”

En: The TipTop Tournée is an interisolary racing series, mostly known for its high speeds, ludicrous sponsorship deals, and high death toll. In Revachol it's held quadrennially on the prestigious Zéro Carrousel racing circuit.
Zh: 大师巡回赛是跨洲举办的赛車活动，主要是以超高速度，荒唐的赞助商，以及高死亡人数而闻名。每隔四年在瑞瓦肖声名赫赫的零卡路斯赛道举办一次活动。

En: An interisolary racing series. You should definitely give it a go if you like motor carriages -- it has fantastic competition. He smiles again.
Zh: “一场跨洲举办的赛車活动。如果你喜欢汽車的话，绝对要去试一试——竞争非常激烈。”他又笑了笑。

En: What's TipTop?
Zh: “大师赛是什么？”

En: I'm pretty sure I like neither motorcars or any 'TipTops'.
Zh: “我很肯定，我既不喜欢汽車，也不喜欢什么‘大师赛’。”

En: TipTop? Adore it!
Zh: “大师赛？喜欢的不得了！”

En: No, I don't care about motorcars, I just know about random things.
Zh: “不，我不在乎汽車，我了解一些随机的事情。”

En: Oh. He looks visibly disappointed. "I guess it's not for everyone."
Zh: “哦。”他明显很失望。“我猜不是所有人都喜欢吧。”

En: Really? the lieutenant looks at you. "Well, I'm not indifferent on the matter either." He adjusts his glasses, visibly pleased to have found a fellow fan.
Zh: “真的吗？”警督看着你。“呃，其实我对这件事也不是漠不关心的。”他调了调眼镜，显然是因为找到了一个同好而开心。

En: Nothing. Nothing. It's probably nothing, forget I brought it up. Please proceed with the carefree lollygagging.
Zh: 没什么。没什么。可能什么也不是，忘了我提过这件事。请继续无忧无虑地虚度时光吧。

En: Motor carriage, motor carriage... Something bad with a motor carriage.... A dark lump rises in your throat.
Zh: 汽車，汽車……汽車发生了什么不好的事……你的喉咙里升起一团黑暗的阴影。

En: What is this sinking feeling I have with the words *motor carriage*?
Zh: 为什么一提到*汽車*这个词，你就有一种沉溺的感觉？

En: The radio DJ stops. For a moment it seems like he has ran out of things to say...
Zh: DJ电台停了下来。有那么一瞬间，他似乎无话可说……

En: Turn the radio back to PRIMELINE.
Zh: 把电台调回总机频道。

En: Turn it off, this isn't music!
Zh: 把它关掉，这根本不叫音乐！

En: He's still avoiding your gaze -- and his ears glow red.
Zh: 他还在回避你的目光——耳朵也变得通红。

En: But seriously, let's quit joking around, alright? We have a case to investigate. He turns back to the cabin.
Zh: “不过，说真的，我们还是别开玩笑了，好吗？我们还有案子要调查呢。”他转身回到車厢。

En: The entirety of Martinaise is watching you!
Zh: 整个马丁内斯都在看你！

En: A flock of seagulls takes off nearby, startled by the roaring radio.
Zh: 一群附近的海鸥被轰鸣的电台吓了一跳，惊慌飞起。

En: I feel deprived of human contact.
Zh: “我感觉被剥夺了人与人接触的机会。”

En: We should try again during business hours.
Zh: “上班时间我们可以再试一次。”

En: Anything else, detective?
Zh: “还有别的事吗，警探？”

En: Change of plans -- *her* number.
Zh: 改变计划——问*她的*号码。

En: Do you like to party, Alice?
Zh: “你喜欢派对吗，爱丽丝？”

En: Now, how can I help you in your *investigation*?
Zh: “现在，我能帮你的*调查*做些什么呢？”

En: The soft purr of electrical kittens -- radio waves cast far and wide over the metropolis. A woman's voice greets you through the static:
Zh: 电子猫发出了轻柔的呼噜声——无线电波在整个大都市里广泛传播。电流里传来一个女人的问候：

En: Hi, Alice, I'm a policeman too. Nice to meet you.
Zh: “嗨，爱丽丝。我也是一名警官。很高兴见到你。”

En: Operating a radio is easy. (Speak into the microphone:) "Come in Delta-Ten, this is Firewalker, copy!"
Zh: “操作无线电很容易。”（对着麦克风说话：）“呼叫德尔塔10号，我是火行者，收到请回答！”

En: Hello... hello... hello.... (Mimic echo.) "Is there anybody out there?"
Zh: “喂……喂喂……喂……”（模仿回音。）“有人吗？”

En: As you tap on the gauge, the indicator pin jerks as if startled. It's in the large orange sector, indicating the engine is warm. Next to the gauge is a red switch labelled HEAT.
Zh: 你拍了拍仪表，指针猛地抖了一下，像是被吓到了。它停留在大片的橙色区域，表明发动机是热的。仪表旁边是一个红色的开关，上面贴着*预热*的标签。

En: As you tap on the gauge, the indicator pin jerks as if startled. It's in the small blue sector, indicating the engine is cold. Next to the gauge is a red switch labelled HEAT.
Zh: 你拍了拍仪表，指针猛地抖了一下，像是被吓到了。它停留在一块小的蓝色区域，表明发动机是冷的。仪表旁边是一个红色的开关，上面贴着*预热*的标签。

En: Now-now. That's enough fun with the foldable headlights. I know they're mesmerising. They're also fragile. I'm not going to turn it on for you again.
Zh: “好了好了。折叠前灯玩得差不多了吧。我知道它们很吸引人，而且也很脆弱。我不会再帮你打开了。”

En: There's no use pressing the HEAT button, he says and jingles his keys. "It won't start without the ignition key."
Zh: “按加热按钮没用的，”他一边说，一边把钥匙晃得叮当响。“没有点火钥匙是不会启动的。”

En: He's downplaying his excitement -- the lieutenant is more than happy to show off his precious carriage.
Zh: 他对自己的兴奋轻描淡写——警督很乐意炫耀他的宝贝汽車。

En: The dashboard lights up with orange glow. The rounds per minute gauge jumps and the engine of the Coupris Kineema comes to life with a whiny growl.
Zh: 仪表盘发出橙色的光芒。转数仪表跳跃着，伴随着一阵低沉的隆隆声，库普瑞斯锐影的发动机启动了。

En: Like a leopard, waking from its sleep. Yawning and roaring at the same time.
Zh: 就像一头从沉睡中苏醒的猎豹。一边打着哈欠，一边咆哮。

En: That sound. You feel the wind in your hair as if you're already driving -- but where?
Zh: 那个声音。你感觉轻风拂过了你的头发，好像自己正在驾驶一样——但是在哪里呢？

En: Yes -- where?
Zh: 是的——哪里？

En: Press the button labelled HEADLIGHTS.
Zh: 按下标志着前灯的按钮。

En: Down a narrow street lined with shabby wooden houses, the 8/81 motor-tract coiling like a serpent high above. Children play under the viaduct. Eyes turn to watch you as you pass...
Zh: 一条狭窄的街道两边排满了破旧的木屋，8/81高速公路盘绕着，宛如一条巨蛇高高在上。孩子们在高架桥下玩耍。当你经过的时候，那些眼睛转向你……

En: What's ahead?
Zh: 前方是什么？

En: The Valley of Dogs -- and before it, a familiar dome, like a ladybug without the colours. The building has a designated parking place for *your* carriage.
Zh: 野狗山谷——在它前面是一个熟悉的圆顶，就像没有颜色的七星瓢虫。大楼里有一个指定的停車位，专门用来停放*你的*汽車。

En: This is Precinct 41 -- as close as you have to home now. It's far away...
Zh: 这里是41分局——近到现在你该回家了。它远在天边……

En: The button stays in the dash for a few seconds, then jumps out and... nothing happens.
Zh: 按钮在仪表盘里停留了几秒，然后弹了起来……什么也没发生。

En: The Coupris Kineema cannot be started without an ignition key.
Zh: 没有点火钥匙的话，库普瑞斯锐影是无法启动的。

En: Press HEAT.
Zh: 按下加热按钮。

En: Oh, that's good, that's very good. He's visibly relieved. "You must have other business then?"
Zh: “哦，那很好啊，非常好。”他显然是松了一口气。“你肯定还有其他事情吧？”

En: I'm obliged by the law to tell you there's been a breach of *ethics*. (Wink and point to the balls.)
Zh: “依照法律规定，我必须告诉你这是违反*道德*的。”（指向那些球，眨眨眼。）

En: Firewalker. I walk in the flames.
Zh: “火行者。我能在火焰里行走。”

En: A beacon of light in the dull shores of mediocrity. You're welcome, Martinaise.
Zh: “它是一座灯塔的光，照在这个平庸又乏味的海滨上。不客气，马丁内斯。”

En: Oh, uh... Well thank you, officer. It certainly lightened the mood. He laughs nervously. "Very cosy, like a little street fair almost."
Zh: “哦，呃……谢谢你，警官。这样我的心情肯定就放松多了。”他紧张地笑了笑。“非常惬意，几乎像是个小市集。”

En: The fuel oil was an environmental hazard. I had to get rid of it somehow.
Zh: “重油会危害环境。我必须想办法除掉它。”

En: Oh... He glances at the burnt writing. "Of course. I didn't think of it. Good job officer!"
Zh: “哦……”他瞥了一眼焦灼的字迹。“当然。我没有想过这一点。做得好，警官！”

En: Did he feel like he *has* to prove he can still pull his weight? Doesn't need hand-outs?
Zh: 他是不是觉得*必须*证明自己能做好分内的工作？不需要别人的施舍？

En: Wait... did he push himself to prove he can pull his weight and doesn't need hand-outs?
Zh: 等等……是你逼着他想要证明自己的能力，不需要他人施舍的吗？

En: That he's sorry?
Zh: “他很抱歉？”

En: Do you think our conversation about his job pushed him to go out there?
Zh: “你觉得是不是我们那段关于工作的对话逼着他那样做的？”

En: The irony isn't lost on me, but I wanted to ask about something else.
Zh: “讽刺对我不起作用的，不过我想问你点别的事。”

En: I'm glad to assist you if I can, officer, he replies, voice tired and breaking.
Zh: “我很高兴能帮到你，警官，”他回复到，声音疲惫而破碎。

En: No, he replies quickly. "René was the most stubborn man in Revachol. Nothing you or I could say would ever *push* him to do anything. The man was completely immovable."
Zh: “不，”他快速回复到。“雷内是瑞瓦肖最固执的男人。不管你或者我说了什么，都不会*逼迫*他做任何事的。那个男人完全不为所动。”

En: He has doubts, but right now he just wants to move on and not think about it.
Zh: 他有些怀疑，不过现在他只想继续向前看，不去想这个问题。

En: 'Do or die -- there is no middle ground' -- that's what you told René. Perhaps he wasn't *completely* immovable after all?
Zh: ‘非生即死——没有中间地带’——这是你告诉雷内的。也许他也不是*完全*不为所动的？

En: It was over so quickly I didn't get a chance to form an opinion.
Zh: “一切结束的太快，我还没机会形成什么看法。”

En: And *then* it hits you -- the brilliant bastard has drenched the entire sandwich in honey mustard. That's incredibly decadent. And delicious.
Zh: *然后*你突然想到了——那个天才混球把整个三明治都浸在蜂蜜芥末里了。实在是太堕落了。而且很美味。

En: No, you are not. Enforcing proper conduct in an amateur pétanque game is not something the RCM involves itself in. He smiles. "It doesn't matter."
Zh: “不，不要这样。在一场业余地掷球游戏中强行执行适当的引导不是RCM该做的事。”他笑了。“没关系的。”

En: (Whisper.) "Kim, cheating in sports is *unacceptable*. I got this."
Zh: （低语到。）“金，在体育比赛中作弊是*无法接受的*。我可以的。”

En: You're right, that's none of our business.
Zh: “你说得对，这不关我们的事。”

En: What is... actually never mind. He smiles a clever smile "I'm sure there's *something* I can help you with, officer."
Zh: “这是……好吧，其实没什么。”他露出一个机灵的微笑。“*有些事*我肯定是能帮你的，警官。”

En: Doesn't matter?! Integrity in sports *doesn't matter*?! Hold me back, sonny-Jim... GODDAMNIT, it's *all* that matters!!!
Zh: 不重要？！保持体育比赛的诚信*不重要*？！快拉住我，孩子……该死，这*才是*最重要的吧！！！

En: Aeons ago. Probably when you were still a child.
Zh: 很久很久以前。可能在当你还是个孩子的时候。

En: A glimmer of respect flashes in René's eyes.
Zh: 雷内的眼睛里流露出尊敬的光芒。

En: ... and it's gone.
Zh: ……它不见了。

En: Here, something to remember your friends by... (Give him the photograph of René and the girl.)\n
Zh: “给你，留给你纪念你的朋友……”（把雷内和女孩的照片交给他。）

En: Here, something to remember your friend by... (Give him the photograph of René and the girl.)
Zh: “给你，留给你纪念你的朋友……”（把雷内和女孩的照片交给他。）

En: Let me see... Gaston takes the photo, hands trembling. "This was 60 years ago! We all went to that parade. Young René looks so happy and Jeannie..." Eyes blurry with tears, he has to stop.
Zh: “我看看……”加斯顿用颤抖的手接过相片。“这是60年前的相片！我们都去参加了那次游行。年轻的雷内看起来那么开心，珍妮……”泪水模糊了他的双眼，他不得不停了下来。

En: I'm sorry, officer, I just... He dries his eyes. "Thank you, *thank you* for this little memorabilia. It really means the world to me."
Zh: “很抱歉，警官，我只是……”他擦干眼泪。“谢谢你，*谢谢*你的这个小纪念品。它对我来说真的太重要了。”

En: That was nice, the lieutenant smiles. "A small thing for us, but invaluable to him. He probably didn't even know René had the photo."
Zh: “那就好，”警督笑了。“对我们来说是件小事，对他来说确实无价之宝。他可能都不知道雷内还有这张相片。”

En: It is what it is, part of life really, he mumbles, only half-listening to you. "But to know someone for 79 years, then one day they're just *gone*..."
Zh: “事实就是这样，这真的就是生活的一部分，”他嘟囔着，你只听到了一半。“一个认识了整整79年的人，有一天他们突然就*走了*……”

En: I just don't know anymore... about anything really. He slowly shakes his head, then remembers your presence. "But you... you must need something?"
Zh: “我真的不知道了……什么都不知道了。”他慢慢摇摇头，然后想起了你的存在。“但是你……你肯定需要什么吧？”

En: Despite their differences these men played such a significant part in each other's lives that now that one of them is gone, the other feels... just lost.
Zh: 尽管两人天差地别，但他们还是在彼此的生命中扮演了相当重要的角色，现在其中一个走了，另一个只会感觉到……失落。

En: We are both very sorry for your loss, the lieutenant bows his head. "Death of a friend is a terrible thing."
Zh: “我们为你的损失感到非常抱歉……”警督低下头。“朋友的离去是一件可怕的事情。”

En: It is what it is... part of life, he mumbles, only half-listening to you. "But to know someone for 79 years, then one day they're just *gone*..."
Zh: “事实就是这样……这就是生活的一部分，”他嘟囔着，你只听到了一半。“一个认识了整整79年的人，有一天他们突然就*走了*……”

En: I'm not afraid of death either.
Zh: “我也不怕死。”

En: I don't want to die.
Zh: “我不想死。”

En: I'm not afraid of dying -- I'm only afraid of not really living.
Zh: “我不怕死——我只害怕没有真正地活着。”

En: I'm pretty much on my wait out myself too... can't wait to die!
Zh: “其实我也在等待自己的那一天……已经等不及要死去了！”

En: He will never play pétanque again.
Zh: 他永远也不会再玩地掷球了。

En: That means the uniform could still be in the guard booth -- something to keep in mind if you *really* plan to give yourself to Revachol.
Zh: 那就意味着制服可能还在那个岗亭——如果你*真的*打算把自己献给瑞瓦肖的话，最好记住这一点。

En: Tell me.
Zh: “告诉我吧。”

En: Something forgettable, like 'See you tomorrow' probably?
Zh: “某种难忘的话，比如说‘明天见’？”

En: Teacup? Oh, because of the ceramic armour. Clever.
Zh: 茶杯？哦，因为那套陶瓷盔甲。真聪明。

En: Let's talk some more about Jeanne-Marie.
Zh: “我们再多聊聊这位珍妮-玛丽吧。”

En: Sweet sweet Jeannie... He nods, smiling. "Ask away officer, I knew her all my life."
Zh: “最最亲爱的珍妮……”他点点头，笑了。“随便问吧，警官，我认识她一辈子了。”

En: I want to go over few more things about René, if that's okay?
Zh: “如果可以的话，我想再重温一些雷内的情况？”

En: Sure, officer. He smiles. "They are the good guys around here. What do you need?"
Zh: “当然可以，警官。”他笑了。“他们是好人。你有什么需要？”

En: Yes? he looks up. "What about, René?"
Zh: “什么事？”他抬起头。“关于谁的，雷内吗？”

En: Nothing wrong with weighing your options first.
Zh: “首先权衡自己的选择完全没错。”

En: Guess he was about to head home. The dockworkers found him in his civilian clothes and not the cockatoo uniform you... ugh... confiscated.
Zh: “我猜他是打算回家吧，因为码头工人发现他的时候，他正穿着便服，而不是那套凤头鹦鹉制服，就是你……呃……没收的这套。”

En: What? Was he killed during the mercenary tribunal?
Zh: “什么？他是在雇佣兵宣判的时候死去的吗？”

En: Oh, he would have liked that. The old man slowly shakes his head. "Violent lives ending violently... That's how he wanted to go. Sadly it was not the case."
Zh: “哦，他倒是很想。”老人慢慢摇摇头。“暴力的生活终将以暴力结束……那就是他想要的结果。可悲的是结果并非如此。”

En: Too bad René's gone, I was hoping to ask him about May bells... (Hold out the flower.)
Zh: “雷内去世真是太糟糕了，我本来还想问问他铃兰的事……”（把花拿出来。）

En: The old man stares at the flower, then sighs and says: "René wasn't really what you'd call a botanist, officer. And believe me, he didn't like Insulindian Lilies."
Zh: 老人盯着花，然后叹了口气，说道：“雷内不是植物学家，警官。相信我，他不喜欢伊苏林迪百合。”

En: Wait, Insulindian Lillies?
Zh: “等等，伊苏林迪百合？”

En: Why didn't he like them?
Zh: “他为什么不喜欢？”

En: Mhmh, he replies with a slight nod. "That's their old name, dating back to the time of kings and crests and all that other stuff he loved so much."
Zh: “嗯，”他轻轻点点头，回复到。“那是它们的旧名字，可以追溯到国王的时代，纹章和其它他钟爱的东西的时代。”

En: There were many reasons, but mostly it was the communards. They called them 'The Bells of Revolution'. A sad smile passes his face.
Zh: “有很多原因，不过主要是因为公社社员。他们称之为‘革命的钟声’。”他的脸上闪过一抹悲伤的笑容。

En: I guess in the end the Insulindian Lillies were just another piece of the Old Insulinde, the royalists had to surrender to the Mazovian insurgents. It doesn't really matter anymore.
Zh: “我猜到了最后，伊苏林迪百合不过是旧伊苏林迪的又一个碎片，保皇派不得不向马佐夫主义的叛乱分子投降。这真的已经不重要了。”

En: But you feel the dried flower in your hand somehow still *does* matter. Although not to this sad old man.
Zh: 但是不知道为什么，你觉得自己手中的干花还是*很*重要。尽管对这个悲伤的老人并不重要。

En: Of course he didn't, flowers are for pansies. Stop wasting time with them.
Zh: 他当然没有，基佬才喜欢花呢。别再浪费时间了。

En: It is such a pleasure to see you again, officer. How may I aid the Citizens Militia on this fine day?
Zh: “又见到你真的很开心，警官。这么好的天气，我有什么能帮到公民武装的吗？

En: Officer! He exclaims. "The mere sight of police in Martinaise makes me feel safer already. How can I help you?"
Zh: “警官！”他大喊到。“在马丁内斯一看到警察，我就已经感觉安全多了。有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Hello, officer. He turns to you. "How might I be of assistance on this fine day?"
Zh: “你好，警官。”他转向你。“今天的天气真不错，我能帮你什么呢？”

En: Yes, that's what you need, Gaston. More padding on that fat ass of yours. I hope your heart gives out.
Zh: “没错，你需要的就是这个，加斯顿。让你的大屁股再肥一点。希望你的心脏停止跳动。”

En: René, tsk-tsk. It's the little pleasures. Life doesn't need to be a.... *mnjam mnjam*... a struggle.
Zh: “雷内，啧啧啧。这叫小小的快乐。生活不需要变成……*吧唧吧唧*……挣扎。”

En: I have really outdone myself... He takes a bite out of his sandwich. "This is divine."
Zh: “我真的超越了自己……”他咬了一口三明治。“这就叫神圣。”

En: Hey, Gaston. No comment on the duds?
Zh: “嗨，加斯顿。对我的行头没什么意见吗？”

En: It... isn't right. The old man doesn't even bother looking away. "René wanted to be buried in his uniform. But I guess it doesn't matter now..."
Zh: “这……不太对。”老人连看都懒得看一眼。“雷内想穿着他的制服下葬。但是我猜，现在已经不重要了……”

En: The lieutenant gives you a long, slow shake of his head.
Zh: 警督慢慢地，久久地对着你摇了摇头。

En: You mentioned Jeanne-Marie Beaulieu. Who is that?
Zh: “你提到了珍妮-玛丽·博利厄。那是谁？”

En: What are the rules of this game?
Zh: “这个游戏的规则是什么？”

En: Tell me, what do you know about the dead man?
Zh: “告诉我，你对那个死者了解多少？”

En: Bye for now. [Leave.]
Zh: 再见。[离开。]

En: Convince Gaston to relinquish his sandwich.
Zh: 说服加斯顿放弃他的三明治。

En: Didn't expose him cheating.
Zh: 不要揭露他作弊的事。

En: Exposed him cheating.
Zh: 揭露他作弊的事。

En: Bonded over health issues.
Zh: 因为健康问题纠结。

En: Threw the *boule* in the sea.
Zh: 把*大球*扔进海里。

En: Replaced the *boule*.
Zh: *大球*的替代品。

En: Drafted him into a vigilante group.
Zh: 征召他加入民兵团。

En: Scared him with pétanque-zeal.
Zh: 用对地掷球的热情吓他一跳。

En: May René rest in peace. (Conclude.)
Zh: “愿雷内能够安息。”（总结。）

En: Yes... A strange expression runs across his wrinkled face, when he repeats: "in peace."
Zh: “是的……”当他重复着‘安息’的时候，他那张满是皱纹的脸上闪过一丝奇怪的表情。

En: I'll be with you in a moment, officer. Let me just finish my sandwich. He nods to his partner. "Talk to angry old René first."
Zh: “我马上就好，警官。让我先吃完这个三明治。”他朝自己的同伴点点头。“先跟那个愤怒的老雷内聊聊吧。”

En: Life... *mnjam-mnjam*... doesn't need to be a struggle. He covers his mouth to hide a burp.
Zh: “生活……*吧唧吧唧*……不需要如此挣扎。”他捂住嘴，想要掩饰打嗝。

En: Shame, I didn't get to ask him how to turn back time.
Zh: “真遗憾。我还想问他怎么回到过去呢。”

En: Real shame... The old man nods, eyes still on the ground. He's not really paying attention to your words.
Zh: “太遗憾了……”老人点了点头，看着地面。他没有认真听你的话。

En: You're not gonna get anything out of this guy. If the old carabineer knew something, it will be buried with him.
Zh: 你不会从他身上找到答案。即使老兵知道些什么，那也已经和他一起长眠地下。

En: No worries, just a small setback. *Movere tempore*, kingsman. For the tenacious no road is impassable. Just got to stay moving.
Zh: 别担心，只是一点小挫折。“继续前进”，骑士。有志者事竟成。你不能停下脚步。

En: Icebreaker, I plough through every ice sheet on my path.
Zh: “破冰者，我将碾碎前进路途上的冰雪。”

En: I uh... He rubs his chin, not knowing what to say. "That's nice. Uh... If I'm in your way, I'll move! Or can I help in any way?"
Zh: “我，呃……”他摸了摸下巴，不知道该说什么。“那很不错。呃……如果我挡了你的道，我会挪开的！或者我还有哪里能帮到你？”

En: Wait, is René dead?
Zh: “等等，雷内死了？”

En: Wait, is René dead?
Zh: “等等，雷内死了？”

En: Where is René?
Zh: “雷内在哪？”

En: This... this isn't right, officer. He shakes his head. "René wanted to be buried in his uniform, but..."
Zh: “这……这样是不对的，警官。”他摇摇头。“雷内想穿着制服下葬，但是……”

En: Officer... The jolliness is gone from Gaston's face. "Care to play a game with a lonely old man?"
Zh: “警官……”欢乐从加斯顿脸上消失了。“想不想跟一个孤独的老人玩一场游戏？”

En: Actually never mind... Wouldn't be the same...
Zh: “其实，没关系的……反正也不一样了……”

En: Oh, sweet Jeannie... He gets a dreamy look in his eyes. "She was the finest woman in all of Revachol. Maybe the entire world."
Zh: “哦，亲爱的珍妮……”他的眼睛里闪烁着梦幻般的光芒。“她是整个瑞瓦肖最好的女人。也许是整个世界上最好的。”

En: Do not *defile* her memory, Gaston. There's an almost imperceptibly small tremble in his voice. "Let her rest in peace."
Zh: “不要*玷污*她的回忆，加斯顿。”他的声音里有一丝几乎难以察觉的颤抖。“让她安息吧。”

En: His tone lacks its usual intensity, like he doesn't feel he has the right to speak on the matter.
Zh: 他的语气不像寻常那样强硬，似乎他觉得自己没有权利谈论这件事。

En: So you both know her?
Zh: “所以你们俩都认识她？”

En: What happened to her?
Zh: “她怎么了？”

En: Sounds like this is between you two. I have other matters to address.
Zh: “听起来这是你们俩之间的事。我还有其他事情要处理。”

En: Sure, officer. He nods. "It's better for  René's flickering ticker too. Not that he'd ever admit anything there needs tinkering."
Zh: “当然，警官。”他点点头。“这样对雷内颤动的心脏更好一些。并不是说他承认那里有什么需要修补的地方。”

En: We knew her, alright... His friendly face lights up. "Lived on the same street our entire lives, just two houses apart. The three of us have been best friends since we were four."
Zh: “我们认识她，没错……”他友好的面孔一下子亮了起来。“这辈子都住在同一条街上，只隔了两栋房子。从四岁开始，我们三个就是最好的朋友了。”

En: She was René's first girl, back when the prick was sixteen. He looks at the carabineer almost gently. "They were courting till he decided he'd rather die for some great *idéal* than just be hap---"
Zh: “那个混蛋16岁的时候，她成了雷内的第一个女人。”他看着老兵，目光几近温柔。“他们一直相亲相爱，直到他决定为了某种伟大的*理想*而献身，而不愿只做一个快乐的男人……”

En: AND THEN YOU STOLE HER FROM ME! He jerks forward, but then grabs his chest and stops.
Zh: “然后你就把她从我这里偷走了！”他猛地朝前挺身，不过很快就抓着胸口停了下来。

En: Easy fellows, no need for this to get ugly.
Zh: “放松点，伙计们，没必要搞得这么难看。”

En: Oh, officer... Gaston is unfazed by the outburst. "It already got ugly nearly eighty years ago when the three of us were just learning to walk and talk."
Zh: “哦，警官……”加斯顿没有因为他的爆发而不安。“80年前，当我们三个人刚刚学会走路和说话的时候，就已经很难看了。”

En: Do not intervene.
Zh: 不要干预。

En: Sorry, but I *really* wanted to just ask some questions.
Zh: “抱歉，但是我*真的*只想再问些问题。”

En: It's okay, officer. He smooths his white whiskers. "You might have just saved René from a heart attack."
Zh: “没事的，警官。”他理了理自己的白胡子。“你可能刚刚把雷内从心脏病发作中解救出来了。”

En: Well... technically *you* stole her from *me*, because we'd been pretty close ever since you two had that falling out over the ink you spilled over her pretty yellow dress...
Zh: “好吧……准确来说，是*你*从*我*这里把她偷走的，你把墨水洒到她那条漂亮的黄裙子上之后，你们俩不是闹翻了吗，从那以后我们俩一直挺亲近的……”

En: You stole her from me, René repeats, trying to steady his breathing and still clutching his chest.
Zh: “是你把她从我这里偷走的，”雷内回复到，试着稳定自己的呼吸，不过手依然紧握在胸口。

En: We were just boys, then. This was different. *You*... The tall veteran looks at you and nods.
Zh: “那时我们只是小孩子。那不一样的。*你*……”高大的老兵看着你，点点头。

En: No point starting this all over again, for the thousandth and the first time. Especially when we have company. He turns to you: "Officer?"
Zh: “没必要再来一遍，已经一千零一次了。特别是有外人在的时候。”他转向你：“警官？”

En: She died of pneumonia two winters ago. It was a quiet passage. Peaceful. He smiles faintly.
Zh: “两年前的冬天，她死于肺炎。那是一条安静的旅程。很平静。”他微微一笑。

En: René and I were both by her bedside when she... He pauses, searching for the right word.
Zh: “雷内和我都守在她的床边，当她……”他停顿了一下，搜索着正确的词汇。

En: Died, he sharply fills the silence and adds: "No use sugar-coating it. Won't bring her back, will it now?"
Zh: “死的时候，”他尖锐地填补了沉默，补充了一句：“没必要粉饰什么。这样她也不会回来了，不是吗？”

En: Departed, his partner finishes his sentence, then chuckles: "Until the very end she couldn't decide between us. The most indecisive woman I've ever met."
Zh: “去世的时候，”他的同伴补完了句子，然后笑了起来：“直到最后，她都没能在我们俩当中做出选择。她是我见过的最犹豫不决的女人。”

En: A quick grimace of pain passes over René's features, but he immediately regains control. His face now a dispassionate mask again.
Zh: 雷内的脸上闪过一丝痛苦的痕迹，不过他很快又控制住了自己。现在他的脸又变得风平浪静了。

En: Did you love him?
Zh: “你爱他吗？”

En: We've hated each other our entire lives. So much in fact that... He falls silent and looks at you, eyes filling up with tears.
Zh: “我们这一生都憎恨着彼此。恨得那么深，事实上……”他陷入沉默，看着你，眼中饱含泪水。

En: You always confused her, couldn't let us be happy... He says with heavy resentment. "Seduced her with your fancy words and *pastries*."
Zh: “你总是让她心烦意乱，不让我们开心……”他愤愤不平地说道。“用你的花言巧语和*点心*引诱她。”

En: Why do you think she was indecisive?
Zh: “你为什么会觉得她犹豫不决呢？”

En: She could never make up her mind about anything. What to have for breakfast, favourite colour or which one of us to marry. The look in his eyes is happy and distant.
Zh: “她对任何事情都拿不定主意。早餐该吃什么，最喜欢的颜色是什么，还有到底该嫁给谁。”他的目光快乐而疏离。

En: She was always leaving one of us for the other, but never long enough to actually get married.
Zh: “她总是在我们俩之间摇摆不定，不过这份时间还不够长，最后也没有成婚。”

En: Heck, he says with a chuckle. "*Technically* we're both still engaged to her."
Zh: “该死，”他笑着说道。“*从严格意义上讲*，我们俩跟她还处于订婚状态呢。”

En: That's a bit odd.
Zh: “那有点奇怪吧。”

En: Women, right?
Zh: “这就是女人，不是吗？”

En: He suddenly remembers *you* are still there, falls silent, and turns away.
Zh: 他突然记起来*你*还在这里，于是一言不发地转过身去。

En: Of course, officer, he says with a smile. "Memories are all we have left."
Zh: “当然了，警官，”他笑着说道。“我们只剩下回忆了。”

En: Thanks for sharing. (Conclude.)
Zh: “感谢你的分享。”（总结。）

En: *Mon dieu*, officer! He looks at your wounds. "It is worse than I thought! Believe me, I know all about that kind of pain, I've had hip trouble for the past... week."
Zh: “*我的天啦*，警官！”他看着你的伤口。“比我想象的还要糟糕！相信我，我非常了解那种痛苦，在过去的……一周里，我的臀部也遇到了一点问题。”

En: The prick is gone, he replies, trying to smile. "I... I can barely believe it, but he's really gone."
Zh: “那个老家伙走了，”他回复到，努力想挤出一丝笑容。“我……我还是不敢相信，但他真的走了。”

En: He is trying to retain his jolly façade, but the underlying sadness casts a deep shadow over his wrinkled face.
Zh: 他试着维持自己那副快乐的模样，不过内在的悲伤在他布满皱纹的脸上投射下深深的阴影。

En: Gone? Gone where?
Zh: “走了？去哪里了？”

En: Gaston sighs and mumbles, more to himself than you: "Hell, most likely. He was an absolute cunt..."
Zh: 加斯顿叹了口气，小声嘟囔着，更像是对他自己，而不是对你：“很可能是地狱吧。他是个十足的杂种……”

En: Was he really that bad?
Zh: “他真有那么糟糕吗？”

En: How did he die exactly?
Zh: “他到底是怎么死的？”

En: Do you know what happened to his medals?
Zh: “你知道他的奖章怎么样了吗？”

En: I offer my sincere condolences. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我向你表示最诚挚的哀悼。”（总结。）

En: Old people die, you better get ready too. (Conclude.)
Zh: “老人迟早会死，你最好也要做好准备。”（总结。）

En: His angry little heart finally gave out. He sighs. "The dockworkers found him in the guard booth this morning. Wasn't even supposed to be working for another week, but he just had to prove how tough he is..."
Zh: “他那颗愤怒的小心脏最终还是停止了跳动。”他叹了口气。“今天早晨，码头工人在岗亭发现了他。他根本不该再工作一星期的，但他就是想证明自己到底有坚强……”

En: Guess he was about to head home, 'cause when the dockworkers found him he was wearing civilian clothes and not the cockatoo uniform I saw him in all the time.
Zh: “我猜他是打算回家吧，因为码头工人发现他的时候，他正穿着便服，而不是我一直看见的那套凤头鹦鹉制服。”

En: Sometimes I thought he was wearing it just to piss me off. Gaston stares at the uniform you're wearing. "Guess the joke's on him now. Won't even be buried in it."
Zh: “有时候，我觉得他穿那套制服只是为了气我。”加斯顿盯着你的夹克。“我猜是上天跟他开了个玩笑，他没办法穿着它下葬了。”

En: Sometimes I thought he was wearing it just to piss me off. Gaston stares at your jacket. "Guess the joke's on him now. Won't even be buried in it."
Zh: “有时候，我觉得他穿那套制服只是为了气我。”加斯顿盯着你的裤子。“我猜是上天跟他开了个玩笑，他没办法穿着它下葬了。”

En: Sometimes I thought he was wearing it just to piss me off. Gaston stares at your pants. "Guess the joke's on him now. Won't even be buried in it."
Zh: “有时候，我觉得他穿那套制服只是为了气我。”加斯顿盯着你身上的制服。“我猜是上天跟他开了个玩笑，他没办法穿着它下葬了。”

En: Sometimes I thought he was wearing it just to piss me off. Gaston smiles a sad smile. "Now the joke's on him, 'cause he's gonna be buried without it."
Zh: “有时候，我觉得他穿那套制服只是为了气我。”加斯顿的笑容很悲伤。“现在上天跟他开了个玩笑，他没办法穿着它下葬了。”

En: Well, you took one of them. He shakes his head. "Still can't believe he gave it to you, but that's how rigid he was. Once the offer carelessly left his lips it was irrefutable..."
Zh: “好吧，你拿走了一件。”他摇摇头。“我还是不敢相信，他居然会把它给了你，不过他的确就是这么死板。一旦不小心开了这个口，就没有辩驳的余地……”

En: He looks at you for a moment and then speaks quietly. "I took them for myself. Took them to remember that old cunt. Nobody knew him better than I did, and I want to remember that old cunt by something."
Zh: 他看了你一会儿，然后低声说道。“我拿走了，为了记住那个老杂种。没人比我更了解他，我希望能够睹物思人。”

En: The other one I took for myself. He looks at you defiantly. "No one knew him better than I did, and I want to remember that old cunt by something."
Zh: “另一件是我拿走的。”他挑衅地看着你。“没人比我更了解他，我需要点东西记住那个老杂种。”

En: Strange how  *old cunt* sounds almost gentle when he says it now.
Zh: 奇怪的是，现在*老杂种*从他嘴里说出来的时候，几乎满满都是温柔。

En: I repeat -- an *absolute* cunt. He turns to look at the crater. "Even his old army buddies didn't want him around. He was like an old viper."
Zh: “我再重复一遍——他是个*不折不扣的*杂种。”他转而看向弹坑。“就连他的老战友都不想留在他身边。他就像条老毒蛇一样。”

En: The only people who could stand to be around him were Jeannie and me... He pauses. "She saw something in him when we were just kids, and..."
Zh: “唯一留在他身边的只有珍妮和我……”他停顿了一下。“当我们还是孩子的时候，她在他身上看见了一些东西……”

En: His voice trembles. "...a-and she never lost sight of it. And I thought if the most beautiful being in the world can love him, then there must be something worth holding on to..."
Zh: 他的声音在颤抖。“……而且她永远看在眼里。我想，如果这个世界上最美丽的人都能爱上他，那他身上肯定有什么值得她坚持的东西……”

En: Yes. I... I loved that angry prick. He didn't deserve it, but I did. He wipes his eyes with a sleeve. "You know what his last words to me were?"
Zh: “是的。我……我爱那个爱发火的老家伙。他不值得，但我值得。”他用袖子擦了擦眼泪。“你知道他最后跟我说了什么吗？”

En: Some right-wing royalist slogan?
Zh: “某种右翼的保皇派口号？”

En: Something mean?
Zh: “一些很刻薄的话？”

En: 'In Guillaume's time you'd have been shot without a trial'. That's what he said to me. The old man gathers himself and wipes his eyes again. "He lived a cunt and he died a cunt. Let's leave it at that."
Zh: “‘要是在纪尧姆的年代，不用审判就能把你枪毙。’他就是这么对我说的。”老人振作了一些，又擦了擦眼泪。“他活着的时候是个杂种，死了还是个杂种。那就随它去吧。”

En: Yes. The lieutenant nods. "We are both very sorry for your loss."
Zh: “是的。”警督点点头。“我们为你的损失感到非常抱歉。”

En: Maybe it's time to slow down, enjoy life a bit? He smiles apologetically. "Anything I can do to assist you?"
Zh: “也许是时候慢下来，稍微享受一下生活？”他充满歉意地笑了。“还有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: I guess it doesn't really matter anymore. You do what you have to, like the rest of us... So how can I help?
Zh: “我猜这已经不重要了。你做你自己该做的事，就像我们其他人一样……所以，我能怎么帮你呢？”

En: Oh... sorry, officer... What do you want?
Zh: “哦……抱歉，警官……你有什么需要？”

En: Host almighty, he's a total bummer now!
Zh: 万能的神明啊，他现在完全就是个废人了！

En: *Mon dieu*, you set it on fire! He looks at the plaza. "What kind of a policeman are you?"
Zh: “*我的天啦*，你把它点着了！”他看着广场。“你到底是什么警察啊？”

En: I uh... don't know. It was an accident. I'm sorry.
Zh: “我，呃……不知道。这是个意外。我很抱歉。”

En: Policeman of the state yet to come.
Zh: “即将到来的国家警察。”

En: The herald of the apocalypse.
Zh: “世界末日的先驱者。”

En: He finds comfort in the thought.
Zh: 他从这个想法中得到了安慰。

En: We all got to go some time, officer. Everyone I love is already there. Probably waiting for me. He smiles faintly. "I'm not afraid to die. It's been a good run and I really miss my friends..."
Zh: “总有一天我们都会离开的，警官。我爱的人都已经离开了。可能正在等我。”他微微一笑。“我不害怕死亡。这是一场美好的旅程，而且我真的很想念我的朋友们……”

En: Yes... A strange expression runs across his wrinkled face, then vanishes without trace.
Zh: “是的……”他满是皱纹的脸上闪过一丝奇怪的表情，然后消失地无影无踪。

En: Hey, Gaston, I found you a new *boule*. (Hold out the ball.)
Zh: “喂，加斯顿，我给你找了一个新的*大球*。”（把球拿出来。）

En: Ghost of the past. The old man removes his hat and sea wind ruffles his grey hair. "Everyone in this story is already dead, officer. I don't wanna talk about them."
Zh: “过去的幽灵。”老人脱下帽子，海风吹乱了他灰色的头发。“这个故事里的每一个人都已经死了，警官。我不想再提他们。”

En: Uh... *everything* really. I probably knew him better than... He stops, realizing something. "You meant the teacup in the tree, didn't you? I don't know anything about him."
Zh: “呃……*一切*的一切，真的。我可能更了解他……”他突然意识到什么，停了下来。“你是说树上那个茶杯，对吗？我对他一无所知。”

En: Keep it, it's not even a real *boule*. He waves you away. "Besides, it doesn't really matter now. I've never seen anyone else play pétanque in Martinaise."
Zh: “你留着吧，这根本算不上是一个真正的*大球*。”他挥手示意你离开。“再说，现在真的不重要了。我永远也不会在马丁内斯看到其他玩地掷球的人。”

En: But you probably had some official business too? he asks and puts the hat back on.
Zh: “不过，你可能也有公务要办吧？”他一边问，一边戴上帽子。

En: Gaston glumly stares at the shot put ball. Several seconds pass. Then he speaks: "It's too late now, officer... you got back too late. René is gone."
Zh: 加斯顿闷闷不乐地盯着铅球。几秒钟过去了。他开口说道：“现在已经太迟了，警官……你回来的太迟了。雷内走了。”

En: The prick survived all the bullets, swords and explosions just to die of a heart attack... He sighs deeply. "Ironic, isn't it -- even his own bitter little heart couldn't stand him."
Zh: “那个老家伙躲过了子弹，长剑和炸药，却被心脏病夺走了性命……”他重重地叹了一口气。“很讽刺，不是吗——就连他自己那颗充满仇恨的心脏都受不了他了。”

En: Hey, I said I wouldn't do it, but I found you a new *boule*. (Hold out the ball.)
Zh: “喂，我说过我不会这么做，不过我还是帮你找了个新*大球*。”（把球拿出来。）

En: I wanted to ask you about the Union again.
Zh: “我想再问问你工会的事。”

En: Officer... he looks up, all wrinkled, old and small. "I just can't do this right now. The Union... it just doesn't matter anymore."
Zh: “警官……”他抬起满是皱纹的脸，整个人显得苍老又矮小。“我现在做不到。工会……已经不重要了。”

En: Well, officer, what did you think? The jolly man leans forward, excited to hear your opinion.
Zh: “好吧，警官，你在想什么？”快乐的老人身体前倾，很想听听你的意见。

En: I ate your sandwich, Gaston.
Zh: “我吃了你的三明治，加斯顿。”

En: It really wasn't bad.
Zh: “其实真没那么糟糕。”

En: Make it... *lively*!
Zh: 让它……*活泼一点*！

En: He stares at you, aghast. "What a waste..."
Zh: 他惊恐地盯着你。“真是浪费……”

En: It was (*burp*) great! Nutritious as fuck! I could go all night on one of these pocket rockets!
Zh: “它真是（*嗝*）太棒了！营养丰富！它的优点我可以连续说上一整晚！”

En: Truly a masterpiece of modern gastronomy. Or should I say... *GASTONomy*. (Give the man an approving wink.)
Zh: “真是现代烹饪手法的杰作。或者我应该说……是*加斯顿烹饪手法*的杰作。”（朝男人赞许地眨眨眼。）

En: I don't mean to keep Gaston's tongue from your rectum, officer, but do you actually have any *police* business with us?
Zh: “我没有让加斯顿隐瞒你的意思，警官，不过你真有*警察*事务要找我们吗？”

En: Very nourishing. Thank you!
Zh: “非常有营养。谢谢你！”

En: I guess you don't really need taste buds for police work. He looks disappointed. "Is there anything else, officer?"
Zh: “我猜干警察的应该不需要味蕾吧。”他看起来很失望。“还有别的事吗，警官？”

En: Ugh... thank you, officer. He looks at you with disgust. "Please tell me there wasn't anything else?"
Zh: “呃……谢谢你，警官。”他嫌恶地看着你。“请告诉我，没有别的事了吧？”

En: Coming from an officer of the law, this appraisal means an awful lot to me. He bows slightly. "Thank you."
Zh: “来自一个执法人员的评价，对我来说真的意义重大。”他微微鞠了一躬。“谢谢你。”

En: It was a sandwich, I don't know what to tell you. I was hungry and now I'm not.
Zh: “它就是个三明治，我不知道能跟你说什么。当时我很饿，不过现在不饿了。”

En: I suggest naming it 'Gaston's Shit Sandwich', that's what I think.
Zh: “我建议给它命名为‘加斯顿的狗屁三明治’，这就是我的看法。”

En: Yes, yes... and the taste? He leans even closer. "How was the taste?"
Zh: “是的，是的……那味道呢？”他靠的更近了。“味道怎么样？”

En: Taste? I just ate it, forgot to check the taste.
Zh: “味道？我就只顾着吃，忘记品尝味道了。”

En: *Very* nourishing indeed. (Rub your belly.)
Zh: “的确*非常*有营养。”（揉揉你的肚子。）

En: Oh... He doesn't look up. "Good. Good for you, officer. I'm just wondering if René ever... never mind. Can I help you with something?"
Zh: “哦……”他并没有抬头。“很好。对你很好啊，警官。我只是想知道雷内会不会……没关系。我能帮你什么吗？”

En: I uh... He rubs his chin, not knowing what to say. "That's nice. Uh... better somehow. Can I help in any way?"
Zh: “我，呃……”他摸了摸下巴，不知道说什么好。“那很好啊。呃……这样更好。我能怎么帮你呢？”

En: Oh, okay... He seems to edge away from you. "It's perfectly alright. No harm done and it's really none of my business anyway."
Zh: “哦，好吧……”他似乎离你远了一些。“完全没问题。没有造成什么伤害，反正真的不关我的事。”

En: Oh, okay... He seems to edge away from you. "Well *I* certainly have full confidence in your competence. And the future," he adds quickly.
Zh: “哦，好吧……”他似乎离你远了一些。“*我*对你的能力完全有信心。还有未来，”他飞快地补充了一句。

En: Apocalypse? He backs away, visibly worried. "I uh... I don't know, what this means, officer. I'm sorry. But thank you! I'm here to cooperate any way I can."
Zh: “世界末日？”他向后靠了靠，显然很担心。“我……我不知道，这是什么意思，警官。我很抱歉。不过谢谢你！我会尽可能配合你的。”

En: Looks delicious. (Point to the sandwich.) "Can I have a bite of that?"
Zh: “看起来很美味。”（指着三明治。）“我能尝一口吗？”

En: When the dissidents come to *rape* our country, he hides, but try to get a bite of his dear sandwich and he gets claws? You're a special kind of a vermin, Gaston.
Zh: “当异见分子来*强暴*我们的国家时，他躲了起来，但是想试着咬一口他亲爱的三明治，他的爪子就伸出来了？你还真是个了不得的寄生虫呢，加斯顿。”

En: Sorry, officer, I'm sorry, I didn't mean it in a bad way, he continues cautiously. "But the sandwich is mine, I'm not gonna share it."
Zh: “抱歉，警官，我很抱歉，我没有恶意的，”他小心翼翼地继续说道：“不过这个三明治是我的，我不会跟别人分享。”

En: Believe me, officer, I wish I could help you, but I need this sandwich to keep my blood sugar stable. He's squirming, avoiding your gaze. "In my age you need to pay attention to these things."
Zh: “相信我，警官，我也想帮你，不过我需要这个三明治来保持血糖稳定。”他扭动着，回避着你的目光。“我这把年纪需要注意这种事情。”

En: FUCK OFF, it's mine! He jerks away, immediately startled by his own reaction.
Zh: “滚开，这是我的！”他猛地躲开了，瞬间把自己都吓了一跳。

En: You're not hearing me, man. I *need* the sandwich!
Zh: “你没听见我说话吗，老兄。我*需要*这个三明治！”

En: *Please*, friend, let's just share it.
Zh: “*求你了*，朋友，跟我一起分享吧。”

En: I'm sorry, officer, but I really don't share food, he says and quickly adds: "Nothing personal, it's just a principle."
Zh: “我很抱歉，警官，但我真的不会跟别人分享食物的，”他话音刚落，又飞快地补了一句：“不是针对你，这是原则问题。”

En: The only one you have.
Zh: “这是你唯一的原则。”

En: The sandwich looks like a culinary wonder, well made and abundant in components. The author sure knew their craft.
Zh: 这个三明治看起来就像是一个烹饪奇迹，制作精妙，材料丰富。作者很了解烹饪这门手艺。

En: Suddenly you realize how hungry you are. The last time you ate must have been... god knows when.
Zh: 突然间，你意识到自己有多饿。距离上次吃东西肯定……天知道是什么时候的事了。

En: Don't be a dick, Gaston, there's plenty for everyone.
Zh: “别这么小气，加斯顿，这玩意够每个人分的。”

En: Please, man, can I just have a bite?
Zh: “拜托，老兄，就咬一口行吗？”

En: Okay... I'm not that hungry anyway.
Zh: “好吧……反正我也没那么饿。”

En: In addition to the obvious slice of ham -- a fat one -- you notice a brim of a tomato peeking from below. And... is that mayonnaise?
Zh: 除了明显的火腿片之外——而且很肥厚——你还注意到下面露出西红柿的边缘。而且……那是蛋黄酱吗？

En: You can barely hear him. The sweet smell of pickles in harmony with garlic butter and marinated onions emanating from the sandwich is driving your mind in a singular direction -- it must be yours.
Zh: 你几乎听不见他的声音了。泡菜的香甜跟三明治散发出的大蒜黄油和腌渍洋葱的香味彼此协调，把你的思维引向了唯一的方向——你一定要拥有它。

En: *chews loudly*
Zh: *大声地咀嚼*

En: Yes, yes, the good old days. The all or nothing days.
Zh: “是啊，是啊，过去的好日子。孤注一掷的日子。”

En: Would you like to play alone tomorrow?
Zh: “你想明天一个人玩吗？”

En: How long have we known each other? Almost eighty years?
Zh: “我们俩都认识多久了？快80年了吧？”

En: You are the angriest man I know, René.
Zh: “你是我见过的最愤怒的男人，雷内。”

En: *Mon dieu*, the pain is exceptionally bad today...
Zh: “*我的天啦*，今天疼的特别厉害……”

En: Not bad, René, you might stand a chance this time.
Zh: “还不赖，雷内，这次你可能还有机会。”

En: Argh.. my back! I swear it's getting worse...
Zh: “啊……我的背！我发誓它越来越糟糕了……”

En: I'll just measure... *ouch*. I can barely move...
Zh: “我只要量一下……*哎哟*。我都快动不了了……”

En: All right then. Change of topic.
Zh: “那好吧。换个话题。”

En: Of course, officer, I hope I can help you. He nods enthusiastically. "What's on your mind?"
Zh: “当然，警官，我希望能帮到你。”他热情地点点头。“你在想什么呢？”

En: So you're not an actual member?
Zh: “所以你并不是真正的成员？”

En: Oh yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: He just fluffs it up.
Zh: 他只是抖抖机灵。

En: Of course -- don't *sink* into anything. Don't sink into any fight. Only sink into your couch.
Zh: “当然——不要*陷入*任何东西。不要陷入任何战斗。只要陷在你的沙发里。”

En: C'mon, you must have heard something...
Zh: “拜托，你肯定听说过什么吧……”

En: Odd... He doesn't seem to be lying, but there's something off here.
Zh: 奇怪……他好像并没有撒谎，不过肯定有点不太对劲。

En: No, no-no, he rushes to assure you. "I'm not holding anything back. I swear. I'm not even anyo---"\n
Zh: “不不不，”他急着向你担保。“我绝对没有有所保留，我发誓。我甚至都——”

En: I'm *not*, he assures you. "I'm not even anyo---"
Zh: “我*没有*，”他向你保证。“我甚至没有——”

En: He means caviar-socialists.
Zh: 他的意思是说一套做一套的工人主义者。

En: And who is this *Evrart* you know?
Zh: “那你认识的这个*艾弗拉特*是谁？”

En: How do you know Evrart?
Zh: “你是怎么认识艾弗拉特的？”

En: Are you a Union member?
Zh: “你是工会成员吗？”

En: Thanks, that's all for now. (Conclude.)
Zh: “谢谢，暂时先这样吧。”（总结。）

En: Evrart *Claire*. Everyone in Martinaise knows the Claire brothers, he says solemnly. "I taught these boys human studies and history in the gymnasium."
Zh: “艾弗拉特·*克莱尔*。马丁内斯所有人都认识克莱尔兄弟，”他郑重其事地说道。“我曾在体育馆给这两兄弟教历史和人类学。”

En: Everyone in Martinaise knows the Claire brothers, he says solemnly. "I taught these boys human studies and history in the gymnasium."
Zh: “马丁内斯所有人都认识克莱尔兄弟，”他郑重其事地说道。“我在文科中学教这些孩子人类学和历史。”

En: What do *you* know about history? The carabineer snaps at Gaston. "You never witnessed history. Only heard about it -- years later -- when it had already moved on. You don't know history."
Zh: “*你*懂什么历史？”老兵冲着加斯顿吼到。“你从来没有见证过历史。只是听说罢了——那都是几年以后——它都已经继续前进了。你根本不懂历史。”

En: Hmm... so Gaston taught both Easy Leo and the Claire Brothers.
Zh: 嗯……所以加斯顿教过简单利奥和克莱尔兄弟。

En: The old soldier mumbles something under his breath and turns to face the sea.
Zh: 老兵低声嘟囔着什么，然后转身面向大海。

En: Let's try not to get caught in the... The lieutenant lowers his voice: "Crossfire."
Zh: “我们还是尽量不要陷入……”警督压低声音：“他们的言语大战。”

En: *The blind leading the blind* doesn't even begin to describe it, is what he said.
Zh: 他的意思是*瞎子领瞎子*根本都无法描述。

En: Lest we leave riddled with bullet holes. This animosity is ancient.
Zh: 以免我们被打成筛子。这里的敌意非常古老了。

En: Are you a *caviar socialist*?
Zh: “你是个*伪左翼分子*吗？”

En: *Gymnasium*. I love that word.
Zh: *健身房*（这是个多义词，也有这种含义）。我喜欢这个词。

En: What a prick, the jolly man declares, with a sigh. "The officer was addressing me, not you, *capitaine*. Where were we?"
Zh: “真是个混球，”快乐的男人叹了口气，断言到。“警官在问我，不是你，*上尉*。我们说道哪里了？”

En: Oh... His cheeks turn red again. "In many ways, yes. Like an honorary member. I attend meetings and parties. Help with little things. Evrart, Edgar, and the older *Débardeurs* all know me."
Zh: “哦……”他的脸又变红了。“在很多方面来说，是的。就像是名誉成员吧。我会参加会议和派对。帮助处理点小事。艾弗拉特，埃德加还有其他年纪大一点的*装卸工*都知道我。”

En: 'In many ways'?
Zh: “‘在很多方面’？”

En: Not in the technical sense... His eyes fix on the boules in the crater. "I don't have a vote or a membership card. But Evrart keeps me on the payroll. Just for the little things."
Zh: “从专业角度来讲，不是……”他的目光固定在弹坑里的大球上。“我没有投票权，也没有会员卡。不过艾弗拉特的工资单上有我的名字。只是做点小事而已。”

En: What are the 'little things' you do for Evrart?
Zh: “那你帮艾弗拉特做了什么‘小事’呢？”

En: Can you help me get inside the harbour?
Zh: “你能帮我进入港口吗？”

En: Of course he's not a member! He's not a member of anything. I knew that. He frowns. "He's a weathervane -- turns to where the wind blows and tries to look important."
Zh: “他当然不是成员了！他不是任何地方的成员。我知道的很清楚。”他皱起眉头。“他就是个风向标——风吹到哪，他就转到哪，想摆出一副自己很重要的样子。”

En: Respects?! The old carabineer can't believe what he's hearing.
Zh: “尊重？！”老兵简直不相信自己听到了什么。

En: So that's what it was, before! Him hiding something. He tries to make it look like he's a big deal in the Union and now the illusion is disintegrating before your -- and René's -- eyes.
Zh: 所以之前是这么回事！他有所隐瞒。他试着装出一副自己是工会大人物的样子，现在这个错觉正在瓦解，就在您的——还有雷内的——眼前。

En: He doesn't know anything, because no one tells him anything. He's an outsider.
Zh: 他什么都不知道，因为没人告诉过他什么。他是个局外人。

En: I hate the socialist rabble, he continues, "but even siding with *them* is better than living your entire life on the fence, never committing to anything. Pick a damn side already!"
Zh: “我恨那些工人主义暴徒，”他继续说道，“不过站在*他们*那边甚至都好过当一辈子的墙头草，永远不做出任何承诺。好歹要挑一边吧。”

En: Writing work mostly, he smiles. "Occasionally he needs something written and I happen to have a way with words, people say."
Zh: “主要是文书工作，”他笑了。“他时不时需要写点东西，而我正好懂得舞文弄墨，大家都这么说。”

En: What kind of things do you write for him?
Zh: “那你为他写了什么东西呢？”

En: Oh, nothing official, I assure you. Just essays for the newspapers. About Martinaise and how things are and how they *could* be. Evrart and I have these long talks where...
Zh: “哦，我可以向你保证，都不是官方的东西。只是在报纸上发表的短文。关于马丁内斯，事情是怎么样的，还有事情*可能*会怎么样。艾弗拉特和我长谈过……”

En: Where he tells his little *penman* exactly what to say! It's commie propaganda, plain and simple. You should be ashamed of yourself.
Zh: “跟他的小*笔者*谈到底该说什么！宣传康米主义，苍白又简单。你应该为自己感到羞愧。”

En: That's why Gaston isn't a Union member. Evrart needs a 'neutral' party to speak his ideas.
Zh: 这就是加斯顿不是工会成员的原因。艾弗拉特需要一个‘中立者’来陈述自己的观点。

En: I do 'little things' for Evrart myself. Nothing wrong with that.
Zh: “我也在帮艾弗拉特做点‘小事’。这不算什么问题。”

En: I do 'little things' for Evrart too. I feel terrible about it.
Zh: “我也在帮艾弗拉特做点‘小事’。我感觉很不好。”

En: Let's stay on the track here, guys.
Zh: “我们要保证走在正确的轨道上，伙计们。”

En: On the Union-track?
Zh: “工会的轨道吗？”

En: That's just the way things work around here, he declares merrily. "No reason to feel bad about it."
Zh: “这里的事情就是这样的，”他愉快地断言到。“没必要为此感到难过。”

En: No, thank you, he nods, smiling wide. "For being consummate professionals. You'll have this case wrapped up in no time."\n
Zh: “不，谢谢你，”他点点头，笑的很灿烂。“你是个完美的专业人士。你很快会解决这个案件的。”

En: Thank you officer, he nods, smiling wide. "For being a consummate professional. You'll have this case wrapped up in no time."
Zh: “谢谢你，警官，”他点点头，笑的很灿烂。“你是个完美的专业人士。你很快会解决这个案件的。”

En: No, I'm sorry, officer. he shakes his head. "I don't even have a keycard. Evrart usually sends someone to get me, when he wants to talk."
Zh: “不，我很抱歉，警官。”他摇摇头。“我甚至连出入证都没有。艾弗拉特想聊聊的时候，通常会派人来接我。”

En: So you *are* a 'weathervane'?
Zh: “所以你*的确*是一个‘风向标’了？”

En: I prefer *highly-adaptable* myself, he chuckles merrily. "Has a better ring to it, don't you think?"
Zh: “我更倾向于*适应力强*这种说法，”他愉快地笑了。“更好听一些，你不觉得吗？”

En: I don't even consider myself a regular socialist. Politics is not really something I involve myself in, officer.
Zh: 老人笑了起来。“我甚至都不觉得自己个合格的工人主义者。我真的不想参与政治，警官。”

En: Wait, how can you write those newspaper pieces if you're not *political*?
Zh: “等一下，如果你不想参与*政治*的话，还怎么给报纸写文章呢？”

En: That's smart. I keep my nose clean too.
Zh: “这样挺聪明的。我也不喜欢惹麻烦。”

En: Officer, I have a vivid imagination. He laughs again. "And like I said, I have a way with words. That's enough. No need to sink into politics."
