Zh: 还剩下0.4秒。厚厚的枪管顶端有六个小黑点，就像一个蜂巢。这是一支*诺克机枪*。只要扣动一次扳机，就能射出6枚子弹。

En: Titus -- behind you -- must be aiming at him right now. Don't forget, there's additional reinforcements. Just survive this...
Zh: 提图斯——在你身后——现在肯定瞄准了他。别忘了，还有额外的增援。只要能挺过这个……

En: There's no way, you're just gonna die. Just close your eyes when it happens...
Zh: 不可能，你会死的。当它发生的时候，只要闭上你的眼睛就好……

En: You got hit. The armour took most of it, but still your rib cage burns. Feels like blood is slowly seeping into your lungs...
Zh: 你中枪了。盔甲承受了大部分的伤害，但你的胸腔还是有灼烧感。感觉鲜血正慢慢渗入你的肺部……

En: The look of vengeance. Framed by melting skin. This is the last thing he will do on Earth -- but he *will* do it. He will end you.
Zh: 复仇的模样。被框在了融化的皮肤里。这会是他在地球上做的最后一件事——但他还是*会做的*。他会干掉你。

En: A look of happiness. His eyes seem unnaturally bright, shining like stars. Something in the fear must distort him somehow. He is evil. And the end.
Zh: 欢乐的模样。他的眼睛似乎亮的不太自然，像星星一样闪烁。恐惧里的某种东西让他莫名地扭曲了。他就是邪恶。他就是结束。

En: The look of vengeance. Framed in blood, lips shaking. This is the last thing he'll do on Earth -- but he *will* do it. He is your end.
Zh: 复仇的模样。被框在了血流不止的颤抖嘴唇上。这会是他在地球上做的最后一件事——但他还是*会做的*。他就是你的结局。

En: The cop! Protect the cop, he's down...
Zh: “警察！保护警察，他倒下了……”

En: Warm blood pools underneath you. It's sticky -- and there's so much of it.
Zh: 温暖的血液从你身下汇聚成池。感觉很粘稠——而且，有好多好多。

En: You're bleeding like a pig, but the bullet *seems* to have gone right through you. Don't go into shock and you might make it.
Zh: 你像头猪一样血流不止，不过子弹*似乎*直接穿过了你的身体。不要休克，你也许能挺住。

En: DON'T GO INTO SHOCK. HOLD ON.
Zh: 不要休克。坚持住。

En: This is a stupid world...
Zh: “这是个愚蠢的世界……”

En: Stay with me! You feel burning hot tears streaming from your eyes.
Zh: “坚持住！”你感觉自己的眼睛里流下滚烫的热泪。

En: Stay with me! You feel burning hot tears streaming from your eyes.
Zh: “坚持住！”你感觉自己的眼睛里流下滚烫的热泪。

En: Yes! Keep talking! The lieutenant pushes down on your wound, hard. "You hear me?! Stay awake!"
Zh: “是的！继续说话！”警督用手重重地按住了你的伤口。“你听见了吗？！清醒一点！”

En: Everyone is so evil.
Zh: “所有人都很邪恶。”

En: I don't want to be anymore. It hurts.
Zh: “我不想再继续了。这样好疼。”

En: It said I have a vast soul. Do I have a... vast soul?
Zh: “它说我有一个广阔的灵魂。我有一个……广阔的灵魂吗？”

En: I can't forget it. Even when I drank so much...
Zh: “我忘不掉。就算是我喝了那么多的时候……”

En: I shouldn't have called her. Now she hates me.
Zh: “我不该打给她的。现在她很恨我。”

En: She would have started loving me again but I called her and now she won't.
Zh: “她本来会重新爱上我的……但是我给她打电话了，现在她不会了。”

En: She would've started loving me again... but I called her and now she won't.
Zh: “她本来会重新爱上我的……但是我给她打电话了，现在她不会了。”

En: Please call her, Kim.... She won't listen to me.
Zh: “请打给她，金……她不会听我说话的。”

En: A big pillow falls on you. Cold nylon and blood. Slumped on you as the lights go out. All the lights. And the sounds. Of the world. Disappear.
Zh: 一个枕头倒在你身上。冰冷的尼龙和鲜血重重地压在你身上，灯熄灭了。所有灯都熄灭了。所有声音。整个世界。都消失了。

En: Of course. I know you do. Everybody leaves, when they get the chance. Go on, keep falling. Deeper... take the door.
Zh: 当然了。我知道你想的。只要抓到机会，每个人都会离开。继续，继续坠落。再深入一些……进门去。

En: Will I be a... ghost now?
Zh: 现在我变成……幽灵了吗？

En: It's the living who are ghosts. The dead are silent. They don't rattle windows or write letters in blood. The living do. Leave them behind. Rest.
Zh: 活着的人们才是幽灵。死去的那些是寂静。他们不会把窗户晃得嘎吱作响，也不会用鲜血留下信息。活着的人才会。把他们抛在身后。休息吧。

En: He's *not* taking it. His body is not taking it. Oh god, no, he's not disintegrating -- he's swelling up instead. Over the hours. Hurting. Moaning in his sleep.
Zh: 他*没有*接受。他的身体也没有。天啦，不，他没有崩溃——相反，他更加膨胀了。时间流逝。疼痛继续。在他的睡梦中呻吟。

En: And rotting. And being disinfected. And smelling of drugs and feeling saliva in his mouth. Drifting in painkillers. Thrashing in his wound sleep.
Zh: 正在腐烂。正在消毒。还有药品的味道，感觉嘴里有唾液。漂流在止痛药里。在满是伤痕的梦中辗转反侧。

En: There is a radio in the distance. A radio of the world. Playing sounds: Good morning, Elysium. Soon you will return to the world.
Zh: 远处有一台收音机。这个世界的收音机。里面正播放出声音：早上好，极乐世界。很快你就会回到那个世界。

En: He can't go. Not before the case is solved.
Zh: 他不能走。案子还没破完之前不可以。

En: You're thirsty. Reach for the glass of water by the bed. The world is still there. Sleep some more...
Zh: 你口干舌燥。把手伸向了床边的玻璃杯。世界还在那里。再多睡一会儿……

En: Hours turn to days. Soon he will get up again and go through it -- again! Again! Finally, we know what the infernal engine was, outside -- the clarion call...
Zh: 数小时变成了一天又一天。很快他会再次醒来，再经历一遍——一遍！又一遍！最终，我们知道地狱的发动机是什么样的，外面——是感人的召唤……

En: No. It was *him*. *He* is the infernal engine, can't you see? He never stops. He only gets worse.
Zh: 不。是*他*。*他*就是地狱的发动机，你难道看不见吗？他只会变得越来越糟糕。

En: The engine of a Coupris Kineema...
Zh: 一台库普瑞斯锐影的发动机……

En: It was *him*. *He* is the infernal engine. He never stops. He only gets worse.
Zh: 不。是*他*。*他*就是地狱的发动机。他永远不知停歇。他只会变得越来越糟糕。

En: Life gets haaaaaaaaaard. But we go ooooooooooooooon...
Zh: 生活很艰难难难难难难。但我们还要继续续续续续续。

En: An unbelievable shot from the lieutenant.
Zh: 警督那让人难以置信的一枪。

En: Around you -- time starts moving again, the sounds of violence and panic.
Zh: 在你周围——时间再次开始流动，传来暴力和惊恐的声音。

En: This "Poste L'Aventurier" mail collection box has been heavily vandalized with graffito. A closer inspection reveals two bullet holes in the front.
Zh: 这个“冒险家邮局”的邮筒被涂鸦严重破坏了。靠近仔细检查一下，会发现前面还有两个弹孔。

En: Good mail delivery box. (Pat the box.)
Zh: “真是个好邮筒。”（拍拍邮筒。）

En: Fuck you, mail delivery box! (Kick it.)
Zh: “去你妈的，邮筒！”（踹它。）

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: The box seems happy.
Zh: 邮筒似乎很开心。

En: There is a hollow ring as you kick the box. It sounds like it's mostly empty. Your toe hurts now.
Zh: 你踢中邮筒的时候，发出一阵空洞的声响。听起来它基本上是空的。现在你的脚趾头疼了起来。

En: Eat shit, pig!, "FUCKED BY THE CUNN" and "Saint-G" with a crown have been scribbled on it. "Jennie is a WHORE" and "*Baise cette* mailbox!" also.
Zh: 上面涂画着“吃屎吧，猪头！”，“坤诺到此一搞”，还有戴着一顶皇冠的“圣徒”。除此之外，还有“珍妮是个婊子”和“搞死邮筒！”。

En: I feel you, mail collection box.
Zh: 我能感觉到你，邮筒。

En: Been there, *Poste L'Aventurier* mail collection box... been there.
Zh: 我也经历过，*冒险家邮局*的邮筒……我也经历过。

En: Mail collection box, you should man the fuck up.
Zh: 邮筒，你他妈应该爷们一点。

En: The mail collection box seems cathartic, thankful even. So do you. You shudder. Then you swallow.
Zh: 邮筒似乎在宣泄，甚至是感激。你也一样。你打了个哆嗦。然后咽了下去。

En: The mailbox does not know how to man up, it is an inanimate object.
Zh: 邮筒不知道怎么爷们一点，它只是一个静物。

En: And then suddenly you see it: over all the other graffitos someone has -- using the tip of a very sharp knife -- cut the words: REVACHOL FOREVER.
Zh: 接着，你突然看见了：在所有其他涂鸦中间，有一个人——用非常锋利的刀尖——刻下了一句话：瑞瓦肖万岁。

En: The mail collection box has no faith in your psychopathic manipulations.
Zh: 邮筒对你病态的操控全无信心。

En: There is a hollow, saddened ring as you kick the L'Aventurier mail collection box. It sounds betrayed, in disbelief even.
Zh: 当你踢中冒险家邮筒时，传来一阵空洞的、悲伤的声音。听起来像是背叛，甚至是怀疑。

En: Cool. The lieutenant nods approvingly. "You really showed that mail collection box."
Zh: “挺酷的，”警督赞同地点点头。“你真的给那个邮筒上了一课。”

En: He does not actually think it's cool. If anything, the lieutenant feels sorry for the poor box -- he's leaning in to inspect the layers of graffito that deface it.
Zh: 他其实根本不觉得酷。如果有什么的话，警督为这个可怜的邮筒感到抱歉——他靠过去查看丑化它的一层层涂鸦。

En: Oh, you're being sarcastic. I get it. It was actually a dick move.
Zh: “哦，你是在讽刺我。我明白了。这其实是个很混账的举动。”

En: (Rub your toe.) "You think so? You think it was cool?"
Zh: （按摩你的脚趾头。）“你真这么认为？你觉得这样很酷？”

En: Say nothing. [Leave.]
Zh: 什么也不说。[离开。]

En: The lieutenant pats the box on the head, ever so slightly, before walking away.
Zh: 在离开之前，警督拍了拍邮筒的顶部，动作非常轻柔。

En: I don't. Mail the letter and let's be done with this.
Zh: “我没有。把这封信寄出去，这件事就算结了。”

En: I don't. Let's go.
Zh: “我没有。我们走吧。”

En: The lieutenant pats the box, ever so slightly.
Zh: 警督拍拍邮筒，动作非常轻柔。

En: Dodged the bullet there... He must see the box as the weaker of the two, and you as a bully -- something he doesn't stand for.
Zh: 躲过了一劫……他肯定把邮筒看成了你们俩中的弱者，而你是霸凌者——他不支持这种事。

En: He must see the box as the weaker of the two, and you as a bully -- something he doesn't stand for.
Zh: 他肯定把邮筒看成了你们俩中的弱者，而你是霸凌者——他不支持这种事。

En: Drop the white envelope into the mailbox.
Zh: 把白信封扔进邮筒。

En: The dented yellow mailbox greets you with it's graffito and bullet holes in the front.
Zh: 凹陷的黄色邮筒用满身的涂鸦和前面的弹孔问候着你。

En: The dented yellow mailbox greets you with it's graffito, bullet holes and an RCM sticker.
Zh: 凹陷的黄色邮筒用满身的涂鸦，弹孔和一张RCM的贴纸问候着你。

En: I knew I'd get to use this mailbox -- for something.
Zh: 我知道我会用到这个邮筒的——因为某些事情。

En: I knew we'd get to use this mailbox -- for something.
Zh: “我知道我们会用到这个邮筒的——因为某些事情。”

En: Other than developing a personal relationship? Looks like you were right.
Zh: 除了发展个人关系之外吗？看来你是对的。

En: There is a hollow, saddened ring as you kick the L'Aventurier mail collection box. It sounds betrayed, in disbelief even.
Zh: 当你踢中冒险家邮筒时，传来一阵空洞的、悲伤的声音。听起来像是背叛，甚至是怀疑。

En: There is a hollow ring as you kick the box. It sounds like it's mostly empty. Your toe hurts now.
Zh: 你踢中邮筒的时候，发出一阵空洞的声响。听起来它基本上是空的。现在你的脚趾头疼了起来。

En: The mail collection box has no faith in your psychopathic manipulations.
Zh: 邮筒对你病态的操控全无信心。

En: The box seems happy.
Zh: 邮筒似乎很开心。

En: Other than kicking? You were right!
Zh: 除了踢它吗？我们是对的！

En: About a week's worth of mail has collected in there. They'll empty this very soon.
Zh: 这里大概积攒了一星期的邮件。很快他们就会把它清空。

En: Let's go back to Evrart. The lieutenant turns towards the industrial harbour. "This better be worth it -- after what we did."
Zh: “我们回去见艾弗拉特吧。”警督转身面向工业港。“这一切最好是值得的——在我们做了这些事之后。”

En: Yes. For sending mail.
Zh: “是的。寄信。”

En: Other than kicking?
Zh: “除了踢它吗？”

En: Alright, let's go back to Evrart. The lieutenant turns towards the industrial harbour. "If we don't mention anything to him, he won't know before it's too late."
Zh: “好吧，我们回去见艾弗拉特。”警督转身面向工业港。“如果我们对他只字不提的话，在一切变得太迟之前，他是不会知道的。”

En: It's not going to be that easy. We need to dirty our hands with the signatures. He looks around. "I assume that's the goal here."
Zh: “不会那么容易的。我们需要用签名的事弄脏我们的手。”他四下看了看。“我认为这才是真正的目标。”

En: It's not going to be that easy. Evrart wants you to dirty your hands getting these signatures and he will quickly find out if you just mail.
Zh: 不会那么容易的。艾弗拉特想用签名的事弄脏你的手，如果你就这么寄出去的话，他很快就会发现的。

En: A faint sticker on the side reads: "RCM Emergencies Desk no 8-100-2", with a slogan: "Mankind, be vigilant!"
Zh: 侧面有一个模糊的贴纸，上面写着：“RCM紧急呼叫台8-100-2号”，还有一条标语：“人类，警惕一些！”

En: Drop the white envelope into the mailbox.
Zh: 把白信封扔进邮筒。

En: But the documents inside the envelope still lack the required signatures...
Zh: 但是信封里面得文件还缺少必要的签名……

En: You drop the white envelope in the darkness. It lands with a soft thud on what sounds like a couple of letters.
Zh: 你把白信封扔进黑暗之中。随着轻柔地砰的一声，它似乎落在了一堆信件上。

En: Drop the white envelope into the mailbox.
Zh: 把白信封扔进邮筒。

En: For good or ill, the deed is done.
Zh: 不管是好是坏，这件事结束了。

En: Your toe has suffered damage. It hurts!
Zh: 你的脚趾头受伤了。疼得厉害！

En: Drop the white envelope into the mailbox.
Zh: 把白信封扔进邮筒。

En: Probably did the right thing. You can't trust that slug Evrart -- you *know* he's going to play you somehow.
Zh: 你可能做了一件正确的事。你不能相信那个懒惰的艾弗拉特——你*知道*他会耍你的。

En: Believe me, I'd know. I *know* spectres. (Rub your temple.)
Zh: “相信我，我知道的。我*知道*什么是幽灵。”（揉揉你的太阳穴。）

En: Exactly! How can one know shit?
Zh: “没错！你能知道什么呢？”

En: The lieutenant sighs. "We should move along."
Zh: 警督叹了口气。“我们该走了。”

En: Definitely a coincidence.
Zh: 绝对是个巧合。

En: Your lack of imagination is baffling. But you do make up for it with... yes. Questions.
Zh: 你的想象力缺乏的实在是太令人沮丧了。不过你的确可以弥补这一点……没错，用问题。

En: You're right of course. Never mind then.
Zh: “你当然是对的。不要紧。”

En: The lieutenant sighs.
Zh: 警督叹了口气。

En: It's a sigh of relief.
Zh: 这是一声解脱的叹息。

En: For all their nihilistic posturing, these young men are not lacking in youthful idealism.
Zh: 尽管有这一切故作姿态的虚无主义，这些年轻人并不缺乏富有朝气的理想主义。

En: SKULLS -- now there's a strong organizational title.
Zh: 骷髅头——现在有一个强势的组织名称了。

En: Okay then -- let's indulge in some intellectual exchange. These young men seem eager to share their beliefs.
Zh: 那好吧——让我们纵情于知识交换吧。这些年轻人似乎很渴望分享他们的信念。

En: These *skull* people are more than an authority. They're deities.
Zh: 这些叫*骷髅头*的人不仅仅是一种权威。他们是神明。

En: Besmertnyé or the Besmertie -- the *immortals* -- are West Revacholian crime syndicates.
Zh: 贝斯梅特纳或者贝斯莫蒂——*不朽者*——是瑞瓦肖西部的犯罪辛迪加组织。

En: SKULLS don't talk to pigs. Or each other. They just fuck and fight.
Zh: “骷髅头不会跟猪头说话。彼此也不交谈。他们只会破坏和战斗。”

En: What does that mean?
Zh: “那是什么意思？”

En: Fucking *philosophy*, man. You can do aggressive shit with philosophy. Justify shit.
Zh: “该死的*哲学*，老兄。有了哲学，你可以用哲学做出激进的事。正当的事。”

En: Like murder. Or wasting your life away.
Zh: 就像谋杀。或者是浪费你的生命。

En: All right -- we have a lead on the gloves.
Zh: 好吧——手套有线索了。

En: Throw him off his game.
Zh: 让他发挥失常。

En: You don't feel very scared.
Zh: 你没觉得有多吓人。

En: What would these guys have to offer to any organization?
Zh: 这些家伙能给任何组织提供什么好处？

En: Nothing.
Zh: “没事。”

En: You wanted to know which jacket I'd wear, right? Neither. I'd wear neither.
Zh: “你想知道我会穿哪件夹克，对吧？都不穿。我两件都不穿。”

En: My clothes make me sad, I need to *fun* it up.
Zh: “我的衣服让我感觉很难过，我需要找点*乐子*。”

En: Odd. There isn't a hint of hate in them. It's like they're 'Pissf****t' and 'Fuck the World' out of some kind moral obligation.
Zh: 奇怪。他们身上没有一丝仇恨的痕迹。感觉他们是出于某种道义上的责任而‘弱水三千只取一尿饮’和‘花花世界我一人干翻’的。

En: The lieutenant, on your left, is unusually lenient toward them.
Zh: 警督，站在你左边，通常对他们都是很宽容的。

En: Okay, we get it. You look like total shit -- we look flashy as hell. What about it? There's a spark of sympathy in the youth's eyes.
Zh: “好吧，我们明白了。你看起来就像坨屎一样——我们看起来光彩照人。那又怎么了？”年轻人的眼睛里闪烁着一丝同情的光芒。

En: Please...
Fuck it, I don't deserve a cool jacket, I suck.
Zh: “妈的，我配不上一件酷炫的夹克，我糟糕透了。”

En: Leather would be such a cool look on me...
Zh: “皮夹克穿在我身上肯定挺酷的……”

En: Fine, fuck it. The young man takes his jacket off. "Here, take it then. I can't handle this sad shit."
Zh: “好吧，去他妈的。”年轻男人脱下夹克。“给你，拿着吧。我受不了这种悲惨的玩意。”

En: It's true. He simply can't -- it's called empathy.
Zh: 这是真的。他就是做不到——这叫做同理心。

En: Wow. I did not see that coming. You better wear that jacket with pride!
Zh: 哇哦。这我倒是没料到。你最好骄傲地穿上这件夹克！

En: Fuck that, you're not getting mine, the other one snaps at you. "My dad's a lawyer in La Delta. He'll have your badge!"
Zh: “去你妈的，我才不会给你，”另一人厉声说道。“我爸爸是德尔塔的一个律师。他会让你交出警徽的！”

En: N-no, uh... He looks startled. "He's like a... like a volunteer lawyer... a total bitch. Cites law and does dishes..."
Zh: “不不，呃……”他看起来吓坏了。“他是一个……志愿律师……彻头彻尾的婊子。引用一下法律条文，刷盘子洗碗……”

En: Wait, World-fucker, you got a rich dad?
Zh: “等等，花花世界我一人干翻，你有个有钱的老爸？”

En: (Turn to Kim.) "Remember when I said I'd like the 'Pissf****t' one? I got it! Wish I'd gotten the other one for you..."
Zh: （转向金。）“还记得我说我想要‘弱水三千只取一尿饮’那件吗？我拿到了！真希望我可以给你搞到另外那件……”

En: (Turn to Kim.) "Fuck it, we don't need the other one. You said that you're more of a Pissf****t kinda guy, so I got the jacket for you!"
Zh: （转向金。）“该死，我们不需要另外那件。你说你更像是‘弱水三千只取一尿饮’那种人，所以我帮你搞到那件夹克了！”

En: Fuck him. We don't need his rich dad jacket anyway.
Zh: 去他的吧。反正我们不需要他有钱老爸的夹克。

En: You know what, cop? Fuck you, that's not what this is about, you're not getting my jacket!
Zh: “你知道吗，死条子？去你妈的，不是这么回事，我的夹克是不会给你的！”

En: No, he won't.
Zh: “不，他不会的。”

En: The lieutenant is lightly amused by the situation.
Zh: 警督略微被眼下这种情况逗乐了。

En: (Turn to Kim.) "I did say I prefer the 'Pissf****t' one, but since I didn't get the other one -- you should have it!"
Zh: （转向金。）“我说过我更喜欢‘弱水三千只取一尿饮’那件，不过既然我没拿到另外那件——你应该拿着这件！”

En: (Turn to Kim.) "I know you said you're more of a Pissf****t kinda guy, but we only have one jacket and I want it."
Zh: （转向金。）“我知道你说你更像是‘弱水三千只取一尿饮’那种人，不过我们只有这一件夹克，而且我自己想要。”

En: That's okay. I'm not wearing that jacket.
Zh: “没事的。我不会穿那件夹克的。”

En: I'm absolutely okay with not having one.
Zh: “即使没有，我也完全没问题。”

En: Okay, well. I'll keep it then.
Zh: “好吧。那我自己留着了。”

En: Do. I'm fine with that.
Zh: “好的。我完全没问题。”

En: Wait -- how?
Zh: 等等——怎么做？

En: Suggest they're massive SKULLS. C'mon!
Zh: 暗示他们是了不起的骷髅头。来吧！

En: He gets it. Passive-aggressive flattery.
Zh: 他明白了。消极对抗型奉承。

En: Now bring it to the jackets and.... yes, start *shouting*!
Zh: 现在往夹克上说，然后……是的，开始*大喊*！

En: You got it. No need for cruelty.
Zh: 你可以的。没必要这么残忍。

En: (Lower your voice.) "Yes -- the jackets."
Zh: （压低声音。）“是的——夹克。”

En: SKULL KING! MAKE SHRUNKEN HEADS OUT OF US!
Zh: “骷髅头国王！把我们的脑袋做成干缩人头吧！”

En: Fuck you, man, take them then... He looks around the plaza -- people are noticing him now.
Zh: “去你妈的，那就拿去吧……”他环顾广场四周——大家现在都注意到他了。

En: (Turn to Kim.) "Since you said you're more of a 'Pissf****t' kinda guy, I'll take the other one."
Zh: （转向金。）“既然你说自己更像是‘弱水三千只取一尿饮’那种人，那我就要另一件了。”

En: (Turn to Kim.) "I know you said you're more of a 'Pissf****t' kinda guy, but I think I should have it for myself."
Zh: （转向金。）“我知道你说自己更像是‘弱水三千只取一尿饮’那种人，不过我还是想自己留着。”

En: (Turn to Kim.) "I said I prefer the 'Pissf****t' one, but you can have it if you like. The other one's cool too."
Zh: （转向金。）“我说自己更喜欢‘弱水三千只取一尿饮’那件，不过如果你喜欢就给你。另一件也挺酷的。”

En: (Turn to Kim.) "Remember when I said I'd like the 'Pissf****t' one? I got it -- you can have the other one!"
Zh: （转向金。）“还记得我说我想要‘弱水三千只取一尿饮’那件吗？我拿到了——你可以拿另外那件！”

En: The lieutenant watches the boys take their jackets off -- with mild amusement.
Zh: 警督看着年轻人脱下身上的夹克——带着一种淡淡的消遣意味。

En: I'm absolutely okay with not having either one, thank you.
Zh: “我完全可以哪件都不要，谢了。”

En: That's gallant of you, but I'm not wearing either jacket.
Zh: “你很英勇，不过我哪件都不穿。”

En: That's okay. I wouldn't wear either one anyway.
Zh: “没事的。反正我哪件都不会穿。”

En: Why not? They're a pair. We could really raise hell. Go undercover. Hard.
Zh: “为什么不呢？这两件是一对的。我们可以引起真正的骚动。去卧底。很硬核那种。”

En: Do. I'm fine with that.
Zh: “好的。我完全没问题。”

En: This case doesn't requires us to go undercover. Or raise hell... In fact I don't think the jackets will be useful at all. I just wanted *them* to not have them anymore.
Zh: “这个案子不需要我们去卧底。或者是引起骚动……事实上，我觉得这些夹克一点用也没有。我只是不想*他们*再穿着这些衣服。”

En: But don't you want to express your individuality?
Zh: “但是，你不想表达自己的个性吗？”

En: I already *am* expressing my individuality.
Zh: “我*已经*在表达自己的个性了。”

En: Well, whatever. I'll take both of them then.
Zh: “呃，随便吧。那两件都归我了。”

En: Still -- it's good to know we have a pair. In case the need arises.
Zh: “不过——知道我们有一套总是好的。说不定以后需要呢。”

En: Cold-hearted cop...
Zh: “冷血条子……”

En: I'm not. I don't have a jacket anymore...
Zh: “我不能了。我没有夹克……”

En: Pity. The jackets are meant to complete each other. If a man were standing alone on a street corner with 'PISSF****T' written on his back, it'd just be an individual that has taken a liking to urine. And 'FUCK THE WORLD' all on its own is, frankly, generic.
Zh: 很遗憾。这两件夹克本来是要成就彼此的。如果一个人穿着一件背后写着‘弱水三千只取一尿饮’的夹克孤零零地站在街上，那就只是一个喜欢尿液的个体。而只靠‘花花世界我一人干翻’自己的话，坦白讲，又太一般了。

En: I don't know, Eric. It's cold out... The dark-haired young man just stands there, defeated. The wind blows.
Zh: 黑发年轻男子站在那里，垂头丧气。起风了。“我不知道，埃里克。外面很冷……”他终于还是对朋友开了口。

En: Yeah, the blond man replies. "Let's get out of here. The cops fucked us."
Zh: “是啊，”金发男子点头回复到。“我们离开这里吧。条子利用了我们。”

En: The need will not arise.
Zh: “这种需求永远不会出现。”

En: Good -- I wanted you *not* to express yours.
Zh: “很好——我希望你*不要*表达自己的个性。”

En: Sure, the jackets are scaring you. That's cool.
Zh: “当然，这些夹克吓到你了。挺酷的。”

En: Do you happen to know what happened to the victim's armour?
Zh: “你不会碰巧知道被害人的盔甲怎么样了吧？”

En: Nope, his mouth makes a popping sound. "But Cindy does."
Zh: “不，”他的嘴里发出一个爆破音。“不过辛迪知道。”

En: Don't tell him that, the other one cuts in, too late. "SKULLS don't tattle, man. Cindy wouldn't."
Zh: “别跟他说那个，”另一个人插了进来，不过太迟了。“骷髅头是不会闲谈的，老兄。辛迪就不会。”

En: Yeah, the girl Piss blathered out, when you asked about the armour. She's a true artist of the future, just like Arno van Eyck or Rietveldt. A real SKULL.
Zh: “是啊，当你问起那套盔甲的时候，那姑娘就会絮叨个没完。她是个真正的未来艺术家，就像阿诺·凡·艾克或者是里特维德。一个真正的骷髅头。”

En: Where can I find this 'Cindy'?
Zh: “我能去哪里找到这个‘辛迪’？”

En: You mean Cindy the SKULL?
Zh: “你是说骷髅头辛迪吗？”

En: No, the young man replies quickly, shaking his head. "A different Cindy. From out of town. She doesn't know shit about armoured gloves, that's for sure."
Zh: “不，”年轻男子一边摇摇头，一边飞快地回复到。“不是同一个辛迪。从城外来的。她完全不知道盔甲手套的事，这一点是肯定的。”

En: He *did* mean Cindy the SKULL.
Zh: 他*的确*指的就是骷髅头辛迪。

En: You already said she's a SKULL... he mumbles apologetically. "The last I saw her, she was hanging around the old apartment building near the pier."
Zh: “你已经说过她是骷髅头了……”他带着歉意地嘟囔着。“我最后一次看见她，她正在码头的一栋旧公寓附近闲荡。”

En: Just don't tell her you heard it from Piss and Fuck, alright? I think she wanted those gloves for herself. There's a plea in his eyes. "Be cool, man, okay?"
Zh: “千万别告诉她，说你是从尿兄和干弟那里听来的，好吗？我觉得她自己想要那双手套。”他的眼睛里带着一丝恳求的意味。“酷一点，老兄，好吗？”

En: I'm so glad you asked!
Zh: “我很高兴你能这么问！”

En: The question was rhetorical, he replies, raising his open hand. "The SKULLS are *the* most vicious gang of the Besmertnyé."
Zh: “这个问题是种修辞手法，”他举起张开的手，回复到。“骷髅头是贝斯梅特纳最恶毒的帮派。”

En: It's not a question, don't get into it.
Zh: 这不是个问题，别深究。

En: So what do you think we know?
Zh: “所以，你觉得我们知道什么？”

En: Sure, sure, the blond man says, quickly nodding. "Understandable. Fuck and I appreciate your effort though."
Zh: “当然，当然，”金发男一边说着，一边飞快地点点头。“能理解。不过，干弟和我还是很感谢你的努力。”

En: Let's just change the topic.
Zh: “我们换个话题。”

En: Couldn't keep up, huh? The young man shakes his head. "Sure, let's try something different then."
Zh: “跟不上了，是吗？”年轻男子摇摇头。“当然可以，那我们就试点别的吧。”

En: It's a threat, the lieutenant answers for the two rebels. "An impotent threat of violence."
Zh: “这是个威胁，”警督代替两个叛逆的年轻人做出了回答。“一种严重的暴力威胁。”

En: A threat? Good. I like those.
Zh: “威胁？很好。我喜欢。”

En: Don't fuck with me, boys. I'm one of the bad cops.
Zh: “别惹我，小伙子们。我可是那种坏警察。”

En: I just wanted to talk about music and now there's a conflict all of the sudden... it's too much. (Nervously shake your head.)
Zh: “我只想聊聊音乐，现在突然之间就冲突上了……太过了。”（紧张地摇摇头。）

En: A threat? Retaliate immediately. You'll see -- they'll fall over like bitch-leaves. These boys ain't got the *cojones*.
Zh: 一个威胁？立即展开报复。你会看见——他们会像树叶一样倒下。这些小伙子根本*没种*。

En: Uh... that's right, the young man says, looking unsure of himself. "You... you should back off and let silence rule supreme."
Zh: “呃……没错，”年轻男子说道，看起来很没自信。“你……你应该退后，让沉默主宰一切。”

En: This is ridiculous. The lieutenant gives you an irritated glance. "Boys, do you know why the gang is called SKULLS?"
Zh: “真是太荒谬了。”警督愤怒地瞥了你一眼。“小伙子们，你们知道为什么这个帮派叫骷髅头吗？”

En: What is wrong with you? Now you're just empowering these punks.
Zh: 你出什么问题了？现在你只是在这些小阿飞赋权。

En: Whoa... c'mon, man... He raises his open palms. "We're just talking here. Just words, nothing wrong with that."
Zh: “哇哦……拜托，老兄……”他举高摊开的手掌。“我们只是聊聊天。只是说说话，这没什么错吧。”

En: You wanna talk? Let's talk, boys. His eyes meet yours. "Ask them."
Zh: “你想聊天？那我们就聊聊，小伙子们。”他的目光与你交汇。“问问他们。”

En: That's right! Way to put them in the little boys corner!
Zh: 没错！把这些小伙子逼进角落！

En: Yeah, no need to throw your authority in our faces.
Zh: “是啊，没必要在我们面前展示你的权威。”

En: He liked how you decisively shut down a situation that could have turned into a farce.
Zh: 你果断地结束了一个可能演变成闹剧的形势，他喜欢这样。

En: I'm going to look you boys up in the police registry, come to both of your houses at night and MURDER YOU IN YOUR GODDAMN FUCKING SLEEP!!!
Zh: “我会去警局查你们的记录，半夜闯进你们俩的家，趁你们俩他妈的睡得正香的时候杀了你们！！！”

En: As your bellow echoes through the street, the boys quiver ever so slightly and gaze down at their shoes, pretending to be deaf.
Zh: 你的吼声在大街上回荡，两个小伙子微微颤抖，低头看着脚下的鞋，假装没有听见。

En: Right. Lieutenant Kitsuragi adjusts his spectacles. "You heard the detective. He's gonna ask you some questions now."
Zh: “好吧。”曷城警督调了调眼镜。“你们听见这位警探的话了。现在他有些问题要问。”

En: But I don't, the lieutenant interjects quickly. "In fact I dislike them so much I'm willing to drag you boys back to the station just to calm myself down."
Zh: “但是我没有，”警督突然插了一嘴。“事实上，我非常不喜欢他们，为了让自己冷静下来，我愿意把你们俩带回警局。”

En: Hey uh... there's no need for that. He raises his open palms. "We're just talking here. Joking too. Stay light, man."
Zh: “喂……没必要这样。”他举起摊开的手掌。“我们只是聊聊天。开开玩笑。放松点，老兄。”

En: Yeah, didn't you cops, like, have some questions about SKULLs or some shit?
Zh: “是啊，你们警察不是要问骷髅头什么之类的问题吗？”

En: Don't ever do that again. But good call, detective.
Zh: 再也不要这么做了。不过说得好，警探。

En: Scared little bitches...
Zh: 像小婊子一样害怕……

En: You tried to punch a twelve year old and almost knocked yourself out, the lieutenant replies with no discernible change in his tone. "I think I'm still pretty much in the clear here."
Zh: “你试过打一个12岁的小孩，而且差点还把自己搞了个筋疲力尽，”警督回复着，口气没有明显的变化。“我觉得自己在这里还是没什么阻碍的。”

En: You punched a twelve-year-old boy in the face. I think I'm still pretty much in the clear here, the lieutenant replies. There's is no discernible change in his tone.
Zh: “你朝一个12岁的孩子脸上打了一拳。我觉得自己在这里还是没什么阻碍的，”警督回复到。他的口气没有明显的变化。

En: I've come to regret my violent ways, Kim....
Zh: “我已经对自己的暴力行为感到很后悔了，金……”

En: Pick a better time, officer. The lieutenant's voice rings cold and certain. "*After* we've dealt with these wannabe cop-killers."
Zh: “挑个更好的时间吧，警官。”警督的声音冷酷又坚定。“在我们对付完这些想成为警察杀手的小伙子*之后*。”

En: He did not appreciate you undermining his authority in front of the two punks.
Zh: 他并不欣赏你在两个小阿飞面前破坏他的权威。

En: If you got something else to ask from them, do it now and let's move on. I don't want to waste any more time here.
Zh: “如果你还有别的问题要问他们，那就赶紧问，然后我们继续调查。我可不想在这里多浪费时间。”

En: You aimed a loaded gun at little girl, the lieutenant replies with no discernible change in his tone. "I think I've got a *little* bit of wiggling room here."
Zh: “你用一支上膛的枪瞄准了一个小女孩，”警督回复到，口气没有明显的变化。“我想我在这里还是有*一点*余地的。”

En: You fired a gun at a little girl, the lieutenant replies with no discernible change in his tone. "I think I've got a *little* bit of wiggling room here."
Zh: “你朝一个小女孩开了一枪，”警督回复到，口气没有明显的变化。“我想我在这里还是有*一点*余地的。”

En: Yeah, man... the other one quickly joins in. "We're just talking, philosophising. No crimes are being committed. And... and you wanted to talk too, right?"
Zh: “是啊，老兄……”另一个很快加入进来。“我们只是说说话，高谈阔论一下。没有犯罪。而且……你也只是想聊聊，对吧？”

En: Ugh... A sudden flash of panic flies across his face. "I... I didn't mean anything by that. Just playing with words here, that's all."
Zh: “啊……”他的脸上闪过一丝恐惧。“我……我没有那个意思。只是玩玩文字游戏，仅此而已。”

En: Yeah, we're not fucking kids! He glances at your partner and quickly adds: "And we're also not fucking confrontational. The mood is chill here."
Zh: “是啊，我们他妈的不是小孩！”他瞥了一眼你的搭档，快速地补充了一句：“而且我们没他妈的刚谁对抗。情绪很冷静。”

En: Still a bit rattled by the earlier shock?
Zh: 还因为刚才的震惊有些慌乱吗？

En: Yeah, I guess you're right. I'm pretty old.
Zh: “是啊，我猜你说得对。我是很老。”

En: No, no, no, he quickly assures you and takes a step back. "Just a bit older than us, ain't that right, Piss?"
Zh: “不不不，”他飞快地向你保证，然后后退一步。“只比我们大一点，对不对，尿兄？”

En: Yeah, the blond man replies with a nod. "That's what I mean. You're young as fuck, don't worry."
Zh: “是啊，”金发男子点头回复到。“我就是这个意思。你年轻的要命，别担心。”

En: But in a non-threatening and definitely legal way, the other one quickly adds and whispers something to his friend.
Zh: “但是要用没有威胁性，而且绝对合法的方式，”另一个人飞快地补充了一句，然后对着朋友小声嘀咕了几句。

En: Are you implying I might be in some sort of danger?
Zh: “你是在暗示我可能有什么危险吗？”

En: We'll fuck the system from the inside later, just be cool now. The damage will be tenfold.
Zh: ‘我们之后会从内部干翻整个系统的，现在先冷静一点。伤害会以十倍计算。’

En: Pssshhew. The young man raises two fingers to his temple and an imaginary hammer hits the cartridge.
Zh: 年轻男子默默举起两只手指放在太阳穴上，想象着扣动扳机发射子弹的样子。“嘶嘶嘶-啪。”

En: Right on, Fuck, the blond agrees and provocatively spits on the pavement. "So what's happening now?"
Zh: “你说的太对了，干弟，”金发男子表示赞同，挑衅地朝人行道上吐了口唾沫。“现在会怎么样？”

En: Ee... what?
Makes sense.
Zh: “有道理。”

En: That is a terrible metaphor.
Zh: “这是个可怕的隐喻。”

En: Blow it up!
Zh: “炸了它！”

En: Hate to admit it, but in a weird way he's got a point.
Zh: 我很不愿意承认，不过奇怪的是，他说的有点道理。

En: Is it a *coincidence* that here we have two bad-ass jackets and two bad-ass cops?
Zh: 这里有两件狂野的夹克，还有两个狂野的警察，这会是巧合吗？

En: That's an origin story for a dynamic duo right there.
Zh: 这是一对活力二人组的起源故事。

En: You always get to be the crazy one, right? Fine, then.
Zh: 你总是疯狂的那一个，对吧？那好吧。

En: So are we done here or...? one of the jacket owners asks impatiently. "You don't need us around for your secret whisper party, do you?"
Zh: “可以到此为止了吗，还是……”其中一件夹克的主人不耐烦地问到。“我们不需要参加你的秘密耳语派对，对吧？”

En: Do I have to answer that?
Zh: “我必须回答吗？”

En: If you don't *have* to answer, then we don't *have* to give you our jackets. The young man shakes his head. "Doesn't sound like you have a reason."
Zh: “如果你*没必要*回答，那我们也*没必要*把夹克交出去。”年轻男子摇摇头。“听起来你找不出什么理由。”

En: You didn't say that.
Zh: 你可没那么说。

En: 'Prospect'? Must be a hierarchical term. Probably in the lower-end.
Zh: ‘预备役？’肯定是等级体系的一个术语。可能是底层的。

En: Wow, you boys are ambitious, the lieutenant's voice rings over the plaza. "Only *prospects* and already planning a coup in the SKULLS? You're destined to go far!"
Zh: “哇哦，你们这些小伙子可真有野心，”警督的声音在广场上回荡。“还只是*预备役*就已经准备在计划颠覆骷颅头了？你们肯定能走的很远！”

En: Shut the fuck up, the youth presses through his clenched teeth, there's panic in his eyes. "Are you trying to get us killed?"
Zh: “妈的闭嘴，”年轻人从紧闭的齿缝里挤出几个词来，眼里满是惊恐。“你是想让害我们暴尸街头吗？”

En: YES, WE WANNA BE A COOL KILLER SKULLS TOO, LIKE YOU GUYS, BUT WE DON'T HAVE SKULL-JACKETS!!!
Zh: “是的，我们也想变成酷炫的杀手骷髅头，像你们一样，不过我们没有骷髅头夹克！！！”

En: Wow. I didn't realize it's that serious. Let's forget about this. (Let it go.)
Zh: “哇哦。我没意识到居然这么严重。那还是忘了吧。”（随它去吧。）

En: Let's just go, Fuck! The blondie -- although visibly relieved -- is nervously glancing at the traffic jam. "This scene is dead anyway."
Zh: “我们走吧，干弟！”金发男子——显然是松了一口气--紧张地瞥了一眼堵塞的交通。“反正这里已经没戏了。”

En: Oh yeah, I'll forget about it alright. And you will too. Until the day the Void comes for you. He crosses his throat with his index finger.
Zh: “哦耶，我早就已经忘记了。你也会的。等到虚无找上你的那一天。”他用食指划过喉咙。

En: FUCK THE FUCKING POLICE!!!!
Zh: “去他妈的警察！！！”

En: Please be quiet! Not much is left of the nihilistic rebel at this point. The young man before you is scared out of his mind. "What... WHAT do you want?!! T-t-the jackets?"
Zh: “安静一点！”此刻此刻，虚无主义叛逆者已经所剩无几。站在你面前的年轻男子吓得魂飞魄散。“什么……你想干什么？！！这，这些夹克吗？”

En: You OFFER us your jackets like that? It'd be impolite to refuse. (Reach out your hand.)
Zh: “你打算把夹克送给我们吗？那样的话，拒绝就太不礼貌了。”（伸出你的手。）

En: Don't say anything. He turns away from you. "Just let them go."
Zh: “什么也别说。”他转过身去。“让他们走吧。”

En: Fuck, the other one sighs deeply.
Zh: “该死，”另一个人重重地叹了一口气。

En: Oh man... His shoulders slump under the weight of sadness. "Okay," he says finally. "I get it. SKULLS don't really wear slogans anyway, this was stupid."
Zh: “天啦……”他的双肩被沉甸甸的悲痛压的抬不起来。“好吧，”他最终开了口。“我明白了。反正骷髅头也没有真正的标语，这真是太愚蠢了。”

En: Hey! Stop right there! How does one know anything?
Zh: “喂！先打住！那人又怎么能知道呢？”

En: Ah -- this sounds like epistemology. A field so occupied by thought that it begins to question thought itself.
Zh: 啊——听起来像是认识论。一种完全被思考占据到开始怀疑本身的领域。

En: I know that you don't know shit.
Zh: “我知道你什么都不知道。”

En: Exactly! How can one know shit? For example: How can one be sure that there truly is a body hanging behind the hostel?
Zh: “没错！你能知道些什么？举个例子：谁能肯定旅社背后真的挂着一具尸体呢？”

En: What if it's art... or just a mere spectre?
Zh: “如果是艺术呢……或者只是个幽灵？”

En: Maybe it's true -- the hanged man is merely a prop in a performance, we are the audience, and the artist is hiding somewhere in the dark?
Zh: 也许是真的——被吊起来的男人只是一场表演中的道具，我们是观众，而艺术家就隐藏在某个黑暗之中？

En: Please explain.
Zh: “请解释一下。”

En: Knowing. We think that we know, but where does the thought originate from?
Zh: “知道。我们以为自己知道，但是这种想法又来自哪里呢？”

En: It's not. A man is dead and we need answers.
Zh: “不是的。一个男人死了，我们需要答案。”

En: I'm not going to entertain you with this any longer.
Zh: “我不会再用这个来取悦你了。”

En: That could be the case, yes... a brilliant work of art!
Zh: “真可能是这样，没错……一件伟大的艺术作品！”

En: It seems that we can only think but never *know*.
Zh: “似乎我们只能思考，但却永远无法*知道*。”

En: The lieutenant raises an eyebrow but does not comment.
Zh: 警督扬起眉头，但却没有发表评论。

En: So you're telling me you don't know anything?
Zh: “所以，你是在告诉我，你什么都不知道？”

En: Precisely.
Zh: “就是这样。”

En: Wouldn't a grave on top of a grave be just a big hole? What's hard about holes?
Zh: 坟上加坟那不就是个大坑吗？坑又有什么难的？

En: Digging them.
Zh: 挖掘它们。

En: Filling them up, baby!
Zh: 把它们填满，宝贝！

En: Though he seemed lacking in vocabulary, it seems that the young man has expertise in at least one field, even if it's rather narrow.
Zh: 尽管他的词汇量似乎很贫瘠，不过这个年轻人至少在某一个领域有专长，尽管真的挺狭隘的。

En: It seems that the young man has a certain expertise in at least one field, even if it's rather narrow.
Zh: 似乎这个年轻人至少在某一个领域有专长，尽管真的挺狭隘的。

En: Because when one fucks everything, he fucks nothing. And that, to me, feels glorious -- sticking your dick into the void.
Zh: “因为当一个人能干翻一切的时候，他其实什么也没干翻。而且那样的话，在我看来，感觉很光荣——让你的下体完全深入虚无。”

En: That's one brutal motor carriage, says the young man with 'PISSF****T' written on his back.
Zh: “那台汽車真够残暴的，”背上写着‘弱水三千只取一尿饮’的年轻人说道。

En: If I were a real SKULL now, I'd jack it, paint it in palm tree livery, then bottom-light it neon-style! His companion wears a simple yet elegant slogan: 'FUCK THE WORLD'.
Zh: “如果现在我是个真正的骷髅头，我会把它顶起来，涂成棕榈树的颜色，然后在底部打上霓虹灯照亮！”他同伴衣服上写着一个更简单但却更加讲究的标语：‘花花世界我一人干翻’。

En: Ahem. He steps in. "While I appreciate the interest you take in my *brutal motor carriage*, I have to stop you right there. The RCM takes threats directed at its property seriously."
Zh: “呃哼。”他介入进来。“我很感激你对我那*残暴的汽車*表现出的兴趣，不过我必须阻止你了。针对RCM财产的威胁，我们一向是很严肃的。”

En: Who are the *SKULLS*?
Zh: “*骷髅头*是谁？”

En: What's with the jackets?
Zh: “这件夹克是怎么回事？”

En: Well, talking with you has definitely been something. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧，跟你说话真是件很不得了的事情。”[离开。]

En: You don't know? What kind of cop are you?
Zh: “你不知道？你到底是什么样的警察啊？”

En: A snazzy shit-ripped SKULL-mobile like this would make a fine trophy. We could, like, hang fucking shrunken heads from the side mirrors! Cops' heads... Scary tribal shit.
Zh: “像这种时髦的屎黄色活动头骨肯定是一件很好的战利品。我们可以，比如说，在后视镜上挂上干缩人头！警察的脑袋……吓人的部落装饰什么的。”

En: Yeah, tribal shit... he agrees. "A cop-carriage like this would have proper SKULL value..."
Zh: “是啊，部落……”他表示赞同。“像这种警車应该有合适骷髅头的价值……”

En: I, uhm, it's just theoretical work, copper. No basis in reality. He turns to his companion: "Man, if we were certified SKULLS right now..."
Zh: “我，呃，这只是理论工作，警察。没有现实基础。”他转向自己的同伴：“老兄，如果现在我们是认证骷髅头的话……”

En: No, I really don't.
Zh: “不，我真不知道。”

En: Of course I do, I'm just testing you boys.
Zh: “我当然知道了，我只是在测试你们这帮小伙子。”

En: Why haven't I seen any SKULLS around Martinaise?
Zh: “我为什么没在马丁内斯附近看到骷髅头？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Non-verbal?
Zh: “非语言？”

En: The SKULLS are the most vicious gang of the Besmertnyé. His voice rings with excitement.
Zh: “骷髅头是贝斯梅特纳最恶毒的帮派。”他的声音听起来很兴奋。

En: If a SKULL spots you, he will pull out his dagger and stab you without saying a word.
Zh: “如果骷髅头发现你，他会掏出匕首，一言不发地刺伤你。”

En: If a SKULL spots you, he will pull out his dagger and stab you without saying a single word.
Zh: “如果骷髅头发现你，他会掏出匕首，一言不发地刺伤你。”

En: The nastiest bunch of psychos ever! Jacking carriages and getting into high-speed chases.
Zh: “有史以来最恶毒的一帮疯子！抢劫汽車，还有飙車追逐。”

En: They usually occupy the Burnt-Out Quarter in Jamrock. Or you can find them loitering around their brightly-painted, bottom-lighted vehicles.
Zh: “他们通常会住在加姆洛克的燃尽区。或是开着颜色鲜艳、底盘发光的汽車到处游荡。”

En: Possessing an infinite amount of fuck-all swagger, infamous for their non-verbal *modus operandi*.
Zh: “他们身上拥有无穷无尽的*干翻一切*的范儿，因为非语言的*作案手法*臭名远扬。”

En: Oh, I can't wait to become a SKULL. Bottom-lights are *wretched* aggressive.
Zh: “哦，我已经等不及要加入骷髅头。底盘灯实在是*该死的*咄咄逼人。”

En: Well, the young man remarks in a voice filled with longing, eyes glazed over, "there's Cindy..."
Zh: “呃，”年轻男子目光呆滞，用充满渴望的声音评论到。“有一个叫辛迪的……”

En: The lieutenant's voice is as calm as usual -- a testament to the violence and death he's witnessed through the sight of his firearm.
Zh: 警督的声音向往常一样平静——这是他用自己的配枪见证过暴力和死亡的证明。

En: Yeah. Cindy the SKULL! He lights up. "A true artist of the future, just like Arno van Eyck or Rietveldt."
Zh: “哦耶。骷髅头辛迪！”他整个人都兴奋了起来。“一个真正的未来艺术家，就像阿诺·凡·艾克或者是里特维德。”

En: Your rhetoric is confusing. Are you a part of the SKULLS or not?
Zh: “你的言辞让人很困惑。你到底是不是骷髅头的成员？”

En: Who's van Eyck?
Zh: “凡·艾克是谁？”

En: I see you kids are into anodic dance music?
Zh: “我看你们俩小子对阳极舞曲挺感兴趣的？”

En: I see you kids are into modern dance music?
Zh: “我看你们俩小子对现代舞曲挺感兴趣的？”

En: Oh, man, yeah! he exclaims, then stops himself, processing the rest of your question. "We're not fucking kids, man!"
Zh: “哦，老兄，当然了！”他大叫出声，然后停了下来，脑子还在加工问题的其余部分。“我们他妈的不是孩子，老兄！”

En: Old man, it doesn't matter. You'll be long gone before his greatness is recognized.
Zh: “老头，没关系的。在他的伟大被认可之前，你早就已经死翘翘了。”

En: A young woman kneels on a sheet of ice as if looking for something lost ages ago. She bends until her right ear touches the frozen water. She listens to it crack, slowly.
Zh: 一个年轻女子跪在一块冰上，好像在寻找多年前丢失的什么东西。她弯下腰，直到右耳碰到了冰冷的海水。她在聆听冰块慢慢裂开的声音。

En: The Union does their share of policing in Martinaise, at least where gangs are concerned, the lieutenant replies instead. "That's why there isn't much organized crime around here."
Zh: “工会在马丁内斯分担了一部分维持治安的工作，至少跟帮派有关的地方是这样，”警督回复到。“这就是附近没有多少有组织犯罪的原因。”

En: What about them?
Zh: “夹克怎么了？”

En: Be wary of the abyss, his blond friend adds ominously and points to his temple.
Zh: “小心深渊，”他的金发朋友指着自己的太阳穴，不安地补充到。

En: Enough about this *skullery* then. (Conclude.)
Zh: “关于*骷髅头*的话题就到这里吧。”（总结。）

En: We're not *franchised* SKULLS -- well, not yet. Once we get our name out there, we'll have a chance to join them.
Zh: “我们不是*认证的*骷髅头——呃，暂时不是。一旦我们弄出点名堂来，就有机会加入了。”

En: I see.
Zh: “我明白了。”

En: And what makes you think that the organization would accept you?
Zh: “你为什么觉得那个组织会接受你呢？”

En: Oh, you'll see for sure once we're in -- it's the last thing you'll ever see before the void consumes you.
Zh: “哦。一旦我们加入，你肯定就能明白——在虚空将你吞噬之前，这会是你看见的最后一样东西。”

En: Because we can be just as psycho and vicious. You'll see.
Zh: “因为我们也能像他们一样病态又恶毒。你等着瞧吧。”

En: Uh... He looks confused. "Well... yeah I mean, we're only saying *practice* things for now, so... We don't mean no harm to the SKULLS brand. Or to you."
Zh: “啊……”他看起来有些困惑。“呃……是啊，我的意思是，我们目前只是在说*实践*，所以……我们无意损害骷髅头的名声。或者是你。”

En: This is definitely something the SKULLS would say, but we're not trying to encroach on the SKULLS brand in any way. On the contrary, we're just here to market it.
Zh: “这绝对是真正的骷髅头会说的话，不过我们也没打算侵害骷髅头的名声。正相反，我们只是来推销它的。”

En: Hold on, why does a criminal gang need marketing?
Zh: “等一下，犯罪团伙要推销干什么？”

En: We think of it more like two franchises merging, you know -- us two and the SKULLS. I really feel like we would add more to the table.
Zh: “我们认为这更像是两个特许权的合并，你知道的——我们俩和骷髅头。我真觉得我们可以给它多增加一些东西。”

En: Ask for the jackets for yourself and the lieutenant.
Zh: 要来夹克给你自己和警督。

En: Demonstrated authority.
Zh: 展示权威。

En: Kim showed good tactics.
Zh: 金表现出良好的策略。

En: Know what makes them nervous.
Zh: 知道怎么让他们紧张。

En: They're just off-brand SKULLS.
Zh: 他们只是未被认可的骷髅头。

En: By the way, if you see Cindy, give her our regards, he adds, returning from whatever void he was just visiting.
Zh: “话说，如果你看见辛迪，带我们像她问好，”他补充了一句，然后回到了他刚刚拜访过的虚空。

En: (Turn to the blond youth.) "Why does your jacket have 'PISSF****T' written on it?"
Zh: （转向金发年轻人。）“为什么你的夹克上写着‘弱水三千只取一尿饮’？”

En: (Turn to the dark-haired youth.) "Why do you have 'FUCK THE WORLD' written on your jacket?"
Zh: （转向黑发年轻人。）“为什么你的夹克上写着‘花花世界我一人干翻’？”

En: Is it a *coincidence* that here we have two bad-ass jackets and two bad-ass cops?
Zh: 这里有两件狂野的夹克，还有两个狂野的警察，这会是*巧合*吗？

En: Spice things up here in Martinaise, you know. Get the old machine of pain and suffering oiled up real good.
Zh: “你知道的，在马丁内斯这里搞搞气氛。给这台充满痛苦和磨难的旧机器加点油，让这里好好运转起来。”

En: Okay, I get what the SKULLS do, but what do you franchise in? Youth unemployment?
Zh: “好吧，我明白骷髅头是干什么的了，不过你们的特许权是什么？青年人失业？”

En: Doesn't it already have enough spice?
Zh: “这里的气氛还不够吗？”

En: Whatever, man. Work is for pussies. The young man frowns, aggrieved. "You wouldn't get it anyway."
Zh: “无所谓了，老兄。娘炮才需要工作呢。”年轻人皱起眉头，忿忿不平。“反正你也理解不了。”

En: So you're just *pretending* to be as nasty and vicious as the SKULLS?
Zh: “所以你只是在*假装*跟骷髅头一样卑鄙恶毒了？”

En: Hey, we can be just as hard! Like pavement on top of pavement, or a brick on top of another brick.
Zh: “喂，我们也能那么肛的好吗！就像道外有道，或者砖上加砖。”

En: Do you know anything about the murder that took place here? (Point to the yard.)
Zh: “你知道这里发生的凶杀案吗？”（指向院子。）

En: Yeah, sure, we'll gladly tell you everything we know about it. He clears his throat. "It was a man."
Zh: “哦耶，当然知道，我们很愿意把知道的一切都告诉你。”他清了清喉咙。“那是个男人。”

En: Like I said before, many men keep searching for *the one*. For so-called true love, which is actually just obsession masquerading as kinship. The thrill of the chase, the hollowness that fills your chest cavity after catching it.\n
Zh: “我之前也说过了，很多男人一直在寻找*某一个人*。为了所谓的真爱，其实那不过是伪装成亲情的痴迷。追逐的兴奋，还有捕捉到之后填满你整个胸腔的空虚。”

En: I can answer that. Many men keep searching for *the one*. For so-called true love, which is actually just obsession masquerading as kinship. The thrill of the chase, the hollowness that fills your chest cavity after catching it.
Zh: “这我能回答。很多男人一直在寻找*某一个人*。为了所谓的真爱，其实那不过是伪装成亲情的痴迷。追逐的兴奋，还有捕捉到之后填满你整个胸腔的空虚。”

En: So, you're saying I'm an old man?!
Zh: “所以，你是在说我老了？！”

En: Yeah -- the skull symbolizes the embrace of death and nothingness, present in all of us, the vehicle of our future operations. Death and nothingness.
Zh: “哦耶——骷髅头象征着拥抱死亡和虚无，它存在于我们所有人心里，是我们未来行动的载体。死亡和虚无。”

En: They're called SKULLS because when you're shot dead in the middle of the street, the skull is the only part the stray dogs won't eat.
Zh: “他们之所以被称为骷髅头，是因为当你在大街正中央被枪杀的时候，流浪狗唯一不啃的只有头骨。”

En: Just trust me and follow my lead.
Zh: 相信我，跟随我的领导。

En: Kim, maybe you shouldn't scare them like that?
Zh: “金，也许你不该那样吓唬他们？”

En: So, boys, wanna feed the dogs?
Zh: “所以，小子们，想喂狗吗？”

En: The other one shuffles uncomfortably, nevertheless continuing to steal glances at the lieutenant's motor carriage.
Zh: 另一个人不安地扭动着，不过还在继续偷瞄警督的汽車。

En: And do what? Walk away, knowing that there are two delusional hoodlums interested in joining a criminal group whose main areas of expertise are jacking cars and killing police officers?
Zh: “那要我怎么做？知道这里有两个妄想的小流氓，有兴趣加入一个专门以劫車和杀警察为主要领域的犯罪团伙，然后就这么走开？”

En: Well...
Don't *well* me. This might be fine by you but it certainly isn't fine by me. We've got to try to nip this kind of behaviour in the bud.
Zh: “别跟我*呃*。这对你来说可能没问题，但是在我这里就不行。我们必须尝试把这种行为扼杀在萌芽状态。”

En: The young men stare at the two of you in confusion.
Zh: 年轻人困惑地看着你们俩。

En: Or a grave on top of a grave.
Zh: “或者是坟上加坟。”

En: Also, he was hanged.
Zh: “还有，他被吊死了。”

En: Anything else?
Zh: “还有别的吗？”

En: Don't fuck around. I am the law.
Zh: “别瞎搅和了。我是法律。”

En: He was hanged from a tree.
Zh: “他被吊死在一颗树上了。”

En: As you already know, it's a statement, not a description of my character.
Zh: “你已经知道了，这是一个声明，不是我个人性格的描述。”

En: Well, first off, it's a statement and not *necessarily* something that characterizes me as a person, even though the statement has character. And I *do* like piss...
Zh: “呃，首先，这是一个声明，*未必*就代表了我这个人的特质，尽管这样的声明的确很有个性。而且我*的确*喜欢尿尿……”

En: The youth looks confused for a moment. "There can always be more. In the end it won't matter. 'Til then..."
Zh: 有那么一会儿，年轻人看起来有些困惑。“总是还能有更多的。最后一切都不重要了。直到那个时候……”

En: The word PISSF****T epitomizes the struggle taking place in the world, things being defined as they seem, not as they are. And -- I guess -- it's also about communal spirit, the future, and *truly* appreciating our differences.
Zh: “弱水三千只取一尿饮这个词概括了发生在这个世界上的挣扎，事物是被它们的表象定义的，而不是它们本身。而且——我猜——这也跟社群精神、未来，还有*真正*欣赏彼此的不同有关。”

En: Also, you've got to admit, it catches the eye. And since the grand piper is slowly but steadily moving towards basing the economy on it -- attention -- it is imperative that the medium itself convey the message.
Zh: “而且，你必须承认，这样很抓人眼球。大风笛手正在缓慢却稳定地朝着将经济基础建立在它之上的方向发展——注意了——拿媒介本身来传达信息就是势在必行的。”

En: What I mean by this is -- we are *all* Pissf****ts. And that the world is inherently meaningless.
Zh: “我的意思就是——我们*全都是*弱水三千只取一尿饮。而且这个世界本来就毫无意义。”

En: Actually -- forget about it.
Zh: “其实——还是忘了吧。”

En: To catch a fish you need to hurl the lure many times, and even then it isn't certain that you'll get anything. If you blow up the lake, though...
Zh: “为了抓住一条鱼，你需要抛很多次饵，即便如此，也不确定你会不会抓到什么。不过，如果你炸掉整个湖的话……”

En: ...you get more fish in a shorter time. And, for time is of the essence and fleeting ever so quickly, one must think of a way to fuck the whole world -- and not get caught up in fucking some *one*.
Zh: “……就能在更短的时间内得到更多的鱼。而且，因为宝贵的时间正在飞快的流逝，我们必须想出一种花花世界我一人干翻的办法——而不是沉浸在某*一个人*身上。”

En: I'm wondering if the poetics come with the jacket or are they derived from something else entirely?
Zh: 我在想，这份诗情是来源于夹克，还是完全衍生于其他完全不同的东西？

En: Hey, Kim... (Lower your voice.)
Zh: “喂，金……”（压低声音。）

En: Yes?
Zh: “什么事？”

En: Do you think it's a coincidence?
Zh: “你觉得这是个巧合吗？”

En: What do you think about their jackets?
Zh: “你觉得他们的夹克怎么样？”

En: What is?
Zh: “怎么了？”

En: Leather jackets adorned with immature writing? The 'ideology' they convey helps the boys justify poor choices in life and fashion. The lieutenant shrugs. "I'm not a fan."
Zh: “装饰着幼稚文字的皮夹克？他们传达的‘意识形态’能帮助小伙子们对于生活和时尚的糟糕选择辩护。”警督耸耸肩。“我反正不是粉丝。”

En: There are two of us and two of these jackets...
Zh: “我们有两个人，这里有两件夹克……”

En: What are you implying? The lieutenant looks confused.
Zh: 警督看起来很困惑。“你在暗示什么？”

En: Why are you always so patronizing when cool *possibilities* cross our path?
Zh: “为什么每次遇到变酷的*可能性*的时候，你总是摆出这种高人一等的态度呢？”

En: Which one would *you* wear?
Zh: “*你*想穿哪件？”

En: You know what I'm implying.
Zh: “你知道我在暗示什么。”

En: I'm not sure I understand you, detective.
Zh: “我不太明白你的意思，警探。”

En: Are you more of a 'PISSF****T' or a 'FUCK THE WORLD' kind of guy?
Zh: “你是更像‘弱水三千只取一尿饮’那种人呢，还是‘花花世界我一人干翻’？”

En: No, I don't. What are you implying?
Zh: “不，我不知道。你在暗示什么？”

En: We should get these jackets.
Zh: “我们应该把那些夹克搞到手。”

En: Fine, if only to end this discussion: Theoretically, if I were a juvenile delinquent -- if I were to already be down that path -- I think 'PISSF****T' is the stronger of the two statements.
Zh: “好吧，我这么说只是为了结束这次的讨论：从理论上讲，如果我是一个失足小青年——如果我已经走上了这条路——那我觉得两种声明中，‘弱水三千只取一尿饮’是比较有力的那个。”

En: For what?
Zh: “做什么？”

En: What do people do with jackets? They wear them.
Zh: “大家会拿夹克做什么？当然是穿啊。”

En: The concept of getting dressed *is* familiar to me. Perhaps more than to you, he adds, glancing at your attire. "But wearing jackets like these isn't appropriate for an RCM officer."\n
Zh: “穿衣服这个概念对我来说*的确*很熟悉。甚至比对你更熟悉，”他瞥了一眼你的装扮，补充了一句，“不过RCM警官穿那种夹克很不合适。”

En: The concept of getting dressed *is* familiar to me, but wearing jackets like these isn't appropriate for an RCM officer. Unless he's *deep* undercover, he adds.
Zh: “穿衣服这个概念对我来说*的确*很熟悉，不过RCM警官穿那种夹克很不合适。除非他是*隐藏很深*的卧底，”他补充了一句。

En: But in theory, Kim, if we were to confiscate these jackets, which one would *you* wear?
Zh: “从理论上讲，金，如果我们要没收这些夹克，*你*可能会穿哪件？”

En: No, no, no! Don't *ask* anything. Be subtle and scary. The boys dream about being SKULLS... use that!
Zh: 不不不！什么也别*问*。狡猾一点，吓人一点。男孩子们梦想成为骷髅头……利用这一点！

En: These guys aren't scary, *you're* not scary -- I have nothing to work with here. It's either begging or bullying. Or both. Just ask them maybe?
Zh: 这些家伙不吓人，*你*也不吓人——这里没我什么事。要么是祈求，要么是霸凌。或者两者都有。也许可以直接问他们？

En: Gentlemen, I need your jackets.
Zh: “先生们，我需要你们的夹克。”

En: Boys, with *those* jackets, you're gonna be the SKULL-kings in no time!
Zh: “小伙子们，有了*这些*夹克，你们肯定很快就会变成骷髅头之王！”

En: What... no! He quickly looks around. "SKULLS don't have kings," he pauses. "I think, and we're not even *in* yet..."
Zh: “什么……不！”他飞快地四下看了看。“骷髅头没有国王，”他停顿了一下。“我是这么觉得的，而且我们甚至还没*加入*呢……”

En: (Raise your voice.) "Not even prospects and already aspiring to be kings?"
Zh: （提高你的声音。）“还不是预备役，都已经立志要当国王了？”

En: Yeah, man, keep your voice down. SKULLS don't take it lightly, when folks pretend to be them. We're not even *prospects* yet.
Zh: “是啊，老兄，小声点。当有人假装自己是骷髅头的时候，他们可不会掉以轻心。我们甚至还不是*预备役*呢。”

En: Uh... why?
Zh: “呃……为什么？”

En: Neither.
Zh: “都不要。”

En: C'mon Kim, it's just a mental exercise!
Zh: “拜托，金，这只是一个思想训练！”

En: (Point at your clothes.) "Look at the shit I'm wearing!"
Zh: （指着你的衣服。）“你看看我穿的这身垃圾！”

En: I need them for official police business.
Zh: “警察公务需要。”

En: I'm not wearing that jacket.
Zh: “我不会穿那件夹克的。”

En: I don't think this case requires us to go undercover. Or raise hell...  In fact I don't think the jacket will be useful at all.
Zh: “我觉得这个案子不需要我们卧底。或者引起骚乱……事实上，我觉得这些夹克一点用都没有。”

En: Why not? You could really raise hell. Go undercover. Hard.
Zh: “为什么不呢？你可以真正的引起骚乱。也可以卧底。很硬核的那种。”

En: I already *am* expressing my individuality.
Zh: “我*已经*在表达自己的个性了。”

En: But don't you want to express your individuality?
Zh: “但是，你不想表达自己的个性吗？”

En: Still. It's good to know that we have it, should the need arise.
Zh: “不过。如果有需求的话，知道它们的存在总是好的吧。”

En: The need will not arise.
Zh: “这种需求永远不会出现。”

En: Look at this cute shit! The dark haired youth points at you -- then turns to his friend. "How can we become SKULLS if you go around fraternising with cops?! Let's get the fuck out of here before anyone sees us."
Zh: “看看这坨可爱的狗屎！”黑发年轻人指着你——然后转向他的朋友。“如果到处跟警察称兄道弟的话，我们还怎么变成骷髅头啊？！我们还是赶紧离开这里吧，免得被别人看见。”

En: Fuck, I'm sorry, man. He hangs his head in shame. "I just... don't like confrontations that's all."
Zh: “他妈的，我很抱歉，兄弟。”他羞愧地低下头。“我只是……不喜欢对抗，就这么回事……”

En: And what kind of business is that exactly?
Zh: “那到底是什么事呢？”

En: Yeah, like you said, total shit. What about it? There's a spark of sympathy in the youth's eyes.
Zh: “哦耶，我说过了，完全就是坨屎。怎么了？”年轻人的眼睛里闪烁着一丝同情的火花。

En: I'm wearing horrible clothes to catch horrible people. It's depressing.
Zh: “我穿着这身可怕的衣服去抓可怕的人。这也太压抑了。”

En: The lieutenant looks down at his bomber jacket and shrugs, obviously content with *his* uniform.
Zh: 警督低头看着自己的飞行员夹克，耸耸肩，明显对*自己*的制服很满意。

En: *Police* business.
Zh: “*警察*事务。”

En: The lieutenant notices your agitation and opens his mouth to speak, but then decides against it and merely shakes his head.
Zh: 警督注意到你很激动，张开嘴想要说什么，不过后来还是决定放弃，只是摇了摇头。

En: That ride is fucking lightning! the young man exclaims.
Zh: “那他妈的是闪电丸！”年轻男人大叫起来。

En: Hey, Pissf****t, look who it is! Shrunken cop head material!
Zh: “喂，弱水三千只取一尿饮，看看是谁来了！做警察干缩人头的材料！”

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: Probably because of how *non-verbal* their mode of operation is going to be, the lieutenant answers for the two rebels. "It's a threat."
Zh: “可能是因为他们的行为模式怎么个*非语言*法吧，”警督替两个叛逆少年做出了回答。“这是一个威胁。”

En: Yep. He nods enthusiastically. "Old as fuck."
Zh: “是的。”他用力点点头。“真他妈老。”

En: Which is not to say that the SKULLS are bitches and f****ts. On the contrary...
Zh: “这并不是说骷髅头就是婊子和混球。正好相反……”

En: Who are you?
Zh: “你是谁？”

En: I can tell you who we're not, cop. We're *not* snitches, f****ts, or SKULLS.
Zh: “我可以告诉你我们不是谁，警察。我们*不是*告密者，混球或者骷髅头。”

En: The lieutenant sighs. "No, they really, really aren't. It's porno-tuning. Say no to the porno."
Zh: 警督叹了口气。“不，真的、真的不行。这样就过度改装了。要拒绝过度改装。”

En: Apart from the Union themselves of course.
Zh: 当然了，除了工会本身。

En: That much is true.
Zh: 这倒是真的。

En: No reply. The blond rebel scratches his chin, deliberately not focusing his gaze on anything. He looks very uncomfortable.
Zh: 没有回应。金发叛逆青年挠了挠脸颊，故意让目光四下游移。他看起来很不舒服。

En: Yeah, but... fuck the police.
Zh: “是啊，但是……去他妈的警察。”

En: When you put it that way... (Turn to the two young men and tap a finger against your head.) "Dog food!"
Zh: “你这么说的话……”（转向两个年轻人，然后用一只手指悄悄自己的脑袋。）“狗食！”

En: The lieutenant sighs. "I... appreciate the effort."
Zh: 警督叹了口气。“我……很欣赏这份努力。”

En: No. You don't get to say that. His voice is sharp as a blade. "Police officers put their lives on the line to protect the people of Revachol, while punks like these joke about joining a cop-killer clan."
Zh: “不。你不能这么说。”他的声音像刀子一样锋利。“警察冒着生命危险保护瑞瓦肖人民的安全，然而这种小阿飞只会拿加入杀警察的犯罪团伙开玩笑。”

En: A young woman kneels on wooden planks, still looking for something lost ages ago. She's nodding her head along to the signal coming in through her headphones.
Zh: 一个年轻女子跪在一块冰上，好像在寻找多年前丢失的什么东西。她跟着耳机里传出来的信号轻轻点着头。

En: Hey, I'm *only* 42!
Zh: “喂，我*只有*42岁好吗！”

En: Holy shit, that's even older than I thought! He bursts out laughing.
Zh: “我的个老天，甚至比我想到还老！”他爆笑出声。

En: You're fucking ancient, man.
Zh: “你真他妈的老，老兄。”

En: Yeah, man. It's like... at death's door. No wonder you know nothing about the future. You won't *be* there.
Zh: “是啊，老兄。就像是……已经一脚踏进坟墓了。难怪你对未来一无所知。你不会*活到*那个时候的。”

En: Judging by that, van Eyck is some sort of musician. Or... killer?
Zh: 据此判断的话，凡·艾克是某种音乐家。或者是……杀手？

En: We don't have time for this. Let's go. [Leave.]
Zh: “我们没时间管这个。还是走吧。”[离开。]

En: Murder?
Zh: “凶杀？”

En: A man was hanged in the backyard of the Whirling-in-Rags.
Zh: “褴褛飞旋的后院吊着一个男人。”

En: Never mind, this is useless.
Zh: “没事了，这样毫无帮助。”

En: The palm tree livery should be, like, pastel green. Fucking tropic shit... He gazes dreamily at Lieutenant Kitsuragi's motor carriage.
Zh: “那就应该是棕榈树了，就像，崧蓝绿色。狗屁热带什么的……”他似梦似幻地凝视着曷城警督的汽車。

En: I can see it, bright as day. Oh, if we were SKULLS right now...
Zh: “我能看见，亮如白昼。哦，如果我们现在是骷髅头就好了……”

En: The part of this presentation you wanna take home with you, cop-man, is: We're not part of the SKULLS. Yet.
Zh: “这部分陈述你可以好好斟酌一下，警察：我们不是骷髅头的成员。暂时不是。”

En: Hey, Pissf****t, look who it is! It's the bully. He ran out of people to be an asshole to.
Zh: “喂，弱水三千只取一尿饮，看看是谁来了！是那个霸凌别人的警察。他已经没人可以欺负了。”

En: Was it a good idea on the lieutenant's part to get into this? Don't even answer that, just leave. You can always come back later -- on your own terms.
Zh: 对于警督来说，介入这件事是个好主意吗？不要回应，直接离开。你之后还有机会回来处理——按你自己喜欢的方式。

En: Do you guys know Cindy the SKULL?
Zh: “你们知道骷髅头辛迪吗？”

En: Why aren't there more SKULLS in Martinaise?
Zh: “为什么马丁内斯没有骷髅头呢？”

En: Apart from the Union themselves of course.
Zh: 当然了，除了工会本身。

En: The Union does their share of policing in Martinaise, at least where gangs are concerned, the lieutenant replies instead. "That's why there isn't much organized crime around here."
Zh: “工会在马丁内斯分担了一部分维持治安的工作，至少跟帮派有关的地方是这样，”警督回复到。“这就是附近没有多少有组织犯罪的原因。”

En: Don't you worry about that. We're gonna make up for the deficit.
Zh: “那个不用担心。我们会弥补不足的。”

En: Yeah we are! The young men exchange approving nods.
Zh: “哦耶，没错！”年轻男子互相赞许地点了点头。

En: By the way, if you see Cindy, give her our regards, he adds, returning from whatever void he was just visiting.
Zh: “话说，如果你看见辛迪，带我们像她问好，”他补充了一句，然后回到了他刚刚拜访过的虚空。

En: Yeah, the other guy lights up too. "A true artist of the future, just like Arno van Eyck."
Zh: “哦耶，”另一个家伙也兴奋了起来。“一个真正的未来艺术家，就像阿诺·凡·艾克。”

En: Oh yeah, Cindy's a right proper SKULL... The young man's eyes glaze over, his voice filled with longing.
Zh: 年轻男子目光呆滞，用充满渴望的声音评论到。“哦耶，辛迪是个名正言顺的骷髅头……”

En: Not so sure about that. She's a bit of a communist.
Zh: “那我就不确定了。她有点康米主义者的倾向。”

En: Actually, Cindy told me herself that she's not a very good SKULL.
Zh: “其实，辛迪本人跟我说过，她不是个很好的骷髅头。”

En: Lies! Cindy's all SKULL. A true artist of the future, just like Arno van Eyck.
Zh: “你撒谎！辛迪是彻头彻尾的骷髅头。一个真正的未来艺术家，就像阿诺·凡·艾克一样。”

En: And you believed that? He laughs. "No, man, Cindy's all SKULL. A true artist of the future, just like Arno van Eyck."
Zh: “你居然信了？”他笑了。“不，老兄，辛迪是彻头彻尾的骷髅头。一个真正的未来艺术家，就像阿诺·凡·艾克一样。”

En: Mhm. He throws a longing glance at the Kineema.
Zh: “嗯。”他瞥了一眼锐影，满眼的渴望。

En: Are you sure a SKULL would say that?
Zh: “你确定骷髅头会说这种话？”

En: These kids have the vocabulary but might be missing a brain.
Zh: 这些孩子词汇量还是有的，不过可能没脑子。

En: Yeah, I mean... duh.
Zh: “哦耶，我的意思是……咄。”

En: These punks don't know anything. Let's just move along.
Zh: “这些小阿飞什么都不知道。我们还是走吧。”

En: That works, I feel more like a 'FUCK THE WORLD' kind of a guy.
Zh: “那就成了，我觉得自己更像是‘花花世界我一人干翻’那种人。”

En: Seems about right, the lieutenant remarks. "Especially considering your... heroic exit attempts."
Zh: “好像是吧，”警督评论到。“特别是考虑到你的……英勇的退出尝试。”

En: No way! If anything, I'm the *PISSF****T* in this scenario.
Zh: “不可能！如果有什么的话，在这种情况下，我才是*弱水三千只取一尿饮*。”

En: Fine, the lieutenant shrugs. "'FUCK THE WORLD' just seemed to fit you more, considering your... heroic exit attempts."
Zh: “好吧，”警督耸耸肩。“‘花花世界我一人干翻’似乎更适合你，考虑到你的……英勇的退出尝试。”

En: I wouldn't wear it anyway.
Zh: “反正我不会穿。”

En: Why not? You could really raise hell. Go undercover. Hard.
Zh: “为什么不呢？你可以真正的引起骚乱。也可以卧底。很硬核的那种。”

En: I don't think this case requires us to go undercover. Or raise hell... In fact I don't think the jacket will be useful at all.
Zh: “我觉得这个案子不需要我们卧底。或者引起骚乱……事实上，我觉得这些夹克一点用都没有。”

En: But don't you want to express your individuality?
Zh: “但是，你不想表达自己的个性吗？”

En: I already *am* expressing my individuality.
Zh: “我*已经*在表达自己的个性了。”

En: Well, whatever. I've got mine and that's just fine.
Zh: “好吧，无所谓了。反正我自己有，而且挺好的。”

En: I'll just let you borrow this jacket... you know, should the need arise.
Zh: “我需要借走这件夹克……你知道的，免得以后有需要。”

En: If you say so.
Zh: “随你怎么说吧。”

En: It's not shit, it's disco...
Zh: “这不是屎，这是迪斯科……”

En: It's shit.
Zh: “这就是屎。”

En: As soon as this conversation's done, *you* put on the Fuck-the-World jacket. We'll take it from there...
Oh, the need *will* arise. Here's how we'll do it:
Look at the cross.
Zh: 查看十字勋章。

En: Look at the sun.
Zh: 查看太阳勋章。

En: A crowned head in front of two crossed rifles. The medal hangs from a blue striped triangle.
Zh: 前面是一个戴皇冠的头，背后是两把交叉的步枪。奖章镶嵌在一块蓝色条纹的三角布上。

En: A small blue star inside an orange sun. It has the word *Vaillance* written below.
Zh: 橙色的太阳里有一颗小蓝星。下面写着*英勇*的字样。

En: Sounds like you're being modest, René.
Zh: “听起来你很谦虚，雷内。”

En: I don't know the reputation of... *anything*. Don't even recognize the war.
Zh: “我不知道*任何东西*的……名声。甚至连那场战争都不知道。”

En: Son, we should never forget our past. Lots of mistakes were made back then, but they were also lessons. His voice takes on a tutor-like quality.
Zh: “孩子，我们不该忘记自己的过去。那时的我们犯了很多错，不过这也是一种教训。”他的声音有一种辅导老师的气质。

En: No -- we were the last ones to keep all the baby killers and rapists in check, and let me tell you, son... His eyes find yours. "If there hadn't been Royal Carabineers, Revachol might not be standing at all."
Zh: “不——我们是最后阻止那些婴孩杀手和强暴犯的人，我告诉你，孩子……”他对上了你的目光。“如果没有皇家骑兵，瑞瓦肖可能根本站不住脚。”

En: Thanks for the story, René! (Conclude.)
Zh: “感谢你的故事，雷内！”（总结。）

En: Bah! His gaze wanders over the bay. "There were many such stories in those days. Many such men too. True Revacholians, men with *backbone*."
Zh: “呸！”他的目光凝望着海湾。“那时候有很多这样的故事。还有很多这样的人。真正的瑞瓦肖人，有*骨气*的男人。”

En: There's something you missed... You will get to it, don't worry.
Zh: 你遗漏了一些东西……你会发现的，别担心。

En: Do *May bells* mean anything to you, René? (Show him the flower.)
Zh: “*铃兰*对你来说有什么意义吗，雷内？”（把花拿给他看。）

En: That's how it is, huh? The man slowly nods, exhales sharply, and takes the ball. "Fine. I guess you did attempt to right your hooliganism... Consider it forgiven."
Zh: “情况就是这样，对吧？”男人慢慢点点头，大口喘着粗气，接过你手中的球。“好吧。我猜你是想纠正自己的流氓行为……就当我原谅你了吧。”

En: The best, huh? he says inspecting the ball. "This isn't even a *boule*, but... Fine. I guess you did attempt to right your hooliganism... Consider it forgiven."
Zh: “最好的，是吗？”他一边端详着球，一边说道。“这甚至都不是个*大球*，不过……好吧。我猜你是想纠正自己的流氓行为……就当我原谅你了吧。”

En: Dark cloud? That sounds unpleasantly familiar...
Zh: 黑云？听起来熟悉地让人不安……

En: He glances at it and frowns. "I prefer the old name -- Insulindian Lily. Girls brought them to young cadets when they entered service. Wearing them on your cap was supposed to bring good luck."
Zh: 他瞥了一眼，皱紧眉头。”我更喜欢它的老名字——伊苏林迪百合。年轻的士官开始服役时，女孩们会送来这些花。把它们戴在帽子上会为你带来好运。”

En: Hold on, is this a *royalist* military tradition?
Zh: “等一下，这是一个*保皇派*的军事传统吗？”

En: Did they bring you good luck?
Zh: “它们给你带来好运了吗？”

En: It used to be, he says with a sigh. "But the communards were fond of them, too. Called them *revolutionary* flowers, bells of the Revolution..."
Zh: “曾经是，”他叹息着说道。“不过公社社员也很喜欢它们。称它们是*革命*之花，大革命的铃声……”

En: You know what... He falls silent and the emerging smile withdraws. "No. They brought me misery, false hope, and disappointment. The *revolutionaries* sullied them."
Zh: “你知道吗……”他陷入沉默，脸上的笑容也消失不见。“不。它们给我带来了苦难，虚假的希望和失望。*革命分子*玷污了它们。”

En: But it wasn't the revolutionaries that *sullied* the idea for you, was it? He looks at the old soldier almost gently. "She gave them to me too and your jealous little heart just couldn't accept it."
Zh: “不过，并不是革命派*玷污*了这个概念，不是吗？”他看着老兵，目光几近温柔。“她也把花送给了我，而你的妒忌心无法接受。”

En: You stirred up some bad memories there.
Zh: 你勾起了一些不好的回忆。

En: She? Must be the woman from that picture -- Jeanne-Marie -- again.
Zh: 她？肯定是相片你的那个女人——珍妮·玛丽——又是她。

En: Why are you so mean to me?
Zh: 你为什么对我这么刻薄？

En: Consuming food is mechanical process for him. He doesn't enjoy it, just goes through the motions and moves on.
Zh: 对于他来说，进食只是一个机械的过程。他并不享受，只是装装样子，然后继续前进。

En: Excellent job bullying that old man, officer, the lieutenant says with a frown. He looks impatient and not happy. "He'll be sure to put in a good word for the RCM in the future."
Zh: “欺负老人，你还真是干得漂亮极了，警官，”警督皱着眉头说道。看起来很不耐烦，也很不开心。“他将来肯定会为RCM*美言*几句的。”

En: I do not approve of this *abuse* of power nor your *redistribution* efforts. His features stiffen. "That's not the Revachol *I* fought for. Here..." He hands the sandwich to Gaston.
Zh: “我即不赞成这种*滥用*权利的行为，也不赞成你的劳力*再分配*。”他板着一张脸说道。“这不是*我*为之战斗的瑞瓦肖。给你……”他把三明治递给了加斯顿。

En: The man looks at his partner with suspicion, then his eyes light up, he pockets the sandwich and chuckles. "*Merci beaucoup, Capitaine Arnoux*!"
Zh: 男人疑惑地看着自己的同伴，接着眼睛突然亮了起来，他把三明治装进口袋，咯咯笑了起来。“非常感谢，阿诺克斯上尉！”

En: Hopefully it will finally take your fat ass to the other side of a cardiac arrest, Gaston. His words are slow and deliberate. "This doesn't mean anything. Doesn't *change* anything."
Zh: “希望它会让你这个死肥猪心跳骤停，加斯顿。”他的话语缓慢而从容。“这并不意味着什么。什么也没有*改变*。”

En: You can feel the jolly man pull back. This hurt. More than he was ready for.
Zh: 你能感觉到那个愉快的男人退却了。这让他心痛。比他准备承受的还要多。

En: He's right, you know? The lieutenant nods towards René. "We're supposed to protect people -- not take advantage of them. Now wrap this up."
Zh: “你知道吗，他说的没错。”警督朝着雷内点点头。“我们应该保护大家——而不是欺负他们。现在结束这一切吧。”

En: He doesn't think it was an excellent job at all -- he's disappointed.
Zh: 他完全不觉得你表现的很出色——他很失望。

En: All of *this*... -- he points to you -- "is very unprofessional. Makes me think the Citizens Militia is nothing but a badly organised band of bullies and jesters. Again: I *don't* like this."
Zh: “所有*这一切*……”——他指向你——“……都太不专业了。让我觉得公民武装不过是一群组织混乱的地痞流氓和小丑。再说一遍：我*不喜欢*这样。”

En: We're not, the officer was just... The lieutenant falls silent, looking for words to justify your sandwich antics.
Zh: “我们不是，这位警官只是……”警督陷入沉默，想找些话替你抢夺三明治的滑稽行为辩护。

En: Should have just eaten the damn thing.
Zh: 你就该直接吃了那个该死的玩意。

En: Like a machine.
Zh: 就像一台机器。

En: He seems agitated. Trying to control himself.
Zh: 他似乎有些焦虑，正试图控制自己的情绪。

En: He's afraid, what might happen if his *opinionated* partner is allowed to speak his mind freely.
Zh: 他很害怕，如果他那位*固执己见*的同伴可以畅所欲言的话，不知道会发生什么样的事情。

En: It's clear these words mean a lot to him.
Zh: 很明显，这些话对他来说意义重大。

En: You're right, I should have thought about it.
Zh: “你说得对，我应该考虑一下的。”

En: That's just *your* opinion.
Zh: “那只是*你的*意见。”

En: No. It isn't. He's absolutely right. He shakes his head. "I don't like it and I feel confident saying absolutely *no one* in your precinct would approve of this either."
Zh: “不。不是的。他说得完全没错。”他摇摇头。“我不喜欢这样，我可以很确信地说你们分局绝对*没有人*会赞同的。”

En: That's right. The former soldier nods, his fists curling in a ball. "And I'm *sharing* it with you so you can better yourself..."
Zh: “没错。”老兵点点头，攥紧了拳头。“而且我跟你*分享*它是为了你能更好地改变自己……”

En: Now, gentlemen... The lieutenant addresses the men in a casual manner. "If my partner doesn't have any other questions, we'll take off."
Zh: “现在，先生们……”警督随意地对男人们说道。“如果我的搭档没有其他问题的话，我们该走了。”

En: Yes, well... The lieutenant looks uncomfortable. "Let's just wrap this up and try to think about these things in the future."
Zh: “是的，好吧……”警督看起来有些不安。“这件事就到这里，以后我们会试着多考虑一下这些事情。”

En: Fine. You live, learn, and move on. The former soldier nods in agreement. "The matter is closed. Now what can this old carabineer do for you, officer?"
Zh: “好吧。活下去，不断学习，继续前进。”老兵赞同地点点头。“这件事就到此为止。现在，我这位老兵能为你做些什么呢，警官？”

En: Not a soul.
Zh: 没有灵魂。

En: Officer, the other one jumps in. "Let's not get sidetracked here with this nasty business. I know you wanted something from old René or me. Let's talk about that instead."
Zh: “警官，”另一个人插了进来。“我们就不要在这件讨厌的事情上分心了。我知道你希望从老雷内或者我这里得到些什么。不如就聊聊那个吧。”

En: You are representing an organisation, an insignia, an idea. The old man continues in a softer tone. "You need to understand that."
Zh: “你代表的是一个组织，一种标志，一个概念。”老人的语气柔和了许多。“你必须理解这一点。”

En: C'mon, I was only joking around.
Zh: “拜托，我只是开个玩笑。”

En: I didn't find it funny.
Zh: “我觉得这一点也不好笑。”

En: Me neither. The lieutenant looks weary of this whole situation. "I don't think anyone did."
Zh: “我也不觉得。”警督已经厌倦了整个情况。“我想任何人都不会觉得好笑。”

En: It was quite funny, officer. He lets out a keen laughter. "I thought it was very funny."
Zh: “挺有意思的，警官。”他发出一阵尖锐的笑声。“我觉得特别好笑。”

En: Unbelievable... How can you even walk up straight with that liquid backbone of yours, Gaston? He shakes his head. "Officer, what do you want?"
Zh: “难以置信……你的脊椎里全是酒精，怎么还能走得直呢，加斯顿？”他摇摇头。“警官，你有什么需要？”

En: I'm just gonna go -- we'll all forget about it at some point. [Leave.]
Zh: “我要走了——在某个时刻，我们全都会忘记的。”[离开。]

En: I'm satisfied with this explanation.
Zh: 我对这个解释很满意。

En: I myself use *speed* to transcend...
Zh: “我用*思必得*来超越自己……”

En: I drink alcohol and go to heaven...
Zh: “我通过喝酒前往天堂……”

En: Pyrholidon helps me connect to higher realms...
Zh: “吡嚯浣酮能帮我连接至高领域……”

En: But imagine -- purest cocaine... Now that would make you feel like a king. Not that you can afford it... But you could do a bit more speed. That would be okay. Would help you stay sharp.
Zh: 想象一下——最纯净的可卡因……肯定会让你感觉像个国王。倒不是说你能负担得起……不过你可以多磕一点思必得。那样也可以的。它能帮你保持清醒。

En: Sounds like there's a story there, but I'd like to talk about something else right now.
Zh: “听起来有故事啊，不过现在我想聊点别的。”

En: Sounds controversial. Let's talk about something else.
Zh: “听起来很有争议。我们先聊点别的吧。”

En: I didn't think you had the stomach for it. He nods grimly. "And I can usually tell."
Zh: “我觉得你没那么胆量。”他冷漠地点点头。“而且我总是能分辨出来的。”

En: Didn't think you had the stomach for it. He nods grimly. "Kids don't these days. I'm used to that."
Zh: “我觉得你也承受不住。”他冷漠地点点头。“现在的孩子啊。我都习惯了。”

En: Whoaa... sounds like you're about to open the gates of conversation -- this man will literally talk your ear off, if you let him wander off to Memory Lane.
Zh: 哇啊……听起来你要让他打开话匣子了——如果你让这个男人开始人生感怀，他绝对会唠叨个没完。

En: Honour is everything to me, he says with grim finality.
Zh: “荣誉对我来说就是一切，”他冷酷而决绝的说道。

En: Listen, René, I might wanna hear that story later, but right now I have more important things to discuss.
Zh: “听着，雷内，也许之后我可以听你讲故事，不过现在我还有更重要的事要讨论。”

En: Fine, he snaps at you, obviously annoyed for the interruption. "What is it then?"
Zh: “好吧，”他厉声说道，显然因为被打断有些恼火。“那你有什么事？”

En: Purple velvet tunic, the lieutenant says thoughtfully. "That isn't exactly *camo*."
Zh: “紫色天鹅绒长袍，”警督若有所思地说道。“那可算不上是什么*伪装*。”

En: So what happened?
Zh: “所以发生了什么？”

En: It's painful to even think about.
Zh: 就连想起来都会痛苦。

En: Hang on to the story -- veterans get sentimental after such *retellings* -- this might yield something useful.
Zh: 抓住这个故事不放——在这类*复述*之后，老兵会变得多愁善感起来——这样说不定就能捕捉到什么有用的信息。

En: He really despises that Drysant fellow.
Zh: 他是真的很鄙视那个叫德里桑特的家伙。

En: Shattered knee... interesting. Actually, can we pick it up later and do questions now?
Zh: “粉碎的膝盖……有点意思。事实上，这个话题能不能之后再继续，我先问你几个问题？”

En: Ugh... The old man looks at you, obviously annoyed by the interruption. "Sure. Yes. Yes, you can ask your questions."
Zh: “唔……”老人看着你，因为被打断明显有些恼怒。“当然。是的，可以，有问题你就问吧。”

En: The bane of horses.
Zh: 马的祸害。

En: I'd give up. Can't imagine living without my jaw. (Touch your jaw.)
Zh: “要我就会放弃。无法想象没有下巴该怎么活。”（摸摸你的下巴。）

En: This would never happen to Johnny Law-jaw. (Point to your jaw.) "My jaw is tight."
Zh: “这种事永远不会发生在警官的下巴上。”（指着自己的下巴。）“我的下巴牢固的很。”

En: I've been through worse.
Zh: “我经历过更糟糕的事。”

En: He didn't. A shadow of respect crosses his face. "I hoisted the prick on my back and started crawling. Kept going until the 59th Cavalry picked us up."
Zh: “他没有。”他的脸上掠过一丝尊敬的幽影。“我把他抗在背上，然后开始往前爬。直到第59骑兵团发现我们。”

En: Right, right... -- he doesn't get sidetracked -- "so I grabbed the prick and started crawling. Kept going until the 59th Cavalry picked us up."
Zh: “好吧，好吧……”他没有分心。“所以我抓住了那个混蛋，开始爬行。直到第59骑兵团发现我们。”

En: I doubt it, he remarks dryly. "Anyway, I grabbed the prick and started crawling. Kept going until the 59th Cavalry picked us up."
Zh: “我很怀疑，”他干巴巴地评论到。“不管怎么说，我抓住了他，然后开始往前爬。直到第59骑兵团发现我们。”

En: Duty, medals, honour, chain of command -- I like this guy. He's not a pansy. I don't like pansies.
Zh: 责任，奖章，荣誉，命令——我喜欢这个家伙。他不是同性恋。我不喜欢同性恋。

En: I'm *not* getting into this with you again. He mumbles through clenched teeth and turns to you. "Officer, was there anything else?"
Zh: “我*不会*再跟你讲这些了，”他咬紧牙关嘟囔着，然后转向你。“警官，还有别的事吗？”

En: Why do you despise Drysant so much?
Zh: “你为什么那么鄙视德里桑特？”

En: Is that... pride in his voice? It's deep down, but -- maybe even unbeknownst to the man himself -- it's there.
Zh: 他的声音里……是有一丝骄傲吗？隐藏地很深，不过——甚至连他自己都不知道——它就在那里。

En: Hold on, you're just a *little bit* proud of René's heroics, aren't you?
Zh: “等一下，你对雷内的英雄事迹有那么*一点点*骄傲，对吗？”

En: Sorry, officer, but you're reading me all wrong. He chuckles. "I'm a man of peace and these kinds of bloody 'heroics' are only impressive to men like René himself. Certainly not to me."
Zh: “抱歉，警官，但是你完全看错我了。”他咯咯地笑了起来。“我是个爱好和平的男人，这种血腥的‘英雄事迹’只会打动雷内那种男人。我肯定没有。”

En: He's lying.
Zh: 他在撒谎。

En: How did *you* find the story to be, officer?
Zh: “*你*觉得这个故事怎么样呢，警官？”

En: This doesn't seem like a good moment to express an opinion. (Lean closer.) "I don't wanna risk it."
Zh: “现在似乎不是表达意见的好时机。”（靠近一些。）“我不想冒这个险。”

En: *Bon dieu*, he mumbles, slowly shaking his head. "You and Gaston must be related. His blood runs yellow too."
Zh: “*神明啊*，”他一边小声嘟囔，一边慢慢摇摇头。“你和加斯顿肯定是亲戚。他的血液也是黄色的。”

En: René, I wanted to ask, what *did* you actually get these medals for?
Zh: “雷内，我想问，你到底*做了*什么才拿到这些奖章的？”

En: René, you were telling me how you got the medals, I wanna hear the rest of the story.
Zh: “雷内，我们说过你是怎么拿到这些奖章的，我想把整个故事听完。”

En: René, the medal story -- Drysant's horse stepped on your knee, then what happened?
Zh: “雷内，关于奖章的故事——德里桑特的马踩碎了你的膝盖，然后发生了什么？”

En: Already forgotten, officer.
Zh: “已经忘记了，警官。”

En: The old carabineer stands quietly like a statue, his features motionless.
Zh: 老骑兵像尊雕像一样默默站着，脸上毫无表情。

En: You should try to come up with a heroic story of your own -- impress this old soldier.
Zh: 你应该试着想一个自己的英雄故事——打动这个老兵。

En: That's why the Filippian kings used cocaine -- for clarity of vision, to aid in their work... Regnum Cocainum -- Revachol's finest years. He seems to grow taller, brimming with pride about the past.
Zh: “这也是菲利普国王吸食可卡因的原因——为了获得清晰的愿景，来帮助他们的工作……可卡因统治期——瑞瓦肖最辉煌的岁月。”他似乎变得更加高大了，内心充满过去的骄傲。

En: Of course, the lieutenant marks dryly. "Clarity of vision. *Awareness*."
Zh: “当然，”警督干巴巴地评论到。“清晰的愿景。*警觉*。”

En: They used cocaine for *clarity of vision*?
Zh: “他们用可卡因来获得*清晰的愿景*？”

En: You can. It's very *asinine*.
Zh: 你可以想象。极度*愚蠢*。

En: Because he was a *god damn* dandy! he exclaims furiously. "Had no business leading men or even being on the battlefield. All he was, was *related*. That's it."
Zh: “因为他是个*该死的*花花公子！”他激烈地大声呼喊。“完全不懂指挥，甚至都没上过战场。他拥有的一切，只不过因为他是国王的*亲戚*。就这样。”

En: Royal blood alone doesn't make army commanders. He was a stupid kid, only interested in horses, hair styles, and *manloving*. He spits. "And *seven hundred and eighty two* Royal Carabineers are dead because of his incompetence."
Zh: “仅凭皇家血统并不能让他成为军队的指挥官。他是个愚蠢的孩子，只对马、发型和*男性之爱*感兴趣。”他吐了一口唾沫。“而且，因为他的无能，*782*位皇家骑兵就这么死去了。”

En: Whooa, *manloving*? Is that even a word?
Zh: 哇哦，*男性之爱*？有这个词的吗？

En: It is not.
Zh: 没有。

En: You must have seen *something* on the night of the murder. Your booth looks right into the yard.
Zh: “在凶杀案当晚你肯定看见了*什么*。你的岗亭正对着后院。”

En: Yes. It does. He nods. "Unfortunately I wasn't working that night. Been on a two-week leave since last Monday."
Zh: “是的，没错。”他点点头。“可惜，那晚我没有上班。自从上周一，我就开始为期两周的休假了。”

En: No one. The booth has been unmanned since last Monday. He looks suddenly very old and tired. "There's no other guard. It's just me."
Zh: “没有人。岗亭从上周一开始就没人看守了。”突然间，他显得苍老又疲惫。“没有其他守卫。只有我。”

En: Hold on, why are you on a leave?
Zh: “等一下，你为什么要休假？”

En: So who was working your shift that night?
Zh: “那天晚上是谁在值你的班呢？”

En: Evrart *created* this job for René, because he knows the Royal Carabineer's pension of honour and PTSD isn't something a man can live off. 'A decorated kingsman collecting tare reflects bad on the whole neighbourhood.' -- his words.
Zh: “艾弗拉特为雷内*创造*了这个工作，因为他知道皇家骑兵的荣誉抚恤金和PTSD是无法让人维持生活的。‘一个授勋的骑士去捡破烂有损整个社区的形象。’——这是他的原话。”

En: It's a private matter, he says with dignity. "Nothing to do with your investigation."
Zh: “这是一件私事，”他带着尊严说道。“跟你的调查没有任何关系。”

En: Look, officers, his partner jumps in. "The container yard doesn't actually need a guardsman. Never had one before René. Mr. Claire had that booth built specially for him. It's mostly *decorative*."
Zh: “你看，警官，”他的同伴插了进来。“集装箱堆场其实并不需要守卫。在雷内之前也没有过守卫。克莱尔先生专门为他造了那座岗亭。基本上就是个*装饰*。”

En: Look, officer, his partner jumps in. "The container yard doesn't actually need a guardsman. Never had one before René. Mr. Claire had that booth built specially for him. It's mostly *decorative*."
Zh: “你看，警官，”他的同伴插了进来。“集装箱堆场其实并不需要守卫。在雷内之前也没有过守卫。克莱尔先生专门为他造了那座岗亭。基本上就是个*装饰*。”

En: You see, officer, René is the kind of man who'd rather die than admit he needs medical assistance or -- god forbid -- seek it. A real man's man. He's just gonna *ride it out*.
Zh: “你看，警官，雷内是那种宁死也不承认需要医疗援助或者——但愿不会发生这种事——主动寻求帮助的人。一个男人中的男人。他只会自己*克服困难*。”

En: The container yard has been unguarded for a week?
Zh: “集装箱堆场有一周都是毫无防备的吗？”

En: I'm fine, goddamnit! Mind your own business! The outburst makes him clutch his chest. "It's nothing, just got to cut back on coffee."
Zh: “我很好，该死！管好你自己的事吧！”突然的爆发让他抓紧了自己的胸口。“这根本不算什么，只要少喝点咖啡就行。”

En: The *possibility* of someone being in there is enough to discourage any ill-minded individuals... he tries to argue.
Zh: “有人守在里面的*可能性*，就足够阻止那些不怀好意的个人了……”他努力争辩着。

En: It's his heart.
Zh: 他的心脏有问题。

En: No one has been guarding the container yard since last Monday?
Zh: “自从上周一以来，就没人看守集装箱堆场了吗？

En: Yes. He nods, before hesitantly continuing. "It's... it's not actually an issue. I mean..."
Zh: “是的。”他点点头，犹豫着要不要继续。“这个……其实算不上什么问题。我的意思是……”

En: Evrart gets it. Big guys looking after the small and everyone working together -- I love it!
Zh: “艾弗拉特做到了。大家伙照顾小家伙，大家一起工作——我喜欢这样！”

En: Such dependency only weakens a man further. Do or die -- there is no middle ground.
Zh: “这种依赖只会让男人变得更加软弱。非生即死——没有中间地带。”

En: René is but one man. We need a program -- get all the elderly back in the job market, keep folks motivated.
Zh: “雷内只有一个人。我们需要一个计划——让所有老年人重回就业市场，让大家保持积极上进的状态。”

En: René should *rent* out his services, invest the profit, get few more guys, expand and repeat. Wagework is a dead end.
Zh: “雷内应该*出租*他的服务，投资利润，稍微雇几个人，扩展业务，然后不断复制。拿死工资是没有出路的。”

En: There's absolutely *nothing* wrong with tare-collecting. It's my side-thing too. (Proudly hold out the tare bag.)
Zh: “废物回收绝对没有*任何*问题。这也是我的副业。”（骄傲地拿出回收袋。）

En: There's a grimace of pain on the old soldier's face. Looks like he wants to argue, but can't find words. Finally his lips move: "Can we conclude the topic of my guard booth now?"
Zh: 老兵的脸因为痛苦而变得扭曲。他似乎很想争辩，但却找不到合适的话语。最后，他的嘴唇动了动：“现在可以结束岗亭的话题了吗？”

En: It's not charity. Not to me... There's a grimace of pain on the old soldier's face. Looks like he wants to add something, but can't find the words. Finally his eyes light up and he says:
Zh: “这不是慈善。对我来说不是……”老兵的脸因为痛苦而变得扭曲。他似乎很想争辩，但却找不到合适的话语。最后，他的眼睛亮了起来，说道：

En: Look, officer... He pauses like he doesn't actually know how to reply. "Can we conclude the topic of my guard booth now?"
Zh: “你看，警官……”他停顿了一下，似乎并不知道该怎么回复。“现在可以结束岗亭的话题了吗？”

En: Yes, yes, yes, the carabineer utters angrily. "Can we conclude the topic of my guard booth now?"
Zh: “是啊，是啊，是啊，”老兵愤怒地回复到。“现在可以结束岗亭的话题了吗？”

En: Oh, I didn't mean to imply there's something *wrong* with that, the jolly man says quickly. "I do it too. Everyone does it. It's an excellent side-thing."
Zh: “哦，我并不是想暗示那有什么*问题*，”愉快的男人飞快地说道。“我也会这样做的。大家都会。这是个很棒的副业。”

En: Still, all you see is an old soldier refusing to replace his uniform with a civilian attire.
Zh: 然而，你看见的只是一个拒绝脱下制服、换上便服的老兵。

En: Anything else I can assist you with, officer? he asks impatiently. "We still have a game to finish."
Zh: “还有什么别的能帮你的吗，警官？”他不耐烦地问到。“我们的游戏还没结束呢。”

En: Is there anything you can tell me about this rifle? (Hold out the antique firearm.)
Zh: “你能跟我说说这支枪吗？”（拿出古董枪。）

En: It's a *Belle-Magrave*, he says, taking the rifle. "4.46 calibre, breech-loading, Revachol-made. Good weapon, accurate and reliable."
Zh: “这是*贝莱-马格雷*，”他接过步枪，说道。“4.46毫米口径，后膛装填，瑞瓦肖产的。很好的武器，精准又可靠。”

En: His moves are quick and precise as he first checks the weapon, then aims it at the sea. This man knows firearms *intimately*.
Zh: 他先检查了一下武器，然后举起枪瞄准大海，动作迅速而精准。这个男人非常*熟悉*这种枪械。

En: This one's inoperable. The bolt-spring is missing and the mechanism is jammed shut. Still a beauty, he adds, handing the rifle back. "Where did you find her?"
Zh: “这支已经不能用了。栓簧不见了，机械装置卡死了。不过还是那么漂亮，”他递回步枪，补充了一句。“你是在哪里找到的？”

En: In the basement there. (Point at the book shop.)
Zh: “在那边的地下室。”（指向书店。）

En: Sorry, I can't divulge that information.
Zh: “抱歉，我不能透露这个信息。”

En: What do you mean *find*? This is my rifle, I always have it with me.
Zh: “你说*找到*是什么意思？这是我的步枪，我一直带在身边的。”

En: I'm not surprised. He looks at the building. "There are probably lots of forgotten war-time weapons lying around here. Back in the day, everyone had *something* stashed away."
Zh: “没什么好惊讶的。”他看着建筑说道。“那里可能有很多种战时遗留的武器。那个时候，大家都会藏些*什么东西*。”

En: He nods. "I understand. Unfortunately, I don't know what else to tell you. These BM's are an antique. No one uses them anymore. The ammunition is impossible to find."
Zh: 他点点头。“我能理解。可惜我不知道还能告诉你什么。这种BM是古董。现在已经没人用了。弹药是不可能找到的。”

En: As for the rifle -- I don't know what else to tell you. He shrugs. "These BM446s are an antique. No one uses them anymore. The ammunition is impossible to find."
Zh: “至于这支步枪——我不知道还能告诉你什么。”他耸耸肩。“这支BM446是古董。现在已经没人用了。弹药是不可能找到的。”

En: Up there I give it all I got. I *earn* my keep. He looks towards the guard booth and sighs. "But you're right officer, there is no middle ground. It's do or die."
Zh: “在那里，我已经尽我所能了。生活费是我自己*挣来*的。”他看向岗亭，叹了口气。“不过你说得对，警官，没有中间地带。要么生，要么死。”

En: Something about him has changed. He's calmer somehow.
Zh: 他有些地方变了。不知道怎么的，他变得更加平静了。

En: He wants to argue, disagree -- but feels he's got no right, being at the mercy of Mr. Claire.
Zh: 他想要争辩，否认——不过他觉得听命于克莱尔先生的自己没有这个权利。

En: Mostly decorative?
Zh: “基本就是个装饰？”

En: So it doesn't matter, if you're there or not?
Zh: “所以不管你在不在，其实都无所谓咯？”

En: He is not going to become an entrepreneur.
Zh: 他不会变成一个企业家的。

En: Thank you for your time! [Leave.]
Zh: “感谢你的宝贵时间！”[离开。]

En: Could it be... guilt?
Zh: 会不会是……内疚？

En: Did you ever use artillery fire against the *communards*?
Zh: “你有没有对*公社社员*使用过炮火？”

En: Pretty damn clever indeed.
Zh: “确实挺聪明的。”

En: Bastards... (Slowly shake your head.)
Zh: “混蛋……”（慢慢摇摇头。）

En: Must have been a bloodpath.
Zh: “肯定是一场血战吧。”

En: May bells don't blossom yet, do they? the lieutenant quickly asks.
Zh: “铃兰还没有开花，不是吗，”警督快速地发问到。

En: Maybe on some remote parts of the city they do. The old carabineer shrugs. "But I think you have to wait for at least a month."
Zh: “也许城市的偏远地区已经开花了。”老兵耸耸肩。“不过我觉得至少还要再等一个月。”

En: He's just making excuses for the king's habits, isn't he?
Zh: 他只是在为国王的习惯找借口，不是吗？

En: He catches your glance and nods. "This is the uniform of the Royal Carabineers in service of Frissel the First, Guillaume *Le Lion*, and the valiant King Filippe the Fifth before him."
Zh: 他捕捉到你的目光，点了点头。“这是侍奉弗里塞尔一世，*狮子王*纪尧姆，还有他之前那位英勇的国王菲利普五世的皇家骑兵制服。”

En: *Croix de Bravoure* and The Setting Sun. (Point to his chest.) "Did you get them for..."
Zh: “*英勇十字勋章*和落日勋章。”（指着他的胸口。）“你是怎么得到的……”

En: *Croix de Bravoure*... (Point to his chest.) "Did you get it for..."
Zh: “*英勇十字勋章*……”（指着他的胸口。）“你是怎么得到的……”

En: The Setting Sun... (Point to his chest.) "Did you get it for..."
Zh: “落日勋章……”（指着他的胸口。）“你是怎么得到的……”

En: The medals. (Point to his chest.) "Did you get them for..."
Zh: “这枚奖章。”（指着他的胸口。）“你是怎么得到的……”

En: For bravery, he interjects.
Zh: “因为勇敢，”他突然插了一嘴。

En: It's a conflicted topic for the old veteran. There must have been a number of controversial episodes in his service days.
Zh: 这个话题让老兵有些抵触。在他服役的日子里，肯定有过不少这样有争议的小插曲。

En: That's what I was going to say, bravery.
Zh: “我也打算这么说，勇敢。”

En: Not for killing babies or...?
Zh: “不是杀害婴孩吗，或者……”

En: Not for raping women or...?
Zh: “不是因为强暴女性吗，或者……”

En: I'm sure. But I know this uniform's reputation: You were also wondering if I got these for raping women or killing babies.
Zh: “我很肯定。但是我知道这身制服的名声：你也想知道暗示我是不是通过强暴女人或者杀害婴孩得来的。”

En: No, I really wasn't, I'm just asking.
Zh: “不，我真没有，我只是随便问问。”

En: So, did you?
Zh: “所以，你有吗？”

En: For bravery?
Zh: “因为勇敢？”

En: What *Monseigneur Modestie* is not telling you is that he crawled over seven kilometres before the cavalrymen found him and Drysant. Two days later that was.
Zh: “这位*谦逊的老爷*没告诉你的是：在骑兵团发现他和德里桑特的时候，他已经爬行了7公里以上。那已经是两天之后的事了。”

En: You sense he's downplaying it -- he did a lot more than his duty. More than anyone's duty. It's in his spine, in his billowing breast. An untarnished self worth.
Zh: 你感觉到他是故意在轻描淡写——他的所作所为已经超出了他自己的职责范围。超出了任何人的职责范围。它就藏在他的脊梁里，藏在他挺起的胸膛。这份自我价值感并未褪色。

En: Do you know anything about the man hanged in the back yard of Whirling-in-Rags?
Zh: “你知道褴褛飞旋后院那个被吊起来的男人吗？”

En: Don't you mean Frissel the Fun?
Zh: “你的意思是享乐派弗里塞尔吗？”
