En: *You* do not speak his name, craven! Although he was a clown... he adds. He turns back to you. "But he was *our* clown. Ours to ridicule -- and to mourn."
Zh: “*你*不配叫他的名字，懦夫！尽管他是个小丑……”他补充了一句，然后转向你。“但他也是*我们*的小丑。只有我们才能嘲笑——和哀悼。”

En: It is the *Croix de Bravoure* -- Cross of Valour. The Cross was the highest battlefield decoration in the Suzerain's armed forces, awarded for exceptional bravery in the line of duty in service of King Frissel the First.
Zh: 这是*英勇十字勋章*，宗主国武装部队最高级别的战争奖章，用于嘉奖弗里塞尔国王一世麾下表现最英勇的战士。

En: *The Setting Sun* was a decoration used to distinguish seasoned combat veterans in service of King Frissel the First during the Revolution.
Zh: *落日勋章*是一种奖章，专门用于表彰大革命中为弗里塞尔国王一世服务的那些久经沙场的老兵。

En: So what *did* you get the medals for?
Zh: “那你是*做了*什么才拿到奖章的？”

En: For doing my duty in the heat of battle, for looking my mortality in the eye, when men like Gaston here hid in the bushes and shat themselves...
Zh: “因为我在战斗最激烈的时候履行了我的职责，因为敢于直面我的死亡，而加斯顿这种人就只会藏在灌木丛里，大小便失禁……”

En: He saved some *maudit* princeling who foolishly strolled into the front line in his gown of velvet and gold.
Zh: “他救了一个*该死*的小王公，傻里傻气地穿着他的天鹅绒和金丝长袍在前线漫步。”

En: Saved a princeling?
Zh: “救了一个小王公？”

En: It was on the first months of the Revolution here in Revachol. Unrest was spreading like wildfire. Marauders had taken most of the Couron and were getting *really* ambitious.
Zh: “那是瑞瓦肖大革命的头几个月。动乱像野火一般蔓延。掠夺者已经占领了科戎的大部分地区，变得*特别*野心勃勃。”

En: King Frissel thought he could end it all in one decisive strike. The old carabineer runs his fingers over the larger medal. "Sent his cousin, Drysant, to put an end to the unrest."
Zh: “弗里塞尔国王认为他可以在一场决定性的战斗中结束这一切。”老骑兵的手指拂过胸前的大奖章。“于是派出了他的堂兄弟，德里桑特来结束动乱。”

En: Alas, the young Drysant was all piss and no vinegar, wearing a tunic of purple velvet and cockatoo feathers to battle. He spits. "Even his rifle was *gold-plated*. Shone from five klicks away. Can you imagine the asininity?"
Zh: “哎，年轻的德里桑特精力过于旺盛，穿着紫色天鹅绒束腰外衣和凤头鹦鹉羽毛上战场。”他吐了口唾沫。“就连他的步枪都是*镀金*的。五公里以外都能看到它的反光。你能想象到有多愚蠢吗？”

En: What a dandy!
Zh: “真是个花花公子！”

En: To keep the long and bloody story short, Drysant marched us against the partisans in Couron. And when I say 'marched', I mean made us walk into captured enemy territory single-file, like toy soldiers, while he rode in front on his giant red stallion.
Zh: “长话短说，德里桑特让我们进军科戎，跟游击队对抗。我说的‘进军’，指的是让我们像玩具士兵一样，排成一列纵队走进敌占区，而他自己骑着那匹高大的红色种马，走在我们前面。”

En: The rebels were smart. They let us come real close before opening fire. Suffice to say, it was carnage.
Zh: “反抗军很聪明。他们等我们靠的很近之后才开火。可以说，那是一场大屠杀。”

En: I got shot in the left shoulder and went down. Just a flesh wound, but just as I turned over, the prince fell into the mud next to me. He was missing his lower jaw.
Zh: “我左肩中了一枪，然后倒下了。只是皮肉伤，不过我刚转身，小王公就摔在了我旁边的泥地里。他的下巴已经没有了。”

En: Then his horse, driven mad by the noise and smell of gunpowder, stepped on my leg and shattered my knee. He pats his right thigh.
Zh: “然后他的马，被噪音和火药的味道逼疯了，一脚踩在我的腿上，踩碎了我的膝盖。”他拍了拍右边的大腿。

En: Okay... then what did you do?
Zh: “好吧……那你是怎么做的？”

En: When I came to, it was all over, he continues, ignoring his companion. "It was just me and jawless Drysant, gurgling in the blood-soaked mud right next to me."
Zh: “当我醒来的时候，一切都结束了，”他没有理会自己的同伴，继续说道。“只剩下我和无颚德里桑特，就在我身边血淋淋的泥地里咕噜直响。”

En: I grabbed my sidearm and shot the beast in the head. Then everything went black.
Zh: “我抓起自己的配枪，朝那头野兽的脑袋开了一枪。然后眼前一切都变黑了。”

En: *Capitaine Arnoux -- le fléau des chevaux*!
Zh: “*阿诺克斯上尉——马之灾祸*。”

En: The dink had taken numerous flesh wounds and lost a lot of blood, but despite missing his jaw he seemed hesitant to die. Tougher than he looked, that one.
Zh: “那呆子身上有不少皮肉伤，还流了很多血，尽管没了下巴，他似乎还在犹豫要不要去死。那家伙，比看起来要坚强多了。”

En: That's no dink, that's a fighter!
Zh: “那不是呆子，那是一个战士！”

En: So I grabbed the *dink*, he says, with a stern look, "and started crawling. Kept going until the 59th Cavalry picked us up."
Zh: “所以我抓住*那呆子*，”他带着严厉的表情说道，“然后开始向前爬，直到第59骑兵团来接我们。”

En: Through some miracle we both survived. And the jawless freak convinced Frissel to give me a medal for not leaving him to die in his own blood, piss, and shit.
Zh: “我们俩奇迹般的活了下来。无颚怪人说服弗里塞尔给我颁发了一枚奖章，因为我没有留下他一个人死在自己的血泊里，满身污秽。”

En: He was the commanding officer and I was on duty. Just doing my job. Shouldn't hand out medals for that... He shakes his head. "Thirteen months later I received 'The Sun'. For distinguished service. It's not worth mentioning."
Zh: “他是指挥官，我正好在执行任务。只是做我自己份内的事。没必要给我颁发奖章……”他摇摇头。“13个月后，我获得了‘落日勋章’。因为卓越的服役贡献。根本不值一提。”

En: And that even whilst crawling with a mangled half-dead prince on his back, he still managed to murder three rebels on his way.
Zh: “即使到了那个时候，他还是背着血肉横飞、半死不活的小王公往前爬，而且途中还杀了三个反抗军。”

En: Maybe, maybe, but also bear in mind, officer... He points to the sun-shaped medal on René's chest. "They don't hand these out for anyone with a service record."
Zh: “也许，也许吧，不过要记住，警官……”他指着雷内胸口的太阳形奖章。“他们可不会随意把这玩意颁给任何服役过的人。”

En: Oh no, you have to get shot. He nods eagerly. "Repeatedly. And you need to get your hands bloody too. Really *really* bloody."
Zh: “哦不，你必须挨枪子。”他急切地点点头。“反反复复。而且你的手也要沾满鲜血。真的非常、*非常*血腥。”

En: Do not speak of what you know nothing about, poltroon! He slams the heel of his boot in the ground. "*Duty* is something you will never understand."
Zh: “别净说些你完全不知道的事，懦夫！”他的后脚跟砰的一声踩在地面上。“*责任*这种东西你永远也理解不了。”

En: You seem to be playing in a crater.
Zh: “你们好像在一个弹坑里玩。”

En: I'll leave you to it... [Leave.]
Zh: “我不打扰你了……”[离开。]

En: Enough, he cuts in sharply. "I can go over these matters *in detail* with you, Gaston, but not while we have company. So officer...?"
Zh: “够了，”他激烈地插了一嘴。“加斯顿，我可以*详细的*跟你重温一遍那些事情，不过现在还有外人。所以，警官们……？”

En: Enough, he cuts in sharply. "I can go over these matters *in detail* with you, Gaston, but not while we have company. So officers...?"
Zh: “够了，”他激烈地插了一嘴。“加斯顿，我可以*详细的*跟你重温一遍那些事情，不过现在还有外人。所以，警官们……？”

En: René... I need to go back in time. Can you help me?
Zh: “雷内……我得回到过去。你能帮帮我吗？”

En: Shush! Ignore them! They don't know what they're doing. They're *old*! You are letting down yourself and the team. Get in the damn game already!
Zh: 嘘！无视他们！他们不知道自己在做什么。他们*老了*！你让你自己和整个队伍失望了。积极行动起来吧！

En: Observe them before you do anything.
Zh: 在你做任何事之前，先观察一下他们。

En: Better observe them first.
Zh: 最好先观察一下他们。

En: See? Your munching and complaining have ruined my concentration. The man throws a metal ball toward a smaller, wooden ball in the sand, missing it by a metre.
Zh: “看见没？你的咀嚼声和抱怨让我难以集中精神。”男人把一个金属球扔向沙地里的一个小木球，差了那么一米。

En: Trying to throw something as close to a predetermined point as possible... measuring... this must be the age-old game of pétanque.
Zh: 尝试把某个东西尽可能地扔到预先指定的地点……测量……这肯定是一种古老的地掷球游戏。

En: Alright, I got this -- ball time!
Zh: 好吧，我可以的——玩球时间到！

En: That's the spirit! Don't even waste your breath asking about the game. They wouldn't know anyway. They're *way* past their prime.
Zh: 就是这种精神！都不用浪费口舌去询问游戏的事。反正他们也不会知道。他们的黄金年代*早就*已经过去了。

En: René, you're a man with a fork in a world of soup. Please... let's just try to enjoy the game, alright? The other one is eating a big sandwich.
Zh: “雷内，你跟这个世界完全格格不入。拜托……我们试着去享受这场游戏，好吗？”另一个人正在吃一个大三明治。

En: No, you got this! There's the ball -- you're the game!
Zh: 不，你可以的！球在那里——你加入游戏了！

En: Shouldn't I ask what game it is first?
Zh: 我不应该先问问是怎么玩的吗？

En: These manly men are playing *balls*. This is a *ball* game. Grab a ball and play it. Don't ask questions. Shoot first, ask questions never.
Zh: 这些充满男子气概的男人在玩*球*。这是一个*球类*游戏。抓起一个球，然后玩它就对了。别问问题。先出手，永远不要问问题。

En: Have you no shame, the man says to his partner, "whining about your back every time you bring out the measuring tape?"
Zh: “你没有羞耻心的吗，”男人对着自己的同伴说道。“每次把卷尺拿出来的时候都要抱怨自己的后背？”

En: A moment of your time, fellows. (Don't get involved in the game.)
Zh: “享受你们的时光吧，伙计们。”（不要加入游戏。）

En: René, you're a man with a fork in a world of soup. Please... let's just try to enjoy the game, alright? This one's still chewing on his sandwich.
Zh: “雷内，你跟这个世界完全格格不入。拜托……我们试着去享受这场游戏，好吗？”另一个人还在吃他的三明治。

En: Sure, officer. I am René, René Arnoux, and my *specially abled* partner here is Gaston Martin. How can we help you? The man in the uniform stands before you tall and proud.
Zh: “当然，警官。我是雷内，雷内·阿诺克斯，我这位*身体特别健全*的同伴是加斯顿·马丁。我们能帮你什么吗？”穿着制服的男人昂首挺胸地站在你面前。

En: You're such a wuss!
Zh: 你可真是个胆小鬼！

En: I hope you pass out from it, you goddamn jellyfish. Men like you are the reason this nation is sinking. Standing tall and proud, he looks at his partner with disgust.
Zh: “我希望你直接晕过去，你这个该死的懦夫。就是因为有你这种男人，这个国家才会沉沦。”他昂首挺胸地站着，一脸嫌恶的看着自己的同伴。

En: Right. The old man stands tall and proud, looking at you inquisitively.
Zh: “好吧。”老男人昂首挺胸地站着，好奇地打量着你。

En: Look. Look! In his hand, going into his mouth! The sandwich, it's hauntingly beautiful!
Zh: 看。快看！在他的手里，要塞到他嘴里去了！那个三明治，美的让人难以忘怀！

En: I'm trying to, but you keep breaking my concentration. You're old, I can see that. We're both old. Now stop grabbing your ass like it's a girl.
Zh: “我在努力，但你一直在分散我的注意力。你老了，我能看出来。我们都老了。现在别把你的屁股当成小姑娘的一样抓个不停了。”

En: Could the objective of the game be to throw the metal ball so it lands by the wooden ball?
Zh: 这个游戏的目标会不会是把金属球扔出去，让它落在小木球旁边？

En: Watch out, Gaston... He points at you. "It's Inspector Oaf. Come to throw more of our stuff into the sea, officer?"
Zh: “小心，加斯顿……”他指着你。“白痴警探来了。怎么，还想把更多东西扔进海里吗，警官？”

En: I like playing in the dark. Sharpens your nocturnal instincts... Feels like being on recon again.
Zh: “我喜欢在黑暗中玩耍。能够让你的夜间直觉变得更敏锐……又有种侦查的感觉。”

En: The purity of snow always reminds me of the purity of a man's soul.... He looks at the falling snow. "If he's got principles."
Zh: “雪的纯净总是让我想到人类纯洁的灵魂……”他看着漫天飞雪。“如果他充满信念的话。”

En: Your munching has ruined my concentration!
Zh: “你吃那么大声干嘛，我都不能集中注意力了！”

En: Offic... argh... He rubs his chest and spits. "Goddamnit... pull yourself together, René." He turns to you. "What do you want?"
Zh: “警官……呃……”他揉了揉胸口，吐了一口唾沫。“该死……振作一点，雷内。”他转向你。“你有什么需要？”

En: Forgetting those times means the mistakes were for nothing. That all those people died for nothing.
Zh: “忘记那些时光就意味着那些错误是毫无意义的。所有那些人都白白牺牲了。”

En: Quite impressive. It's men like René who made Revachol great once.
Zh: “挺让人印象深刻的。就是有了雷内这种人，瑞瓦肖才能一度变得强大。”

En: I'm not impressed. All those heroics and where is he now? No, you gotta play to win. Look after *you* first.
Zh: “我没什么感觉。就算有那么多英雄事迹，看看他现在什么地方？不，玩游戏就是要赢。先照顾好*你*自己吧。”

En: René, I found your guard booth.
Zh: “雷内，我发现你的岗亭了。”

En: Yes, the Débardeurs' Union pays me to stand vigil during the nights. He looks down. "Not out of any *political* allegiance, mind you."
Zh: “是的，装卸工会付钱让我晚上守夜。”他低下头。“请注意，这不代表我向任何*政治*效忠。”

En: I'm an old man, don't sleep more than a few hours every night anyway and... money is tight, he adds with a slight sigh.
Zh: “我是个老年人，反正每天晚上也睡不了几个小时，而且……我手头很紧，”他轻叹一声。

En: He feels like he has to justify himself for some reason.
Zh: 他觉得必须找些为自己辩护的理由。

En: Got it, thanks. (Conclude.)
Zh: “明白了，谢谢。”（总结。）

En: Yes, yes. Like I said, he brings it up again, "I would be up *anyway*, so might as well keep an eye out. It keeps my senses sharp."
Zh: “是的，是的。就像我说过的，”他又提了一遍，“*反正*我也睡不着，所以不妨留心一下。这样能让我保持敏锐的直觉。”

En: His features stiffen and he gets a cold look. "She... is nobody. This is none of your concern and I refuse to discuss my private affairs with the RCM."
Zh: 他面容僵硬，表情冷淡。“她……谁也不是。这跟你没关系，我拒绝跟RCM讨论我的私人问题。”

En: The lady is Jeanne-Marie Beaulieu. Gaston speaks with a soft voice. "And she sure as hell wasn't a nobody."
Zh: “那位女士叫珍妮-玛丽·博利厄。”加斯顿柔声说道。“她肯定不是什么无关紧要的人。”

En: I understand Jeanne-Marie meant a lot to you?
Zh: “根据我的理解，这位珍妮-玛丽对你来说一定很重要吧？”

En: I was 22 when I returned from King Guillaume's Ikeira Operation in the south and found my sweetheart in the arms of this wretch...
Zh: “我从纪尧姆国王的南方伊卡里亚行动归来时，正好22岁。结果发现我的甜心就躺在这个卑鄙小人的怀抱里……”

En: There's nothing for you to *understand* here, he snaps. "It is not *her* death you are investigating."
Zh: “这里没什么需要你*理解*的，”他厉声说道。“你调查的又不是*她*的死亡案件。”

En: Were the circumstances of her death in any sense...*unusual*?
Zh: “从任何意义上讲，她死亡的情况是不是有有些……*不寻常*？”

En: Absolutely not. His voice is coarse. "She died of pneumonia in her bed at the age of 79. This is highly... usual."
Zh: “绝对没有。”他的声音很粗哑。“她79岁的时候，因为肺炎死在了自己的床上。这个非常……正常。”

En: Alright, let's back up to a less sensitive subject, okay?
Zh: “好吧，我们还是说回不那么敏感的话题吧，好吗？”

En: His face is motionless like a marble mask. He doesn't comment.
Zh: 他的脸就像大理石面具一样波澜不惊。他没有做出任何评论。

En: What happened with you, Gaston, and Jeanne-Marie?
Zh: “你，加斯顿，还有珍妮-玛丽是怎么回事？”

En: 'The Ikeira Operation' was a seven-year campaign during which Suzerain Guillaume's army forcefully united the people in the southeastern part of Le Petit Continent, collectively known as the Ikeira tribes, under the Revacholian banner.
Zh: ‘伊卡里亚行动’是一场历时七年的战役，那时封建主纪尧姆的部队在瑞瓦肖旗帜的引领下，强行统一了佩蒂特大陆东南部的人民，这些人统称为伊卡里亚部落。

En: He gives Gaston a hateful look. "I won her back, but while I was dealing with some... issues..."
Zh: 他看了加斯顿一眼，满脸的憎恶。“我把她夺回来了，但是当我处理一些问题的时候……”

En: You were like a dark cloud sucking the joy out of every living thing around you, and you... He quickly glances at you. "You... *hurt* her."
Zh: “你就像一团黑云，吸干了周围所有生灵的欢乐，而且你……”他飞快地瞥了你一眼。“你……*伤害*了她。”

En: Where was the photo of you two taken?
Zh: “你们俩的那张相片是在哪里照的？”

En: I... uh... I... He looks down at his boots, lips moving, but the words are inaudible.
Zh: “我……呃……我……”他低头看着脚下的靴子，嘴唇蠕动着，但是听不见任何话语。

En: Those days and memories are gone. He nods and looks René with something resembling compassion.
Zh: “那些日子和记忆都已经过去了。”他点点头，带着一种类似怜悯的神情看着雷内。

En: The old soldier says nothing, but when his glance quickly runs over Gaston's face, there's an odd look in his eyes.
Zh: 老兵什么也没说，不过，当他的目光快速掠过加斯顿的脸时，还是流露出一副奇怪的神情。

En: Revachol Fair of '91 in the Faubourg district. His eyes turn to the sea. "A parade was held to honour Guillaume *Le Lion's* name-day. And the carabineers marched in the place of honour."
Zh: “瑞瓦肖第91期集市，在市郊举办的。”他的目光转向大海。“为了纪念*狮子王*纪尧姆的命名日，这里举办了一场游行。骑兵们在这个荣耀的地方列队行军。”

En: You looked happy in the picture. Smiling.
Zh: “相片上的你看起来很开心。在微笑。”

En: Okay, thanks.
Zh: “好吧，谢谢。”

En: He nods.
Zh: 他点点头。

En: This was the happiest day of my life. This is said in such a matter-of-fact tone, it leaves no room for doubt.
Zh: “那是我一生中最快乐的一天。”这是一种实事求是的口吻，完全没有留下质疑的余地。

En: We're still waiting for a replacement for the *boule* you sent sinking.
Zh: “我们还在等待被你沉入海底的*大球*替代品。”

En: Vigilance, officer! He salutes you. "What can this old carabineer do for you?"
Zh: “警戒，警官！”他向你致意。“我这个老骑兵能为你做些什么呢？”

En: René, you are the angriest man in the world.
Zh: “雷内，你是这个世界上最愤怒的男人。”

En: Ah, *mon dieu*! The pain in my back is unbearable. I can't even say if it's in my back or hip anymore. Feels like it's in *both*! He tries to measure the throw.
Zh: “啊，*我的天啦*！我的背，疼的让人难以忍受。我甚至分不清到底是背还是屁股了。感觉*两边*都疼！”他尝试测量投掷距离。

En: This man seems to be experiencing a mild to medium level of congestive heart problems, either due to narrowing of the arteries or high blood pressure.
Zh: 男人似乎患有轻微或一般水平的心力衰竭问题，不是因为动脉变窄就是高血压。

En: Can you please stop whining for one second and let me take the shot.
Zh: “你能不能停止抱怨一秒，让我瞄准一下。”

En: I found you guys a new *boule*. (Hold out the ball.)
Zh: “我给你们找了个新*大球*。”（拿出球。）

En: Now-now, no need to get angry again, René. I'm sure the officer tried his best. It's not like there's a *boule*-kiosk here in Martinaise.
Zh: “现在，没必要再生气了，雷内。我肯定这位警官已经尽力了。再说了，又不是说马丁内斯有专门的*大球*零售店。”

En: The old veteran stares at the shot put ball in silence, then speaks in a reserved voice: "What is this? Are you mocking us? This isn't for pétanque."
Zh: 老兵沉默地盯着铅球，然后冷冷地说道：“这是什么？你在嘲笑我们吗？这不是玩地掷球的。”

En: Fine. He takes the *boule*. "You tried to right a wrong. It's still a gram better than actual *nothing*."
Zh: “好吧。”他接过*大球*。“你试图纠正一个错误。总比*什么都没有*要稍微好一些。”

En: Grab the ball and show them how it's done!
Zh: 抓起球，让他们好好看看该怎么玩！

En: Know the game is pétanque.
Zh: 知道这个游戏叫地掷球。

En: Acting without hesitation.
Zh: 毫不犹豫地采取行动。

En: René told you his war story, now impress him with *your* heroics.
Zh: 雷内给你讲述了他的故事，现在用*你的*英雄事迹打动他吧。

En: Honoured his old unit.
Zh: 尊敬他的老部队。

En: Manipulated his patriotic feelings.
Zh: 操纵他的爱国情绪。

En: Exposed Gaston cheating.
Zh: 揭露加斯顿在作弊。

En: Complained about old age.
Zh: 抱怨年纪大了。

En: Scared Gaston.
Zh: 害怕的加斯顿。

En: Formed a patriotic vigilante group.
Zh: 成立一个爱国民兵团。

En: Threw the *boule* in the sea.
Zh: 把*大球*扔进海里。

En: Replaced the *boule*.
Zh: *大球*的替代品。

En: Said 'dependency' only weakens him.
Zh: 说‘依赖’只会让他变得软弱。

En: Exactly. And I *really* did try.
Zh: “没错。而且我*真的*试过了。”

En: There's not gonna be an alternative. I suggest taking it.
Zh: “不会再有其他选择了。我建议你还是收下它吧。”

En: I'm very sorry. It's the best I could find.
Zh: “我非常抱歉。这是我能找到最好的了。”

En: *Trying* is worth as much as is accomplished. In this case -- almost nothing.
Zh: “*尝试*和成就一样有价值。在这种情况下——几乎就是没有。”

En: I said I wouldn't do it, but I found you a new *boule*.
Zh: “我说过我不会这么做，不过我还是帮你找了个新*大球*。”

En: See, René, I told you he was one of the good guys. He chuckles. "You should have more faith in people."
Zh: “看见没，雷内，我告诉过你，他是个好人。”他咯咯地笑了。“你应该对别人更有信心一些。”

En: He mumbles something to himself. It seems to be a variety of curses.
Zh: 他自己小声嘀咕了几句。就像是某种诅咒。

En: That's what it was all about? He takes the sandwich. "Gaston is a snivelling rat -- I've known this my whole life -- but an RCM officer bullying an old man? Robbing him of his meal?!"
Zh: “就是为了这个吗？”他接过三明治。“加斯顿是一个爱哭鼻子的卑鄙小人——我这辈子再清楚不过了——不过一个RCM警官居然会欺负一个老人？抢他的饭吃？！”

En: You deserve it more than anyone. (Hold the sandwich out to René.)
Zh: “你比任何人都应该得到它。”（把三明治递给雷内。）

En: NO, the old soldier exclaims sharply. "I don't understand, what you're trying to accomplish with this... sandwich *bonanza*, but I already don't like it." He crosses his arms.
Zh: “不，”老兵尖声大叫。“我不明白，你这到底是想干什么……*幸运*三明治，但是我已经不喜欢它了。”他抱起双臂。

En: Listen, guys, in these dire times we are all brothers. Let all four of us dine together as one.\n
Zh: “听着，伙计们，在这种悲惨的时刻，我们都是兄弟。让我们四个人一同分享吧。”

En: Listen, guys, in these dire times we are all brothers. Let us dine together as one. (Hold out the sandwich.)
Zh: “听着，伙计们，在这种悲惨的时刻，我们都是兄弟。让我们一同分享吧。”（拿出三明治。）

En: Cautious nature prevents him from voicing more objections, but he will always remember the cop who bullied him -- of that you can be sure.
Zh: 他天生谨慎，没有表达更多反对意见，不过他会永远记住这个欺负他的警官——这一点你可以肯定。

En: The old man looks devastated as he observes you and René strengthen your  manly bond over his culinary wonder.
Zh: 看着你和雷内因为他精湛的厨艺强化了彼此之间的兄弟情，老人看起来很是沮丧。

En: *Silence!* he snaps at Gaston, then turns to you: "Since you put it that way, I *symbolically* accept your cordial gesture." He eats the sandwich-half in two well-measured bites and nods. "For Revachol."
Zh: “*安静！*”他大声呵斥加斯顿，然后转向你：“既然你这么说，我就*象征性地*接受你友好的姿态吧。”经过精心的计算，他两口咬掉半个三明治，然后点点头。“瑞瓦肖万岁。”

En: What? No! He gasps, looking like he's about to cry. "Old angry René doesn't even have taste buds -- it would be a complete..."
Zh: “什么？不！”他气喘吁吁，看起来快要哭出来了。“愤怒的老雷内甚至连味觉都没有——这完全就是……”

En: Let us -- true Revacholians -- symbolically share this meal. For Revachol! (Give half the sandwich to René and eat your half.)
Zh: “让我们——真正的瑞瓦肖人——象征性地分享这顿餐食吧。瑞瓦肖万岁！”（把半个三明治递给雷内，然后吃掉你自己那半。）

En: No-no, officer! René doesn't know what he's saying. The jolly one jumps in.
Zh: “不不不，警官！雷内根本不知道自己在说什么。”那个快乐的同伴插了进来。

En: No reason, never mind. (Keep the sandwich.)
Zh: “没有理由，不要紧的。”（留下三明治。）

En: Everything *you* do reflects on the idea and the people you are representing. When we, he says, pointing to Gaston, "think of the RCM, we think of *you*. Please make it a pleasant thought."
Zh: “*你*做的每一件事都会反映出你的理念，还有你代表的那群人。当我们，”他指着加斯顿说道，“想到RCM的时候，我们就会想到*你*。请让它成为一个令人愉快的想法。”

En: Yes, he says impatiently. "I was standing right here. I saw it. Why are you telling me this?"
Zh: “是的，”他不耐烦地说道。“我就站在这里。我看见了。你为什么要跟我说这个？”

En: I managed to get my hands on Gaston's sandwich.
Zh: “我设法弄到了加斯顿的三明治。”

En: Judging by his attitude -- probably the second one.
Zh: 从他的态度判断——可能是第二种。

En: Wait, what team?
Zh: 等等，什么队伍？

En: I'm on it, coach!
Zh: 我来，教练！

En: Does it matter what team? Pick a team, any team, the blue team -- you're letting them down! GET IN THE GAME AND THROW THE GODDAMN BALL!
Zh: 什么队伍重要吗？挑一支队伍，任意一支队伍，蓝队——你让他们失望了！赶紧给我认真一点，把那个该死的球扔出去！

En: EYES ON THE BALL, DINKY WINKY!
Zh: 眼睛看球，弱鸡！

En: What is it about this old soldier that makes him stand so proud?
Zh: 是什么让这个老兵能如此骄傲地站立着？

En: Threw the *boule* in the sea.
Zh: 把*大球*扔进海里。

En: Know of Filippe the Third.
Zh: 了解菲利普三世。

En: Discussed the monument.
Zh: 讨论纪念碑。

En: Honoured his old unit.
Zh: 尊敬他的老部队。

En: Manipulated his patriotic feelings.
Zh: 操纵他的爱国情绪。

En: Exposed Gaston cheating.
Zh: 揭露加斯顿在作弊。

En: Formed a patriotic vigilante group.
Zh: 成立一个爱国民兵团。

En: Replaced the *boule*.
Zh: *大球*的替代品。

En: Know René's job-situation.
Zh: 了解雷内的工作情况。

En: Shared a patriotic meal.
Zh: 共享爱国大餐。

En: I saw the statue of Filippe III near the roundabout.
Zh: “我在环岛附近看见了菲利普三世的雕像。”

En: I saw a horseback monument near the roundabout.
Zh: “我在环岛附近看见一座骑马的纪念碑。”

En: All you observe is a veteran refusing to let go of the past and his old uniform. This is not uncommon.
Zh: 根据你的观察，这是一位拒绝放下过去和他的旧制服的老兵。这并不罕见。

En: As René turns from you to his partner and back, the medals on his chest rattle and glare. He keeps his spine straight and his ribcage lifted, displaying them proudly.
Zh: 雷内来回打量着他的同伴和你，胸前的奖章咯咯作响，散发耀眼的光芒。他昂首挺胸，骄傲地展示着昔日的荣光。

En: Not this again... you hear the lieutenant mumble under his breath.
Zh: “别又来一遍……”你听见警督低声咕哝着。

En: Two. The larger one is shaped like a cross, while the smaller medal resembles the sun.
Zh: 两枚。大一点的是十字形，小一点的很像太阳。

En: How many medals are there?
Zh: 他有多少枚奖章？

En: Ah, yes, King Filippe III on his steed -- a reminder of what Revachol once was...
Zh: “啊，是的，骑着战马的菲利普国王三世——提醒着瑞瓦肖昔日的模样……”

En: How should a true king rule?
Zh: “一个真正的国王该如何统治他的国家？”

En: What was that about cocaine?
Zh: “可卡因是怎么回事？”

En: Oh absolutely... He smiles as if reliving a pleasant memory. "At the mercy of a cocaine-snorting tyrant who emptied the treasury so he could sleep on a bed of gold?"
Zh: “是这样的……”他笑了，仿佛在重温一段愉悦的回忆。“听命于一个烫吸可卡因，还掏空国库让自己睡在金子上的国王？”

En: ...a superpower, feared and respected. A testament to what this country *can* be under the leadership of a True King, someone who knows how to rule.
Zh: “……一个超级大国，威震四方，万人敬仰。这是一种证明：在真正懂得统治之道的国王带领下，这个国家*能够*变成什么样。”

En: *Decisively*. Without fear of offending the sensibilities of the frail and weak-minded among his subjects! This is something the insurgents never understood...
Zh: “*果断决绝*。不怕冒犯手下那些意志薄弱和优柔寡断的臣民的感情！这是叛乱分子永远也理解不了的……”

En: You can't even take responsibility for yourself -- how could you fathom the responsibility weighing on the shoulders of a ruler? he asks, obviously annoyed.
Zh: “你甚至都不能为自己负责——又怎么能理解统治者肩负的重担呢？”他问到，明显有些恼怒。

En: Oh yes, René, yes... The jolly man nods meekly. "Men were bigger, girls were prettier, and everyone was a *fascha* -- Lord, please bring those days back, if you can!"
Zh: “是的，雷内，没错……”快乐的男人温顺地点点头。“男人更强壮，姑娘们更漂亮，大家都是*法西斯分子*——神啦，如果可以的话，请把那些日子带回来吧！”

En: I... saw a picture in there. You were in it. You looked... happy. Who's the girl?
Zh: “我……在那里看见一张相片。里面有你。你看起来……很高兴。那个女孩是谁？”

En: I... saw a picture in there. You were in it. Who's the girl?
Zh: “我……在那里看见一张相片。里面有你。那个女孩是谁？”

En: Let's talk about something else.
Zh: “我们说点别的吧。”

En: Cocaine? Cocainum? Sounds like our kind of king. And -- just imagine! -- what kind of cocaine a king would have had...
Zh: 古柯碱？可卡因？听起来很像我们的国王。而且——想象一下！——国王会吸什么样的可卡因……

En: Oh, old Filippe was a *big* fan of the purple nose candy the nobility loved so much. A cocaine-connoisseur of sorts. He chuckles. "His egocentricity is borderline legendary."
Zh: “哦，老菲利普可是紫色粉末毒品的*忠实*粉丝，那也是贵族的挚爱。算是个可卡因鉴赏家吧。”他咯咯地笑了起来。“他的自私自利堪称传奇。”

En: Seems to me a leader should take care of his *people* before himself.
Zh: “在我看来，一个领导者应该优先照顾自己的*臣民*，而不是自己。”

En: Powerful leaders -- not afraid to do what *must* be done -- that's what this country needs.
Zh: “强大的领导——不害怕去做那些*必须*要做的事情——这正是这个国家所需要的。”

En: I'm not sure how I feel about that.
Zh: “我不确定自己是怎么想的。”

En: No, he really believes what he's saying -- he has completely accepted the narrative of transcendence through cocaine use.
Zh: 不，他真心相信自己说的话——他完全能接受通过吸食可卡因来超越自我的说法。

En: You haven't lived as long as I have, son. With age, a man learns certain things about real power -- or else ends up a spineless socialist rat, like Gaston here.
Zh: “孩子，你活的可没我长。随着年龄的增长，一个男人就会了解到什么是真正的权利——否则他就会变成一只懦弱的老鼠，就像加斯顿一样。”

En: Revachol would be a different place if more people realized that. He gently rubs his chest. "We could still be the..."
Zh: “如果更多人意识到这一点，瑞瓦肖就会变得不同。”他轻轻地揉了揉胸口。“我们还能……”

En: A nation is only as strong *as* its leader. That's why it was such madness to try to...
Zh: “一个国家的强大程度*取决于*它的领导者。这也就是为什么会如此疯狂的去……”

En: No one and nothing can change this man's mind -- he is as rigid as they come, still in that antique uniform -- it's a symbol for him.
Zh: 没有任何人，或者任何东西能改变这个男人的想法——他还是像从前一样死板，还穿着那套古董制服——这是他的一个象征。

En: And the worst part of that, officer, he says, face terrified, "is having to spend your days playing pétanque with an angry snake."
Zh: “最糟糕的是，警官，”他害怕地说道，“整天跟一条愤怒的毒蛇一起玩地掷球。”

En: Don't get started on that again. What happened, happened. There is some weariness in his voice now -- he's heard this rant many times before.
Zh: “别再提这件事了。过去的事就已经过去了。”现在他的声音里多了一丝疲惫的味道——这种豪言壮语，他之前已经听过很多遍。

En: The carabineer doesn't reply, but his entire being communicates unbreakable resolve.
Zh: 老骑兵没有回答，不过他的整个身体传达出牢不可破的决心。

En: But imagine -- purest cocaine... Now that would make you feel like a king. Not that you can afford it. But a man can dream, right? In another time and place...
Zh: 想象一下——最纯净的可卡因……肯定会让你感觉像个国王。倒不是说你能负担得起。不过做做梦总可以吧？在另一个时间，另一个地方……

En: Filippe III was even brought into this world with the help of cocaine -- the court medic administered a dose to his mother when she was in labour. And it is well known that with the help of cocaine -- only the purest, of course -- he was able to connect with higher realms...
Zh: “菲利普三世甚至是在可卡因的帮助下降生到这个世界的——宫廷医生在他母亲分娩的时候给她打了一剂药。众所周知，在可卡因的帮助下——当然了，只能是最纯净的可卡因——他才能连通至高领域……”

En: But imagine -- purest cocaine... Now that would make you feel like a king. Not that you can afford it... But what about speed? Speed is cheap. And good.
Zh: 想象一下——最纯净的可卡因……肯定会让你感觉像个国王。倒不是说你能负担得起……那思必得呢？思必得很便宜。而且很好。

En: 'Higher realms'? Of course -- it all makes sense...
Zh: ‘至高领域？’当然了——这一切都说得通……

En: I too have travelled to higher realms...
Zh: “我也去过至高领域……”

En: What was that about higher realms? Sounds interesting.
Zh: “至高领域是怎么回事？听起来很有意思。”

En: Seems like irresponsible behaviour for a monarch. Drug users shouldn't even operate heavy machinery, much less rule countries.
Zh: “似乎是帝王的不负责任的行为。吸毒人员甚至都不应该操纵重型机械，更不用提统治国家了。”

En: It's really not. He turns to René. "Please do spare us the cocaine fairy tales."
Zh: “这还真不是。”他转向雷内。“请别给我们讲可卡因的童话故事了。”

En: Yes, indeed. We are not here to investigate the drug trade of centuries past.
Zh: “是的，没错。我们来这里可不是为了调查好几个世纪以前的贩毒案件。”

En: Such responsibility requires a boost every now and then. I sometimes need one too...
Zh: “这种责任偶尔也需要鼓励一下。我有时候也需要……”

En: Please, officer! Don't encourage him! He quickly turns to René. "Do spare us the cocaine fairy tales. The RCM isn't interested in them."
Zh: “拜托，警官！别再怂恿他了！”他快速转向雷内。“别再给我们讲可卡因的童话故事了。RCM对这些完全没兴趣。”

En: Thanks for clearing that up. I have some other questions now.
Zh: “感谢你的澄清。现在我还有些其他问题。”

En: I'm listening. He doesn't sound too pleased to have his praises to the miracle of cocaine-fuelled kingship cut short.
Zh: “你说吧，我在听。”他听起来不太高兴，因为他对可卡因王权奇迹的赞美诗被打断了。

En: From what I've seen of the officers of the RCM... he begins, looking you over, then stops himself. "But I don't want to get into a debate about drug policies."
Zh: “据我所见，RCM的警官……”他开始打量你，然后停了下来。“不过，我不想卷入跟毒品政策有关的辩论。”

En: I feel 20 years younger every time it rains.
Zh: “每当下雨的时候，我就觉得自己年轻了20岁。”

En: I'm fine, just a little heart burn...
Zh: “我很好，只是有些烧心……”

En: *Mon dieu*, how much can a man whine, Gaston?
Zh: “*我的天啦*，你到底有多少牢骚，加斯顿？”

En: Goddamn ticker...
Zh: “该死的心脏……”

En: It's a different game in the snow.
Zh: “下雪的时候就是个完全不同的游戏了。”

En: Wouldn't even pick it up, if my heart wasn't in it.
Zh: “如果我的心思不在这里，就无法继续战斗了。”

En: Build a solid routine and stick to it. That's all there is.
Zh: “建立一个固定的惯例，然后坚持下去。就这么回事。”

En: So the wind is... yes, perfect.
Zh: “所以这风……没错，很完美。”

En: Is my aim really *that* off?
Zh: “我瞄的真有*那么*偏吗？”

En: Strange, I could have sworn...
Zh: “奇怪，我可以发誓……”

En: Stop complaining and hurry up!
Zh: “别再抱怨了，赶紧的！”

En: Don't get mayonnaise on the measuring tape.
Zh: “别把蛋黄酱弄到卷尺上。”

En: He doesn't know about the crime. Your time is better spent discussing *politics*.
Zh: 他不知道罪案的事。你最好把时间花在讨论*政治*上。

En: If I knew, I would not be *afraid* to tell you. I simply don't. I am an old man, not a coward. The daily business of the riff-raff no longer concerns me.
Zh: “如果我知道，我*不害怕*告诉你。我就是不知道。我是老了，但不是胆小鬼。我已经不再关心那帮乌合之众的日常业务了。”

En: 'Tis true sire. He knows nothing.
Zh: 这是真的，大人。他什么也不知道。

En: This is a man with a lot of past, but little present. And almost no future.
Zh: 这个男人太过于沉湎过去，很少活在当下。而且几乎没有未来。

En: The RCM doesn't discriminate, the lieutenant marks dryly. "Women are held to the same standards as men. And -- as reflected in the quarterly statistics -- are equally effective in their jobs."
Zh: “RCM没有任何歧视，”警督干巴巴地评论到。“大家对男性和女性的标准是一样的。而且——根据季度统计数据来看——她们的工作表现都很高效。”

En: Unlike men, they also have to deal with the very same mentality you two are displaying right now. He frowns. "If it weren't for *this* they would outperform us."
Zh: “不像男人，她们还必须处理你们俩现在表现出来的这种心态。”他皱起眉头。“如果不是因为*这个*，她们会比我们更胜一筹。”

En: I know the kind, he says with a frown. "Gaston here is the same. A weathervane. Never stood up for anything in his life either."
Zh: “我知道那种人，”他皱眉说道。“跟这位加斯顿一样。一个风向标。这辈子从来没有为任何事情挺身而出过。”

En: Oh, René... He smiles wide and friendly. "If you only knew life beyond confrontations, conflict and extremities..."
Zh: “哦，雷内……”他露出善意而又灿烂的微笑。“如果你能明白对抗、冲突和极端之外的生活……”

En: That woman, who couldn't choose between him and Gaston -- Jeanne-Marie -- *she* must be the reason René is so bitter. He holds her indecisiveness against all women.
Zh: 那个女人，无法在他和加斯顿之间做出选择——珍妮-玛丽--*她*肯定就是雷内变得如此尖刻的原因。他因为她的犹豫不决而仇视所有女人。

En: So, again: you don't know anything?
Zh: “所以，再确认一遍：你什么都不知道？”

En: Unfortunately, I don't. He shrugs. "Unlike most of the locals, I have no qualms about assisting law enforcement, but this affair has passed me by completely."
Zh: “很可惜，我不知道。”他耸耸肩。“不像大多数本地人，我对协助执法没有任何顾虑，不过这件事对我来说已经完全过去了。”

En: And... most of the locals?
Zh: “而且……大多数本地人也是这么想的？”

En: In Martinaise, the Union is the law, so can you really blame them?
Zh: “在马丁内斯，工会就是法律，所以你真能责怪他们吗？”

En: But you don't have a problem with cops?
Zh: “但是你对条子没意见？”

En: *Cop* is a pejorative term. I don't have a problem with policemen. On the contrary, I admire the effort to bring order to our streets.
Zh: “*条子*是一种贬义的说法。我对警察没有任何意见。正好相反，你们为维持街道秩序所作出的努力，让我感到十分钦佩。”

En: What about policewomen?
Zh: “那女警察呢？”

En: I'm confident they are indispensable in regard to all the paperwork and other administrative duties.
Zh: “我确信，在处理所有文书工作和其他行政职务时，她们是必不可缺的。”

En: Not to mention keeping the station tidy and the men fed and happy.
Zh: “更不用提保持警局的整洁，还有让大家吃饱喝足了。”

En: There are no duties RCM women couldn't carry out.
Zh: “没有什么职责是RCM的女性不能履行的。”

En: You better not be implying that women are unfit for fieldwork...
Zh: “你最好不要暗示女性不适合跑外勤……”

En: No man can afford to turn his eyes to the horizon if his rear isn't secured. If the womenfolk take care of that, then men can tackle the bigger issues.
Zh: “如果后方不安全，就没有人敢把视线遥望前方。如果女性能处理好那个，那男人就能处理更大的问题。”

En: I try to avoid voicing opinions on controversial matters. Let's just drop it.
Zh: “我尽量避免对有争议的事情发表意见。还是就此打住吧。”

En: I am merely stating that Nature, in her infinite wisdom, has made men more fit to perform certain... more challenging tasks than women.
Zh: “我只想说明：大自然拥有无穷无尽的智慧，相比女人来说，男人更适合去做某种……某种比较有挑战性的工作。”

En: Remaining headstrong, making tough decisions on the spot, or even tackling physical obstacles are a few examples of such *challenges*.
Zh: “勇往直前，当场做出困难的决定，甚至是克服身体上的障碍，只是这类*挑战*的几个例子而已。”

En: But you must agree that Nature, in her infinite wisdom, has made men more fit to perform certain... more *challenging* tasks, don't you, officer?
Zh: “但是你必须赞同一点：大自然拥有无穷无尽的智慧，相比女人来说，男人更适合去做某种……某种比较有挑战性的工作，不是吗，警官？”

En: Yes, I guess that's true.
Zh: “是的，我想那是真的。”

En: No, I don't think there's *any* evolutionary inequality at play here.
Zh: “不，我不觉得这里存在着*任何*进化上的不平等。”

En: You're a bit of an asshole, aren't you, old man?
Zh: “你可真是个混球，不是吗，老家伙？”

En: Tell me something you are really good at and I'll find a woman who's better.
Zh: “跟我说件你特别擅长的事，我会找出一个比你更棒的女性来！”

En: Really, officer? He smirks. "Match an average woman against an average man in a dark alley and see who comes out on top."
Zh: “真的吗，警官？”他笑了笑。“在一个黑暗的小巷里，让一个寻常的女人跟一个寻常的男人对抗，看看最后谁会胜出。”

En: Gender equality is a very noble, very *modern* idea, but in real life, primal roles prevail. But I do not wish to discuss this matter further.
Zh: “性别平等是一种非常高尚，而且非常*现代*的想法，不过在现实生活中，原始角色还是占上风的。不过我不想进一步讨论这个话题了。”

En: No reason to even feel bad about that, it's just the way of the animal kingdom.
Zh: “没理由为此感到抱歉，这只是动物王国的运作方式罢了。”

En: There's no point in getting angry at nature, officer. This is just the way we are *made*.
Zh: “没必要为大自然的天性而生气，警官。我们就是这样被*造出来*的。”

En: I don't want to get into this argument with a police officer, he replies calmly. "Let's just say that in the last eighty-two years I have repeatedly seen my beliefs confirmed in full."
Zh: “我不想和警察发生争执，”他平静地回复到。“这么说吧，在过去的82年里，我不断地看见自己的信念得到充分地证实。”

En: Great minds think alike, René. You and I really seem to have it all figured out.
Zh: “英雄所见略同，雷内，你和我似乎把一切都想的很透彻。”

En: I guess, it might not be as black and white, but disagreeing makes me uncomfortable.
Zh: “我猜，事情并不是非黑即白的，不过分歧让我感觉不太舒服。”

En: It's as good a time as any to turn our attention to a different topic, officer, he says, adjusting his cap.
Zh: “现在把注意力转移到不同话题上是最好不过了，警官，”他整了整帽子说道。

En: You know, I'm getting second thoughts about this.
Zh: “你知道吗，我正在重新考虑这件事。”

En: The soldier quickly glances at the lieutenant, then turns back to you. "I'm glad at least *some* people keep a level head in these turbulent times."
Zh: 士兵飞快地瞥了一眼警督，然后又看向了你。“很高兴至少*有些*人能在这种动荡的岁月里临危不乱。”

En: Well, I'm glad at least some people bearing your insignia have kept a level head in these turbulent times.
Zh: “好吧，我很高兴，至少佩戴那个徽章的人还能在这种动荡的岁月里临危不乱。”

En: *Some* would still say you're a cop, but I guess we're beyond that now.
Zh: *有人*还是会说你是个警察，但是我猜现在已经远远不止了。

En: Just talk. It'll smooth things over. Old people like attention.
Zh: 跟他们聊聊吧。这样能让事情变得顺利。老人喜欢关注。

En: Yes... why *did* you come here? It's unlikely they know anything about the murder.
Zh: 是的……你*为什么*会来这里？又不是说他们知道凶杀案的事。

En: You never know, he *might* know something. This is a good vantage point.
Zh: 你永远不会知道，他*可能*知道些什么。这是一个很好的优势。

En: There are no two more harmful words an athlete can hear than *good job*. And this was downright embarrassing!
Zh: 对于一个运动员来说，没有什么比*干得好*更伤人心的话了。而且实在是太尴尬了！

En: Mossback and his shitty ball are mocking you. You need to get angry. Use swear-words. Mention *spunk*.
Zh: 老顽固和他的小破球正在嘲笑你。你必须发火。说点脏话。提一下*体液*。

En: Something about it was completely unnatural. Corrupted. Your muscle memory knew what to do and went for it, but there were gaps...
Zh: 有些东西感觉一点都不自然。腐坏了。你的肌肉记忆知道该怎么做，而且也尽力做了，不过还是有差距……

En: Yeah! Gaps you tried to fill with *thinking*. We don't *think* in sports. We *DO*!
Zh: 是啊！你想用*思考*来填满的缺口。搞运动的时候根本不用*想*。我们*就是干*！

En: It felt like you were going for a thrust or a lunge -- there was definitely gonna be jumping. Maybe you scored a point, but this is a *fiasco*.
Zh: 感觉你打算用力推或者是猛扑——肯定还打算跳跃。也许你得了一分，但这是一次*惨败*。

En: Look who's talking -- that cockatoo uniform must give him a real advantage. When fighting in *the circus*.
Zh: 看看是谁在说话——那套凤头鹦鹉制服肯定给他带来了真正的优势，特别是在*马戏团*打架的时候。

En: The cold metal ball is surprisingly smooth against your neck. It has a pattern on it. (Probably a sponsored ball.) Yours would only be covered with bumps of learning and scars of victory.
Zh: 冰冷的金属球靠在你的脖子上，感觉出奇地光滑。上面有个图案。（可能是赞助的球。）你的球上只会布满学习的伤痕和胜利的疮疤。

En: West, in the Grand Couron Arena, a sharp overhand left from Arsène-Luc Edelbrock, sends the Samaran heavyweight champion, Kostya 'Mjasnok' Koštunica, flying to the mat. The crowd goes wild. Revachol loves her heroes.
Zh: 西边，在大科戎竞技场，猛地一计过肩投掷，亚森-吕克·艾德布罗克，让萨马拉裔重量级冠军——科什焦·亚斯诺克·科斯图里卡朝软垫飞去。观众疯狂了。瑞瓦肖热爱她的英雄们。

En: Behold the fear and confusion reflecting in the eyes of the two feeble geezers -- they are in awe of your superiority. You are a God to them.
Zh: 看啦，那两个衰弱的老头子眼里流露出的恐惧和混乱——他们敬畏你的优越性。对于他们来说，你就是神明。

En: Oh, pétanque, I understand...
Zh: “哦，地掷球，我明白了……”

En: No, no you don't! Our pétanque game is ruined! He's getting angry again. "We want our *boule* back."
Zh: “不，你不明白！我们的地掷球游戏被你毁了！”他又发火了。“把我们的*大球*还回来！”

En: I thought it was shot-put.
Zh: “我还以为是推铅球呢。”

En: Well, it damn well isn't, it's pétanque, the man snaps, raising his voice again. "You ruined a pétanque game. We want our *boule* back!"
Zh: “该死的，当然不是，它是地掷球，”男人的声音又高了几个八度。“你毁掉了地掷球游戏。把我们的*大球*还回来！”

En: Sorry, an honest mistake. Are we good?
Zh: “对不起，这真是我的无心之过。我们可以和好了吗？”

En: Yes, pétanque, the man says, raising his voice again. "You ruined our pétanque game. We want our *boule* back."
Zh: “是的，地掷球，”男人的声音又高了几个八度。“你毁掉了地掷球游戏。把我们的*大球*还回来！”

En: No, we are not *good*, goddamnit! A vein on his forehead is bulging alarmingly. "We want our *boule* back!"
Zh: “不，我们不*好*，该死！”他额头上的血管暴起，有点吓人。“把我们的*大球*还回来！”

En: Pee-tank?
Zh: “地-掷球？”

En: Okay, I will try to fix this. (Accept the task.)
Zh: “好吧，我会想办法补救的。”（接受任务。）

En: Good. He nods. "Mistakes are forgiven, when men at least *try* to right their wrongs. I believe you will try. Now why did you approach us?"
Zh: “很好。”他点点头。“至少你还在*尝试*纠正自己的错误，那错误就是可以原谅的。我相信你会努力的。现在你为什么要接近我们？”

En: A fine example you are setting here today, officer. He says, slowly shaking his head. "I will remember this. And Gaston will too."
Zh: “你今天在这里树立了一个很好的榜样，警官。”他慢慢摇摇头，说道。“我会记住的。加斯顿也会。”

En: Listen, guys, the ball is gone, and I have a murder to solve, alright? (Refuse task.)
Zh: “听着，伙计们，球是没了，不过我手上还有个凶杀案要破，好吗？”（拒绝任务。）

En: It's okay, officer. Forgotten completely. It was my *boule* and I have a spare. He produces another ball from his pocket. "Everything is good and we're ready to assist any way we can!"
Zh: “没事的，警官。已经全忘记了。那是我的*大球*，而是我还有多余的。”他从口袋里又掏出一个球。“没事了，我们已经准备好全力协助你了！”

En: The lieutenant says nothing, but his face is rigid. It seems he does not approve of your athletic displays.
Zh: 警督什么也没说，不过他脸上的表情很僵硬。似乎他对你的运动表现并不怎么满意。

En: You are an officer of the law with way more important things to worry about than *boules*. Don't take this crap.
Zh: 你是一名执法人员，除了*大球*，你还有更重要的事情要担心。别理会这点破事。

En: Take it easy, René. The jolly one tries to defuse the situation. "This is just a misunderstanding, isn't it, officer? No harm done."
Zh: “放松点，雷内。”快乐的老头尝试缓和紧张的局势。“这只是个误会，对吗，警官？反正也没什么伤害。”

En: Of course there's harm done, you orange slug! The heel of his cavalry boot slams in the ground. "You owe us a goddamn *boule*!"
Zh: “当然有伤害，你这只橙色鼻涕虫！”老兵的靴跟砰的一声踩在地面上。“你欠我们一个该死的*大球*！”

En: He's trying to avoid conflict at any cost.
Zh: 他正在不惜一切代价避免冲突。

En: It's your refusal to take responsibility I don't like.
Zh: 我不喜欢你这种拒绝负责的态度。

En: A man his age getting worked up like that? Better watch that blood pressure.
Zh: 像他那把年纪还这么激动？最好注意下血压。

En: I'm sorry.
Zh: “对不起。”

En: With a pinch of fear.
Zh: 还带着一丝恐惧。

En: A *whorehouse of shit*?
Zh: 妈的见鬼了？

En: It wasn't *whorehouse of shit*. The shot was at least 23 metres. Probably 24 and then some. Nothing to be embarrassed about.
Zh: 一点也不*见鬼*。这一下至少23米远。也许远远超过了24米。没什么好尴尬的。

En: I don't have a *boule*, but will this do? (Hold out the shot put ball.)
Zh: “我没有*大球*，不过这个可以吗？”（拿出铅球。）

En: Will this.... He falls silent and stares at the ball. "No, it will *goddamn* not..."
Zh: “这个……”他默默地盯着铅球。“不，*该死的*，绝对不行……”

En: Thank you, officer. The jolly man quickly takes the ball and bounces it in his hand. "This is really something... Honestly I think it's better than our old *boule* even!"
Zh: “谢谢你，警官。”快乐的老头飞快地从你手里接过球，在手里掂量了几下。“这玩意真的不错……说实话的，我觉得它比我们之前的那个*大球*还要好！”

En: *Mon dieu*... he mumbles, rubbing his temples. "Alright. Alright. Fine. What do you want, officer?"
Zh: “*我的天啦*……”他揉了揉太阳穴，嘟囔到。“好吧。好吧。行吧。你想怎么样，警官？”

En: *Mon dieu*, the jolly man exclaims. "Good job, officer. That was an excellent throw!"
Zh: “*我的天啦*，”快乐的男人大叫起来。“干得好，警官。这下扔的真是太漂亮了！”

En: Perfect throw? Well, I still feel defeated.
Zh: “完美一掷？我还是感觉很挫败。”

En: That's just how Johnny Ballgame is -- extraordinary.
Zh: “男人的球类运动就是这样——非比寻常。”

En: Right, the old soldier says slowly. "Was the demonstration *it*, or do you still need something from us, officer?"
Zh: “好吧，”老兵慢悠悠地说道。“是需要*示范*一下吗，或者你还有别的需求，警官？”

En: You vandalized our game, son. He tries to calm himself. "We can't play pétanque with five *boules*."
Zh: “你破坏了我们的游戏，孩子。”他试着让自己冷静下来。“只有5个*大球*的话，我们就没法玩地掷球游戏了。”

En: This felt... wrong. Wrong like touching your sister's breast. You threw your sister's breast.
Zh: 感觉……不太对。就像不小心碰到亲姐妹的胸部一样。你把亲姐妹的胸部扔出去了。

En: What the HELL is your problem?
Zh: “你到底有什么毛病啊？！”

En: Not a weak right triceps, that's for sure.
Zh: “有一点很肯定，我右边的三头肌一点也不虚弱。”

En: God, this is right -- you feel the familiar tremble of excitement and adrenaline that precedes every victory. Time has frozen.
Zh: 天啦，这种感觉很对——就像每一次获胜之前，因兴奋而熟悉的颤抖，还有肾上腺素飙升的感觉。时间冻结在了这一刻。

En: I don't care if you are a cop -- you *do not* just ruin someone's game. It's so goddamn disrespectful!
Zh: “我不在乎你是不是警察——你*不能*就这么毁掉别人的游戏。这真是*该死的大不敬！”

En: Wait... what?
Zh: “等等……什么？”

En: Don't be a sore loser, it's still a fair game between you two. Second place is a podium position.
Zh: “别输不起嘛，对于你们俩来说，这还是一场公平的游戏。第二名也能登上领奖台的。”

En: I'm sensing anger. And I don't understand why.
Zh: “我感觉到了愤怒。但我不明白是为什么。”

En: Your guess is as good as mine, officer. Maybe you're just one of those men who's never satisfied with anything? He shrugs. "Regardless, what do you need from us?"
Zh: “我也不知道，警官。也许你是那种永远不会感到满足的人？”他耸耸肩。“不管怎么说，你需要我们做什么？”

En: The damn ball was slippery as fuck! How do you expect me to throw this piece of *shit* when it's got old man spunk all over, Mossback?!
Zh: “这个该死的球滑溜溜的跟鬼一样！这上面都是老人的体液，你还指望我能把这个*狗屎玩意*扔出花来吗，老顽固？！”

En: The throw was terrible and you know it. No need to mock me.
Zh: “我扔的很糟糕，而且你也知道。没必要嘲笑我。”

En: Spring slush on bare feet can make a man feel like a failure. He points at your naked toes. "For a reason. Now, do you need something from us?"
Zh: “光脚踩在春季的烂泥上的确会让人产生挫败感。”他指着你裸露的脚趾。“事出有因。现在，你需要我们做些什么？”

En: What are you talking about? You just executed a pretty much perfect pétanque throw! His tone is full of admiration.
Zh: “你在说什么呢？你刚刚做出了一个完美的地掷球动作！”他的口气满满都是赞赏。

En: How are you ever going to get the officer's shit off your nose, Gaston? Or even climb out of his ass? He shakes his head in extreme disdain.
Zh: “你还打算怎么舔这位警官，加斯顿？跪舔吗？”他极度鄙视地摇摇头。

En: The inertia can be contained no more than a bullet leaving a gun. Let go. Be the bullet. (Continue.)
Zh: 惯性是无法控制的，就像离开枪口的子弹。放手吧。成为那枚子弹。（继续。）

En: Let go. Be the ball. (Continue.)
Zh: 放手吧。成为那个球。（继续。）

En: Take in the surroundings.
Zh: 感受周围的环境。

En: It wasn't *merde*. The shot was at least 23 metres. Probably 24 and then some. Nothing to be embarrassed about.
Zh: 它一点也不*见鬼*。这一下至少23米远。也许远远超过了24米。没什么好尴尬的。

En: Already your muscles are adjusting to the weight, the nervous system calibrating, until you and the ball have merged into a single entity. The *manball* is ready.
Zh: 你的肌肉正在适应重量，神经系统在校正，直到你和球合为一体。*人球*已经准备好了。

En: There is time for a last glance inward. Who am I?
Zh: 还有时间可以最后检视一下你的内心。我是谁？

En: An embodiment of pure motion. A fine-tuned *locomotor* running at maximum efficiency.
Zh: 一种纯粹运动的体现。微调的*运动体*正在以最大化的效能运行。

En: Feel the ball.
Zh: 感觉这个球。

En: Problem? I don't understand.
Zh: “毛病？我不明白。”

En: A chilly breeze ruffles your hair as you stand there, feet firmly planted. All sounds, smells, even the wind -- everything fades until the only thing left is the union of Man and Ball.
Zh: 你站在那里，双脚牢牢着地，一阵寒冷的微风吹乱了你的头发。所有声音，味道，甚至就连风——全都慢慢褪色，直到最后，整个弹坑只剩下人和球的联合体。

En: Probably because those rooster pants are squeezing you senseless. What ever happened to practical? Durable? Revachol-made? He shakes his head. "Now what can I do for you?"
Zh: “也许是因为那条花里胡哨的裤子把你挤压地没有知觉了。实用性怎么了？耐穿性呢？瑞瓦肖本土制造呢？”他摇摇头。“现在，我能为你做些什么？”

En: Yes, officer... The lieutenant gives you a quick side-glance. "What *do* we need from these gentlemen?"
Zh: “是的，警官……”警督飞快地瞥了你一眼。“我们找这两位先生是想*做什么*？”

En: I'm a god of track and field. Why would I have a problem with anything?
Zh: “我是田径之神。为什么我要对任何事有意见？”

En: You are immediately surprised by the ball's lack of weight. No matter. You'll make it work.
Zh: 你立即就被轻飘飘的球吓了一跳。没关系。你能行的。

En: Hold on, why *did* it feel so wrong?
Zh: 等一下，为什么*感觉*这么不对劲？

En: I thought something way more spectacular would happen.
Zh: “我还以为会有更惊人的事情发生。”

En: I was going for something cool, but I screwed it up, like always...
Zh: “我本来想耍耍酷，但我还是搞砸了，就像往常一样……”

En: The man in the uniform says nothing. His gaze is fixed on the horizon.
Zh: 穿制服的男人不发一言。目光固定在地平线上。

En: His merry partner is staring at the ground like he's trying to see right through to the other side.
Zh: 他那位快乐的同伴直直地盯着地面，似乎想要看穿另一边。

En: The lieutenant is looking at you. His face is expressionless.
Zh: 警督也看着你。他的脸上毫无表情。

En: Everything has gone very quiet. It seems as even the waves have ceased their crashing against the promenade. It feels heavy.
Zh: 一切都很安静。就连海浪也停下了对海滨的冲击。感觉非常沉重。

En: *Merde*! the old soldier yells in outrage. "*Bordel de merde*!!!"
Zh: “*见鬼*！”老兵愤怒地大喊到。“*妈的见鬼了*！！！”

En: René... I need to go back in time. Can you help me?
Zh: “雷内……我得回到过去。你能帮帮我吗？”

En: The lieutenant flashes you a quick side-glance.
Zh: 警督飞快地瞥了你一眼。

En: His posture tenses.
Zh: 他绷紧了身体。

En: What are you up to now?
Zh: 你现在要干什么？

En: What?! The old soldier scowls at you, unamused.
Zh: “什么？！”老兵恼火地对你低吼。

En: He thinks you're playing a joke on him.
Zh: 他觉得你在拿他寻开心。

En: Now, now... His partner raises both hands in a calming gesture. "I'm sure the officer means it figuratively. Like a forensic technique or something, right?"
Zh: “嘿，嘿……”他的伙伴举起双手安抚你们。“我想这位警官只是打个比方。这是你的某种语言技巧，对吧？”

En: I'm dead serious. Can you help me get back to the time when *love* was still possible?
Zh: “我非常严肃。你能帮我回到一个人们依然能*爱*的年代吗？”

En: No, I need to go back to a time when *love* was still possible.
Zh: “不，我得回到一个人们依然能*爱*的年代。”

En: I'm not kidding. This is for Revachol. I need to return to the past.
Zh: “我没在开玩笑。这是为了瑞瓦肖。我得回到过去。”

En: No, I literally need to go back in time. For Revachol.
Zh: “不，我是真的得回到过去。为了瑞瓦肖。”

En: Right, right... A forensic technique. Can you help me or not?
Zh: “没错，没错……一种语言技巧。你到底能不能帮我？”

En: Forget it. Let's move on. (Drop it.)
Zh: “忘了它吧。我们换个话题。”(放弃。)

En: Forensic techniques are your forte, not mine, officer. Something stiffens in the man. He shakes his head. "Find another fool, I will not be part of your... joke."
Zh: “语言技巧是你的强项，警官，不是我的。”这个男人变得严肃起来。他摇了摇头。“去愚弄别的傻瓜吧。我不会当你的……笑料。”

En: I thought you were a busy man, officer. Or at least you should be, so I won't comment on this... eccentricity. Now what?
Zh: “我以为你是个大忙人，警官。或者说你应该是，所以我就不评价你的……古怪行为了。还有什么问题吗？”

En: *Bon sang*, son. You must have a loose screw up there or something. He taps on his temple. "Sounds like you really mean it."
Zh: “*我操。*孩子，你一定是这里哪个螺丝松了。”他点点自己的太阳穴。“听起来你是认真的。”

En: Alright, I will entertain this... *fantasy*, if you tell me, what interest does an RCM officer have in... 'time travel'?
Zh: “好吧，那我就满足你这个……*幻想*。但你得告诉我，一个RCM警官要……‘时间旅行’干什么。”

En: Oh, you're in for a treat, officer. Seeing the playful side of old René is about as rare as a red rainbow.
Zh: “哦，这下你可有好戏看了，警官。老雷内的童心就和红色的彩虹一样稀有。”

En: Not actually *that* rare. It's just that red, or monochrome, rainbows usually occur at sunrise or sunset, when blue and green wavelengths are scattered before reaching water droplets. Fewer eyes to see them then.
Zh: 其实也没有*那么*稀有。只是红色，或者说单色的彩虹常常出现在日出和日落时，这时蓝绿光在照射到水滴之前就散射了。因此也很少有人见过它们。

En: He doesn't know anything, he's too old. Doesn't have that *fire* in him anymore. But no worries, *we* will carry on. *Semper fidelis*, kingsman!
Zh: 他什么也不知道，他太老了。再也没有*火焰*了。但别担心，*我们*会继续前景。*永远忠诚*，骑士！

En: Love. It's about love.
Zh: “爱。这与爱有关。”

En: It's always about love, isn't it?
Zh: “总是和爱有关，不是吗？”

En: Was that a smile on his wrinkled... nevermind, it's gone.
Zh: 他那布满皱纹的脸上泛起了一抹怎样的微笑啊……眨眼之间它便消失了。

En: But it's never *only* about love, so what else, officer?
Zh: “但也总是*不只*和爱有关。所以还有什么，警官？”

En: Men have lost their way. I want to guide them back. Like a beacon.
Zh: “人们处在迷惘之中。我得指引他们回到正道。像一座灯塔那样。”

En: The old soldier nods. "Even after the Revolution, after the Beachhead, there was a chance. Had we just stayed true to our *values*..."
Zh: 老兵点点头。“即使在革命和抢滩登陆之后，我们依然还有机会。如果我们曾经忠于我们的*价值观*……”

En: I'm not sure, to be honest. Just going with the flow.
Zh: “老实说我也不确定。只是随波逐流罢了。”

En: It happened slowly, like a seedling growing into a tree. Tiny incremental steps. Imperceptible till the thicket was so thick you couldn't clear it with a flamethrower...
Zh: “它是缓慢发生的，像幼苗逐渐长成参天大树。一小步一小步前进，让你觉察不出来。直到树丛变得如此繁茂，你才惊觉用火焰喷射器都无法清除它们了……”

En: You could *definitely* clear it with a flamethrower.
Zh: 用火焰喷射器*肯定*可以清除它。

En: I *know* I can get history back on the right track.
Zh: “我就*知道*我能让历史回到正确的道路。”

En: I just want a second chance. Gonna do it right this time.
Zh: “我只是想要第二次机会。这次我一定会做对。”

En: Well, that depends on the flamethrower...
Zh: “这个取决于火焰喷射器的型号……”

En: It really does, doesn't it? The old soldier produces a strange croaking sound -- with laughter-like qualities -- then quickly massages his chest. "But honestly, why?"
Zh: “它确实很重要，不是吗？”老兵发出一阵古怪的低笑——然后马上揉了揉胸口。“但是说真的，为什么？”

En: Keep the war-crime jokes coming and you'll end up on his good side.
Zh: “继续讲战争罪行的笑话，他会认可你的。”

En: The 'right track'? This *is* the right track! The only track. He gets visibly annoyed. "This is the world we shaped, a reflection of what we are: cowardly, ugly, and numb."
Zh: “‘正确的道路’？这*就是*正确的道路！唯一的道路。”他显然生气了。“这是我们塑造的世界——它也折射出我们的懦弱、丑陋和麻木。”

En: 'This time'?! He's becoming visibly annoyed. "What happened to the first time? Did you make a mistake? Did you *fuck up*? Everyone did!"
Zh: “‘这一次’？！”他肉眼可见地恼火了起来。“第一次是怎么回事？你犯了什么错误？你搞砸了吗？我们全都搞砸了！”

En: And there are no second chances. We don't deserve them! You can't just go back and restart -- that would make everything *MEANINGLESS*! A shadow of pain comes over his face.
Zh: “再也不会有第二次机会了。我们不配！你不可能就这样倒带重启——那会让一切都*没有意义*！”一道痛苦的阴影掠过他的面颊。

En: There's something *substantial* moving in him, trying to get out.
Zh: 他的体内有某种东西*蠕动*着，试图破壳而出。

En: Exactly what history needs. He spits on the ground between the two of you. "More drifters, confused wanderers, weightless men..."
Zh: “正是历史需要的东西。”他对着面前的地面吐了口痰。“越来越多的流浪者，迷途的旅人，浮萍游子……”

En: There's so much bitterness in his voice it barely even registers as sarcasm.
Zh: 他的声音中有太多的苦涩，几乎盖过了讽刺意味。

En: I can't help you officer, he says coldly. "There are no second chances in life. We don't deserve them. This is the world we shaped -- a reflection of what we are. Wasted, ugly, and numb."
Zh: “警官，我没法帮你。”他冷酷地说道。“人生没有第二次机会。我们不配得到它。这是我们塑造的世界——它也折射出我们的懦弱、丑陋和麻木。”

En: The lieutenant clears his throat. "I think we're done here unless you have questions *not* relating to 'time travel' for the gentlemen?"
Zh: 警督清了清嗓子。“我想我们讲完了。除非你还有和‘时间穿越’*无关*的问题，需要询问这位先生？”

En: It *is* an ugly world...
Zh: “它*确实*是个丑陋的词语……”

En: You know, it's not actually that bad.
Zh: “你知道吗，其实没有那么糟糕。”

En: Just nod.
Zh: 点点头。

En: Such a downer, I'm not even sure how to respond...
Zh: “真是个丧气的家伙。我甚至不知道该说什么……”

En: A heavy silence falls over the crater. The man in the checkered suit doesn't know where to look or what to do with his hands. Finally he speaks:
Zh: 弹坑陷入沉重的寂静。穿着方格西装的男人好像不知道该看向何处，或是用双手做什么。最终他打破了沉默：

En: Only to break the silence.
Zh: 只是为了打破沉默。

En: Officer, you must forgive Old René. Holding a conversation has always been... problematic with him. He flashes you a quick smile. "Try asking him something else."
Zh: “警官，你得原谅老雷内。他向来不那么擅长……交谈。”他对你微笑了一下。“试着问他一点别的。”

En: Don't worry, he doesn't know anything useful, the man's almost expired. No fire in him. But *you* will carry on the flame. *Semper fidelis*, kingsman, anything is possible.
Zh: 别担心，他不知道任何有用的信息。这个人已经快过期了。胸中没有激情。但*你*会传递这火焰。*永远忠诚*，骑士，一切皆有可能。

En: Easy, René, take it easy. There's genuine concern in his face. "You're right, absolutely right. What was, *was* and that's it. Isn't that right, officer?"
Zh: “雷内，别生气，别生气。”他的脸上有着真诚的担忧。“你是对的，你完全没错。过去的都*过去*了，就是这样。对吧，警官？”

En: His eyes seem to be pleading with you.
Zh: 他的双眼中流露出乞求的神色。

En: He would sooner die than let it surface.
Zh: 他宁可早点死去也不想让你发现。

En: What is it?
Zh: 怎么了？

En: Even thinking about it is invading his privacy. Better let it go.
Zh: 即使只是这么想想也是在侵犯他的隐私。还是不要刺探了。

En: Regret.
Zh: 后悔。

En: Honour walks and virtue follows. You are truly a Detective-Saint, for you refuse to veer even when *no one* is looking. Bravo!
Zh: 荣誉先行，道德从之。你真是个警探——圣人，在*没人*注意的时候也要保持慎独。真棒！

En: Sure, why not?
Zh: “当然。”

En: *Everyone* deserves a second chance.
Zh: “*每个人*都应该拥有第二次机会。”

En: Look, I just want to turn back time. Can either of you help me or not?
Zh: “你看，我只想回到过去。你们中到底有没有人能帮我一下了？”

En: *Imbécile, un putain d'imbécile*... The old veteran clutches his chest and tries to steady his breathing.
Zh: “*蠢货*，他妈的*蠢货*……”老兵按住胸口，试图让呼吸平稳下来。

En: *Something* about this topic was very personal to him, but the moment has passed.
Zh: 这对他来说似乎是一个*非常*私人的话题。但那个瞬间很快过去了。

En: *Something* about this topic was very personal to him, but the moment has passed.
Zh: 这对他来说似乎是一个*非常*私人的话题。但那个瞬间很快过去了。

En: *Imbécile, un putain d'imbécile!*... The old veteran clutches his chest and tries to steady his breathing.
Zh: “*蠢货*，他妈的*蠢货*……”老兵按住胸口，试图让呼吸平稳下来。

En: Enough, officer, the other one says with surprising resoluteness. "We can't help you. Not with this. Such an *emotional* topic..."
Zh: “够了，警官。”加斯顿带着令你惊奇的坚定说道。“我们帮不了你。这件事不行。这个话题太*情绪化*了……”

En: But don't worry, I'm sure there's something else old René or I can assist you with.
Zh: “但是别担心。我相信老雷内和我能在别的事情上帮到你。”

En: The carabineer is too old and feeble to be useful. But you will carry on. *Movere tempore*, kingsman. For the tenacious no road is impassable.
Zh: 老兵已经年老体弱，毫无用处。但是你还年轻。“继续前进”，骑士。有志者事竟成。

En: *Imbécile, un putain d'imbécile*... The old veteran clutches his chest and tries to steady his breathing.
Zh: “*蠢货*，他妈的*蠢货*……”老兵按住胸口，试图让呼吸平稳下来。

En: No, officer. He shakes his head meekly. "We can't help you with that, maybe there's something else we *can* help you with?"
Zh: “不，警官。”他无力地摇摇头。“这件事我帮不了你。也许我们*能*帮到你别的？”

En: Regret about what?
Zh: 后悔什么？

En: Bonded over flamethrowers.
Zh: 和火焰喷射器蜜里调油。

En: Heard about Jeanne-Marie.
Zh: 听说过珍妮·玛丽。

En: No, this is going too far. I should respect his privacy!
Zh: 不，这太过了。应当尊重他的隐私！

En: Him.
Zh: 他。

En: You know the answer, don't think -- just say it!
Zh: 你知道答案，别想了——说出来！

En: You catch the old carabineer's gaze slowly leaving his opponent's wrinkled face as his dark eyes meet yours -- whatever turmoil raged in him a moment ago is quelled for now.
Zh: 你发现老兵的目光慢慢地从他搭档那布满皱纹的脸上移开，黑色的双眼望向你——刚才在他心中翻腾的怒火，不管那是为了什么，现在已经平息。

En: Like the last rays of the evening sun gently kissing the day goodbye, before giving way to unfathomable darkness.
Zh: 如同夕阳的最后一缕光芒轻柔地向白日吻别，让位于沉郁的黑暗。

En: Willed back into the darkest unexplored depths of his mind -- never meant to be shared, seen or confronted.
Zh: 他的思绪沉入未知的黑暗角落——他不愿为外人得知与辩驳的地方。

En: A true master of his emotions.
Zh: 情绪管理大师。

En: Our lives are unique, and I have neither the means nor the will to help you change that. A polite nod. "But I will gladly assist you with police matters."
Zh: “我们的生活是独一无二的，我既没有能力也没有意愿帮助你改变它。”他礼貌地点点头。“但我很乐意在其他警察事务上协助你。”

En: Hopelessly alone behind the unbreakable walls he spent a lifetime erecting. No one will ever know him.
Zh: 他绝望而孤独地屹立在牢不可破的高墙之后。这一生不会有人真正理解他。

En: The carabineer is too old and feeble to be useful. But you will carry on. *Movere tempore*, kingsman. For the tenacious no road is impassable.
Zh: 老兵已经年老体弱，毫无用处。但是你还年轻。“继续前进”，骑士。有志者事竟成。

En: Gun!
Kim?
War.
Zh: “战争。”

En: Dad?!
I'm not gonna say anything.
Zh: “我什么都不会说的。”

En: Where?! His head drops between his shoulders he snaps around with surprising speed.
Zh: “哪里？！”他原本垂在胸前的头颅猛地抬起，以惊人的速度转向你。

En: Retired or not, there's still a trained killer in there.
Zh: 即使在退伍之后，他依然是个训练有素的杀手。

En: Goddamnit, son... He turns back to you, frowning. "As an officer of the law you should know better than... damn it! Are we done here?"
Zh: “他妈的，孩子……”他转向你，皱起眉头。“作为一名执法人员，你不应该……操！你讲完了吗？”

En: No... not gun. Something repressed and painful. Too late. The moment has passed.
Zh: 不……不是枪。是某种压抑而痛苦的东西。太迟了。机会已然逝去。

En: *Meaningless* is what I think this line of questioning is. Call me a sceptic, but I don't think time travel is going to solve our murder case.
Zh: “我只能说这个问题*毫无意义*。你可以认为我有多疑症，但我不认为穿越时间能够帮助我们解决这个谋杀案件。”

En: So... Is there anything else you want from these gentlemen, or can we get back to it?
Zh: “所以……你还有什么想问这两位先生的话吗，还是我们可以继续查案了？”

En: W-war? The word seems to derail his train of thought. "I mean yes. Yes... It changes men, changes lives and uh..."
Zh: “战——战争？”这个词语仿佛令他的思维火车脱了轨。“我想是的。是的……它改变了人们，改变了生命，还有，呃……”

En: What the hell are we talking about here, officer? He rubs the back of his head. "Look, we just want to play our game, so unless there's something else..."
Zh: “我们到底在讨论什么鬼东西，警官？”他抓了抓后脑勺。“你看，我们只想玩点小游戏，所以除非你有别的问题……”

En: War isn't tied with regret for him. Nevermind, the moment is gone.
Zh: 战争并不是令他悔恨的东西。没关系，机会已然逝去。

En: Shouldn't have brought him into this. He doesn't know pain like you do.
Zh: 不应该把他牵扯进这种事情。他不像你那样惯于忍受痛苦。

En: No, not that one.
Zh: 不，不是这个。

En: Wh...? Oh, I see. He takes a step back and nods, jaw clenched tight. "You're a comedian after all. Funny guy. *Unpredictable*."
Zh: “什……？哦，我明白了。”他后退一步，点点头，紧咬牙关。“你是个小丑。搞笑的家伙。*令人意外*。”

En: Alright, you got your laugh, ruined my concentration and our game... *again*. Anything else you want to ruin, or can we start a new one?\n
Zh: “行了，你找够了乐子，分散了我的注意力，毁了我们的游戏……*再一次*。你还想问什么吗，还是我们能重新开始游戏了？”

En: Alright, you got your laugh, ruined my concentration and our game... Anything else you want, or can we start a new one?
Zh: “行了，你找够了乐子，分散了我的注意力，毁了我们的游戏……你还想问什么吗，还是我们能重新开始游戏了？”

En: That's just *typical*! The carabineer looks at you with disgust. "All you young guys these days. You want to be opinionated, controversial, opposition leaders..."
Zh: “真*典型*！”老兵嫌恶地看着你。“你们这些年轻人。你只想炫耀争议性的观点，把自己塑造成意见领袖……”

En: But as soon as the talk turns real and there's a smallest amount of friction you're suddenly 'not gonna say anything'. Better to be wrong than a goddamn coward!
Zh: “但只要我们的讨论牵扯到现实，或是遇到了一丁点儿矛盾，你马上‘什么也不说’了。哪怕说错话也他妈的比做一个懦夫要好！”

En: No, I didn't mean it like that.
Zh: “不，我不是这个意思。”

En: Did I say that out loud?
Zh: “我刚刚是把这句话大声说出来了吗？”

En: Oh save it. He waves you off. "Men don't have substance anymore, I know that, but seeing such spinelessness in a man bearing an insignia..."
Zh: “别来这一套。”他挥挥手让你滚蛋。“人们变得空虚了，我早就知道，但看到你这样一个软骨头戴着警徽……”

En: You know what you meant, but taking this kind of scolding does *not* feel good.
Zh: 你知道你是什么意思，但受到这样的责骂感觉并*不*好。

En: You must forgive mean old René, officer! The other one jumps in. "He can't stop being himself more than a scorpion can stop being a sco..."
Zh: “你得原谅老雷内，警官！”他的伙伴居中调停。“他固执己见得好似一只蝎子……”

En: Enough yacking! the carabineer cuts Gaston off mid-sentence. "We have a game to finish, so let's get back to it, unless there's something else?"
Zh: “别东拉西扯了！”老兵打断加斯顿的话。“我们的游戏还没有结束，所以我们要继续玩下去，除非你还有别的问题？”

En: Damn it, not this again...
Zh: 操，不要又来这一套……

En: Yes. The lieutenant turns to look at you as well. "What else, officer?"
Zh: “是的。”警督转向你。“还有什么，警官？”

En: The lieutenant rubs his forehead and mumbles something under his breath.
Zh: 警督揉了揉额头，嘀咕了一句什么。

En: 'Here we go again.'
Zh: “又来了。”

En: Anything else you want or can we get back to our game?
Zh: “你还想问什么别的问题，还是我们能继续玩游戏了？”

En: Him?
Who?
There's tenderness in the carabineer's look. Tenderness that's curdled into pain or something darker.
Zh: 老兵的目光中含着柔情，而这柔情已沉积为痛苦或甚至比之更黑暗的情感。

En: Ex-love, ex-tenderness...
Zh: 旧爱，过往的柔情……

En: Even worse, a love aborted and smothered, stamped beneath his brilliant boot heel.
Zh: 甚至更糟，被遗弃的窒息的爱，被他华丽的靴跟践踏在脚下。

En: The old carabineer looks at you with wary expectation.
Zh: 老兵疲惫地看着你。

En: Go long, go deep, go unconventional!
Zh: 更远，更深入，更不传统！

En: Unfortunately there doesn't seem to be anything worth mentioning among your achievements. You should resort to good old lying.
Zh: 不幸的是，您的成就当中似乎没什么值得一提的。您应该求助于谎言了。

En: My story doesn't have gunfire, blood or spilled guts, but let me give you a *different* take on heroism.
Zh: “我的故事里没有炮火，鲜血或者飞溅的内脏，不过让我为你提供一个*不同的*英雄主义视角。”

En: It must have been -- there was a magnum-sized bottle in my room...
Zh: “肯定是的——我的房间里有一支大酒瓶……”

En: No, I have found *The Source*. The Source of The Flow. I have found... Al Gul.
Zh: “不，我发现了*源头*。发现了心流的源头。我发现了……阿尔古。”

En: You mean alcohol?
Zh: “你的意思是酒精？”

En: Yes. Alcohol.
Zh: “是的。酒精。”

En: SILENCE! You know not of which you speak! I have danced with Al Gul for one thousand nights!
Zh: “闭嘴！你根本不知道自己在说什么！我已经跟阿尔古共舞过几千个夜晚了！”

En: Everyone can see that, yes.
Zh: “大家都能看出来，没错。”

En: Yet you do not *comprehend* the suffering of enlightenment I have endured.
Zh: “然而你还是不能*理解*我忍受的开悟之苦。”

En: This deity of yours has quite the following in Martinaise... He straightens his back. "None as zealous as you it seems, though."
Zh: “你说的这位神明在马丁内斯有不少追随者……”他挺直了后背。“不过似乎没人像你一样热忱。”

En: All of this, accompanied by a musical composition telling of the heroism of old, when men were bigger in both body and heart. You feel strange kinship to this old soldier. You and him are the same now.
Zh: 所有这一切，伴随着讲述古老英雄气概的乐曲，那时的男人拥有更加强壮的身体和心脏。你对这个老兵产生了一种奇怪的亲切感。现在你和他是一样的了。

En: This feels *honourable*. You did the *right* thing not taking the medal. The *good* thing. Your chest is buzzing with pride.
Zh: 这让你感觉很*光荣*。没有收下奖章是一件*正确*的事情。一件*好事*。你的胸中充满了骄傲。

En: Feels as though you should do more honourable things. As if there's some kind of a... secret reward. If you just commit enough honourable acts.
Zh: 感觉你应该做些更加光荣的事情。就像是某种……秘密奖励。只要你能做出足够荣耀的举动就行。

En: I don't think there was anything else he wanted. Was there? You got the medal, now let's solve the crime.
Zh: “我想他没什么想要的了。不是吗？你已经拿到了奖章，现在让我们继续破案吧。”

En: He'll get it. Don't worry, just continue -- he's *gonna* be impressed.
Zh: 他会明白的。别担心，继续努力——他*肯定*会被您打动的。

En: Imagine knowing nothing but the sweet oblivion only to wake into unbelievable pain...
Zh: “想象一下，除了甜蜜的湮灭之外一无所有，只会在难以置信的痛苦中醒来……”

En: Imagine waking up with a terrible headache.
Zh: “想象一下，带着糟糕的头疼醒来。”

En: Doesn't look like he's imagining it.
Zh: 看来他并没有在想象。

En: ... and realize your mind -- everything that was *you* -- is gone.
Zh: “……意识到你的思想——还有关于*你*的一切——全部消失了。”

En: ... and realize the man-body, with *you* in it, is just a husk now. All memories -- erased.
Zh: “……意识到这具人类的身体，装着*你*的身体，现在只是一个空壳。所有记忆——全被抹去。”

En: ... and realize you've pushed yourself too far. Past the point of no return.
Zh: “……意识到你逼着自己走得太远。已经再也无法回头。”

En: The lieutenant looks up from his notes and nods to the old carabineer.
Zh: 警督抬起埋在笔记本里的脑袋，朝着老兵点点头。

En: I still feel terrible, René. Sickly, panicky, outright sad -- you name it.
Zh: “我依然能感觉到恐怖，雷内。病态，恐慌，绝对的悲伤——应有尽有。”

En: The lieutenant sighs audibly, but keeps his eyes on the notebook.
Zh: 警督大声地叹了口气。不过眼睛还是固定在笔记本上。

En: You're talking about getting black-out shitfaced drunk and going into delirium, right? he asks impatiently.
Zh: “你说的是喝的伶仃大醉，然后变得神志不清吧？”他不耐烦地问到。

En: Okay, I admit it -- I got so drunk I forgot even *who* I am.
Zh: “好吧，我承认——我喝的太醉，甚至都忘了自己是*谁*。”

En: Hey, a man needs to decompress every now and then -- nothing wrong with that -- I got it in check.
Zh: “喂，男人时不时都需要缓解一下压力吧——这没什么错——我能控制好的。”

En: Yes, from the looks of it you deserve a medal for the *excellent* handle you have on things. He snorts. "Here, have one of mine!"
Zh: “是的，从表面上看，你*出色*的处理能力应该得到一枚奖章。”他嘲弄到。“来，我的给你吧！”

En: Your sarcasm will be your undoing, old man.
Zh: 你的讽刺终究会毁掉你的，死老头。

En: His tone is like that of a drill-sergeant addressing new recruits.
Zh: 他的口气就像是一名军事训练军官正在对新兵讲话。

En: He mumbled: "You certainly had me fooled."
Zh: 他嘟囔到：‘你肯定是在耍我。’

En: Extremely rigid principles this old soldier has, don't leave room for a change of heart or backing out. The medal is yours.
Zh: 这个老兵拥有极其严格的原则，没有留下任何改变心意或是退缩的余地。奖章是你的了。

En: I knocked Cuno out (Point to the yard.) "The kid needed discipline."
Zh: “我把坤诺打晕了。”（指向院子。）“那个孩子需要管教。”

En: The twelve-year-old junkie, whose alcoholic father beats him? His eyes light up. "Why did you do that?"
Zh: “那个12岁的瘾君子，被酒鬼老爹打的那个？”他的眼睛亮了起来。“你为什么要这么做？”

En: I needed to make him respect me.
Zh: “我需要让他尊重我。”

En: No, okay -- we're actually cool now. I even stole some drugs from his dad.
Zh: “不，没什么的——现在我们已经没事了。我甚至还从他爸爸那里偷了点毒品。”

En: You're right, I shouldn't have hit him.
Zh: “你说得对，我不该打他的。”

En: He was screaming that I'm trying to molest him, while I clearly wasn't.
Zh: “他尖叫着说我想骚扰他，显然我并没有。”

En: Officer, he says with obvious disdain, "this is unacceptable for anyone bearing an insignia. A man should lead by example, not terror."
Zh: “警官，”他带着明显的鄙视说道，“带着徽章的人做出这种事是让人无法接受的。一个男人应该以身作则，而不是威逼恐吓。”

En: If you don't have any more questions we'll return to our game now. By the looks of it he's still trying to wrap his head around what you told him.
Zh: “如果你没有别的问题，我们要继续玩游戏了。”看他那副样子，肯定还在尝试理解你说的那些话。

En: For a moment frozen in time this old soldier is your superior officer, inspiring you to be a better man. It feels good. Comforting.
Zh: 时间凝固在这一刻，这个老兵就像你的上级，激励你变成一个更好的男人。这种感觉很好。倍感安慰。

En: Well, if you phrase it like that it doesn't sound too good.
Zh: 呃，如果你用那种措辞的话，听起来肯定不太好。

En: Actually nevermind, I don't wanna lie.
Zh: “其实吧，没关系的，我不想撒谎。”

En: The old soldier shakes his head in disapproval. "What do you want, officer?"
Zh: 老兵不以为然地摇摇头。“你有什么需要，警官？”

En: It took us nearly seven hours. Fifi died in my arms, when the water was only knee deep... She was a good dog.
Zh: “我们花了大约7个小时。最后菲菲死在了我的怀抱里，当时水只有齐膝的深度……它是条好狗。”

En: Please stop.
Zh: “请别说了。”

En: You're right, this isn't very believable, is it?
Zh: “你说得对，这不是很可信，是吗？”

En: I'm not gonna dignify this with an answer. He frowns. "We're gonna get back to our game now if there isn't anything else, officer?"
Zh: “我都不屑于回答。”他皱起眉头。“如果没有别的事，我们就继续玩游戏了，警官。”

En: Son... He's slowly shaking his head. "I don't know why you're feeding me this horse shit, but I'm not biting. Can we get back to our game now?"
Zh: “孩子……”他慢慢摇摇头。“我不知道你为什么要跟我说这些胡话，但是我不会上当的。现在可以放我们继续玩游戏了吗？”

En: René... (Lower your voice.) "I'm ex-army too. Special Forces. Black ops. Secret soldier."
Zh: “雷内……”（压低声音。）“我之前也是军人。特种部队。黑色行动。秘密士兵。”

En: Really? Suddenly it feels like he's entitled to question you. "What was the unit?"
Zh: “真的吗？”突然间，他觉得自己有资格询问你。“什么部队？”

En: The Perrywinkle Berets.
Zh: “玉黍螺蓝贝雷帽。”

En: The Cyan Berets.
Zh: “青色贝雷帽。”

En: This information is classified.
Zh: “这是机密信息。”

En: The 5th?
Zh: “第5军团？”

En: Harry's Hounds.
Zh: “哈里的猎犬。”

En: Something with colours and headwear. Soldiers identify with those things.
Zh: 有颜色和头饰的东西。士兵对那些东西很有认同感。

En: Say it's *classified*. An old soldier should understand.
Zh: 说这是*机密*。老兵会理解的。

En: Mumble incoherently.
Zh: 语无伦次地咕哝。

En: Actually I don't wanna resort to lying after all.
Zh: “其实我根本不想撒谎。”

En: Good call. Not a muscle in his face moves as he slowly nods. "Think we can get back to our game now?"
Zh: “说得好。”他慢慢点头，面部没有任何肌肉跟着动作。“现在可以放我们继续玩游戏了吗？”

En: That's what I thought. He nods. "We'll get back to our game now if you don't mind."
Zh: “我也是这么想的。”他点点头。“如果你不介意的话，我们继续回去玩游戏了。”

En: Are you asking me or telling me?
Zh: “你是在问我，还是要告诉我？”

En: Asking?
Zh: “问你？”

En: Telling.
Zh: “告诉你。”

En: I'll stop you right there, son. He raises his hand. "I'm old, but I'm not stupid. I *know* a soldier when I see one."
Zh: “我必须阻止你了，孩子。”他举起手。“我老了，但是我不傻，是不是士兵，我看一眼就*知道*。”

En: We'll get back to our game now if you don't mind.
Zh: “现在，如果你不介意的话，我们就继续回去玩游戏了。”

En: That's what I thought. He nods. "We'll get back to our game now if you don't mind, *officer*."
Zh: “我也是这么想的。”他点点头。“现在，如果你不介意的话，我们就继续回去玩游戏了，*警官*。”

En: Mhmh... He nods. "We'll get back to our game now if there isn't anything else?"
Zh: “嗯……”他点点头。“如果没有别的事，我们就继续回去玩游戏了？”

En: Strange, he didn't buy it.
Zh: 奇怪，他没有相信。

En: Strange, he didn't buy it.
Zh: 奇怪，他没有相信。

En: Odd, he didn't buy it.
Zh: 奇怪，他没有相信。

En: You tell me.
Zh: “你跟我说说吧。”

En: I'd rather just ask him some questions.
Zh: 我宁愿问他问题。

En: Of course, it's your call. But he would have been *really* impressed.
Zh: 当然，这是您自己的决定。不过他*真的*会被您打动的。

En: Was there anything else, officer? he asks impatiently. Looks like they both want to continue the game.
Zh: “还有别的事吗，警官？”他不耐烦地问到。看起来他们都想继续游戏。

En: Unless you wanna tell him how you took Cuno down. The decisiveness you exhibited in that situation would surely impress a man fond of order and discipline.
Zh: 除非你想告诉他，你是怎么把坤诺打倒的。你在那种情况下展现出来的决断力肯定能打动一个喜欢命令和纪律的男人。

En: Lying just isn't my style. I'd rather get back to questions.
Zh: 撒谎不是我的风格。我宁愿继续问问题。

En: But of course, sire. Looks like they're waiting for you to speak.
Zh: 当然了，大人。看来他们在等您说话呢。

En: Just like that. He analysed the situation, accepted the facts and moved on. Like a soldier.
Zh: 就像那样。他分析事态，接受现实，然后继续前进。像一个士兵。

En: And I thought getting my knee shattered and surviving on rat carcasses in the trench was bad... here, have one of my medals! You've earned it, *officer*.
Zh: “我觉得膝盖粉碎和靠着战壕里的老鼠尸骸活下来就已经很糟糕了……给你，拿着我的这枚奖章！这是你应得的，*警官*。”

En: Thank you, René, I am honoured. (Reach out your hand.)
Zh: “谢谢你，雷内，我很荣幸。”（伸出你的手。）

En: I don't deserve this. (Don't take the medal.)
Zh: “这不是我应得的。”（不要收下奖章。）

En: Kim, should I take it?
Zh: “金，我该收下它吗？”

En: Flashes of bloody and terrible scenes of war. Men dying, horses shrieking, blood sinking into sand and snow and asphalt and fertile black earth...
Zh: 血腥和恐怖的战争画面一闪而过。垂死的人，嘶鸣的马，被血浸透的沙地、白雪、沥青和肥沃的黑土。

En: The man shakes his head, mumbling something under his nose, then turns back to you: "Can we get back to our game now?"
Zh: 男人摇摇头，低声嘟囔着什么，然后他转向你：“现在可以放我们继续玩游戏了吗？”

En: Of course not. He doesn't even look up from his notebook.
Zh: “当然不该。”他甚至都没头都没抬一下。

En: I uh... I had a dog -- 'Fifi'. Took her fishing late one autumn but the boat rocked and she fell in, so I jumped after...
Zh: “我，呃……有过一只狗——‘菲菲’。有一年深秋，我带它去钓鱼，但是船晃了一下，它掉进了水里，我也跟着跳了下去……”

En: Imagine just flickering into existence from nothingness.
Zh: “想象一下，虚无中那若隐若现的存在。”

En: Imagine pain that makes giving birth seem like a mosquito bite. But *inside your head*. (Point to your head.)
Zh: “想象一下，相比这种痛苦，分娩痛就像是蚊虫叮咬一般。不过是在*你的脑袋里面*。”（指着你的脑袋。）

En: Right when you are willing to do just about anything to make it stop, you open your eyelids...
Zh: “就在你愿意做任何事来让它停下来的时候，你睁开双眼……”

En: I'm talking about so much more than that.
Zh: “我说的可远远不止那样。”

En: Well... (Drag out the pause.) "In essence, yes."
Zh: “呃……”（长长地停顿一下。）“本质上来说，是的。”

En: How dare you belittle my voyage through the void?!
Zh: “你怎么敢轻视我穿越虚无的旅程？”

En: Was the vessel you boarded the 'HMS Commodore Red'?
Zh: “那你登上的那艘船是‘海军准将红朗姆’吗？”

En: How so, then? He leans in with his hands on his hips.
Zh: “为什么会这样呢？”他靠近了一些，双手放在臀部上。

En: Look, I don't think anyone's ever been so drunk. It must be a record.
Zh: “你看，我觉得没人像我这么醉过。这肯定破记录了。”

En: The old carabineer stares at you for a moment, then tension leaves him and he asks: "Was there something else you wanted to take from us?"
Zh: 老兵看了你一会儿，接着，紧张感消失了，他问到：“你还想从我们这里得到些什么？”

En: The man stares at you, silently frowning.
Zh: 男人看着你，默默地皱起了眉头。

En: I'm not following you.
Zh: “我没明白你的意思。”

En: The carabineer crosses his arms and spits in the crater.
Zh: 老兵抱起双臂，朝弹坑里吐了一口唾沫。

En: Sure, officer. Already he looks sceptical. "Let's hear your *take*."
Zh: “当然，警官。”他看起来已经很怀疑了。“我们就听听你的*视角*吧。”

En: For your information, alcoholism is a terrible, debilitating ailment.
Zh: “仅供参考，酒精是一种很恐怖，能使人衰弱的疾病。”

En: You already threw their ball into the water, took a sandwich from one of them, and a medal from the other. What more could we possibly want? Perhaps we're done bullying these citizens?
Zh: “你把他们的球扔进了海里，从其中一个人手里拿到了三明治，另一个人手里拿到了奖章。我们还能索要什么？也许欺负市民的行为可以到此为止了？”

En: You already threw their ball into the water and took his medal, perhaps we're done bullying these citizens?
Zh: “你把他们的球扔进了海里，还拿走了他的奖章，也许欺负市民的行为可以到此为止了？”

En: The veteran extends his right arm with the sun-shaped medal. It glimmers in his wrinkled and scarred palm.
Zh: 老兵伸出佩戴着太阳形奖章的右臂。它躺在那布满皱纹和伤疤的手掌心，闪闪发光。

En: The veteran of numerous armed conflicts extends his right arm with the *Setting Sun* in his clenched fist and an expression of utter disbelief in his eyes.
Zh: 这位经历过许多武装冲突的老兵伸出右臂，将*落日勋章*紧握在手中，眼睛里流露出极端不信任的神情。

En: The carabineer stares at you with a frown. Doesn't look like he's buying it.
Zh: 老兵眉头紧锁，直直地盯着你看。看起来他并没有相信。

En: The boat capsized and sank. Fifi was already tired. She was almost 11. I took her on my back and started swimming...
Zh: “船翻了，沉了。菲菲已经筋疲力尽。它已经快11岁了。于是我把它抗在背上，开始向前游……”

En: The name echoes through the deep dark halls of history... it calls to you and your kin!
Zh: 这个名字回荡在历史的黑暗大厅里……它在呼唤你和你的同类！

En: I'm sorry, I think I just fell off the wagon that's all. I lack character...
Zh: “很抱歉，我想我只是旧瘾复发了，仅此而已。我缺乏个性……”

En: It was a transcendent event. I saw behind *The Veil*.
Zh: “那是一次超凡的事件。我看到了*面纱*背后的模样。”

En: *Heavy* artillery fire you say? That's the best kind of artillery fire. Very interesting.
Zh: 你说*重型火炮？那是最棒的火炮。很有意思。

En: (Look around) "I understand. I'd bomb this place too."
Zh: （环顾四周）“我理解。我也会把这个地方炸平的。”

En: This place is the damn Beachhead, son, he says, pointing to the bay. "They didn't do it because they didn't *like* it. They had to soften the commies up first."
Zh: “因为这里是该死的滩头阵地啊，”他指着海湾说道。“必须先软化那些康米党员。”

En: He finds your lack of historic knowledge troubling. A sign of mental deterioration in the proceeding generations.
Zh: 你很缺乏历史知识这一点让他觉得有些麻烦。这是后代心智衰退的迹象。

En: Thinking men have *opinions* on these things. Present one.
Zh: 有思考能力的人对这种事情是有*想法*的。提一个吧。

En: Some manner of self-deceit is present in his thinking. Sounds like this Guillaume abandoned him and he doesn't want to admit it.
Zh: 他的想法中有种自欺欺人的感觉。听起来就像纪尧姆抛弃了他，而他并不想承认。

En: He slouches as he says that. It makes him smaller, admitting they left the king to the mob.
Zh: 他说这话的时候无精打采的。承认他们把国王留给暴徒之后，他看起来更加渺小了。

En: Soon they will forget everything. Him too... Then he chooses anger over melancholy.
Zh: 很快他们会忘记一切。他也是……然后他会选择愤怒而不是悲哀。

En: An extremely inadvisable strategy against automatic weapons. Be sure not to make the same mistake.
Zh: 在面对自动武器时，这是一种极度不明智的策略。注意不要犯相同的错误。

En: Wait, who are the 'communards', again?
Zh: “等等，再说一遍‘公社社员’是什么人？”

En: Was it made by a sea monster rising from the depths?
Zh: “是不是海底深处冒出来的海洋巨兽造成的？”

En: No, he says without even smiling. "It was left by heavy artillery fire."
Zh: “不，”他的脸上没有一丝笑意。“那是重型火炮留下的。”

En: No, he replies, gaze wondering over the bay. "It was left by heavy artillery fire."
Zh: “不，”他凝望着海湾，回复到。“那是重型火炮留下的。”

En: Was there an earthquake here?
Zh: “这里发生地震了吗？”

En: Does it have anything to do with all the bullet holes I've been seeing around?
Zh: “这跟我在周围看见的弹孔有关系吗？”

En: Yes, he replies with a reluctant nod. "It was left by heavy artillery fire."
Zh: “是的，”他不情愿地点点头。“那是重型火炮留下的。”

En: Yes... The man nods. "The terrain here provides an interesting variety to a familiar game."
Zh: “是的……”男人点点头。“这里的地势为熟悉的游戏提供了一些有趣的多样性。”

En: Why what?
Zh: “什么为什么？”

En: Did *you* use artillery fire against them?
Zh: “*你*用炮火对付过他们吗？”

En: Do you know what created it?
Zh: “你知道这是什么东西造成的吗？”

En: Why was heavy artillery used?
Zh: “为什么要用重型火炮？”

En: I do, he nods, straightening his back. "Fire from heavy artillery."
Zh: “我知道，”他点点头，挺直了后背。“重型火炮的弹药。”

En: Okay, it's a crater left by artillery fire -- but why?
Zh: “好吧，这是炮火留下的弹坑——但是为什么呢？”

En: Commies, communists, socialists, anarchists -- call them what you like. They just chose the name to feel special. The carabineer frowns. "Senseless sentimentality."
Zh: “康米党，康米主义者，工人主义者，无政府主义者——随你怎么叫。他们选择这个名字只是为了感觉特别一点。”老兵皱起眉头。“毫无意义的多愁善感。”

En: Sadly, no. It was the foreigners who brought them to their knees. We fought valiantly -- too valiantly. So valiantly we got licked, he adds, squeezing a *boule* in his fist.
Zh: “可惜没有。是外国人让他们屈服的。我们英勇地战斗过——太过英勇。所以我们被英勇地打败了，”他一边补充，一边握紧手中的*大球*。

En:
Should've fought dirty. Like they did with their suicide sex cult propaganda and *mad anarchist women* strapped to shrapnel bombs. We didn't though. And we lacked *calibre*. God bless him, but the suzerain's cannons simply weren't big enough.“	“本来应该用些更卑劣的手段。就像他们那些自杀式的性崇拜宣传，还有把榴弹绑在身上的*疯狂的无政府主义女人*。但我们没有那么做。而且我们缺少*火力*。神明保佑，但是宗主国的大炮根本不够大。”
0x010000070000E032	Dialogue Text	PLAZA / RIVER CRATER	Logic	It was probably a bit more complicated than that.	可能比那个还要更复杂一些。
0x010000070000E049	Dialogue Text	PLAZA / RIVER CRATER	You	Why shell them here in Martinaise?"
Zh: “为什么要朝马丁内斯开火呢？”

En: Because this place is the damn Beachhead, he says, pointing to the bay. "Had to soften the commies up first."
Zh: “因为这里是该死的滩头阵地啊，”他指着海湾说道。“必须先削弱那群康米党员。”

En: The Beachhead?
Zh: “滩头阵地？”

En: Yes, he nods, inspecting you with some disdain. "The military-coordinated amphibious landing to take back Revachol."
Zh: “是的，”他点点头，鄙视地打量着你。“军事协调的两栖登陆收回了瑞瓦肖。”

En: Martinaise was used as one of the three footholds in Revachol during Operation Death Blow in '08. The other two are off in Stella Maris and the Delta. He points to the northeast.
Zh: “在08年的致命打击行动期间，马丁内斯是瑞瓦肖的三个据点之一。另外两个在德尔塔和斯特拉里斯。”他指向东北方。

En: This here is blood ground, where Coalition boots first made landfall and cleaned those rabid dogs out. Most likely, he says, looking down at the soil, "we're playing pétanque on their mangled corpses."
Zh: “这里是一个被鲜血浸染的地方，联盟的铁蹄首先在这里着陆，把那些疯狗都清理掉了。很有可能，”他低头看着脚下的土地，说道，“我们此刻就站在他们残破的尸骸上玩地掷球。”

En: Blood ground... The other one shakes his head. "You got old René going there. Like he isn't angry enough already..."
Zh: “鲜血浸染的大地……”另一个人摇摇头。“你真是让老雷内兴奋起来了。好像他还不够愤怒一样……”

En: Hold on -- the Coalition?
Zh: “等一下——联盟？”

En: Is that why everything is so bombed out?
Zh: “所以这里都被炸毁了？”

En: Ah, that explains all the war damage.
Zh: “啊，这就解释了战争造成的所有伤害。”

En: Mhmh, he grunts. "Don't get me wrong, officer. I hate those foreign dogs. But the enemy of my enemy and all that. They're the lesser evil."
Zh: “嗯，”他咕哝着。“别误会我的意思，警官。我痛恨那些外国狗。但是敌人的敌人那一套什么的。他们反而没那么邪恶。”

En: Actually I already know all this. I just wanted to know if you do too.
Zh: “其实这些我都知道。我只想确认你是不是也知道。”

En: Yes, dammit -- I know! They laid the fire of hell on this city before they stormed it. And it worked, too. There is a strange gleam in his eyes.
Zh: “是的，该死——我知道！他们让地狱之火降临了这座城市，然后朝这里发起猛攻。而且，它奏效了。”他的眼睛里闪烁着一丝奇怪的光芒。

En: The rest of the city got cleaned up, but Martinaise they keep as a *monument*. A dark shadow runs across his face. "And now the Union socialists are practically running the place."
Zh: “城市的其他部分已经清理干净，不过马丁内斯成为了他们的*纪念碑*。”他的脸上掠过一道阴影。“现在，这里基本上归工会那帮工人主义者管辖了。”

En: He approves of this radical approach. Knows it was *necessary*.
Zh: 他赞成这种激进的做法。他知道这是*必要*的。

En: That's how it should be. Soft socialists paving the way for the *hard* working class to take over.
Zh: “理应如此。弱化工人主义者为*硬核*工人阶级的接管铺平了道路。”

En: Foreign powers cleaned up our mess and now they rule us. (Shake your head in shame.)
Zh: “外国势力清理了我们的烂摊子，结果现在成了我们的统治者。”（羞愧地摇摇头。）

En: This Coalition seems quite capable, actually.
Zh: “实际上，联盟似乎很有能力。”

En: Commies just don't understand how money works.
Zh: “康米主义者不理解钱是怎么运作的。”

En: Damn right, son. They laid the fire of hell on this city before they stormed it. And it worked, too. There is a strange gleam in his eyes.
Zh: “该死，你说的没错，孩子。他们让地狱之火降临了这座城市，然后朝这里发起猛攻。而且它奏效了。”他的眼睛里闪烁着一丝奇怪的光芒。

En: They're *not*. And I'm sorry it had to be them. After eight years of fighting those hyenas, boiling cats for food and drinking piss in the mountains... He takes a deep breath.
Zh: “他们*没有*。很抱歉，但是必须是他们。跟那群土狼战斗了8年，把猫煮来吃，在山里喝尿……”他深深地吸了一口气。

En: Instead, all that is just, holy, and beautiful in the world was wiped away and now it's neon signs with toothpaste ads everywhere. Foreign influence peddling garbage and stupid music on the radio. He sighs.
Zh: “相反，世界上所有公正，神圣和美丽的东西全都被抹去了，现在到处只剩下牙膏的霓虹灯广告。外国放债者兜售着他们的垃圾，收音机里播放着愚蠢的音乐。”他叹了口气。

En: I *would* have preferred if the right honourable King Guillaume returned to Revachol or even if that damn clown, Frissel, had risen from the grave and led us. Sadly that was not the case.
Zh: “我*宁愿*纪尧姆国王陛下重返瑞瓦肖，甚至是那个该死的小丑，弗里塞尔从坟墓里爬出来引导我们。可悲的是，事实并非如此。”

En: This *Royal* failure weighs heavily on him.
Zh: *皇室*的失败对他的打击很大。

En: This is just what the commies wanted. This was their plan all along. *This* is what they wanted to replace the rule of the Suzerain with.
Zh: “这就是康米主义者想要的。一直以来，这就是他们的计划。*这*就是他们想要取代宗主国统治的东西。”

En: Who was this Frissel?
Zh: “这个弗里塞尔是谁？”

En: You mentioned Guillaume?
Zh: “你提到了纪尧姆？”

En: Hmm... what exactly is a *suzerain*? (Conclude.)
Zh: “嗯……*宗主国*到底是什么？”（总结。）

En: No, they don't. But I'm sorry it had to be the Coalition. After eight years of fighting rabid commies, boiling cats for food and drinking piss in the mountains... He takes a deep breath.
Zh: “不，他们不懂。很抱歉，但必须是联盟。跟那群土狼战斗了8年，把猫煮来吃，在山里喝尿……”他深深地吸了一口气。
