En: I'm sorry it had to be them. He looks down at his boots. "After eight years of fighting those hyenas, boiling cats for food and drinking piss in the mountains..."
Zh: “很抱歉，但是必须是他们。”他低头看着自己的靴子。“跟那群土狼战斗了8年，把猫煮来吃，在山里喝尿……”

En: Damn Frissel -- he was the king we couldn't protect. The carabineers failed him... and the crown. The old veteran falls silent and massages his chest. "He died in the hands of the *hoi polloi* in a very public execution."
Zh: “该死的弗里塞尔——他是我们没能守护的国王。骑兵们辜负了他……还有皇冠。”老兵陷入沉默，按摩着自己的胸口。“他死在了*民众*的手里，被公开处决了。”

En: A true King in both blood and mind. Led Revachol before Frissel. He would have been better, but the damn commies drove him into exile.
Zh: “不管是血脉还是精神，他都是一个真正的国王。在弗里塞尔之前统领瑞瓦肖的人。他会是更好的选择，不过那些该死的康米主义者把他流放了。”

En: Wait, Joyce said Guillaume ran and his nephew, Frissel, was shot in his place.
Zh: 等等，乔伊斯说纪尧姆跑了，而他的侄子，弗里塞尔是代替他被杀的。

En: Exiled? Joyce said Guillaume ran and his nephew, Frissel, was shot in his place.
Zh: 流放？乔伊斯说纪尧姆跑了，而他的侄子，弗里塞尔是代替他被杀的。

En: Commies didn't drive Guillaume out. He left, when things got bad and left Frissel to take the hit.
Zh: “康米主义者没有驱逐纪尧姆。当局势变坏的时候，他跑了，留下弗里塞尔来承担压力。”

En: This is communard propaganda! Guillaume would never do something like that. He is *LE LION*.
Zh: “这是公社社员的宣传！纪尧姆永远不会做出这样的事。他是*狮子王*。”

En: The Suzerain is the King. Has everyone forgotten already? He then slowly nods and says to himself: "They've forgotten already."
Zh: “宗主就是国王。难道大家都忘了吗？”然后，他慢慢点点头，自言自语到：“他们已经忘了。”

En: It's no use talking to you. You were still in daddy's balls when it happened. When *we* took our last stand against the filth and rode the cavalry straight into gunfire.
Zh: “跟你说是没用的。这一切发生的时候，你还没有出生呢。那时，*我们*还在跟那些人渣进行最后的殊死搏斗，骑着战马直直地冲入枪林弹雨之中。”

En: There is not a shadow of a doubt in his mind that this is the truth.
Zh: 这就是事实，他的心里没有一丝怀疑。

En: Oh, okay.
Zh: “哦，好吧。”

En: What else?
Zh: “还有别的吗？”

En: Because that's what happens when communards hijack your country, execute your supreme leadership, and turn your capital into a slaughterhouse. You use *heavy ordnance* to clean up your home.
Zh: “因为当公社劫持国家的时候，就会发生这种事情，处决最高领导者，将首都变成一个屠宰场。你要使用*重型武器*来清理你的家园。”

En: Maybe we can do this history lesson later.
Zh: “也许我们可以之后再上历史课。”

En: Fine, the carabineer replies sharply. "What do you want now then?"
Zh: “好吧，”老兵厉声回复到。“那你有什么需要？”

En: They should have chosen a place away from people and buildings.
Zh: “他们应该选择一个远离民众和建筑的地方。”

En: This place is the damn Beachhead, son, he says, pointing to the bay. "They had to soften the commies up first."
Zh: “因为这里是该死的滩头阵地啊，孩子，”他指着海湾说道。“必须先软化那些康米党员。”

En: Nod thoughtfully and turn to look northeast.
Zh: 若有所思地点点头，然后看向东北方。

En: Shake your head and look down at the crater.
Zh: 摇摇头，低头看着弹坑。

En: Death Blow... sounds grim.
Zh: “致命打击……听起来很残忍。”

En: Preposterous! Surely you don't mean it. He frowns. "I'm just sorry it had to be them. After eight years of fighting those commie hyenas, boiling cats for food and drinking my piss in the mountains..."
Zh: “荒谬！你肯定不是这个意思。”他皱紧眉头。“我很抱歉，不过必须是他们。跟那群康米党的土狼战斗了8年，把猫煮来吃，在山里喝尿……”

En: How does Filippe the Third factor into this? (Point towards the monument.)
Zh: “菲利普三世对此有什么影响呢？”（指向纪念碑。）

En: He doesn't, the veteran replies sharply. "That was one hundred years ago. Ain't got nothing to do with anything."
Zh: “他没有，”老兵尖锐地回复到。“那是100年前的事了。跟什么事都没什么关系。”

En: You don't even *begin* to truly understand the players on the table, let alone the specific circumstances surrounding the... He stops mid-sentence and turns to you. "What do you think?"
Zh: “你甚至还没*开始*真正理解台面上的玩家，更不用说周围的具体情况了……”他中途停了下来，转向你。“你有什么想法？”

En: Well, it's your own damn fault, the jolly man remarks. "You, we, the Coalition, Revachol -- whoever you wanna blame -- never finished the job. Officially the Party never surrendered. Of course they still hold influence."
Zh: “呃，这是你自己的错，”快乐的男人评论到。“你，我们，联盟，瑞瓦肖——不管你想责怪谁——永远没能完成工作。党派从没正式投降。当然，他们还是有影响力的。”

En: Nothing. I *don't* think. I just *do*.
Zh: “没什么。我*没有*想。我只会*做*。”

En: I'm just so damn sorry it had to be the Coalition... He isn't really paying attention to your words anymore. "After eight years of fighting those hyenas, boiling cats for food and drinking piss in the mountains..."
Zh: “我真的觉得很抱歉，但必须是联盟……”他不再理会你的话语。“跟那群土狼战斗了8年，把猫煮来吃，在山里喝尿……”

En: Something moves in your gut. It's almost like a tug.
Zh: 你感到像是有人重重地扯了一下你的胃。

En: That's our guy! The way he speaks about the war and the bygone days -- Kingsman through and through -- ask him how to turn back time!
Zh: 这才是我们的好伙计！他对战争和过往岁月那滔滔不绝的样子——经历了一切的骑士——问问他如何回到过去！

En: Nuthin' I *can't* do. I'm the job for every man...
Zh: “没什么是我做不到的！我什么工作都会做……”

En: The things I've seen... (hic) and said...
Zh: “我见过的东西……（打嗝）还有说过的话……”

En: I've been kicked out of every joint in Revachol!
Zh: “瑞瓦肖的每一个娱乐场所都把我踢了出来！”

En: C'mon... (hic) the night's still young.
Zh: “拜托……（打嗝）夜晚还长着呢。”

En: That's my song! That's a... nah, 'tis's s-sshit...
Zh: “那是我的歌！那是……不，这是狗屎……”

En: Better mouth your watch 'round me, boy... (hic).
Zh: “在我周围说话小心点，小子……（打嗝）。”

En: M-m-music's my life, man (hic).
Zh: “音-音-音乐就是我的生命，老兄（打嗝）。”

En: Lsss-sen, boy, I don't start trouble, I fix it.
Zh: “听着，小子，我不会主动找麻烦，我会解决它。”

En: Her gloves -- you get the feeling that you need them. You have a dead body to deal with, after all.
Zh: 她的手套——你感觉自己需要它们。毕竟，还有一具尸体等着你处理。

En: Psst! Her gloves -- they'll help you with the autopsy.
Zh: 喂！她的手套——它们能帮你做尸检。

En: She has changed the subject and won't let you get back to this.
Zh: 她改变了话题，不会让你再谈这个了。

En: In the West is the miracle...
Zh: 西边有个奇迹……

En: Thank you. [Leave]
Zh: “谢谢你。”[离开]

En: It's so late and you're still here.
Zh: “这么晚了，你还在这里。”

En: I'm a night hawk, what can I say? She flashes a smile.
Zh: “我是个夜猫子，我能说什么呢？”她露出一丝微笑。

En: Odd...
Zh: 奇怪……

En: There's a little meanness in that smile.
Zh: 那个笑容隐含着一丝刻薄的味道。

En: She's still here, because you're still here.
Zh: 她还在这里，因为你也还在这里。

En: You don't have to stay up because of us.
Zh: “你不用因为我们熬夜的。”

En: I like the city at night. Don't you? She looks into the darkness and smiles again.
Zh: “我喜欢晚上的城市。你不一样吗？”她望着眼前的一片漆黑，又笑了。

En: She's uncomfortable. Maybe you should drop this line of questioning.
Zh: 她不舒服。也许你应该放弃这些问题。

En: The place looks the part. There's graffito everywhere and the traffic jam behind you is completely *unregulated*.
Zh: 这地方看起来就是如此。到处都是涂鸦，你背后的交通堵塞完全*没有管制*。

En: Even all the way over here, there's a drop of death in the spring air.
Zh: 即使一路跑到这里，春风中还有一丝死亡的气息。

En: Pier? Buildings? Apartments? You should *explore*, maybe scale a wall?
Zh: 码头？建筑？公寓？你应该*探索*一下，也许还可以爬墙？

En: She's very well composed. Back straight.
Zh: 她非常镇定，背挺得笔直。

En: Really? *None.* Maybe just a *tiny* bit of annoyance.
Zh: 真的*没有*吗？也许只有*一点点*困扰吧。

En: Evrart confirmed, he sent you to spy on me.
Zh: “艾弗拉特确认过了，他派你来监视我们的。”

En: Evrart confirmed, he sent you to spy on us.
Zh: “艾弗拉特确认过了，他派你来监视我们的。”

En: Is that so? Her face remains expressionless as she shrugs. "I don't know anything about that. Must be a misunderstanding."
Zh: “是这样吗？”她耸耸肩，脸上还是毫无表情。“我毫不知情。肯定是个误会吧。”

En: She's a pro. Nothing short of coming face to face with the fatman himself would make her admit it right now.
Zh: 她是个专家。除非和胖子本人当面对峙，她现在是不会承认的。

En: No. I don't want to say that.
Zh: 不。我不想这么说。

En: Okay. The stimuli end.
Zh: 好吧。刺激结束。

En: What is this *fuck the police* business?
Zh: “这个*搞死警察*是怎么一回事？”

En: Excuse me? She doesn't understand.
Zh: “你说什么？”她没有理解你的意思。

En: This street sign says fuck the police. (Point to it.)
Zh: “这个路标说搞死警察。”（指向它。）

En: Never mind.
Zh: “没事了。”

En: What about the other sign. *Pigs go home*? Who are these pigs?
Zh: “那其他标志呢。*猪头滚回去*？谁是猪头？”

En: It's alright, I didn't mean to startle you.
Zh: “没事的，我不是有意要吓你一跳。”

En: I don't know anything about that either. As I said, I didn't write it.
Zh: “那个我也不知道。我说过了，不是我写的。”

En: *Pig* is a widely-used term for members of the Police. It's not loving.
Zh: *猪头*是一个广泛使用的术语，泛指警局的成员。这可不是个友善的称呼。

En: Even if she didn't write it, she should CLEAN IT.
Zh: 就算不是她写的，她也应该清理干净。

En: You could clean it up if you get a chance. Be a citizen -- be a winner.
Zh: “如果有机会的话，你应该把它清理干净。做一个好市民——做一个胜者。”

En: It's alright, I'm just asking.
Zh: “没事的，我只是问问。”

En: No need to worry, the lieutenant steps in, "we're not saying you did."
Zh: “不用担心，”警督上前一步，“我们没说是你做的。”

En: Okay. She straightens her back.
Zh: “好吧。”她挺直了后背。

En: Okay, she replies, shoulders relaxing.
Zh: “好吧，”她一边回复，一边放松了肩膀。

En: She shifts in her seat awkwardly.
Zh: 她坐在椅子上，笨拙地挪了挪身体。

En: We need directions.
Zh: “我们需要指引。”

En: The police need directions.
Zh: “警察需要指引。”

En: I have to run. [Leave.]
Zh: “我得走了。”[离开。]

En: Can I borrow your gloves? I'm doing an autopsy.
Zh: “能把你的手套借给我吗？我要做验尸。”

En: I've investigated the body. Shouldn't be long till we get it down.
Zh: “我已经调查过尸体了。不久我们就会把他放下来。”

En: Oh. Okay. She doesn't quite know what to say. "Thanks for keeping me in the know, sir."
Zh: “哦。好吧。”她也不知道说什么好。“谢谢你告诉我这件事，先生。”

En: The body is down.
Zh: “尸体下来了。”

En: Down...? The gardener straightens her back. "Do you mean the body is finally gone?"
Zh: “下来……？”园丁挺直后背。“你的意思是尸体总算是不在了？”

En: No, the body is down on the ground.
Zh: “不，尸体在地面上。”

En: Oh... right. She scratches her cheek. "Can you tell me when you've taken him away then?"
Zh: “哦……好吧。”她挠了挠面颊。“那你能告诉我，你打算什么时候把他带走呢？”

En: The body is gone.
Zh: “尸体没了。”

En: Like gone-gone? The girl repeats, her face beaming with appreciation.
Zh: “就像是，一去不复返的那种？”女孩重复到，脸上流露出喜悦的感激之情。

En: No, just gone.
Zh: “不，就是没了。”

En: It's not in the yard anymore. It's in... some other place now.
Zh: “他已经不在院子里了。现在，他在……别的地方。”

En: In heaven? A quick smile. "Thank you, officer. Looks like I'll get mint in the ground by April."
Zh: “在天堂里？”她笑了笑。“谢谢你，警官。看来四月份我就能种上薄荷了。”

En: Gone to heaven? A quick smile. "Thank you, officer. Looks like I'll get mint in the ground by April."
Zh: “去天堂了？”她笑了笑。“谢谢你，警官。看来四月份我就能种上薄荷了。”

En: The corpse -- where is it exactly?
Zh: “尸体——到底在哪里？”

En: I'm looking for a dead body.
Zh: “我在找一具尸体。”

En: Oh that... She points north. "That's right there, in the yard."
Zh: “哦，那个啊……”她指向北边。“就在那边，院子里。”

En: It's gone from the *tree*.
Zh: “他不在*树上*了。”

En: Oh... right. She scratches her cheek. "But not from the yard. I understand."
Zh: “哦……好吧。”她挠了挠面颊。“但不是从院子里。我明白了。”

En: An autopsy? Wow. Sure, keep them. She hands you the rubber gloves. "I have another pair."
Zh: “验尸？哇哦。当然，给你吧。”她把橡胶手套递给你。“我还有一副。”

En: It was clear just an hour ago...
Zh: “一小时之前还是晴天的……”

En: It felt like springtime just a few days ago...
Zh: “几天前感觉还像春天一样……”

En: She realizes she's about to criticize your work -- or lack thereof.
Zh: 她意识到自己打算批评你的工作——或者说是工作的缺失。

En: There's discomfort. She stops mid sentence.
Zh: 有种不安的情绪在里面。她话说道一半就停下来了。

En: Go on, it's okay.
Zh: “继续，没事的。”

En: There's a corpse there, keeping you from doing your work.
Zh: “那里有具尸体，妨碍你工作了。”

En: Yes. She looks relieved. "It smells pretty bad, so I have to take breaks."
Zh: “是的。”她看起来松了一口气。“味道真的很难闻，所以我必须休息一下。”

En: As you already know, there's a corpse there. Hanging from a tree. It smells pretty bad, so I have to take breaks.
Zh: “你已经知道了，那边的树上挂着一具尸体。味道真的很难闻。所以我必须休息一下。”

En: As you *probably* know, there's a corpse hanging from a tree there. It smells pretty bad. So I have to take breaks.
Zh: “你*可能*也知道，那边的树上挂着一具尸体。味道真的很难闻。所以我必须休息一下。”

En: But?
Zh: “但是？”

En: Well... as you already know there's a corpse there, hanging from a tree. It smells pretty bad. So I have to take breaks.
Zh: “好吧……你已经知道了，那边的树上挂着一具尸体。味道真的很难闻。所以我必须休息一下。”

En: Well... as you *probably* know, there's a corpse hanging from a tree there. It smells pretty bad. So I have to take breaks.
Zh: “好吧……你*可能*也知道，那边的树上挂着一具尸体。味道真的很难闻。所以我必须休息一下。”

En: Don't worry, miss. We're here to clean it up -- you can get to work soon.
Zh: “别担心，小姐。我们就是来清理的——你很快就能工作了。”

En: She's black.
Zh: 她是黑人。

En: You're black.
Zh: “你是黑人。”

En: *Should* I say that?
Zh: 我*应该*这么说吗？

En: So what? I'm also black.
Zh: 那又怎么样？我也是黑人。

En: Uhm... are you sure? In the mirror it looked like you were pink, grey and more than a little reddish.
Zh: 呃……你确定？镜子里的你看起来是粉色的，灰色，还有一点点红。

En: What if I told you there's a *mirror* on your bathroom wall, upstairs? You *may* want to take a look.
Zh: 如果我告诉你，楼上你房间浴室的墙上挂着一面*镜子*呢？*也许*你想去看一眼。

En: You *really* need to take a look at that mirror upstairs.
Zh: 你*真的*需要去楼上的镜子里看一眼。

En: I don't know -- what's the problem? You're just *reacting to stimuli*.
Zh: 我不知道——有什么问题吗？你只是*对刺激做出了反应*。

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: So, what kind of music do you listen to nowadays?
Zh: “所以，现在你们都听些什么样的音乐？”

En: Nothing.
Zh: “没什么。”

En: I don't know... She looks around. "Whatever's on the radio?"
Zh: “我不知道……”她四下看了看。“收音机里播什么就听什么？”

En: We should probably proceed with our business in Martinaise. The lieutenant interrupts her, turning to you. "Unless you didn't have any more *official* questions?"
Zh: “也许我们应该继续在马丁内斯的工作。”警督打断了她，转向你。“除非你还有更*官方*的问题？”

En: We should proceed with our business in Martinaise. The lieutenant turns to you.
Zh: 警督转向你。“我们应该继续在马丁内斯的工作。”

En: She doesn't like that *giant* boat. Nor does she like the woman on it.
Zh: 她不喜欢那艘*大*船。也不喜欢船上的那个女人。

En: Yes. A representative of the harbour. She should have set up operations nearby.
Zh: “是的。港口的代表。她应该就在附近行动。”

En: Oh, that must be the woman on the giant boat. She points northwest. "There's one docked on the pier. It arrived a couple of weeks ago. It's very hard to miss."
Zh: “哦，那肯定是大船上的那个女人。”她指向西北方。“码头停着一艘船。几周以前来的。不容易错过的。”

En: Yes. That's the one.
Zh: “是的。就是那个。”

En: You don't like the boat?
Zh: “你不喜欢那条船？”

En: Let it go.
Zh: 随它去吧。

En: I'm okay with it. Was there something else?
Zh: “我没什么意见。还有别的事吗？”

En: She would probably prefer not to go into it. It wasn't easy to pick up on either...
Zh: 她宁愿不去深究。而且捡起来也没那么容易……

En: Rows of stalls under a broad roof, where silvery fish were heaped on newspapers. Water, water everywhere -- pouring from the heavens, in the shadow of the old church.
Zh: 宽阔的屋顶下是一排排的小摊子，报纸上堆满了银灿灿的鱼。海水，到处都是海水——在旧教堂的阴影里，从天堂倾斜而下。

En: What kind of a fish market is this?
Zh: “是什么样的鱼市？”

En: I don't know... the abandoned kind? It used to gather every spring, but there's nothing to do there now. Just drug addicts.
Zh: “我不知道……废弃的那种？以前每到春天都人群聚集，不过现在那边已经没什么可以做的了。只有瘾君子。”

En: Shake it off, you seem to be under some kind of cold spell...
Zh: 摆脱它，你似乎中了某种寒冷魔咒……

En: Anything else? The girl watches you rub your sides for warmth.
Zh: 女孩看着你摩擦身体寻求温暖：“还有别的事吗？”

En: She hides it well, but behind the sweat and dirt there is something... else. In her rigid posture.
Zh: 她隐藏地很好，不过在汗水和泥土背后，还有些……别的东西。在她僵硬的姿势里。

En: There's more to you than that.
Zh: “你远远不止这么简单吧。”

En: Is there?
Zh: “有吗？”

En: The quickness of the reply certainly does not prove you wrong.
Zh: 她回答得这么迅速，说明你的判断肯定没错。

En: Nothing to see here. Just a regular law official reacting to every day stimuli. Nothing noteworthy here.
Zh: 这里没什么好看的。只是个普通的执法者对寻常的刺激做出了反应。没什么值得注意的。

En: Working on what?
Zh: “做什么工作？“

En: I have a greenhouse in the yard there. She gestures over her shoulder.
Zh: “我在院子里有个温室。”她回头看了看。

En: I have a greenhouse in the yard there. She gestures over her shoulder. "I've been trying to get some work done..."
Zh: “我在院子里有个温室。”她回头看了看。“我在尝试完成自己的工作……”

En: It's there. She points north. "In the yard, right through the hole in the fence."
Zh: “就在那里。”她指向北边。“在院子里，穿过围栏上的洞就是了。”

En: She's relieved someone has come for it -- finally.
Zh: 终于有人来处理这件事，让她感到很欣慰。

En: You sound surprised.
Zh: “你听起来很吃惊。”

En: I have some questions for you.
Zh: “我有些问题要问你。”

En: We don't see a lot of police around here, that's all.
Zh: “我们这里警察并不常见，就这样。”

En: Of course. What can I help you with?
Zh: “当然。我能帮你什么吗？”

En: Later. [Leave.]
Zh: “回见。”[离开。]

En: You're back.
Zh: “你回来了。”

En: Who are you exactly?
Zh: “你到底是谁？”

En: She must be very diligent about the glove-use, because her hands are clean and delicate.
Zh: 她的手套用的肯定很勤，因为她的手干净又娇嫩。

En: She must be very diligent about the glove-use, because her hands are clean and delicate.
Zh: 她的手套用的肯定很勤，因为她的手干净又娇嫩。

En: The RCM in Martinaise? The young woman looks up at you. "What can I help you with?"
Zh: “RCM来马丁内斯了？”年轻女子抬头看着你。“有什么我能帮你的吗？”

En: It's raining again... The young woman looks up at the overladen sky.
Zh: “又开始下雨了……”年轻女子抬头仰望沉甸甸的天空。

En: It's raining again... The young woman resting by the wheelbarrow sighs.
Zh: “又开始下雨了……”坐在独轮手推車旁边休息的年轻女子叹了口气。

En: I can't believe it's snowing again... The young woman watches the falling snow and sighs.
Zh: “不敢相信，居然又下雪了……”年轻女子看着飘落的雪花，叹了口气。

En: I can't believe it's snowing again... The young woman resting by the wheelbarrow sighs.
Zh: “不敢相信，居然又下雪了……”坐在独轮手推車旁边休息的年轻女子叹了口气。

En: Cool. And what are you doing here?
Zh: “挺酷的。那你在这里做什么？”

En: I am pleased to meet you too, officer.
Zh: “我也很高兴见到你，警官。”

En: Good to meet you, Just-A-Gardener. Another question then.
Zh: “很高兴见到你，普通的园丁小姐。我还有个问题。”

En: Me? I'm just a gardener.
Zh: “我？我只是个普通的园丁。”

En: Thank you. She smiles politely. "My head is about to explode from all the salts I've had to inhale."
Zh: “谢谢你。”她礼貌地笑了。“吸入这么多刺激物，我的脑袋都快爆炸了。”

En: I'm working.
Zh: “我正在工作。”

En: No, those gloves aren't really my style. [Leave.]
Zh: 不了，这些手套真的不符合我的风格。[离开。]

En: Of course, I won't hold you back. She wipes her brow with the canary yellow glove.
Zh: “当然，我不会妨碍你的。”她用淡黄色的手套擦了擦眉头。

En: If there's a corpse, then you're going to need those gloves for the autopsy.
Zh: 如果真有一具尸体的话，你会需要这些手套来验尸的。

En: One more thing... Can I borrow your gloves?
Zh: “还有一件事……能把你的手套借给我吗？”

En: See you around, officer.
Zh: “回见，警官。”

En: Sure, keep them. I have another pair. She hands you the rubber gloves with no visible annoyance.
Zh: “当然，你留着吧，我还有一副。”她把橡胶手套递给你，没有表现出明显的为难。

En: Sure -- I'm done with it. She takes a small capsule out of her breast pocket and hands it to you.
Zh: “当然可以——我已经用完了。”她从胸前的口袋里拿出一个小胶囊递给你。

En: Do you still have your salts? I think I could use some.
Zh: “你还有盐吗？我想我可能用得着。”

En: My partner told me you may have ammonia -- can I have some?
Zh: “我的搭档跟我说你可能有氨——能给我一些吗？”

En: Hello again, officer. How are things?
Zh: “又见面了，警官。最近怎么样？”

En: Oh? She cranes her neck. "Well, I didn't write it there. I'm just sitting here."
Zh: “哦？”她伸长了脖子。“呃，那不是我写的。我只是坐在这里。”

En: Since the street sign's messed up... She nods. "Okay. What do you need?"
Zh: “既然路标都乱了……”她点点头。“好吧。你有什么需要？”

En: Of course. Where to?
Zh: “当然了。你们要去哪里儿？”

En: Where am I?
Zh: “我在哪？”

En: Yes, sir. District of Martinaise... She looks around, thinking what else to say. "This intersection is called Roundabout North."
Zh: “是的，先生。马丁内斯区……”她四下看了看，想找点别的话说。“这个十字路口叫做环岛北路。”

En: In Martinaise, sir. In the city of Revachol... She looks around, thinking what else to say. "This intersection is called Roundabout North."
Zh: “在马丁内斯，先生。瑞瓦肖城……”她四下看了看，想找点别的话说。“这个十字路口叫做环岛北路。”

En: What is in the East?
Zh: “东边有什么？”

En: Thanks, that's all for now.
Zh: “谢谢，暂时先这些吧。”

En: No problem.
Zh: “没问题。”

En: No problem. She nods, brushing a fleck of soil off her cheek.
Zh: “没问题。”她点点头，拂去了脸颊上的一小撮泥土。

En: The harbour gates. Some kind of commotion, I think? I don't follow the local politics. She shrugs. "A Frittte store too."
Zh: “港口大门。我想，那里有些骚乱？我不太了解地方政治。”她耸耸肩。“还有一家弗利多商店。”

En: What is in the South?
Zh: “南边有什么？”

En: What is in the West?
Zh: “西边有什么？”

En: Some shops and a bridge. The canal bridge leads to the coast, but it's broken, I think? Some kind of accident probably.
Zh: “一些商店，还有一座桥。运河大桥可以通往海岸，不过我想好像是坏了？可能是发生事故了吧。”

En: What is up in the North?
Zh: “北边有什么？”

En: There's the pier, the Capeside apartment buildings. Some more tenements... Not a lot, really.
Zh: “那里是码头，岬岸公寓就在那边。还有一些房子……其实也没有很多，真的。”

En: It's just water... She looks at the water, squinting. "No, actually, I think they call it the Martinaise Inlet. There are some islands in the bay, but they're hard to reach."
Zh: 她眯起眼睛，看着大海。“只是大海吧……不对，事实上，他们好像叫它马丁内斯海湾。海湾边有些小岛，不过很难过去。”

En: What is on the other side of the canal?
Zh: “运河的另一边有什么？”

En: Just coast. There's a little fishing village there... and a fish market. But that got closed down ages ago.
Zh: “只有海岸。那里有座小渔村……还有个鱼市。不过几年前就关了。”

En: Do you know where I could find the representative for Wild Pines?
Zh: “你知道我能在哪里找到野松公司的代表吗？”

En: Wild Pines? You mean the company woman?
Zh: “野松公司？你是说那个公司的女人？”

En: Tell me, what exactly have you been doing in your greenhouse -- in *March*?
Zh: “告诉我，你究竟在温室里做什么——现在可是*三月*？”

En: Do you listen to disco?
Zh: “你听迪斯科乐吗？”

En: She seems more perplexed than threatened.
Zh: 与其说她受到了威胁，倒不如说是不知所措。

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: Uh... I'm gonna say no.
Zh: “呃……我的回答是否。”

En: Sure thing. She narrows her eyes.
Zh: 她眯起眼睛。“当然可以。”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: I'm a bit disoriented. This is Revachol, right?
Zh: “我有点搞不清楚方向。这里是瑞瓦肖，对吗？”

En: I'm... disorientated. I don't know where I am.
Zh: “我……迷失了方向。我不知道自己在哪。”

En: Just tell me where we are, okay?
Zh: “只要告诉我这是哪里就好，行吗？”

En: We're in Martinaise, sir. She looks around. "This intersection is called... Roundabout North, I think."
Zh: “我们在马丁内斯，先生。”她四下看了看。“这个十字路口……叫做环岛北路，我想是吧。”

En: He knows where we are. He just wants directions. The lieutenant seems uncomfortable with the level of disorientation you are displaying.
Zh: “他知道我们在哪。他只是需要些指引。”警督对你表现出来的迷失水平似乎有些不安。

En: Yes. What are you doing here?
Zh: “当然了。你在这里做什么？”

En: Maybe. (Nod.) "Pleased to meet you, Just A Gardener. I have another question."
Zh: “也许吧。”（点点头。）“很高兴见到你，普通的园丁小姐。我还有个问题。”

En: Those gloves would really come in handy for the autopsy.
Zh: 如果要尸检的话，那双手套肯定很好用。

En: Ask her about music -- it'll get the convo flowing, don't worry!
Zh: 问她音乐的问题——这样能让对话流畅地进行下去，别担心！

En: She's not okay with it. It's a display of power and she feels threatened by it. You don't know why.
Zh: 她完全不觉得没问题。这是一种权利的展示，她感觉自己被威胁了。你不知道为什么。

En: You didn't say she wrote it, you asked what it was doing there.
Zh: 你没说是她写的，你问的是那是干嘛的。

En: I didn't say you wrote it.
Zh: “我没说是你写的。”

En: Sir, it was *implied* in your question.
Zh: “先生，你的问题在*暗示*这一点。”

En: The timidness is gone for a second. Something else takes it's place. A readiness.
Zh: 羞怯消失了一秒。别的什么东西占据了它的位置。一种准备就绪的感觉。

En: Okay, well, I didn't, she leans back, shoulders relaxing.
Zh: “好吧，呃，我没有，”她身体后仰，肩膀放松了下来。

En: She's probably not gonna do it.
Zh: 她可能不会这么做。

En: Salts? Ammonium salts? Perhaps useful for later.
Zh: 盐？铵盐？也许以后有用。

En: Go easy on that stuff. It gave me a terrible headache.
Zh: “用的时候一定要有节制。它让我头疼的厉害。”

En: Sure -- I'm done with them. She takes a small capsule out of her breast pocket and hands it to you.
Zh: “当然——我已经用完了。”她从胸口的口袋里掏出一个小胶囊递给了你。

En: Absolutely gone.
Zh: 完全消失了。

En: Can you tell me when you've taken him away then. Because I'm sort of waiting for *that*.
Zh: “那你能告诉我，你什么时候能把他带走吗？因为我算是在等人*这么做*。”

En: Yeah, keep her in the *loop*. It's strangely enjoyable to report back on the minute progress you've made. Even to strangers.
Zh: 是啊，有消息随时*告诉*她。报告你每分钟的进展能带给你一种奇怪的乐趣。即使对陌生人说也一样。

En: No! Do *not* keep this random civilian in any loops.
Zh: 不！*不要*把任何消息随意告诉这个普通民众。

En: No! Do *not* report back to this random civilian about anything.
Zh: 不！*不要*把任何消息回报给这个普通民众。

En: It was sent to processing in the morgue.
Zh: “他被送到停尸房去处理了。”

En: I'm glad to hear that. A quick smile. "Thank you, officer. Looks like I'll get mint in the ground by April."
Zh: “很高兴听到这个消息。”她笑了笑。“谢谢你，警官。看来四月份我就能种上薄荷了。”

En: You get a strange feeling, looking at that smile. It spoils the moment. It is disingenuous.
Zh: 看着那张笑脸，您产生了一种奇怪的感觉。它破坏了这个时刻。它是虚伪的。

En: What's going on here?
Zh: 这里出什么事了？

En: Surely it was nothing, sire. Just paranoia.
Zh: 当然是没什么事了，大人。只是妄想而已。

En: I don't care, I just wanna enjoy the nice moment.
Zh: 我不在乎，我只想享受这美好的时刻。

En: Of course, sire, of course.
Zh: 当然，大人，当然了。

En: Snow has nutrients in it -- helps everything green up in the spring. At least that's what my grandma always told me. She smiles shyly.
Zh: “雪里面有营养素——帮助万物在春天变得绿意盎然。至少我的外婆总是这么说的。”她害羞地笑了。

En: Well uh... She points to her wheelbarrow, surprised at the question. "This might be the last snow we get. At least I hope so..."
Zh: “这个嘛……”她指向自己的独轮手推車，显然是被你的问题吓到了。“这可能是最后一场雪了。至少我希望如此……”

En: Yes, think about the cute grandma, not the weird snow.
Zh: 是的，想想可爱的外婆，而不是这场诡异的雪。

En: The grandma is a bit too much.
Zh: “外婆说得有点过了。”

En: A bit much? What are you talking about? That's what my grandma told me, okay?
Zh: “有点过了？你在说什么呢？外婆就是这么跟我说的，好吗？”

En: She feels interrogated now. It's hard to say if she's lying.
Zh: 现在她感觉自己被审问了。很难说她到底是不是在撒谎。

En: Relax, miss. This is not an interrogation. We are just checking some facts.
Zh: “放松点，小姐。这不是一场审讯。我们只是在核实一些事实。”

En: Squint your eyes and look at her intently.
Zh: 眯起眼睛，聚精会神地看着她。

En: Disingenuous grandma.
Zh: 不诚实的外婆。

En: Thinking she's black.
Zh: 想到她是个黑人。

En: Thinking about disco.
Zh: 想到了迪斯科。

En: Stop looking at her, look around! What do you see?
Zh: 别看她了，看看周围！你看见了什么？

En: All you can see is the fact that her skin is a different colour from yours. That's literally all. You're gonna have to run with that.
Zh: 你唯一能看见的，只有她的肤色跟你的不同这个事实。基本上这就是全部。你必须接受这一点。

En: All you can see is the fact that her skin is a different colour from yours. That's literally all. You're gonna have to run with that.
Zh: 你唯一能看见的，只有她的肤色跟你的不同这个事实。基本上这就是全部。你必须接受这一点。

En: All you can see is the fact that her skin is a different colour from yours. That's literally all. You're gonna have to run with that.
Zh: 你唯一能看见的，只有她的肤色跟你的不同这个事实。基本上这就是全部。你必须接受这一点。

En: Okay then. Snow it is.
Zh: “那好吧。就是雪了。”

En: Mhmh. She looks at her wheelbarrow, where the snow is slowly melting.
Zh: “嗯。”她看着自己的独轮手推車，那里的雪正在慢慢溶化。

En: What nutrients?
Zh: “什么营养素？”

En: Nitrogen and sulphur mostly. She points to the sky. "And whatever factories and aerostatics exhale too I guess."
Zh: “主要是氮和硫磺。”她指向天空。“我猜，还有工厂和静风舰排放出来的东西吧。”

En: The traffic jam?
Zh: 塞車？

En: You're black.
Zh: “你是黑人。”

En: Why am I thinking this, I said I'm black as fuck.
Zh: 我想这个干什么，我说过我他妈的黑人。

En: Are you entirely sure you're black as *fuck*? You should really check out that mirror thing. For now, go with she's black. It's important to the case.
Zh: 你完全完全确定自己*真他妈*是黑人吗？你真的应该找面镜子好好看看。眼下，就接受她是黑人的事实。这对案子很重要。

En: No, you don't. Just back up, cancel this.
Zh: 不，不可以。马上退出，取消。

En: Yeah, I'm not gonna say that.
Zh: 是啊，我不会这么说的。

En: Better to get off before it starts. Nothing left to gain here.
Zh: 最好在开始之前就下来。这样什么也得不到的。

En: Ask me a normal question or leave me alone.
Zh: “要么问我个正常点的问题，要么就别来烦我。”

En: An entrance to the yard?
Zh: 院子的入口？

En: The plaza?
Zh: 广场？

En: That's right. And the canal, the bookstore, the harbour gates... This is a great vantage point -- for keeping an eye on *you*.
Zh: 没错。还有运河，书店，港口大门……这是个非常有利的地点——可以密切关注*你*。

En: Are you by any chance keeping an eye on me?
Zh: “你不会碰巧是在监视我吧？”

En: Are you by any chance keeping an eye on us?
Zh: “你不会碰巧是在监视我们吧？”

En: Noted, but I'll keep it to myself.
Zh: 注意到了，不过我不会说出去。

En: No, of course not! I don't understand what this is about... She looks towards the yard and her expression clears.
Zh: “没有，当然不是！我不明白这是在干什么……”她看向院子，表情明朗了很多。

En: The kid did this, right? The red-haired rat? Can't say a sentence without *f****t* or *kipt*... He's always giving me trouble.
Zh: “是那个小孩做的，对吗？红头发那个？每句话里肯定带着*他妈的*或者*吉皮特*的字眼……他一直在给我惹麻烦。”

En: I was talking to him, yes.
Zh: “我跟他聊过了，是的。”

En: That's not important right now.
Zh: “现在这不重要。”

En: Maybe you shouldn't be. I mean... you do your job, but that kid is beyond help. And he certainly won't help *you*. She shakes her head.
Zh: “也许你不应该这么做。我的意思是……你做好自己的工作就行，那孩子已经无药可救了。而且他肯定不会帮*你*的。”她摇摇头。

En: It is, because he sent you to harass me, didn't he? You shouldn't listen to him. She points to the defaced sign above her on the corner. "It was probably him, who wrote that."
Zh: “是的，因为是他让你来骚扰我的，对吗？你不该听他的话。”她指向角落里那个损坏的标志。“那个可能就是他写的。”

En: It is, because he sent you to harass me. He's playing you. She shakes her head. "He hates the police, okay? A lot of people do around here."
Zh: “是的，因为是他让你来骚扰我的？他在玩弄你。”她摇摇头。“他恨警察，好吗？这里很多人都一样。”

En: You've been resting here for quite a *while*, haven't you? The lieutenant intervenes, looking at her intently.
Zh: “你在这里休息*好一阵子*了，对吧？”警督插了进来，一心一意地看着她。

En: Yes. I'm tired.
Zh: “是的。我累了。”

En: I understand -- the RCM isn't welcome here and the locals want to keep an eye on us.
Zh: “我能理解——RCM在这里不受欢迎，本地人想要关注我们的举动。”

En: There's silence. The smallest of smiles.
Zh: 沉默无言。还有最浅的微笑。

En: That's okay, miss -- do what you have to do. The lieutenant nods at you. "I think we're done here. Let's go."
Zh: “没事的，小姐——做你该做的事。”警督朝你点点头。“我想这里已经差不多了。我们走吧。”

En: Glad to have been of service. Now you know -- the locals are keeping tabs on you.
Zh: 很高兴为你服务。现在你知道了——本地人在监视你。

En: Yes, I'm black, how many times are you gonna say that?
Zh: “是的，我是黑人，你到底还要说多少遍？”

En: That was the last time, miss. He looks at you with a stern expression. "Our questioning here is concluded. Let's go."
Zh: “这是最后一次，小姐。”他看着你，表情很严肃。“这里的询问已经结束了。我们走吧。”

En: He didn't think you were about to bust a case.
Zh: 他不觉得你是想破案。

En: What does that mean? She doesn't flinch.
Zh: “那是什么意思？”她没有退缩。

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: So, what kind of music do you listen to nowadays?
Zh: “所以，现在你们都听些什么样的音乐？”

En: We should proceed with our business in Martinaise. The lieutenant turns to you.
Zh: 警督转向你。“我们应该继续在马丁内斯的工作。”

En: Nothing.
Zh: “没什么。”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: I don't know... She looks around. "Whatever's on the radio I guess. What does this have to do with snow, or gardening in March?"
Zh: “我不知道……”她四下看了看。“我猜收音机里播什么就听什么吧。但是这跟雪，或者在三月做园艺工作有什么关系吗？”

En: We should probably proceed with our business in Martinaise. The lieutenant interrupts her, turning to you. "Unless you didn't have any more *official* questions?"
Zh: “也许我们应该继续在马丁内斯的工作。”警督打断了她，转向你。“除非你还有更*官方*的问题？”

En: Uh... I'm gonna say no.
Zh: “呃……我的回答是否。”

En: Do you listen to disco? I need some fresh jams to get out of this rut.
Zh: “你听迪斯科乐吗？我需要一些新鲜玩意来摆脱这种冲动。”

En: Do you listen to disco?
Zh: “你听迪斯科乐吗？”

En: She seems more perplexed than cornered.
Zh: 与其说她被逼入绝境，倒不如说是不知所措。

En: Sure thing. She narrows her eyes.
Zh: 她眯起双眼。“当然。”

En: If you don't have anything normal to say I had something I needed to think about.
Zh: “如果你没有什么正经事要说的话，我有些事情需要考虑。”

En: Nothing, this is just something my brain told me.
Zh: “没什么，只是我的大脑这么告诉我的。”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: What are you waiting for, ask about the disco already. You need some fresh jams to get out of this rut.
Zh: 你还在等什么，问问迪斯科的事啊。你需要一些新鲜玩意来摆脱这种冲动。

En: You see a set of tyre tracks in the brown slush that covers the plaza mosaic.
Zh: 你看见一组沾着棕色烂泥的轮胎印，覆盖在广场的地砖上。

En: Not now. [Leave.]
Zh: 现在不行。[离开。]

En: The hole in this fence doesn't look *that* huge.
Zh: 围栏上的洞看起来并没有*那么*大。

En: Reconstruct the movement.
Zh: 重现运动轨迹。

En: Torque dork!
Zh: 扭矩呆子！

En: Found sunken motor carriage.
Zh: 找到沉没的汽車。

En: No, these tracks are not interesting at all. Let the street sweeper just sweep them away.
Zh: 不，这些痕迹一点意思也没有。就让清洁工把它们全部清扫掉吧。

En: The tracks are as they have ever been. A bit more worn, perhaps. Fortunately, the street sweeper still hasn't noticed their presence.
Zh: 这些痕迹还是原来的样子。也许更陈旧了。幸运的是，清洁工还没有注意到它们的存在。

En: ...and then accelerated straight into the fence. Left a hole *big enough for the Franconigerian cavalry,* according to the cafeteria manager.
Zh: ……然后加速冲向围栏。根据餐厅经理说的，留下一个*大到弗兰考格斯骑兵都能穿过去*的洞。

En: ...and then accelerated straight into the fence. Left a hole *big enough for the Franconigerian cavalry,* according to the cafeteria manager. (Why that pang of guilt again?)
Zh: ……然后加速冲向围栏。根据餐厅经理说的，留下一个*大到弗兰考格斯骑兵都能穿过去*的洞。（为什么又会有那种负罪感？）

En: ...and then accelerated straight into the fence.
Zh: ……然后加速冲向围栏。

En: I think I got it. [Leave.]
Zh: 我想我明白了。[离开。]

En: What kind of vehicle drove through here?
Zh: 到底是什么样的車从这里冲过去了？

En: Hard to say.
Zh: 很难说。

En: The tyre tracks were left here by an unknown event that took place some days ago. It's a message, written in the language of burnt rubber.
Zh: 几天前，一起未知事故留下了这串轮胎印。这是一条用烧焦的橡胶写下的信息。

En: Some of that rubber stuck to the tiles right in front of the Whirling-in-Rags. This is point A. The driver started there...
Zh: 有些橡胶粘在褴褛飞旋正前方的瓷砖上。这里是A点。司机就是从那里出发的……

En: The driver proceeded to back out of the yard, barely stopping before hitting the adjacent building...
Zh: 司机从院子里倒了出去，但他没有刹住车，撞到了隔壁的大楼……

En: ...before heading south. Must have been in a hurry.
Zh: ……然后朝南边开去。肯定很匆忙。

En: This is significant somehow.
Zh: 不知道为什么，这个感觉很重要。

En: Maybe. Probably.
Zh: 也许吧。可能吧。

En: Why am I looking at this?
Zh: 我为什么要看这个？

En: Cop habit. You look at everything.
Zh: 警察的习惯。你什么都看。

En: This isn't case-related, you think.
Zh: 这跟案子没有联系，你想到。

En: You could *follow* the tracks south. There seems to be a canal there. See where they went. (If you find the time.)
Zh: 你可以*跟踪*痕迹往南走。那边似乎有一条运河。看看它们会通向什么地方。（如果有时间的话。）

En: As a matter of fact, you don't even know why you stopped here. You would never drive recklessly like this. And you don't have a car.
Zh: 事实上，你都不知道自己为什么会在这里停下来。你永远也不会这么肆无忌惮地开車。更何况你也没車。

En: Is this connected to the case?
Zh: “这跟案子有关吗？”

En: I'm not sure... there are plenty of traffic accidents waiting to happen in Martinaise. With the jam right here on the roundabout. I would keep them separate.
Zh: “我不确定……马丁内斯有不少交通事。环岛那里现在还在大塞車。我不认为这跟吊人的案子有什么联系。”

En: The hole is not that impressive.
Zh: 这个洞也没那么让人印象深刻。

En: A car drove through the fence.
Zh: “一辆車穿破了围栏。”

En: You are correct. This is a rather motor carriage-friendly city.
Zh: “你说得对。这是一座对汽車特别友好的城市。”

En: An ominous warning of bad things ahead.
Zh: 一种不祥的预兆：前面有不好的事情发生了。

En: This is the point of departure of the motor carriage that crashed into the Samaran Butter sign, right?
Zh: 那辆撞倒萨马拉黄油广告牌的汽車，就是从这里出发的，对吧？

En: Why yes it is! Good connection.
Zh: 当然是！这是个很好的联想。

En: This is where I started off with my motor carriage.
Zh: 我就是从这里开車出发的。

En: What happened next?
Zh: 接下来发生了什么？

En: Hard to say. Your vision is blurred and you are having difficulty concentrating thanks to your relentless hangover.
Zh: 很难说。因为宿醉的缘故，你的视线很模糊，很难集中精神。

En: Okay. What happened next?
Zh: 好吧。接下来发生了什么？

En: Yes. And ended up half-sunken in the sea. You must have been drunk when you left off.
Zh: 是的。最后半沉没在海中。你离开的时候一定是喝醉了。

En: Damn bartender.
Zh: 该死的酒保。

En: He was exaggerating. People blame *cops* for everything that goes wrong in the world. This has nothing to do with you.
Zh: 他夸大其词了。大家会把世界上一切的坏事都归咎到*警察*身上。这跟你一点关系也没有。

En: This is where I started off with my motor carriage... before sinking it in the sea.
Zh: “我就是从这里开車出发的……在把車开进海里之前。”

En: Yes, it is. Well, he gestures toward the gaping hole in the fence, "you did provide us with a very convenient access point to the crime scene."
Zh: “是的，没错。不过，”他朝着围栏上的破洞比了个手势，“你的确给我们提供了一个通往犯罪现场的捷径。”

En: This is the point of departure of the vehicle that crashed into the Samaran Butter sign.
Zh: “那辆撞倒萨马拉黄油广告牌的汽車，就是从这里出发的。”

En: Well, he gestures toward the gaping hole in the fence, "you did provide us with a very convenient access point to the crime scene."
Zh: “好吧，”他朝着围栏上的破洞比了个手势，“你的确给我们提供了一个通往犯罪现场的捷径。”

En: No wonder the cafeteria manager seemed frustrated when he was giving us directions to the yard.
Zh: “难怪餐厅经理给我们指示通往院子的方向时，模样似乎有些沮丧。”

En: The lieutenant follows the tracks with his gaze. "That's plausible. A similar... reckless abandon seems to be in evidence."
Zh: 警督的目光追随着轮胎的痕迹。“似乎有些道理。一种近似……显而易见的无节制放纵。”

En: Somehow that makes you feel... scared. You don't know why.
Zh: 这莫名让你感觉有些……害怕。你不知道为什么。

En: The fuckin' piss-shitter...
Zh: “该死的王八蛋……”

En: Y'r ded to me, y'hear y'r DEAD!
Zh: “你他妈已经死了，听见没，你死定了！”

En: T's shit ain't nothing to me...
Zh: “这对我来说根本不算什么……”

En: I's hard you know... It's j'st so hard sometimes... (hic).
Zh: “真的很难，你知道吗……有时候真是太难了……（打嗝）。”

En: I'm scum, just a scummy ol' scum...
Zh: “我是人渣，人渣中的人渣……”

En: I work hard, I play hard... *Nobody* works harder!
Zh: “我工作很努力，我玩得很疯狂……*没人*比我工作更努力！”

En: All I got I built with my bare hands...
Zh: “我得到的一切，都是我用两只手建造的……”

En: They don't know me... no one *knows* me... (hic).
Zh: “他们不懂我……没人*懂*我……（打嗝）。”

En: Y'gotta problem with me?
Zh: “你要跟我过不去吗？”

En: Sod off ye pisser!
Zh: “滚开，你这个撒尿包！”

En: Y'gotta problem with me too?
Zh: “你也要跟我过不去吗？”

En: I'm so sorry, me missus!
Zh: “对不起，夫人！”

En: I ain't got shit...
Zh: “我没搞砸……”

En: I'm a scum, just a scummy ol' scum...
Zh: “我是人渣，人渣中的人渣……”

En: MY MISSUS! She be better without me...
Zh: “夫人！没有我，她会更好……”

En: I don't deserve 'er, I don't deserve anybody...
Zh: “我配不上她，我配不上任何人……”

En: I make my own way, t's all. Always have, always will.
Zh: “我要走出自己的路，就是这样。总是这样，一直这样。”

En: Hmm-hmm-hmm. You see a sturdy woman humming to herself. She seems to be browsing books.
Zh: “嗯。”你看见一个壮实的女性正在喃喃自语。她似乎在浏览书本。

En: The ship takes off like a silvery mosquito... unfolding its rotor blade arms... She mumbles reading. Then puts down the softcover.
Zh: “船像一只银色的蚊子起飞了……展开了转子叶片的臂膀……”她小声嘟囔着。然后放下了平装书。

En: If she's such a *working class* woman, why isn't she working?
Zh: 如果她是一个*女工人*的话，为什么没有在工作呢？

En: Have a good day. [Leave.]
Zh: “祝你度过愉快的一天。”[离开。]

En: This was a tremendously useful interlude.
Zh: “这是一个非常有用的小插曲。”

En: What are you doing?
Zh: “你在做什么？”

En: Looking for something to read. She reverts her attention to a worn out paperback.
Zh: “找点可以看的东西。”她把注意力转回到一本破旧的平装书上。

En: Annette's a sweet sprout, but she doesn't know anything about marriage. Why am I even talking about this...
Zh: “安妮特是个小甜心，不过她对婚姻一窍不通。我为什么要说这个啊……”

En: He's totally missing.
Zh: 他绝对是失踪了。

En: Where is this going, officer?
Zh: “你这是想干嘛，警官？”

En: Right, got it, thanks.
Zh: “好吧，明白了，谢谢。”

En: She gives you a short nod and shifts her attention back to books.
Zh: 她稍微朝你点了点头，然后又把注意力转回到了书本上。

En: Suit yourself then. I don't really care if your husband is missing.
Zh: “那就随你便吧，我真不在乎你丈夫是不是失踪了。”

En: She has though. The husband is totally lost. You should tell her that it's okay.
Zh: 她丈夫绝对是失踪了。你应该告诉她没关系的。

En: Maybe you´re right. Maybe he isn't missing indeed.
Zh: “也许你是对的。也许他真的没有失踪。”

En: Of course he's not. It's not like he's a pocket watch. I wouldn't just lose him.
Zh: “他当然没有。他不是怀表什么的。我不会把他弄丢的。”

En: Let go.
Zh: 放手吧。

En: Nanny. Where are they?
Zh: “保姆。她们在哪？”

En: You know where they are. They're at home. *Smoking*. Giving the ladder of vices a chance.
Zh: 你知道她们在哪。她们在家。*抽烟*。给罪恶阶梯一个机会吧。

En: No, they're at a disco. Getting down. Stupid disco dancing teenagers.
Zh: 不，她们在迪斯科舞厅。堕落。跳迪斯科的愚蠢青少年。

En: What? That's just -- my daughters are perfectly *fine*. They're with their friends down in Jamrock! There's *nothing* to worry about.
Zh: “什么？她们只是——我的女儿*好的很*。她们只是跟朋友在加姆洛克玩！根本*没什么*好担心的。”

En: They're almost grown up now anyway. They're past the age they need me protecting them from everything now.
Zh: “她们现在也差不多是成年人了。她们已经过了需要我事事保护的年纪。”

En: Did someone say... party? You could use a party. Hunt it down!
Zh: 好像有人说……派对？这个很可以。把它找出来！

En: Maybe you're right. Maybe they aren't missing after all. Tell me something else...
Zh: “也许你是对的。也许她们真的没有失踪。再跟我说说别的……”

En: My youngest girl, Jolie, is just shy of sixteen. Jennie, she is turning eighteen next month. But we shouldn't even be talking about them!
Zh: “我最小的女儿，朱莉，是个害羞的16岁少女。珍妮下个月就满18岁。不过，我们根本就不该提到她们的事吧！”

En: She's lying. She's god damn lying, she has smokes!
Zh: 她在撒谎。她很会撒谎，她有烟！

En: Just one, I beg you. Just one. GIVE ME THE DAMN CIGARETTE, MA'AM!
Zh: “只要一根就行，我求你了。就一根。该死的，快把烟给我，夫人！”

En: I *know* you're lying, please just give me a cigarette.
Zh: “我*知道*你在撒谎，请给我一支烟就好。”

En: The situation is oddly intimate -- your cheek is pressed against her shoulder, her hand is trapped between your chest. Five seconds pass and their passing seems so unimaginably long. Then she coughs and says again:
Zh: 眼下的状况有种古怪的亲密感——你的脸颊贴在她的肩膀上，她的两只胳膊困在你的胸膛之前。五秒钟过去了，时间长的简直让人难以想象。然后她咳了几声，说道：

En: Keep hugging her.
Zh: 继续拥抱她。

En: Let go.
Zh: 放开。

En: She steps back and returns her attention to books, pretending this hug didn't happen.
Zh: 她退后一步，把注意力转回到书本上，假装这个拥抱没有发生过。

En: Uh... what are you doing, officer?
Zh: “呃……你在做什么，警官？”

En: The feeling continues. Warm and comfy on the cold shoreline. You hear a question rising to the surface, trying to break the sensation.
Zh: 这种感觉还在继续。在冰冷的海岸线上，它是多么的温暖和舒心。你听见一个问题浮出水面，试着打破这种感动。

En: Let it form.
Zh: 让它成形。

En: Silence your thoughts. I don't want to answer anything.
Zh: 让你的想法沉默。我不想回答任何问题。

En: DON'T COME BACK HERE!
Zh: 别想起来！

En: OH MY GOD, WHY ARE YOU CALLING ME? STOP CALLING ME! ARE YOU DRUNK AGAIN?
Zh: 我的天啦，你为什么要打给我？别再打给我了！你又喝醉了吗？

En: Khm, officer. A coarse female voice pulls you out of your reverie. "What are you doing?" A swash of water hits the coast behind her figure.
Zh: “咳咳，警官。”一阵沙哑的女声将你从幻象中拉了出来。“你在做什么？”在她身后，翻腾的海水拍打着海岸。

En: Let's talk about your husband again.
Zh: “我们再聊聊你丈夫的事吧。”

En: Well, I found him nevertheless. I'm Dick Mullen.
Zh: “尽管如此，我还是找到他了。我就是迪克·马伦。”

En: Well, I found him nevertheless. I'm that good.
Zh: “尽管如此，我还是找到他了。我就是那么棒。”

En: Wait! Did she just imply that you're *fat*? You're not fat! The body type she's referring to is called a *Franconigerian hard-body*.
Zh: 等等！她刚才是不是暗示你很*胖*？你不胖！我确信她提到的这种体型叫做*弗兰考格斯时代的健美体格*。

En: I even tried throwing it away once, but he just dug it out of the bin. Can you believe it? She looks back at you, shaking her head.
Zh: “有一次，我甚至试过把它扔掉，不过他又从垃圾堆里翻出来了。你能相信吗？”她看着你，摇摇头。

En: She genuinely wants you to, now. Don't make her ask.
Zh: 她真心希望你去，就是现在。别逼她问出来。

En: Yes, I will bring him back home to you. (Take the case.)
Zh: “是的，我会把他带回家的。”（接下案子。）

En: Sorry, we've got our hands full at the moment. Maybe some time later. (Don't take the case.)
Zh: “抱歉，我们现在有点忙。也许之后再说吧。”（不要接下案子。）

En: Our hammer toting comrade was at the bottom of those stairs.
Zh: “那位拿锤子的同志就站在梯子底下。”

En: What *with*? A lot of things! For example, people tend to go *missing*.
Zh: 为*什么*？很多东西啊！比如说，大家会有*失踪*的倾向。

En: This citizen thinks she can do without your assistance? Don't buy it. They all need help.
Zh: 这位市民觉得她不需要你的协助？别相信。他们都需要帮助。

En: Help her by... carrying things? Uhm... maybe she needs a weightlifter? Maybe she needs you to *fight* her husband? No, that's not it...
Zh: 帮她……搬东西？嗯……也许她需要一个举重运动员？也许她需要你跟她的丈夫*打一架*？不，不对……

En: But he *is*... you can feel it. Or maybe it's something else then!
Zh: 但他*就是*……你能感觉到。不然，那也可能是别的事情！

En: Okay! Maybe it's something else then.
Zh: 好吧！也许是别的事情。

En: What is this *doubt* you have then? Something *has* happened here.
Zh: 那你还有什么*疑问*呢？这里*确实*发生了一些事情。

En: Okay. It must be asked then -- what *are* you doing here? Why are you pursuing this? Is it a *hunch*?
Zh: 好吧。那就一定要问问——*你*在这里做什么？你为什么要追逐这个？这是一种*直觉*吗？

En: For what it's worth -- I agree. But cockatoos can't be stopped when they get like this. It's better to indulge him at this point.
Zh: “不管怎么说——我同意。但是凤头鹦鹉变成这样的时候，谁也无法阻止。此时此刻，还是满足他比较好。”

En: Great. Got it.
Zh: “很好。我懂了。”

En: Wonderful.
Zh: “太好了。”

En: It's gotta be something else then. Why else would you be asking her if it wasn't?
Zh: 那肯定是别的事情。如果不是，那你为什么还要问她呢？

En: Kids are always going missing. Weak, pathetic kids. Always getting themselves killed.
Zh: 小孩总是会失踪。脆弱，可怜的小孩。总是会把自己害死。

En: Hah! Blam! Knew it.
Zh: 哈！看见没！我就知道。

En: But you told me your husband's all right.
Zh: “但是你说自己的丈夫没事。”

En: There. She's worried now.
Zh: 看。现在她担心了。

En: You're wrong there. Incompleteness is the essence of mystery.
Zh: 你弄错了。不完整才是神秘的精华。

En: Who cares about the cold when you have your *cool jacket* to wear? You can completely sympathize.
Zh: 如果有*酷炫的夹克*穿上身，谁还在乎冷不冷啊？你完全支持。

En: She's thinking about him out in the cold -- in some park or on the coast -- and it's making her more and more worried.
Zh: 她想到他待在寒风中——在某个公园里，或者是海岸边——这让她越来越担心了。

En: Gone for around 36 hours then? Damn, this *is* a missing persons case.
Zh: 那就是已经离开36小时了？该死，这*还真是*一起人口失踪案。

En: I will. Of course, officer. As I said, it's probably nothing. The woman takes the slip of paper. It's a phone number.
Zh: 女人接过它。那是一个电话号码。“我会的。当然了，警官。就像我刚才说的，可能没什么事。”

En: Forget it. It's too personal.
Zh: “算了吧。这样太私人了。”

En: Some things *are* officer. She nods. Her cheeks are red from the cold.
Zh: “有些东西*是的*，警官。”她点点头。她的脸颊被冻得通动。

En: No, you're not. Don't think that... she says, slowly shaking her head. The wind brings a strand of hair to her rough face. "You're the cockatoo who offered to find my stupid husband. Let's leave it at that."
Zh: “不，你没有。不要那样想……”她慢慢摇着头说道。风把一缕头发吹到了她粗糙的脸庞上。“你是提出要帮忙找我那个蠢丈夫的凤头鹦鹉。这个话题到此为止吧。”

En: She doesn't want you to be the fuckupatoo.
Zh: 她不想你做个操蛋凤头鹦鹉。

En: No, you're not. Don't think that way... she says, slowly shaking her head. The wind brings a strand of hair to her rough face. "You're just a regular talkative cockatoo and let's leave it at that, okay?"
Zh: “不，你没有。不要那样想……”她慢慢摇着头说道。风把一缕头发吹到了她粗糙的脸庞上。“你只是一只健谈的普通凤头鹦鹉。这个话题到此为止吧，好吗？”

En: The wind brings a strand of hair to her rough face. "I have a strange cop offering to find my stupid husband. What a day..."
Zh: 风把一缕头发吹到了她粗糙的脸庞上。“你是一个奇怪的警官，主动提出要帮我寻找那个蠢丈夫。今天真特别……”

En: Hmh? She adjusts her scarf, cheeks rough and ruddy from the wind.
Zh: “哈？”她调整了一下围巾，脸颊被风吹的粗糙而泛红。

En: She tries to maintain a brave front, even though her eyes reveal the opposite.
Zh: 她试着想要保持勇敢的表象，尽管眼睛里流露出了完全相反的信息。

En: It is clear from her voice that she is shattered.
Zh: 从她的声音里明显可以感觉到心烦意乱。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: I'm a policeman.
Zh: “我是一个警察。”

En: Oh... There's a strange sensation as the woman strengthens her grip -- she's hugging you back.
Zh: “哦……”女人加重了手劲——她在回应你的拥抱，你产生了一种奇怪的感觉。

En: Right, uh... She pats you on your back, before gently letting go.
Zh: “好吧，呃……”她拍拍你的后背，然后慢慢松开手。

En: Alright, got it, thanks.
Zh: “好吧，明白了，谢谢。”

En: Mhmh. She returns to her book.
Zh: “嗯。”她又回去看书了。

En: I don't want to talk about it.
Zh: “我不想说这个。”

En: Good. She lets go of your hug.
Zh: “很好。”她放开了你的怀抱。

En: And can you describe me their appearance? Any features that stand out, something to make identifying a little easier?
Zh: “你能跟我描述一下她们的外貌吗？有没有什么突出的特征，能让辨认更容易的细节？”

En: Why do you need to know this? Haven't I repeatedly told you that they are not missing? That they're in Jamrock, safe and well, at some stupid party.
Zh: “你为什么要知道这些？我不是一再告诉你她们没有走丢吗？她们在加姆洛克，安全得很，正在参加什么愚蠢的派对。”

En: I could do with a party.. A *killer* party, not a lame-o one!
Zh: “派对我可以的。一个*杀手*派对，而不是无聊透顶的派对！”

En: It's for the investigation. I'm trying to be professional.
Zh: “为了调查。我想表现地更专业一点。”

En: A 'killer' party? What is it with you and *pulp* staples? My god. Please, no more talk about my daughters. They are fine! She picks up a book and tries to concentrate. A flock of seabirds passes by.
Zh: “‘杀手’派对？你到底怎么回事，还有这*粗制滥造*的订书钉。我的天啦。请不要再提我的女儿了。她们很好！”她拿起一本书，试着集中精神。一群海鸟从头上飞过。

En: Report what? He's just out, drinking with his friends... I'm sure the police has better things to do than to chase down local goofballs.
Zh: “报什么案？他只是出去了，跟朋友喝酒……相比追查本地的傻瓜，我肯定警察还有更好的事可以做吧。”

En: Ma'am, just to be completely clear -- do you want to report it to the police?
Zh: “夫人，先明确一点——你想要向警方报案吗？”

En: There is no *investigation* here, I can tell you that! She picks up a book and tries to concentrate. A flock of seabirds passes by.
Zh: “我可以告诉你，这里没什么好*调查*的！”她拿起一本书，试着集中精神。一群海鸟从头上飞过。

En: I wouldn't be so sure, he replies, before turning to face the working class woman.
Zh: “要我就不会那么肯定，”他回复到，然后转向了那个女工人。

En: No, please. I don't really want to remember.
Zh: 不，拜托了。我真不想记起来。

En: Honestly, not that different from you. She eyes you from head to toe.
Zh: 她从头到脚打量了你一番：“老实说，跟你没什么区别。”

En: What does your husband look like?
Zh: “你丈夫看起来什么样？”

En: Are you a police man or a nanny? She's definitely disturbed by now.
Zh: “你是警察还是保姆？”她现在肯定很心烦。

En: So you *are* going to look for him?
Zh: “所以*你*打算去找他吗？”

En: I think I got it, thanks. (Proceed.)
Zh: “我想我明白了，谢谢。”（继续。）

En: No, I'm the funeral cockatoo.
Zh: “不，我是葬礼凤头鹦鹉。”

En: I don't mean to disrespect, sir, but *you* are being a bit of a cockatoo here.
Zh: “我无意冒犯，先生，但是*你*有点像只凤头鹦鹉。”

En: Right around the corner. He has pissed himself.
Zh: “就在角落里。他尿湿裤子了。”

En: Protect and serve, madam! I found your husband.
Zh: “保护市民，服务群众，夫人！我找到你的丈夫了。”

En: There. (Point to the working class drunk down near the sea.)
Zh: “那边。”（指向海边醉醺醺的那个工人。）

En: Mhm. You're welcome. She reverts her attention back to the book stand.
Zh: “嗯。不客气。”她的注意力又回到了书摊上。

En: Very well, then. Where is he? She's getting impatient, her hands now picking on a random book cover.
Zh: “那很好啊。他在哪？”现在她有些不耐烦，手随意地翻弄着一本书的封面。

En: And I specifically added that I didn't *need* to know where he was.
Zh: “而且我还专门补充过，我不*需要*知道他在哪。”

En: No, I'm the bang-bang cockatoo.
Zh: “不，我是砰砰凤头鹦鹉。”

En: Maybe your husband is missing?
Zh: “也许你的丈夫失踪了？”

En: Maybe your children are missing?
Zh: “也许你的孩子失踪了？”

En: Watch her browse books.
Zh: 看着她浏览书籍。

En: Thanks. I'll get going then. [Leave.]
Zh: “谢谢。那我走了。”[离开。]

En: That's all for the moment. I'll let you read. [Leave.]
Zh: “暂时先这样吧。我不打扰你看书了。”[离开。]

En: Yes, but... She looks around and takes a deep breath, a little annoyed. "I don't really need to know where my husband is. Not all the time."
Zh: “是的，但是……”她看了看周围，深吸一口气，有些烦闷。“我真不想知道自己的丈夫在哪。不是一直都想。”

En: But you said you didn't know where he was.
Zh: “但是你说过你不知道他在哪。”

En: Hush, baby.
Zh: “嘘，安静点，宝贝。”

En: Right, thanks.
Zh: “好吧，谢谢。”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: Maybe your *cockatoo* is missing?
Zh: “也许你的*凤头鹦鹉*不见了？”

En: Okay. So where are they?
Zh: “好吧。那她们在哪？”

En: No. Absolutely not. Her words come out quick as gunshots.
Zh: “不。绝对没有。”她的话像子弹一样飞快地蹦了出来。

En: Hello. (No ceremonies. Just hello.)
Zh: “你好。”（没有客套。只是打招呼。）

En: Hello. She nods, her attention fully focused on reading.
Zh: “你好。”她点点头，注意力完全集中在看书上。

En: Who are you?
Zh: “你是谁？”

En: Shouldn't a working class woman be working?
Zh: “女工人难道不应该工作吗？”

En: [Leave her be.]
Zh: [不要去打扰她。]

En: Me? She looks up briefly. "No one, I'm just a working class woman."
Zh: “我？”她略微抬头看了一眼。“谁也不是，我就是个女工人。”

En: No, I'm the Major Majestic cockatoo.
Zh: “不，我是威严凤头鹦鹉。”

En: That's one way to put it, yes. No offence. I'd also add that he's a little bit chubby.
Zh: “这是一种说法，没错。无意冒犯。我还说过他有一点胖。”

En: Not all the time. Right now I'm browsing books. She pauses. "Even a working class woman needs something to read."
Zh: “不会一直如此。现在我在看书。”她停顿了一下。“就算是女工人也需要看书的。”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: No, I'm the fuckupatoo.
Zh: “不，我是操蛋凤头鹦鹉。”

En: I'm a policeman.
Zh: “我是一个警察。”

En: I know you are.
Zh: “我知道你是。”

En: What with? She tries hard to focus on the book stand.
Zh: “为什么？”她努力把注意力集中在书摊上。

En: Everyone can see that. The rectangles. He points to his sleeve.
Zh: “大家都能看出来。那个矩形。”他指着自己的袖子说道。

En: Do you need the help of a policeman?
Zh: “你需要警察的帮助吗？”

En: Good then.
Zh: “那很好啊。”

En: Mhm. She reverts her attention to the soggy books on the book stand.
Zh: “嗯。”她又把注意力转回到了那些沉闷的书本上。

En: You're right, let's not talk about your daughters. I don't know what got into me.
Zh: “你说得对，我们还是不谈你的女儿了。我不知道自己是怎么了。”

En: Excuse me?! She blinks. "I don't follow."
Zh: “你说什么？！”她眨眨眼。“我没明白。”

En: Intimacy? It's a catastrophe waiting in the wings.
Zh: 亲密行为？那是一场即将到来的大灾难。

En: Hey, I hug whomever I please! I'm a hug monster.
Zh: “喂，我愿意抱谁就抱谁！我是个拥抱怪兽。”

En: I am fighting for the working class.
Zh: “我为了工人阶级战斗。”

En: So where could he be?
Zh: “那他可能在哪？”

En: My husband? No, he's not.
Zh: “我丈夫？不，他没有。”

En: I don't know, at home now? Out drinking with his friends? Working?
Zh: “我不知道，现在也许到家了？跟朋友在外面喝酒？在工作？”

En: Is there anything else I can help you with?
Zh: “还有什么我能帮你的吗？”

En: So what I'm hearing is: you don't really *know* where your husband is.
Zh: “所以我听到的是：你其实不想*知道*自己的丈夫在哪。”

En: Okay, thanks.
Zh: “好吧，谢谢。”

En: I wonder how long has it been since you last hugged someone?
Zh: 我在想，距离你上次拥抱别人已经过去多久了？

En: It's okay. We all feel out of sorts at times. She starts patting your back. "There you go, officer."
Zh: “没事的。我猜有时候我们都会感觉不太高兴。”她开始拍打你的后背。“好了，警官。”

En: The what now?!
Zh: “现在又是什么？！”

En: I had the strangest... I'm not even sure.
Zh: “我有种最奇怪的……连我自己都不太确定。”

En: God damn it, I already told you -- my husband isn't missing! She crosses her arms.
Zh: 她抱起双臂：“该死，我已经告诉过你了——我的丈夫没有失踪！”

En: So tell me, how does it feel -- to be close to someone, skin to skin?
Zh: 所以告诉我，感觉怎么样——这么接近一个人，肌肤相亲？

En: Do we have a problem with *police*?
Zh: “你对*警察*有意见吗？”

En: Police? I don't follow. She narrows her eyes, trying to understand.
Zh: “警察？我没明白。”她眯起眼睛，尝试去理解。

En: The fuckupatoo. It's the one who always fucks everything up.
Zh: “操蛋凤头鹦鹉。就是那种总是把一切都搞砸的人。”

En: No. But I am an officer of the law and you have just insulted my work uniform.
Zh: “不。不过我是一名执法人员，你刚才辱骂了我的工作制服。”

En: Oh, thank god, no! She nearly begins to laugh. "It hasn't come down to *this* yet."
Zh: “哦，谢天谢地，没有！”她几乎笑出了声。“还没到*这种*地步呢。”

En: So let me guess... he's *disco*?
Zh: “让我猜猜……他很*迪斯科*吗？”

En: Hold on! Did she just laugh at your disco pants? You're not going to leave it there, are you?
Zh: 等一下！她刚才是不是嘲笑你的迪斯科裤子了？你不会就这么算了吧？

En: I see. What else?
Zh: “我明白了。还有什么？”

En: No, no! Her eyes widen at your misunderstanding. "He is definitely not a cop. I meant that he's a little chubby, that's all."
Zh: “不，不是！”她因为你的误解而瞪大了双眼。“他肯定不是警察。我的意思是他有点胖，仅此而已。”

En: Right -- we're dealing with a cop.
Zh: “好吧——我们要对付的是一个警察。”

En: What problem?
Zh: “什么意见？”

En: Technically speaking, your trousers could be considered part of your work gear. This means she has insulted *the police*.
Zh: 从技术角度来讲，你的裤子可以被认作是你工作服的一部分。这就意味着她刚才辱骂*警察*了。

En: I don't know, the most talkative one? She's ready to put that question aside.
Zh: “我不知道，话最多的那个？”她准备把那个问题放在一边。

En: Wow, ma'am! Do we have a *problem* here?
Zh: “哇哦，夫人！你这个有什么*意见*吗？”

En: I'm not *chubby* and neither is your husband. What you meant to say is that we both share a *Franconigerian hard-body*.
Zh: “我没那么*胖*，你的丈夫也没有。你的意思是，我们俩都有*弗兰考格斯时代的健美体格*。”

En: Like a late-stage alcoholic then.
Zh: “那就像是晚期酒鬼一样吧。”

En: Do we have a problem with *disco*?
Zh: “你对*迪斯科*有什么意见吗？”

En: No, no... she backs off, "my relationship with disco remains irrefutably healthy."
Zh: “不，没有……”她退后一步，“我跟迪斯科之间的关系很健康，这一点毋庸置疑。”

En: Oh, no! That's not what I meant! She takes a step back.
Zh: “哦，没有！我不是这个意思！”她退后一步。

En: Keep your eyes closed for a brief longer.
Zh: 再闭一会眼睛。

En: You had the strangest what? She hasn't let go of your embrace.
Zh: “奇怪的什么？”她没有放开你的拥抱。

En: Do I remember?
Zh: 我还记得吗？

En: Less talk, more feeling. Keep your eyes closed, soak in the closeness. See what the feeling entails.
Zh: 少说点话，多去感受。闭上眼睛，沉浸在这份亲密里。看看这种感觉中蕴含着什么深意。

En: It all comes together now. I was built to be close with other beings.
Zh: 现在全都明白了。我生来就是为了与其他存在亲近。

En: She seems to be confused. Your hands are wrapped around her polyester coat. The fabric feels cold, moist from the sea nearby.
Zh: 她似乎有些困惑。你的双手环抱在她的涤纶大衣上。因为附近的海风，布料感觉冰冷又潮湿。

En: The woman looks baffled by your personal revelation. "You truly are one *strange* cop, aren't you?" she says, slowly shaking her head.
Zh: 女人对你的个人启示有些困惑。“你真是个*奇怪*的警察，不是吗？”她一边说，一边慢慢摇头。

En: Mhm, I see. Thanks. She tries to read again.
Zh: “嗯，我明白了。谢谢。”她尝试继续看书。

En: The Major Majestic cockatoo. It's the most impressive one.
Zh: “威严凤头鹦鹉。最让人印象深刻的那种。”

En: Okay, great. Just making sure.
Zh: “好吧，太好了。我只是为了确认一下。”

En: The funeral cockatoo. It's the most depressing one.
Zh: “葬礼凤头鹦鹉。最让人印象深刻的那种。”

En: The bang-bang cockatoo. It's the most common one.
Zh: “砰砰凤头鹦鹉。最普通的那种。”

En: What if something awful has happened... what if they're in the sewers.
Zh: “如果发生了什么可怕的事情……如果她们在下水道呢。”

En: They're not missing, sir.
Zh: “她们没有失踪，先生。”

En: An array of neurons fires up with joy. Bum her a cigarette -- lest it turn to pain.
Zh: 一系列神经元因为愉悦兴奋了起来。向她讨一支烟——以免它变成痛苦。

En: It's the kind of place where everyone does.
Zh: “这里是那种大家都会这么做的地方。”

En: From the kiosk? There's one near the harbour, it's a Frittte. You can look it up.
Zh: “小卖部里有？港口附近有一个，弗利多连锁店。你可以去看看。”

En: Do you know where I could get a new pack?
Zh: “你知道我能去哪里弄包新的吗？”

En: You're right, we do smoke worse than chimneys here.
Zh: “你说的没错，我们抽烟抽的比这里的烟囱还凶。”

En: Why do you think that I smoke? She looks up, slightly startled.
Zh: 她抬头仰望，稍微有些吃惊。“你为什么会觉得我抽烟？”

En: Sorry, officer? She stares at you in disbelief.
Zh: “抱歉，警官？”她怀疑地盯着你看。

En: Just gimme your cigarettes, okay? Don't lie.
Zh: “只要把烟给我就行，好吗？不要撒谎。”

En: I already told you -- I don't have any. Go bother someone else.
Zh: “我已经跟你说过了——我没有。你去烦别人吧。”

En: It's not like yours look much better, she says while eyeing yours.
Zh: “也不是说你的看起来会更好一些，”她看着你，说道。

En: Your hands look like they belong to a heavy smoker.
Zh: “你的手看起来就像烟鬼的手。”

En: I suspect I may possess supra-natural abilities.
Zh: “我怀疑自己可能拥有某种超能力。”

En: No, you don't. She puts her hands into her coat pockets.
Zh: “不，你没有。”她把手揣进了大衣口袋。

En: I know for a fact that you smoke.
Zh: “我肯定你抽烟。”

En: Her hands move over the book covers. The tips of her fingers look rough, stained with yellow. It seems like she has spent a lot of time at work, smoking.
Zh: 她的手在书本封面移动。指尖看起来很粗糙，被染上了黄色。似乎她花了很多时间工作，抽烟。

En: Okay, thanks, no problem. (Move on.)
Zh: “好吧，谢谢，没问题。”（继续。）

En: She sighs.
Zh: 她叹了一口气。

En: Wouldn't you like to?
Zh: “难道不想吗？”

En: No. She looks you straight into the eye. Her right foot is tapping nervously.
Zh: “不。”她直直地看着你的眼睛。右脚紧张地拍打着地面。

En: I can totally help you find your missing husband.
Zh: “我完全可以帮你找到失踪的丈夫。”

En: Why are we still talking about this? I haven't lost my husband.
Zh: “我们为什么还在说这个？我的丈夫没有失踪。”

En: It's all right to not know where your husband is. Nothing shameful in that.
Zh: “不知道你丈夫在哪也没关系。没什么好羞愧的。”

En: Who said anything about shame? Stop talking down to me. She puts her foot down. "My husband is not missing."
Zh: “谁说我羞愧了？别再用高人一等的口气跟我说话。”她放下脚。“我的丈夫没有失踪。”

En: When did you last see him?
Zh: “你最后一次见到他是什么时候？”

En: Yesterday morning. He went to the library. Her eyes become cold with recollection. "He went to retrieve my book and he promised, he *promised* he'd walk straight back home."
Zh: “昨天早晨。他去了图书馆。”她的眼睛因为回忆变得冰冷起来。“他去帮我还书，而且他还答应，他*答应*会直接回家的。”

En: Why not?
Zh: “为什么不呢？”

En: Because people either show up on their own or you'll never find them, he says with a flash of teeth.
Zh: “因为人们要么自己出现，要么你永远也找不到他们，”他说道。

En: I'd say there's nothing mysterious about incompleteness. It's in fact rather unpleasant.
Zh: “要我说这种不完整没什么神秘的。事实上挺让人不愉快的。”

En: You both share *what*? She doesn't understand.
Zh: “你们都有*什么*？”她没明白。

En: *Franconigerian hard-body*. Softly round, yet still in shape. Ladies dig it.
Zh: “*弗兰考格斯时代的健美体格*。柔和的圆润，不过外形依然良好。女士们喜欢这种。”

En: I'm not sure about the rest, but softly round sounds about right. He's not in great shape. She stops to think.
Zh: “我对其他部分不太确定，不过柔和的圆润听起来是对的。他的体型不是特别好。”她停下来想了想。

En: Hmm-hmm-hmm... She's still searching for a book, her eyes wandering over the colourful grid of softcovers.
Zh: “嗯……”她还在搜寻一本书，眼睛在色彩斑斓的封面上流连。

En: Another comrade! Years of labour are pressing down on her shoulders. She deserves a hug -- for all the work she's done for mankind.
Zh: 又是一位同伴！多年的辛苦劳作压在了她的肩头。她应该得到一个拥抱——为了她为人类所做的一切。

En: Give her a hug.
Zh: 给她一个拥抱。

En: Thanks. I feel better already.
Zh: “谢谢。我已经感觉好多了。”

En: I'm sorry, I don't really understand what you're talking about. She lets go of your hug.
Zh: “很抱歉，我真的不明白你在说什么。”她放开了你的怀抱。

En: Fighting for it how? She hasn't let go of your embrace yet.
Zh: “怎么个战斗法？”她还没有放开你的怀抱。

En: A hug monster? How do these two go hand-in-hand? She hasn't let go of your embrace yet.
Zh: “一个拥抱野兽？这两者是如何相辅相成的呢？”她还没有放开你的怀抱。

En: She's still browsing books.
Zh: 她还在看书。

En: I police whatever I want. Where are they?
Zh: “我想怎么管就怎么管。她们在哪？”

En: This little girl over there told me you have a bad husband.
Zh: “那边的小姑娘告诉我说你有个坏丈夫。”

En: Excuse me, a *bad husband?* Her back straightens. "What do you mean?"
Zh: “请原谅，*坏丈夫*？”她挺直了后背。“你这话是什么意思？”

En: Not a nice and helpful sort.
Zh: “不好，也没什么用场的那种。”

En: Do *police men* just go around repeating what some kid off the street said?
Zh: “*警察*就只会重复街上的小孩说的话吗？”

En: Those were her words, not mine. I'm merely asking.
Zh: “那是她说的，不是我。我只是问了一嘴。”

En: So you don't have a bad husband?
Zh: “所以你有个坏丈夫吗？”

En: No, I don't. I have a good husband, the kind and helpful sort. And he's *not* missing.
Zh: “不，我没有。我有个好丈夫，心地善良又乐于助人。而且，他*没有*失踪。”

En: She isn't just some 'kid off the street'. She seems very mature for her age.
Zh: “她不是什么‘街上的孩子’。对于她的年纪来说，她很成熟。”

En: Is she now? She dries her hands in the apron underneath her coat. Her eyes narrow.
Zh: “是吗？”她用大衣下面的围裙擦干双手，眯起了眼睛。

En: Can I give you some friendly advice, mister policeman? Don't listen to kids.
Zh: “我能给你提些友好的建议吗，警官先生？别听孩子的话。”

En: Hmm-hmm-hmm... She's still browsing books.
Zh: “嗯……”她还在看书。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: She tries to concentrate on books, but her eyes are full of bewilderment.
Zh: 她试着把注意力集中在书上，不过眼睛却写满困惑。

En: She doesn't mean to be antagonistic, but she can't help coming off defensive.
Zh: 她不是有意要跟你对抗，但她就是不能放下防备。

En: Hug-by-hug.
Zh: “拥抱。”

En: One hug at a time.
Zh: “一次只拥抱一下。”

En: A hug a day keeps the bourgeoisie away.
Zh: “每天一拥抱，资产阶级远离我。”

En: I guess it's better than nothing. Keep fighting on, my fellow comrade. Then she breaks up the hug, her cheeks flushed.
Zh: “我猜这样总比没有的好吧。继续战斗吧，我的同胞！”说完，她松开了你的怀抱，脸涨的通红。

En: But isn't *that* mysterious?
Zh: “但*那样*不是很神秘吗？”

En: Well, I still want one. I want a missing person's case!
Zh: “呃，我还是想要。我想要一个人口失踪案！”

En: Okay, I don't want it. Let's move on. (Don't take the case.)
Zh: “好吧，我不想要。我们继续吧。”（不要接下案子。）

En: Great, let's do it.
Zh: “太棒了，那就走吧。”

En: All right then... He sighs. "He has *questions* now."
Zh: “那好吧……”他叹了口气。“现在他有些*问题*。”

En: I went and read up on them, just like you said. And I even decided on what kind of a cockatoo I am. Want to know?
Zh: “就像你说的，我仔细研究了一下。甚至还决定了自己到底是哪种凤头鹦鹉。你想知道吗？”

En: They are at home, right? Smoking cigarettes.
Zh: “她们在家，对吗？在抽烟。”

En: I know where they are. At the disco, dancing nasty.
Zh: “我知道她们在哪。在迪斯科舞厅，捎首弄姿地跳舞。”

En: Take a look at your hands.
Zh: 看看你的手。

En: She is right -- your hands look even worse than hers, with tiny cuts and gushes covering your skin like a spiderweb. Your fingertips have become an ugly shade of brown.
Zh: 她说的没错——你的手甚至比她的还糟糕，皮肤上布满蜘蛛网般的小切口和伤疤。指尖已经变成了难看的棕色。

En: Just give me a cigarette please.
Zh: “请给我一支烟。”

En: I can't give you what I don't have. She turns away from you, focusing on the books again.
Zh: “我没有怎么给你。”她转过身去，又集中到书上去了。

En: No, I'm not lying. Why should I lie? A pause. "Please go to the Frittte kiosk."
Zh: “不，我没撒谎。我为什么要撒谎？”她停顿了一下。“请去弗利多杂货店看看吧。”

En: You focus on the hug and for a moment everything feels serene. You hear her heart beating -- or perhaps it's your own.
Zh: 你集中在拥抱上，有那么一瞬间，一切都感觉那么的平静。你听见她的心跳声——或者是你自己的。

En: It's good, but I'd prefer a man. A solid muscularity would support me better.
Zh: 这样很好，但我更愿意找个男人。结实又发达的肌肉应该能更好的支撑住我。

En: Muscles, I said muscles! I want manly-manly muscles.
Zh: 肌肉，我说肌肉！我想要强壮又结实的肌肉。

En: You were eavesdropping on your subconsciousness.
Zh: 你在窃听自己的潜意识。

En: PLEASE OPEN YOUR EYES!
Zh: 请睁开双眼！

En: Keep them closed.
Zh: 继续闭着眼。

En: A strangest conversation. Something went wrong in my head.
Zh: “一次奇怪的对话。我的脑袋里面好像出了点问题。”

En: A glitch of a sort, I think. Inside my mind.
Zh: “我想，是某种小故障吧。在我的脑子里。”

En: I DON'T WANT TO SEE YOU. I DON'T WANT TO SEE YOU EVER AGAIN! STOP CALLING ME! GET LOST FROM MY LIFE, YOU RUINED IT!
Zh: 我不想看见你。我永远也不想再见到你！别再打给我了！从我的生活中消失吧，你毁了它！

En: Something begged me to open my eyes. It was scary.
Zh: “有什么东西祈求我睁开眼睛。很可怕。”

En: The lieutenant looks worried. "We can take a break if you're feeling unwell," he says to you under his breath.
Zh: 警督看起来有些担心。“如果你感觉不太好，我们可以休息一下，”他低声对你说道。

En: SHE'S LYING, LYING, LYING!
Zh: 她在撒谎，撒谎，撒谎！

En: You'd lie too, wouldn't you? You'd lie and steal and cheat! Ask her again, squeeze the truth out of her. Keep pressing. Ask, ask, *ask*!
Zh: 你也会撒谎，不是吗？你撒谎，偷东西，欺骗！再问问她，逼她说出真相。继续施压。问，快问，快*问*啊！

En: I'm afraid the danger is now greater than ever. Tell me, how old are they?
Zh: “恐怕现在的危险比以前遇到的都要大。告诉我，她们多大了？”

En: You step in and close your arms around this foreign body, wandering astray in touch. There's a small movement beneath your hands, as you shut your eyes. Then a tiny voice breaks out and asks:
Zh: 你靠近一步，环抱住这个外来物，迷失在这种接触之中。你闭上双眼，感觉手下有一个小小的动作。接着，一个微弱的声音蹦了出来，问到：

En: What is happening?
Zh: “出什么事了？”

En: Today the makeshift consolations: the shared cigarette, the masculine jokes. Today the embrace before hurting.
Zh: 今天的临时安慰：分享香烟，男性之间的玩笑。今天，在受伤之前的拥抱。

En: It's still happening. I'm in delirium.
Zh: “它还在继续。我精神错乱了。”

En: She's getting upset. Her voice has risen, as she tries to convince herself that her daughters are safe.
Zh: 她变得心烦意乱。她提高了声音，试着说服自己的女儿们安全无恙。

En: What the fuck was that?
Zh: 那又是什么鬼？

En: Open your eyes!
Zh: 睁开你的眼睛！

En: These are the hands on someone who's smoked for at least twenty years, as evidenced by the discolouration of fingers. Marks on your skin may point to past quarrels.
Zh: 这双手至少拥有20年的吸烟史，从变色的手指就能看出来。皮肤上的痕迹可能指向了过去的争吵。

En: The lieutenant sighs.
Zh: 警督叹了口气。

En: I found a working class drunk and I thought he might be yours.
Zh: “我发现一个醉酒的工人，我觉得他可能是你丈夫。”

En: Right, 'cause working class women come with alcoholic husbands. She glances over your shoulder towards the drunk. "You know what?"
Zh: “好吧，因为女工人肯定有一个醉鬼丈夫。”她看向你身后的醉鬼。“你知道吗？”

En: Remember, about that *what* -- never-ever say *what*.
Zh: 记住，关于那个*什么*——永远也不要说*什么*。

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: Something bad I guess? I'm sorry, I say stupid things sometimes, I didn't mean to annoy.
Zh: “我猜不是什么好事？很抱歉，有时候我是会说些蠢话，但我不是有意要惹恼你的。”

En: I'm not saying 'what'.
Zh: “我是不会说‘什么’的。”

En: Because we talked about this. We talked about not wandering off again! She scoffs. "I don't know what to do! I honestly don't know what to do with his addiction... It just makes me feel weak."
Zh: “因为我们聊过这个问题。我们说好不要再到处乱跑！”她嘲弄到。“我不知道该怎么做！说实在的，我不知道该怎么处理他的酒瘾……这样只会让我感觉很无力。”

En: What does anything I said have to do with police? Or is *Police* some kind of disco phenomenon as well?
Zh: “我的话跟警察有什么关系吗？还是*警察*也是某种迪斯科现象？”

En: Yes, I make disco become a police thing -- and you have just insulted my style.
Zh: “是的，我把迪斯科变成了一种警察的东西——而你刚刚侮辱了我的风格。”

En: There's something else hiding in her voice though -- a trace of worry.
Zh: 不过，她的声音里还隐藏着其他什么东西——那是担忧的痕迹。

En: I haven't seen him for... Well, to hell with him! She has completely forgotten about her books, staring blank into the distance instead.
Zh: “我没见到他已经有……算了，让他见鬼去吧！”她完全忘了眼前的书，茫然地凝望着远方。

En: Yes. Right. A wave of despair sweeps over her face. "He wasn't missing anyway," she tries to say while struggling to maintain a courageous front, "I'm sure he'll be home."
Zh: “是的，没错。”她的脸上闪过一丝绝望。“反正他也没失踪，”她挣扎着想要保持勇敢的表象，“我肯定他会回家的。”

En: Do you have any money?
Zh: “你有钱吗？”

En: Money? She looks up to you, a confused smile crossing her lips. "Does the working class ever have any money?"
Zh: “钱？”她抬头看了你一眼，嘴角露出困惑的笑容。“工人阶级什么时候有钱过？”

En: I wasn't thinking much, maybe like a few cents or so?
Zh: “我没想要太多，也许有个几分钱之类的？”

En: You're right, I should find myself a *capitalist*!
Zh: “你说得对，我应该给自己找个*资本家*！”

En: No, sorry, she puts her hands into her coat pockets, as if cold from wind. "I don't have anything to spare."
Zh: “不，抱歉，”她把手放进大衣口袋，似乎被风吹得有些冷。“我没有多余的了。”

En: So your *money* is *missing*?
Zh: “所以你的*钱*也*失踪*了吗？”

En: Sad. I'll bum somebody else then.
Zh: “可悲啊。那我就去求别人好了。”

En: No! No-no-no. I'm not missing my money. End of story. She returns her attention back to the book stand.
Zh: “不！没有。我的钱没丢。就是这么回事。”她把注意力又转回到了书摊上。

En: Mhmh. Exactly. Go ask for an investment. Her words are clipped, but not unamused. Her eyes turn back to the book stand.
Zh: “嗯。没错。去找人给你投资吧。”她的话很简短，但是没有不悦。她的眼睛又回到了书摊上。

En: She starts coughing. It's definitely a smoker's cough.
Zh: 她开始咳嗽。肯定是烟鬼的那种咳嗽。

En: That cough you had. It's a smoker's cough.
Zh: “你刚才那声咳嗽，是烟鬼才会有的那种。”

En: Her eyes shimmer over yours. "You're a keen observer," she remarks.
Zh: 她的目光有些闪烁。“你是个很敏锐的观察者，”她评论到。

En: No, you don't. And it's better that way.
Zh: 不，你不记得。而且这样更好。

En: What *do* I remember?
Zh: 我*能*记得什么？

En: But I *need* to remember.
Zh: 但是我*需要*记起来。

En: Nothing.
Zh: 什么都不记得。

En: Well, if you say it's better...
Zh: 好吧，如果你说这样更好的话……

En: No thoughts to come back to, no scenes to replay.. It feels calm the way deep sleep is supposed to be.
Zh: 没有思绪可以回想，没有场景可以重放……深度睡眠就应该感觉如此平静。

En: Phenomenal.
Zh: “一眼就能看出来。”

En: It is.
Zh: “是的。”

En: Do you smoke?
Zh: “你抽烟吗？”

En: No, I don't.
Zh: “不，我不抽。”

En: They don't. I just forced them this way. That's why I am a monster.
Zh: “它们没有。是我逼着它们这样做的。所以我是一只野兽。”

En: Well, you're not actually entitled to another person's body. Hugging can be considered a rather monstrous thing to do.
Zh: “呃，实际上你无权享有其他人的身体。拥抱可以被认为是一件相当荒谬的事。”

En: You were right that I'm a cockatoo.
Zh: “你说得对，我是一只凤头鹦鹉。”

En: Excuse me?
Zh: “对不起？”

En: You were right. I do have an alcoholic husband. Although not that one.
Zh: “你说得对。我的确有个酒鬼丈夫。不过不是那个。”

En: But didn't you say your husband was kind and helpful?
Zh: “你不是说自己的丈夫心地善良又乐于助人吗？”

En: So is he missing as well?
Zh: “所以他也失踪了？”

En: I did and he is. She takes a step back. "He's also an alcoholic."
Zh: “我是说过，而且他的确没事。”她退后了一步。“他也是一个酒鬼。”

En: No, he's not. Or maybe he is, I don't know. He's probably in the park, or in Jamrock somewhere. Drinking with his *friends*. She looks away.
Zh: “不，他没有。或者，也许他有，我不知道。他可能在公园里，在加姆洛克，或者别的什么地方。跟他的*朋友*一起喝酒。”她移开了目光。

En: Right. What about him? Her face becomes anxious again.
Zh: “好吧。他怎么了？”她的脸又变得忧虑起来。

En: What else... He was wearing a dark brown leather jacket with a bright blue inner lining. The lining is hand-sewn, I made it myself. She sighs, her voice slightly quivering when she adds:
Zh: “还有什么……他穿着一件带亮蓝色内衬的深棕色皮夹克。内衬是手工的，我自己缝的。”

En: I'm sure he will too. When he does, would you let Precinct 57 -- Kim Kitsuragi -- know. He gives her a slip of paper.
Zh: “我肯定他会的。如果他这么做了，请通知57分局——金·曷城。”他递给她一张纸条。

En: Thank you! You sense gratitude in her voice. "Please, do... Even though I'm sure he will return home by himself. I'm still sure of that."
Zh: “谢谢你！”你感觉到她声音里的感激。“拜托了……不过我肯定他会自己回家的。这一点我还是很确信的。”

En: The woman before you nods and returns to her reading.
Zh: 站在你面前的女人点点头，又继续回去看书了。

En: Hmm-hmm-hmm... She's still mumbling to herself, now reading another paperback.
Zh: “嗯……”她还在喃喃自语，又换了一本平装书。

En: Hmm-hmm-hmm... She's still browsing books.
Zh: “嗯……”她还在看书。

En: It's okay.
Zh: “没事的。”

En: She turns away from you in an attempt to recover.
Zh: 她转过身，试着让情绪平复下来。

En: No, I was talking to myself. I should hush. I'm a baby.
Zh: “不，我在跟自己说话。我应该安静下来。我是个宝宝。”

En: No, I was talking to myself. I should stop. It's okay.
Zh: “不，我在跟自己说话。我应该停下来。没事的。”

En: What do you mean I'm being a cockatoo?
Zh: “你说我有点像凤头鹦鹉是什么意思？”

En: Ma'am, I was asking about your cockatoo. Is it missing?
Zh: “夫人，我在问你的凤头鹦鹉。它失踪了吗？”

En: I don't even have a cockatoo. And guess what?
Zh: “我根本就没有凤头鹦鹉。你猜怎么着？”

En: It's a trap. Never ever say *what*.
Zh: 这是个陷阱。永远也不要说*什么*。

En: Even if I had, it wouldn't be missing.
Zh: “就算我有，它也不会失踪的。”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: Alright, cockatoo not missing. I just wanted to make sure.
Zh: “好吧，凤头鹦鹉没有失踪。我只想确认一下。”

En: Great. She turns her attention back to the bookstand.
Zh: “太棒了。”她又把注意力转回到了书摊上。

En: Just one more question: what did you mean by me being a cockatoo?
Zh: “还有一个问题：你说我像凤头鹦鹉是什么意思？”

En: Nothing. Go read up on them if you're so interested. There's a great book in the bookstore.
Zh: “没什么。如果你这么感兴趣的话，那就去看看相关的书吧。书店里有一本很棒的书。”

En: Maybe you should... What if the cockatoo is your *astral captain*? Or your *heraldic bird*?
Zh: 也许你应该这么做……如果凤头鹦鹉是你的*星际舰长*呢？或者是你的*纹章鸟*？

En: Maybe you should check that cockatoo book again... What if the cockatoo is your *heraldic bird*?
Zh: 也许你应该再去看看那本凤头鹦鹉的书……如果风头鹦鹉是你的*纹章鸟*呢？

En: Actually, that's exactly what I'm going to do, thanks for the tip. (Get task.)
Zh: “事实上，我正打算这么做，感谢你的提示。”（接受任务。）

En: You're being sarcastic. Let's talk about something else. (Move on.)
Zh: “你这是挖苦我。我们还是说点别的吧。”（继续。）

En: Wonderful. The store is open.
Zh: “太棒了。书店是开着的。”

En: Please, let's do.
Zh: “请吧。”

En: She doesn't really want to be disturbed that much...
Zh: 她真的不想被打扰……

En: Good, good. (Nod.)
Zh: “很好，很好。”（点点头。）

En: A good one? (Point at the book.)
Zh: “这是本好书吗？”（指着那本书。）

En: Yes, hello. She nods, her attention fully focused on reading.
Zh: “是的，你好。”她点点头，注意力完全集中在看书上。

En: Is that Loos, Radio City" you're reading?"
Zh: “你看的是‘卢斯，无线之城’吗？”

En: Yes, a sci-fi, she says without reverting her attention from the book.
Zh: “是的，一本科幻小说，”她回复到，不过注意力并没有从书本上移开。

En: What do you mean by that?
Zh: “你这话是什么意思？”

En: Kim, is it just me or do we have a missing person's case here?
Zh: “金，只是我这么觉得，还是我们碰到一起人口失踪案了？”

En: Don't worry, I'm going to find him and bring him back home, promise.
Zh: “别担心，我会找到他，把他带回家的，我保证。”

En: Well, if he's with his *friends*, then he's not really missing. No need for the police to get involved. (Don't take the case.)
Zh: “呃，如果他跟他的*朋友*在一起，那就不是真的失踪。不用警察插手。“（不接案子。）

En: You're right, unfortunately our forces are limited. I'm sure he'll return on his own. (Don't take the case.)
Zh: “你说得对，不幸的是，我们人手有限。我肯定他会自己回家的。”（不接案子。）

En: Not at all! The RCM is ready to chase down every goofball in town. We care about you!
Zh: “完全不会！RCM已经准备好追查城里的每一个傻瓜。我们很关心你！”

En: Honestly, I just think missing persons cases are mysterious.
Zh: “老实说，我只是觉得人口失踪案总是特别神秘。”

En: They're not all they're cracked up to be, officer.
Zh: “它们并不全是别人吹捧的那样，警官。”

En: He's right. Missing person cases most often involve runaway teenagers or demented old people. The victims usually turn up by themselves after a day or two spent missing. There's not much searching to do.
Zh: 他说得对。人口失踪案一般都是逃跑的青少年或者精神错乱的老人。这就意味着被害人通常会在失踪一两天后自己出现。没有多少搜查工作要做。

En: Okay, I get it. They're kinda boring, right? Because eventually everyone returns home on their own.
Zh: “好吧，我明白了。这种案子比较无聊，对吧？因为最后所有人都会自己找回家。”

En: Or they don't -- and you'll never find them, he adds with a flash of teeth.
Zh: “或者是不会——而且你永远也找不到他们，”他补充了一句。

En: She sighs, but you can detect a slight hint of gratitude and relief from her face.
Zh: 她叹了口气，不过你从她的脸上察觉到一丝感激和放松。

En: She just nods, still staring into the distance. Some seagulls fly over her head.
Zh: 她只是点点头，目光依然凝望远方。一群海鸥从她头顶飞过。

En: Alright, go ahead. Do you have any questions?
Zh: “好吧，继续。你还有什么问题吗？”

En: What kind of questions?
Zh: “哪一类的问题？”

En: The working class woman doesn't say anything, her eyes still fixed on something invisible in the distance. Some seagulls fly over her head.
Zh: 女工人什么也没说，眼睛依然固定在远方某个看不见的东西上。一群海鸥从她头顶飞过。

En: Well, he's... slightly chubby.
Zh: “呃，他……稍微有点胖。”

En: Hold on! Did she just laugh at disco? You're not going to leave it there, are you?
Zh: 等一下！她刚才是不是嘲笑迪斯科了？你不会就这么放着不管吧？

En: What I was trying to say before is that he's a little bit chubby, that's all. I didn't mean anything offensive!
Zh: “我之前想说的是，他有一点胖，就这些。我没有任何冒犯你的意思！”

En: It's his *cool jacket*. God knows it's too cold to run around in this, but he refuses to change.
Zh: 她叹了一口气，接着补充了一句，声音稍微有些颤抖：“那是他的*酷炫夹克*。天知道穿着它到处跑有多冷，但他就是不肯换。”

En: Well, if that jacket is really *that cool*, then I can totally understand...
Zh: “好吧，如果那件夹克真有*那么酷*的话，那我完全能够理解……”

En: He *took it out of the bin*? Wow. That's filthy.
Zh: “他*从垃圾堆里又翻出来了*？哇哦。那也太脏了吧。”

En: Well, what can you do... I hope that at least that extra lining helps him keep warm at night. I wouldn't like him to catch cold.
Zh: “哎，你还能怎么办……我只能希望那层额外的内衬至少能让他晚上感觉暖和一点。我可不想他感冒。”

En: What if something horrible has happened? What if they're dead. That's the bad vibe you got before.
Zh: 如果已经发生了什么可怕的事情？如果她们死了呢。你之前也有过这种可怕的共鸣。

En: Why did you say that your husband resembles me then?
Zh: “那你为什么说你丈夫跟我有点相似呢？”

En: She gives you a short nod, before returning to her books.
Zh: 她朝你微微点点头，然后又继续回去看书了。

En: Oh my...
Zh: “我的天啦……”

En: Not sure about this one...
Zh: “这我就不太肯定了……”

En: Wait... is this where he fakes his own death?
Zh: “等等……这本不是讲他自己假死的吗？”

En: Think I read this one already.
Zh: “我好像已经看过这一本了。”

En: Oh... it's a prequel?
Zh: “哦……它是前传吗？”

En: So melodramatic. Victor would hate it.
Zh: “太夸张了。维克托会很讨厌的。”

En: I really should get this. For myself.
Zh: “我真应该买这本。买给自己。”

En: It must be true, if it's in a book.
Zh: “如果书里是这么写的，那肯定是真的。”

En: Wonder if I could bring some of my old books here?
Zh: “不知道我能不能把自己的旧书拿到这里来？”
